Elizabeth marched up to the second floor and casually pushed open a door, phone wedged between her shoulder and cheek as she reached back to unzip her evening gown.
She needed to get out of the uncomfortable dress, but just as the zipper reached her waist, the bathroom door swung open.
Ethan Parker stood frozen in the doorway.
Elizabeth's heart skipped for just a moment before she composed herself, pretending not to notice him as she continued undressing while speaking into the phone. "Anyway, Andrew doesn't deserve your sacrifices."
"I know."
"Oh, I called to tell you I'm filming a series in Crestwood. Let's meet up when you have time."
"Definitely."
"I'm home now. Stop working so late and get some rest."
"Alright. Goodbye."
After hanging up, Elizabeth turned to find Ethan with his back to her, his ears already flushed crimson.
She smirked and said casually, "You've been with every type of woman out there, Mr. Parker. Why are you acting like an inexperienced boy?"
Ethan pressed his lips together and explained his presence. "There's a leak in my bathroom. I didn't know you were coming back tonight."
"My dear cousin insisted I return home." Elizabeth snorted. "Shouldn't you be with Amanda Mitchell tonight? I didn't realize you finished so quickly."
Ethan's Adam's apple bobbed, but Elizabeth had spoken offhandedly—she didn't actually expect an answer. "Anyway, I'm exhausted. Time for my bath and beauty sleep, so if you're done with the bathroom, Uncle Ethan, please leave."
"At least get dressed first—"
"Already am."
Ethan turned to see Elizabeth wrapped in a loose bathrobe, a cigarette poised between her fingers.
Though his lips twitched, Ethan kept his thoughts to himself and simply walked out.
As the door closed, Elizabeth's feigned composure dissolved into a scowl.
This was exactly why she had avoided returning all these years.

The night was still young elsewhere.
A slender figure stood before a glass wall overlooking the glittering cityscape.
Her fair fingers, polished in crimson, swiped casually across a tablet displaying dozens of high-definition photographs.
Each image showed William Levine gently draping his suit jacket over a woman's shoulders.
"Find out everything about her family background. I want every detail available—the more comprehensive, the better."
"Yes, ma'am," her bodyguard responded respectfully from behind her.

Morning arrived, and Elise Scott awoke with a throbbing headache.
How much had she drunk last night? She couldn't even remember how she got home!
The last clear memory was leaving the banquet hall—everything after that was hazy, including William...
She sat up abruptly and saw she was still wearing the same evening gown.
Her heart pounding, she threw off the blankets to get out of bed.
As she did, she noticed the band-aids carefully applied to her ankles.
Her heartbeat quickened further.
Her heels had been cutting into her skin all evening, though she'd never shown it.
She was surprised William had noticed and taken care of it.
She bit her lip.
She had to admit... he was dangerously perceptive.
###Chapter 50

Elise rose from her bed, her head throbbing with the remnants of last night's indulgence. Her eyes fell upon a glass of golden liquid resting on her nightstand. A small note was tucked beneath it.

She picked it up, her fingers tracing the sharp, familiar script. It was the same handwriting from the banquet invitation.

[Honey for the hangover. Eat breakfast when you wake up. An empty stomach after vomiting isn't good for you. P.S.: Business trip. Back in Crestwood in a week.]

She lifted the glass, the cool sweetness of the honey spreading through her, a soothing balm. A strange, fluttering sensation bloomed in her chest. Why had William been so insistent on driving her home last night? Was it because he was leaving, and they wouldn't see each other for a while?

Placing the glass down, she moved to the bathroom. She peeled off the stained gown, the memory of the previous evening clinging to it. After a long, hot shower, she tossed the garment into the trash. It was a freebie, no great loss.

Returning to the living room, exhaustion washed over her. The hangover demanded she lie down on the couch. But William's note echoed in her mind… an empty stomach…

She pushed herself up and headed to the kitchen. Just as she was about to prepare a simple meal, her phone vibrated on the counter. She glanced at the screen. Samuel. Her thumb moved to decline the call without a second thought.

She focused on making breakfast, the simple motions calming. Sitting at the table with her food, she scrolled through news feeds. Notifications from WhatsApp popped up. Samuel. Again. Multiple messages.

Her lips pressed into a thin line. Against her better judgment, she tapped the notification.

Photos filled the screen. William, his grip firm on Elizabeth York's arm. Elizabeth's face was a mask of annoyance, yet she wasn't pulling away. The dynamic between them was clearly complicated.

Another message arrived.

[Stop dreaming, Elise. William Levine will never be faithful to you. You're just a plaything to him, a pretty distraction. A man like that has countless women. You're just one of many.]

She remembered James saying his daddy liked Elizabeth.

Elise took a slow bite of her toast. Her fingers moved calmly over the screen.

[We're both adults, Samuel. Who's to say who's really playing whom?]

With that, she blocked his number.

At Mitchell Manor, Samuel had been awake for hours, anxiously scanning the news. Relief washed over him. There was nothing about Henry Levine's birthday, his own public humiliation, or any exclusive scoop on William and Elise. The Levines had clearly managed the media blackout.

But the relief was short-lived. The memory of the banquet, the utter degradation, surged back. He, Samuel Mitchell, accustomed to admiration and envy… How could he ever show his face again?

Clenching his jaw, he had sent those photos to Elise. If he was suffering, she would suffer too.

Her reply made his blood run cold, then boil. He typed out a furious, mocking response, his fingers flying across the screen.

A notification appeared instantly.

[Message failed to send. The recipient has blocked you.]

He nearly hurled his phone across the room. She blocked him?!

"Samuel." His father, Richard Mitchell, descended the stairs.

Samuel quickly composed himself.

"Our family was disgraced last night," Richard said, his voice heavy. "Your sister has been crying nonstop since she got home."

Samuel's family had left the banquet earlier than he had. He had been hiding in the shadows of the venue's garden, so he had missed the scene where William stood up for Elise and turned his intimidating attention toward Amanda.
###Chapter 51

Richard Mitchell's voice cut through the tense silence.
"Since Elise is clearly involved with William Levine now, you should publicly announce your relationship with Sophia. We kept it secret because we feared Elise would cause problems. That's why we waited for her to break off the engagement first. But now, that's irrelevant. If we delay any longer, we'll become the laughingstock of Crestwood."

Samuel nodded slowly. "I'll speak with Sophia. We'll make the announcement soon."

"Organize a proper, glamorous wedding," Richard commanded, though his anger from yesterday's events still simmered beneath his composed exterior. "We need to restore the dignity our family lost."

After Richard left, Samuel immediately called Sophia.

She was lounging in bed, scrolling through news articles on her phone. She noted with interest that there was no media coverage about yesterday's Levine Holdings banquet. The Levines truly operated on a different level—controlling exactly what the public could see.

When Samuel's call came through, she frowned before answering. "Samuel?"

"Did I wake you?" he asked.

She feigned a sleepy voice. "Yes, you did. What time did you leave last night? I didn't see you when my parents and I departed."

"Later than most," he said vaguely, clearly avoiding details about the previous evening. "My father thinks we should go public with our relationship."

"What?!" Sophia exclaimed, genuine alarm in her voice.

"Why are you surprised? Don't you want everyone to know we're together?"

"No, it's not that," she quickly recovered. "I'm still concerned about Elise causing trouble. If she decides to make a scene, neither of our families would be able to handle the fallout."

"Elise is with William Levine now!" Samuel snapped impatiently. "Do you really think she cares about us anymore?"

Sophia bit her lip. She had no intention of publicly committing to Samuel now.

Truthfully, she didn't want the relationship at all anymore. He'd lost his appeal the moment Elise showed no interest in him.

Besides, Sophia had set her sights on a much bigger prize.

"I'm still worried, Samuel," she said, already formulating her excuse.

"About what?" His tone grew irritated.

"You know how vindictive my sister can be. Even if she's with William now, she might still try to sabotage us. I can handle the personal attacks, but your reputation as CEO of Mitchell Corp could suffer."

She spoke with such apparent concern for his wellbeing that Samuel actually hesitated, remembering how formidable Elise had become recently.

"It hasn't even been two months since your planned wedding with Elise. People will talk if we announce our relationship too soon. Sometimes it's better to wait. What matters is that we're together, right? What others think shouldn't affect us."

Samuel considered her words and found them reasonable. "I suppose I'll have to ask you to wait a little longer then."

"As long as I'm with you, nothing else matters," Sophia purred sweetly.

They exchanged affectionate words for a few more minutes before ending the call.

The moment she hung up, Sophia's expression hardened, her eyes turning cold and calculating.

For now, she would keep Samuel on the hook—a safe backup plan in case her new ambitions failed.

Not that Samuel was a bad option... but Sophia had no intention of failing.

That perfect man would belong to her, one way or another.
###Chapter 52

A full week had slipped by.

Elise Scott had already pulled two late nights at the office.

Now, she sat at her desk, meticulously organizing the completed design drafts.

She was acutely aware that relying on the design department was futile. They were all loyal to Sophia Mitchell.

Picking up her phone, she instructed Matthew Johnson. "Call a meeting in half an hour. I want all department heads and the design supervisors present. We're reviewing the templates for the upcoming season's collection. Attendance is mandatory."

"Understood, Ms. Scott."

Exactly thirty minutes later, every executive was seated in the conference room at Stellar Group headquarters. Their punctuality was superficial, a mere formality to avoid giving Elise any grounds for criticism.

That was the extent of their cooperation. None of them intended to do any real work. They were simply waiting for Stellar to fail and for Elise to be ousted.

As always, Elise got straight to the point. "The design team has been working tirelessly with significant overtime. We've finalized the templates for next season. I'm very pleased with the results, and I want everyone to see them."

A murmur rippled through the room. Sophia was thoroughly confused.

The design department had produced nothing of substance all week. How could the templates be finished? Missing the deadline for the next season's launch was the expected outcome, and Sophia was anticipating Elise's humiliation.

She watched quietly as Elise presented the first design. Her jaw nearly dropped, as did those of the other department heads.

The design was a radical departure from Stellar's stale aesthetic. It boldly employed vibrant colors and intricate traditional floral patterns, creating a stunning, modern fusion. It perfectly captured the current international trends. It was dazzling.

All the department heads, poised to criticize, were rendered speechless. The design supervisors stared, utterly bewildered.

They had worked at Stellar for years, but they had never seen designs of this caliber.

Who in the world had created these?

Elise calmly elaborated on the design concepts for the season. "I want to thank everyone in the design department for their contributions, and especially Ms. Mitchell for her relentless dedication. Finance, please ensure a $500 bonus is added to this month's payroll for every design team member. Ms. Mitchell will receive a $3,000 bonus."

The design department staff and Sophia were completely lost. When had they contributed to these designs?

The other department heads, however, were visibly displeased. Hadn't they all agreed to withhold help from Elise?

It seemed they had been collaborating with her all along, producing these exceptional templates!

"Since there are no objections," Elise declared, "we will proceed with production using these templates, adhering to our standard production quotas. Sophia, please see me in my office. The rest of you are dismissed."

With that, Elise exited with Matthew. Sophia headed toward the office under the disapproving glares of her colleagues.

"You wanted to see me, Elise?" She maintained a respectful tone, unsure of Elise's intentions.

"I want to discuss changing our brand ambassador."

"Replacing Elizabeth York?" Sophia exclaimed in surprise. "But she's been performing well. She's been our ambassador for three years."

"Her contract is also nearing its expiration."

"Her fee is reasonable, and she's very cooperative and easy to work with. Is a change really necessary?"

"She lacks a distinct personality. She doesn't stand out in the competitive fashion landscape."

Sophia frowned. "Do you have a specific replacement in mind?"

"Zoe York."
###Chapter 53

Sophia’s eyes lit up instantly. “Elizabeth York?! She’s one of the most sought-after names in Hollywood right now. Her endorsement fees are astronomical—definitely seven figures, maybe more. Our budget at Stellar Group could never stretch that far.”

“Let me handle the financial side,” Elise replied calmly. “I just need your opinion on her suitability.”

There was no denying Elizabeth’s star power. Industry insiders often called her the ‘brand ambassador queen.’ If Stellar Fashion could secure her, their market position would skyrocket overnight.

“You have excellent taste,” Sophia admitted, though privately she doubted Elise could pull it off. “But are you sure this is feasible?”

“Leave the details to me,” Elise said with a quiet confidence. “That’s all for now. You can return to your duties.”

Sophia studied her sister for a long moment. Everything about Elise seemed different today—more determined, more strategic.

She considered asking about the sudden change but decided against it. The design templates Elise had presented were far superior to anything Stellar had produced before. If word got out that Elise was the creative force behind them, her reputation would soar.

Some questions were better left unasked.

As Sophia turned to leave, Elise’s voice stopped her at the door.

“Sophia, wait.”

“Yes?”

“Please keep our conversation confidential. Don’t mention anything until we have something concrete.”

Sophia paused, then nodded in understanding. “Of course. Your secret is safe with me.”

Elise watched her sister disappear down the corridor, a faint smile touching her lips.

From the corner of her eye, she noticed one of the secretaries watching them intently before quickly returning to her computer.

Perfect. The conversation had served its purpose—to stir curiosity among the department heads and their informants.

Back in her office, Elise began mapping out her strategy.

Her interest in Elizabeth York wasn’t just a whim. Stellar Fashion needed a dramatic rebranding to compete in the crowded luxury market. While strengthening their internal operations was crucial, a powerful brand ambassador could provide the external boost they needed.

Elizabeth, with her impeccable reputation and widespread appeal, had been on Elise’s radar for months. Her research showed that Elizabeth hadn’t signed any fashion endorsements yet, though several major brands were courting her.

Time was of the essence.

Elise pushed aside any personal reservations. Business came first.

She picked up her phone and scrolled through her contacts until she found the name she needed.

The line connected after two rings. “Ms. Scott,” a warm, familiar voice answered. “This is a pleasant surprise.”

“Mr. Parker,” she began, “I hope I’m not disturbing you. I have a favor to ask.”

She’d gotten Ethan Parker’s number after he’d taken her to the hospital a year ago and insisted on paying her medical bills. She’d kept it to reimburse him, though he’d never accepted the money.

“Of course. What can I do for you?” His tone remained friendly and open.

Even though they weren’t close, Ethan had always been approachable.

“I understand Elizabeth York is starring in your current project,” Elise said. “I’d like to discuss an endorsement opportunity with her, but I don’t have any direct connections. Would you be willing to make an introduction?”

Silence stretched on the other end.

Elise’s confidence wavered. Had she overstepped?

“If it’s too much trouble—” she began.

“No, it’s not that at all,” Ethan interrupted. “I’m just surprised you’re asking me instead of… someone else who might have more influence with her.”
###Chapter 54

Elise knew that if William made the call, she wouldn't need any referral to secure Elizabeth York's endorsement.

"From my perspective, you're the perfect person for this, Mr. Parker," Elise stated confidently.

Ethan didn't hesitate. "I'll check her schedule and get back to you."

"Thank you."

"My pleasure."

On the other end, Ethan was sitting before a video monitor, taking a brief break between shooting scenes.

He turned to his assistant. "Bring me Elizabeth York's schedule for the next week."

"Right away, Mr. Parker."

Soon, Ethan had the schedule in his hands. After studying it carefully, he stood and moved to a quiet corner to make a call.

"William," he said when the call connected.

"Yes?"

"Elise Scott just contacted me." Ethan got straight to the point.

Silence filled the other end of the line, and Ethan smiled faintly. "She wants me to arrange a meeting with Elizabeth to discuss an endorsement deal."

"Is that so?"

"I agreed. If everything works out, they could meet this evening."

Their filming schedule remained relatively light since production had just begun.

"You two seem quite close." William's cool voice carried a distinct edge.

"Not at all—just acquaintances who happened to cross paths," Ethan said with a light chuckle. "Anyway, I wanted to give you a heads-up. I need to get back to work now."

William abruptly ended the call, and Ethan chuckled again.

The man was as predictably petty as ever.

Ethan slipped his phone back into his pocket and turned to find Elizabeth.

She noticed the smile on his face but remained expressionless as she walked past him.

"Elizabeth," Ethan called out, and she paused.

"You only have two scenes this afternoon, so you should be finished by four."

"And your point is?" Elizabeth asked flatly.

"A friend wants to discuss an endorsement with you. If you're available, you could have dinner with her—"

"I'm having dinner with Samantha." Elizabeth cut him off immediately. "If it's about an endorsement, talk to my manager. You have her number, don't you? Or do you need me to give it to you again?"

"It's your brother's friend, Elise Scott," Ethan said bluntly.

At Henry Levine's birthday banquet, she had been observing from a corner while her maternal grandfather, parents, and brother greeted guests. She had certainly noticed the drama involving her brother and Elise.

Though her brother had spent years abroad, she kept close tabs on his personal life. He never had any women around him, except for one particular woman... But since nothing had happened between them after all this time, it seemed unlikely anything would.

Yet somehow, Elise had managed to break through his celibate streak?!

Fine.

Elizabeth could admit to herself that she felt at least somewhat curious about Elise.

"I'll arrange the time and place if that works for you," Ethan continued.

"Are you coming along?" Elizabeth asked. "To the dinner, I mean."

Ethan met her gaze steadily. "I can stay out of it if you prefer."

"Then let me know once you've made the arrangements."

"Will do."

As Ethan watched her walk away, his expression darkened slightly.

He texted Elise the details, confirming the time and location.

Elise was surprised by his efficiency. He had handled the matter so quickly! Still, she had sensed his reliability ever since he took her to the hospital. After thanking him, she sent another message: [Would it be appropriate if I visited the set?]

She wanted to demonstrate her sincerity as a potential business partner.

[Of course. Elizabeth should finish around four.]

Ethan sent her the location as well.

Elise checked the time and called Matthew. "Order some premium confectionery from Pheasant Court and have it delivered to the address I'm sending you by three o'clock. They're filming Pomp and Circumstance there right now. Just say it's from Elizabeth York."

"Yes, Ms. Scott."

"Also, you'll accompany me there at two."

"Understood."

After hanging up, Elise returned to her work, determined to complete everything before leaving.
###Chapter 55

The clock struck two in the afternoon.

Elise arrived at the suburban filming location with Matthew.

Elizabeth and Ethan were shooting their period drama series there.

A massive castle town had been constructed as the primary set.

A crew member escorted Elise toward Ethan.

He was seated behind a large video monitor, intently directing the scene.

Ethan glanced up, offered a brief, polite nod, then returned his focus to his work.

Elise settled quietly nearby, observing the organized chaos.

Elizabeth and her co-star, Benjamin Abbott, were taking their positions for the next shot.

It was her first time witnessing a professional film set in action.

"Scene three, sequence one, take one. And... action!" Ethan's voice cut through the air.

In an instant, Benjamin cornered Elizabeth against a faux-stone wall.

Their eyes locked in a tense, charged stare.

Elise was immediately captivated by the raw emotion in Elizabeth's gaze.

It was mesmerizing.

Benjamin began to lean in, his lips slowly closing the distance to hers.

A flicker of conflict shone in Elizabeth's eyes before they fluttered shut.

A single tear escaped, tracing a path down her cheek.

Their lips were millimeters from touching when Elizabeth abruptly turned her face away.

The spell was broken. Both actors dropped character.

"Cut!" Ethan's voice was sharp with frustration.

"I'm so sorry," Elizabeth murmured, quickly wiping her tear. She addressed the crew. "My mistake."

The script had called for a kiss, and she had avoided it.

Ethan took a calming breath. "Five-minute break for this unit. We're moving to the next scene first."

Elizabeth stepped off the set, looking drained.

Her assistant, Penelope, rushed over with a bottle of water. "Elizabeth, are you alright? What happened?"

"Nothing. I just... lost focus for a second," Elizabeth replied, her voice soft.

"Kissing scenes are always awkward with new co-stars," Penelope sympathized. "Should I have Ms. Linda speak with the director? We could reschedule it."

"No," Elizabeth said firmly. "That's not necessary."

She knew most productions filmed intimate scenes early to help build chemistry.

This wasn't about Benjamin. It was something deeper, a visceral reaction she couldn't control.

"Okay. Let's get you to your trailer to rest for a bit."

Elizabeth nodded. As she turned to leave, her eyes landed on a familiar face. She blinked in surprise.

"Elise Scott?"

"Ms. York," Elise greeted with a warm smile, stepping forward to shake her hand. "It's a pleasure to meet you in person. You're even more stunning than on screen."

"The feeling is mutual," Elizabeth said earnestly. "It's a real shame you aren't an actress."

She genuinely found Elise's beauty striking. Her features were precisely to Elizabeth's own aesthetic taste.

Her brother certainly had an impeccable eye.

"You're too kind. My acting skills are practically non-existent," Elise demurred with a light laugh.

Elizabeth smiled. "Ethan mentioned you're interested in discussing an endorsement?"

"Yes, but I don't want to interrupt your work. Perhaps we could have dinner after you wrap up today?"

"That works for me," Elizabeth agreed. "Actually, a friend is joining me for dinner tonight. I hope you don't mind if she comes along?"

"Not at all. The more, the merrier."

"Oh, and thank you for the confectionery delivery. That was you, right?"

"It was. My pleasure."

"You really shouldn't have, but thank you."

With the pleasantries concluded, Elizabeth continued toward her trailer.

Elise watched her retreating figure.

She had anticipated potential friction, but Elizabeth was proving to be far more approachable than expected.
###Chapter 56

Elise drew a sharp breath.

She had always maintained a strict separation between professional matters and personal affairs.

Her phone screen lit up with another message notification. She glanced down at it, then deliberately looked away.

William sat in the executive office of the Levine Holdings branch in Los Loco.

He frowned at his silent phone, which refused to chime.

Samuel Wilson stood nearby, not daring to utter a single word.

The branch inspection had gone exceptionally well. Every operation and project was performing above expectations.

Yet his boss radiated palpable tension.

"Samuel."

"Yes, sir?"

"Book the earliest flight tomorrow. We're returning to Crestwood."

"But we have that crucial meeting with the city's key figure tomorrow afternoon," Samuel reminded him. They had scheduled important client and partner meetings during this inspection trip.

"Then book an evening flight," William amended curtly.

But he was determined to return to Crestwood tomorrow, no matter what.

"Understood, Mr. Levine," Samuel responded respectfully. They wouldn't finish until midnight, but William's mind was clearly elsewhere.

Samuel had noticed William constantly checking his phone since arriving in Los Loco. Sometimes he simply stared at the dark screen, lost in thought.

Love certainly made powerful men behave unpredictably.

Back at the film set in Crestwood, they were reshooting the kiss scene.

After collecting herself, Elizabeth had spoken with Benjamin Abbott to ease the lingering awkwardness between them.

The camera assistant called out, "Scene three, sequence one, take two. Action!"

Elizabeth and Benjamin immediately slipped into character.

Since the movement sequence from the first take was usable, they only needed to redo the kiss.

Once again, Benjamin pinned Elizabeth against the wall and leaned in slowly.

But just as their lips were about to meet, Elizabeth turned her head away again.

"My apologies."

This time, Benjamin was left hanging awkwardly mid-air.

"Cut!"

Ethan rose from the camera monitoring area and strode directly toward the actors. "Elizabeth, a word please."

Elizabeth pressed her lips together and followed him to a quiet corner.

"I'm cutting the kiss scene. Prepare for the next scene instead," Ethan told her firmly.

Elizabeth stared at him in surprise.

She had expected him to reprimand her for delaying the shooting schedule.

She had never worked with Ethan before and wouldn't have taken this role if not for her brother's insistence.

Still, the entertainment industry was small, and she'd heard plenty about Ethan Parker - the most handsome director in the business, but also the most demanding.

Rumors said he would make actors retake scenes over a hundred times until he was satisfied. Some even called him the 'Director Demon'.

"No, that's not necessary," Elizabeth refused firmly.

Ethan raised an eyebrow as she continued, "If the kiss wasn't essential, it would have been cut from the script during pre-production. I don't want to be responsible for changing the creative vision."

With those words, she walked past Ethan decisively.

His Adam's apple bobbed as he watched her retreat.

The script hadn't been written with Elizabeth in mind. It was completed before the sudden lead actress change and had received investor approval. While changes were difficult before shooting, adjustments could certainly be made during production based on circumstances.

He returned to the camera area while Elizabeth and Benjamin prepared to try again.

"Scene three, sequence one, take three. Action!"

Benjamin approached Elizabeth once more. She closed her eyes tightly, her hands balling into fists at her sides.

In her five-year career, Elizabeth had appeared in over ten films and drama series. Yet would anyone believe this was her first on-screen kiss?
###Chapter 57

The kiss finally happened between Benjamin and Elizabeth.

The camera smoothly transitioned from a wide establishing shot to an intimate close-up.

The lens clearly captured Benjamin sliding his tongue into Elizabeth's mouth.

Elizabeth's eyes widened in shock.

Her hands tightened into white-knuckled fists at her sides.

Yet she remained perfectly still until Ethan's sharp voice cut through the tension. "Cut!"

The moment the word was spoken, Elizabeth shoved Benjamin away with surprising force.

The actor knew exactly why she was furious, but he couldn't control himself.

She was breathtakingly beautiful.

Her lips felt impossibly soft and inviting.

He might have continued the kiss indefinitely if the director hadn't intervened.

If Elizabeth hadn't physically pushed him away.

Without a backward glance, Elizabeth turned and began storming off the set.

She didn't care whether the take was usable or not.

"Elizabeth, wait!" Benjamin called after her, quickly catching up.

She spun around to face him, her expression glacial.

"I'm sorry, I just..." Benjamin stammered an apology before lowering his voice. "Ethan told me to commit fully to the scene. He said it needed to feel authentic for our characters to connect..."

A cold, mocking smile touched Elizabeth's lips. "So Ethan Parker specifically instructed you to use your tongue?"

Benjamin immediately realized his mistake.

Her earlier anger had transformed into something sharper, more dangerous.

Her eyes were blazing with barely contained fury.

"Yes, for authenticity's sake," Benjamin murmured defensively.

"Hmmm..." Elizabeth's smirk deepened.

That man had promised he could modify the kissing scene for her.

Instead, he'd allowed this actor to take liberties.

She should have expected such deception from him by now.

As she led her assistant toward her trailer to remove her makeup, Elise was already waiting outside in her car.

She had witnessed the entire scene, including Benjamin's inappropriate behavior.

She'd also noticed Ethan sitting rigidly beside the camera, his expression dark.

"Sorry to keep you waiting," Elizabeth said as she slid into the car.

She had already donned a baseball cap, face mask, and oversized aviator sunglasses.

"It's no problem. My schedule is clear for the rest of the day," Elise replied politely.

"Let's go."

Elise instructed Robert to drive them to an upscale restaurant.

Only after they were seated in the private VIP room Elise had reserved did Elizabeth remove her disguise.

Just as they were settling into their chairs, the door opened again.

"Samantha," Elizabeth greeted warmly.

Samantha Summer entered, her curious gaze immediately landing on Elise.

"This is Elise Scott, my bro—"

Elizabeth caught herself mid-sentence. "She's discussing a potential endorsement deal with me."

"Since when did you become so business-oriented?" Samantha teased, clearly comfortable with the additional company.

"Please have a seat, Ms. Summer," Elise said, extending her hand. "I'm Elise from Stellar Group."

"I recognize you," Samantha replied with a friendly smile. "You're Samuel Mitchell's fiancée."

"Former fiancée," Elise corrected smoothly.

Samantha looked momentarily flustered. "My apologies."

"That chapter is long closed," Elise said dismissively, deftly changing the subject. "It's a pleasure to meet you."

"The feeling is mutual."

The three women settled around the table. Elise had sent Matthew home earlier since this was strictly a ladies' gathering. As waiters began serving their meal, Elise spoke seriously. "Ms. York, if you're comfortable, I'd like to discuss a potential endorsement opportunity with Stellar Fashion..."

Elizabeth responded with surprising bluntness. "I've reviewed your company's portfolio. Stellar Fashion's designs are rather conventional. They don't stand out significantly from what other brands are offering."

Having never spoken with Elizabeth before, Elise hadn't expected such directness.

In her experience, most successful entertainment industry figures tended to be diplomatic to a fault.

She found Elizabeth's honesty refreshing.
###Chapter 58

Elise didn't waste any time. She got straight to business. "Since you're being direct, Ms. York, I'll do the same. You might not know this, but Stellar Group was under my father's management until recently. I've taken over now. What I mean is, while Stellar Fashion's previous designs were mediocre, that's changing immediately. Here are our templates for next season. Please take a look."

Elizabeth studied Elise carefully.

She didn't know much about Elise personally, but she'd heard all the rumors. The dramatic stories circulating about her. Elizabeth had assumed Elise was just another socialite relying on men, especially after learning about her history with Samuel Mitchell.

But Elise's straightforward confidence was changing Elizabeth's perception. She was clearly capable, direct, and knew how to stand her ground.

Apparently, rumors were just that—rumors.

Elizabeth accepted the tablet Elise offered. Her eyes immediately widened when she saw the designs.

She'd agreed to this dinner mostly out of obligation. Earlier, bored in her trailer, she'd browsed Stellar Fashion's online catalog. Nothing had impressed her. She knew she couldn't refuse this endorsement deal anyway—not with her own brother pushing for it.

But these new designs completely transformed her opinion of Stellar Fashion.

Elise's earlier statement—'that's changing immediately'—was clearly no exaggeration.

"These are impressive," Elizabeth acknowledged with a nod.

Elise pressed her advantage. "Since our products meet your standards and you're willing to endorse us, let's discuss your fee."

"What's your offer?" Elizabeth asked.

"Your standard endorsement rate is three million dollars, correct?" Elise maintained eye contact. "We're prepared to pay that amount."

"You can have a discount," Elizabeth said bluntly.

Elise was taken aback.

"I'm worried I might literally break a leg if I don't," Elizabeth joked lightly.

Elise grew even more surprised.

"Just pay me half. Otherwise my management company will protest. One point five million—that's my price for becoming Stellar Fashion's brand ambassador." Elizabeth paused meaningfully. "Exclusively."

Meaning she wouldn't endorse competing brands while representing Stellar.

Elise felt both delighted and uneasy.

Elizabeth was clearly doing her a huge favor—far beyond what she'd expected.

She'd thought the best outcome would be a polite refusal from Elizabeth, especially since she'd attended Henry Levine's birthday banquet.

Elizabeth must have seen William defending her that night. That alone should have ruined any chance of negotiation!

"Meet my manager Linda tomorrow with your contract. I'll notify her in advance."

"Thank you, Ms. York. Here's to a successful partnership for both of us." Elise extended her hand.

Elizabeth shook it warmly. "Let's be friends from now on. Call me Elizabeth."

"Elizabeth," Elise responded immediately. "And please call me Elise."

Elizabeth nodded, then asked, "Do you drink?"

"Of course."

"Can you handle your liquor?"

"Reasonably well."

"Then let's get properly drunk tonight. It's a special occasion," Elizabeth proposed.

"Don't you have filming tomorrow?" Samantha reminded her.

"That's in the afternoon. My morning is completely free." Elizabeth waved dismissively. "I invited you here to drink, remember?"

Samantha knew Elizabeth too well. Nobody was leaving sober tonight.

They began drinking enthusiastically.

At first, Elise held back, still unfamiliar with the other women. But after several drinks, and discovering how down-to-earth Elizabeth was compared to her public persona, the atmosphere became lively and cheerful.

Female friendships formed easily, especially over good drinks.
###Chapter 59

The clock had long passed eleven.

Three women stumbled out of the bar, their laughter echoing in the quiet night.

Elise's vision swam slightly. She blinked, trying to clear the haze. "Let me give you both a ride home," she offered, her words careful.

Elizabeth let out a loud burp. "No need! Samantha and I are heading the same direction." She grabbed Samantha's arm, pulling her toward the waiting car.

Elise followed them outside, the cool night air helping clear her head slightly.

Though they'd all been drinking, none had completely lost control. Samantha appeared remarkably composed considering how much they'd consumed.

Elise usually maintained strict limits, but meeting Elizabeth for the first time called for exceptions. The actress had an impressive drinking capacity, matching glass for glass without slowing down. Yet Samantha had kept pace without seeming affected.

Elise watched them climb into a dark sedan before returning to Robert's car. She slumped into the leather seat, her stomach churning uncomfortably.

Through the window, Crestwood's skyline glittered against the dark horizon. Countless lights painted patterns across the cityscape.

Her phone chimed.

She glanced at the screen. [Remember to drink honey water when you get home.]

Elise didn't respond, simply lowering the device onto her lap.

William had been texting her daily since his departure. She read every message but never replied.

Meanwhile, the dark sedan pulled through Levine Manor's gates. Elizabeth had already fallen asleep in the backseat.

Just moments earlier, she'd been bragging to William about getting Elise properly drunk. Now she slept soundly, her breathing even.

Samantha marveled at Elizabeth's ability to pass out instantly after drinking, yet wake up completely refreshed. The woman truly possessed a unique talent for handling alcohol.

Samantha stepped out of the car, attempting to rouse her friend. As she struggled to support Elizabeth's weight, another car pulled up to the entrance.

Ethan emerged from the driver's side.

Spotting them, he approached quickly. "What happened?"

"Elizabeth had too much to drink," Samantha explained weakly.

Ethan frowned slightly.

"Could you help carry her inside?" Samantha asked, her arms straining. "I'm not strong enough..."

"Of course." Ethan nodded, moving toward the car. He bent down, effortlessly scooping Elizabeth into his arms.

Samantha watched, impressed. Though Ethan always appeared refined and gentle, he clearly possessed surprising strength.

"Drive safely, Ms. Summer," he said politely.

"Thank you." Samantha nodded.

As Ethan carried Elizabeth toward the manor, Samantha watched their retreating figures. The sight triggered a memory from long ago.

She and Elizabeth had been high school classmates. During their sophomore year, Elizabeth had called her late one night, bursting with excitement about falling in love.

Back then, their feelings were pure and unrestrained. Samantha could still recall the sheer happiness radiating through the phone.

But after that night, Elizabeth never mentioned her romantic life again.

Eight years had passed since that conversation.

Yet Samantha remembered clearly—Elizabeth had confessed the boy's name was Ethan Parker.
###Chapter 60

After Ethan put Elizabeth to bed, he lingered in her room.

His eyes remained fixed on her face, flushed from the wine they had shared earlier.

Her lips looked particularly tempting, moist and slightly parted in sleep.

He couldn't help but recall the passionate kiss scene she had filmed earlier that week.

Swallowing hard, he reached out with his long fingers, gently tracing her lips as if trying to erase something.

He continued this solemn ritual for what felt like an eternity, until her lips became swollen and crimson.

Just as he began to lean closer, he caught himself and straightened up abruptly.

He turned and walked out, closing the door firmly behind him.

He never saw the single tear that escaped from beneath Elizabeth's closed eyelids and traced a path down her temple.

Elise woke with a pounding headache.

Going to work while hungover was absolute torture.

Elizabeth could hold her liquor far better than she could.

Elise had spent half the night vomiting, feeling as if her world had been turned upside down.

She sipped her black coffee, hoping it would clear the fog in her brain.

Matthew entered her office politely. "Ms. Scott? The contract is ready for signing. Elizabeth York's manager called to confirm they're available to sign today."

Elise took a deep breath, rising from her chair despite the persistent throbbing in her temples. "Let's get this done."

Though she trusted Elizabeth after their evening together, part of her still found the endorsement deal too good to be true.

Having William involved in their dynamic made everything more complicated.

Getting the contract signed would finally put her mind at ease.

"Keep this confidential until the official announcement," Elise instructed Matthew, repeating the same warning she had given Elizabeth and her manager.

"Understood, Ms. Scott." Matthew nodded promptly.

She felt grateful for her new secretary—her previous one would have leaked information everywhere.

The thought of how much of Stellar Group remained under Benjamin Scott's control made her feel utterly helpless.

Still feeling unwell, Elise decided to leave work early.

She returned to her apartment, ate some takeout, took a long shower, and collapsed into bed.

Out of habit, she checked her phone before sleeping.

She opened WhatsApp, but found no new messages from William.

Well, that was to be expected.

There was no need for formal goodbyes when nothing had truly begun between them.

They could simply go their separate ways without any lingering attachments.

Elise set her phone aside and fell into a deep sleep.

She slept soundly until persistent doorbell chimes jolted her awake.

Blinking in the darkness, she saw that it was still pitch black outside.

The city seemed unusually quiet at this hour.

What time was it?

Still disoriented, she glanced at her phone—3:07 AM.

Just as she wondered if she was dreaming, the doorbell rang again.

Someone was actually at her door at this ungodly hour!

Elise quickly got up and went to check the door monitor.

William stood outside, looking surprisingly composed for the late hour.

Her heart skipped a beat.

Hadn't he said he would be away for a week?

It had only been four days.

More importantly, she had assumed things were over between them after not hearing from him all day.

Even with her limited knowledge of him, she knew he had his pride.

After a moment's hesitation, she finally opened the door.

His cheeks were slightly flushed, and she could smell alcohol on him.

Yet he stood perfectly straight, maintaining his usual regal composure.

"Did you get the wrong apartment?" Elise asked, her voice still heavy with sleep.

William simply smiled, his gaze slightly unfocused. "I'm looking for Elise Scott."

That bewitching smile of his... Men could definitely use their beauty as a weapon too.
###Chapter 61

Elise grumbled, "I was sleeping."

"My apologies for disturbing you." William offered his regret.

Still, she never sensed any genuine remorse in his apologies—as if he merely spoke the words out of obligation.

"Is something wrong?"

"Just wanted to let you know I've returned."

"Noted," Elise replied coolly, then added upon noticing his weary appearance, "You could have just texted. There was no need to come all this way."

"I assumed your phone was dead," he retorted, clearly implying she had ignored his messages.

"I've been occupied," Elise simply provided an excuse.

"Elise…"

"It's late, you should go home," Elise interjected, cutting him off. "I have work in the morning."

As William swallowed his unspoken words, she concluded, "Goodnight."

With that, she closed the door firmly in his face, leaving him staring at the solid wood barrier.

He had been right—it wasn't his imagination.

Elise was deliberately creating distance between them.

But he hadn't done anything inappropriate that night... In fact, his instincts told him Elise was beginning to open up to him.

So what had changed? Why was she pushing him away again?!

William left Elise's apartment building and noticed the car that had brought him was gone.

Scowling, he dialed a number.

Samuel answered immediately when his phone rang. "Yes, Mr. Levine?"

"Where are you?" William demanded coldly.

"Weren't you visiting Ms. Scott?"

"Get back here now!"

"Right away, sir!" Samuel promptly instructed the driver to turn the vehicle around.

Even as they headed back, Samuel wondered if Elise had actually kicked William out.

Incredible... She truly was different from other women!

Any other woman would be thrilled to have William spend the night at their place, even throwing themselves at him!

They quickly returned to where William's car waited, and Samuel stepped out, opening the door respectfully.

William fixed his gaze on Samuel, who flinched—he dreaded these silent confrontations most.

Predictably, William ordered, "Your phone and wallet."

Samuel's hands trembled as he handed everything over.

William then slid into the car and barked, "Drive!"

The sedan sped off, leaving Samuel stranded on the empty street at 3 AM, watching the taillights disappear...

If he had any tears left, he would be crying them now.

Over the following month, Elise poured all her energy into the autumn fashion line launch, overseeing everything from major strategic decisions to the smallest details.

However, most of Stellar Group remained under Benjamin Scott's control. Beyond other challenges, production delays were becoming increasingly severe.

"What's the problem?" Elise demanded, her gaze sharp as she confronted Jonathan Locke, the production manager, in the conference room.

There was no doubt Jonathan was on Benjamin's payroll too!

"It's not us causing delays, Ms. Scott—we can't do anything when the workers are striking," Jonathan said with a dismissive shrug.

"Striking? For what reason? And why wasn't there any advance notice? You know our timeline is tight!"

"You're the board chairman and always busy. How could we bother you with every minor company issue?" Jonathan replied with a sarcastic tone.

"So you're saying you can handle this situation?"

"I'll do what I can, but I can't make any promises."

"Understand this clearly, Mr. Locke," Elise stated with authority. "If the company suffers any financial losses, I'll hold you personally responsible for every dollar! You'd better decide whether you can bear that responsibility!"

Jonathan flushed deeply at her direct threat!
###Chapter 62

With those final words, Elise Scott turned and walked out of the conference room. Her exit was as commanding as her entrance.

Jonathan Locke couldn't believe a woman in her twenties had just threatened him, a man in his forties. He immediately called Benjamin Scott to report the situation.

"Don't worry about her. Just follow my instructions," Benjamin said dismissively, still underestimating his daughter. "I'll handle any consequences."

"Yes, Mr. Scott."

A sinister smile spread across Jonathan's face.

He would wait and see how long Elise could maintain this act.

Elise brought Matthew Johnson with her to Stellar Group's production plant. She wondered why the workers had suddenly decided to strike.

The facility was located in the outskirts of Crestwood, requiring a long drive that lasted until afternoon. Despite skipping lunch, Elise went straight inside to meet Patrick Longman, the factory supervisor.

Like Jonathan, Patrick showed no respect toward Elise. They were clearly both aligned with Benjamin.

"I heard our new chairwoman was young, but this young?" he remarked, his tone dripping with sarcasm. "You're about the same age as my daughter, who still runs to me for everything. The comparison really stings, doesn't it?"

Elise ignored his condescending words and got straight to business. "Jonathan Locke informed me about the worker strike. What's happening here?"

"What does it look like? They're not being paid enough!"

"According to our records, wages here match industry standards, and our benefits package exceeds competitors..."

"But you're the one who accelerated production, demanding overtime. Without proper compensation, nobody's happy, are they?"

Elise narrowed her eyes.

Nobody had reported the plant's actual situation to her. Under these circumstances, she would have authorized special overtime pay.

She had to admit Benjamin had been systematically undermining her all along.

"Any other questions, Ms. Scott? I have work to attend to if you're finished," Patrick said before walking away.

Elise pressed her lips together and turned to leave with Matthew. Before they could reach the front gate, an angry mob of workers surrounded them.

"You're the new chairwoman, aren't you?" shouted the lead worker, his face contorted with anger. "Forcing overtime without pay—where's your conscience? Pay us now, or you're not going anywhere!"

"You think you can just replace us? Never! I've worked here for seven years! Don't think you can force us out!"

"Pay us! Pay us now!"

The workers' shouts grew louder as they tightened the circle around Elise and Matthew.

Matthew moved to shield Elise, concerned they might become violent.

"Everyone calm down!" Elise raised her voice. "Once I verify the situation, everyone will receive proper compensation. No Stellar Group employee will be treated unfairly!"

"Lies!" the lead worker snapped. "She'll forget about us the moment she leaves! Don't let her escape!"

"Pay up or you're staying right here!"

Elise was shocked by the workers' aggression. Clearly, this strike had been carefully planned.

Even so, she never would have realized how serious things were if she hadn't inquired about the production delays herself.
###Chapter 63

Dean stood by the second-floor office window, a sinister smile twisting his lips. Below, Elise Scott was surrounded by angry workers. They shouted and shoved, trapping her in their midst.

He pulled out his phone, his fingers moving quickly. "Mr. Scott? The workers have Elise surrounded. She won't be leaving anytime soon."

In his spacious office at Scott Enterprises, Benjamin Scott took a slow drag from his cigarette. A cruel smile played on his lips. "Make sure she learns her lesson," he commanded.

"Understood, sir. I'll handle it perfectly."

Benjamin hung up, satisfaction washing over him. Experience always triumphed over youthful arrogance. Elise thought she could challenge him? It was like an egg trying to shatter a boulder.

Across from him, Sophia looked up, her expression one of mild surprise. "Father, Elise actually went to the production plant?" She couldn't believe her stepsister had the nerve to venture into that den of lowlifes. Those people were crude, uneducated brutes, destined to remain in their miserable poverty.

"Don't concern yourself with her," Benjamin dismissed with a wave. "I called you here to discuss something more important."

"Of course," Sophia replied, her voice sweet and compliant.

"The Mitchells contacted me. They want to officially announce your relationship with Samuel Mitchell. They're even pushing for a quick marriage." He paused, studying her. "They mentioned you seem... hesitant."

Sophia bit her lower lip, a carefully crafted look of unease crossing her features. "Father, I'm not sure now is the right time."

"Why? I thought you were in love with him."

"I am, but..." She let out a soft, worried sigh. "Our family's current situation concerns me more. Elise has clearly entangled herself with William Levine. You saw them together at Henry Levine's birthday banquet."

"William is merely amusing himself with her," Benjamin scoffed, unconcerned. "A man of his standing? His family would never accept a woman plagued by scandals like Elise."

"But I saw how he looked at her. His interest seemed genuine."

"She's cast some sort of spell on him. So what? Men are easily tempted by a pretty face. I should know..." Benjamin caught himself, remembering he was speaking to his daughter. He cleared his throat. "It's a fleeting infatuation. Once he grows bored, he'll discard her."

"Even if that's true, she is connected to him now. And we both know how much she despises us. What if she persuades William to target our family?"

Benjamin's confidence wavered. Elise was notoriously stubborn. If she truly enlisted William Levine's power against them, Scott Enterprises would be crushed instantly.

"What do you propose?" he asked, his tone turning serious.

Sophia met his gaze, her eyes filled with feigned determination. "I believe I should pursue William Levine myself."

Benjamin was taken aback. His delicate, refined daughter was suggesting something so bold.

"If I can win his affection, our family would be secure. I'm willing to do this for our family's sake. To protect us, I'll set aside my own feelings and sacrifice my relationship with Samuel." Her voice trembled slightly, painting a picture of noble self-sacrifice.

The idea appealed to Benjamin immensely. An alliance with the Levine family would elevate the Scotts to unchallenged prominence in Crestwood. No one would dare oppose them again.

"And what about the Mitchells?" he pressed.

"Let's not refuse them outright. Please, Father, find a way to manage them, to keep them hopeful. If I succeed with William, the Mitchells become irrelevant. If I fail... they remain a viable option for us."

Benjamin considered her plan, and a wide, approving grin spread across his face. "Very well. I'll deal with the Mitchells. You focus entirely on captivating William Levine. Whatever resources you need, they are yours."

"Thank you, Father." Sophia gave him her most angelic smile.

She refused to believe she couldn't capture a man that Elise had managed to seduce.
###Chapter 64

The atmosphere at the Stellar Group production plant was explosive.

Angry workers surrounded Elise Scott, their shouts drowning out her attempts to speak.

She knew this was Benjamin Scott's doing. Simon had orchestrated this chaos.

Arguing with this manipulated mob was pointless. They wouldn't listen.

Elise made a quick decision.

She leaned closer to Matthew, her voice barely audible over the roar. "Find a way out. Call the police."

"But you'll be alone here, Ms. Scott-"

"I can handle this. Just go."

Matthew nodded, slowly releasing her arm as he began maneuvering through the crowd.

The workers' attention remained fixed on Elise, allowing Matthew to slip away unnoticed.

He was impressed by her composure under pressure, though he feared they might not escape unscathed.

After making the emergency call, police sirens soon pierced the air.

The sound ignited fresh fury among the workers.

"They brought cops!"

"Elise Scott called them! She never intended to resolve this!"

The scene descended into greater chaos as police officers rushed into the plant.

Elise seized the distraction, trying to push through the turbulent crowd.

Matthew watched anxiously from the sidelines, his eyes never leaving Elise.

Suddenly, his blood ran cold. "Ms. Scott! Behind you!"

A worker swung a steel pipe directly at Elise's back.

Her heart hammered against her ribs. This impact could cause serious injury.

Trapped with no escape route, and police too distant to intervene, she braced for impact.

A heavy thud echoed through the sudden silence.

Even the attacker seemed stunned by his own actions.

Police immediately subdued the man, pinning him to the concrete floor.

Elise, expecting searing pain, found herself instead enveloped in strong, protective arms.

She opened her eyes to discover William Levine shielding her, having taken the full force of the blow.

"Mr. Levine!" Samuel Wilson stood frozen nearby.

When William learned Elise was heading to the factory, he'd abandoned everything—including a critical afternoon meeting—racing to North City's industrial district.

They arrived to find workers rioting and police swarming the plant.

Samuel had no idea how William had spotted Elise in the chaos or moved fast enough to protect her from that swinging pipe.

"William..." Elise breathed, her voice trembling.

William coughed, pain evident in his strained voice. "I'm alright."

But how could he be fine after such a brutal strike?

"Don't be ridiculous!" Elise turned to Samuel. "Get him to a hospital! Now!"

"Right away!"

Samuel rushed forward to help support William, his face pale with concern.
###Chapter 65

Elise hesitated for a brief second.
Then she quickly slipped into William's car after him.

"Miss!" One of the officers called out. "We need you to accompany us to the station to give your statement."

"Matthew, go with the police. Robert will drive you home later."

"Of course. You should go, Ms. Scott," Matthew agreed immediately.

Meanwhile, Elise and Samuel helped William into his Maybach.

The car sped toward the finest private hospital in Crestwood.
Samuel sat in the front passenger seat, already calling the Levines' family physician.

Elise kept stealing glances at William.
He was leaning back against the seat, eyes shut.
His face grew paler with each passing moment.

Where exactly had that steel pipe struck him?
Had it fractured any bones?
Could it have caused internal bleeding?

Elise refused to let her thoughts spiral further.
Her eyes remained fixed on the car's navigation system, desperately wishing they would arrive at the hospital instantly.

Still, the journey took two long hours.

A line of white-coated doctors was already waiting when they pulled up.
Nurses swiftly helped William out of the vehicle.

The moment someone touched him, Elise distinctly heard William groan in pain.

Was he in that much agony?!

Elise's fingers curled into tight fists.
She followed closely as the medical team rushed William toward the emergency room on a gurney.

"It's going to be alright," William managed to reassure her, just before the doors closed behind him.

Elise bit down hard on her lower lip.
A chaotic storm of emotions swirled inside her regarding this man.

It had been over a month since he returned from his business trip.
She had maintained a firm, neutral distance from him since.

She would join him for dinner if James was present.
But she spoke only to James throughout those meals, pointedly ignoring William and barely looking his way.

William had made efforts initially.
Eventually, he seemed to grow accustomed to her cold shoulder.
He became quieter, more reserved himself.

Yet, he still visited her with James every couple of days.
She assumed it was solely to appease the little boy's clear attachment to her.

That was precisely why his sudden appearance today had thrown her completely off balance.

Elise stared anxiously at the sealed emergency room doors, her knuckles white with tension.

Three hours later, the doors finally swung open.
William was wheeled out, surrounded by doctors and nurses.

Samuel rushed forward immediately. "Doctors, how is he?"

Elise stayed where she was, her heart hammering violently against her ribs.

"He's stable. However, he has extensive soft tissue damage across his back. There's a minor fracture to one right-side rib. We also noted slight renal contusion. We recommend strict bed rest for up to four weeks. We'll monitor his recovery progression before determining the next steps."

Samuel released a loud, relieved breath. Thank God his boss was okay.

Elise felt her own composure slowly return.

"We're transferring him to his private room now," one of the doctors informed them.

"Okay, yes, of course," Samuel responded quickly.

The group moved toward the prepared VVIP suite.

William lay silently in the hospital bed, eyes closed.
It was impossible to tell if he was in pain or simply resting.

Samuel and Elise listened intently to every instruction and precaution the medical team provided.
It felt like an eternity before the doctors finally departed.

Several nurses and physical therapy staff remained in the room, however.

Eventually, Elise found herself drawn to William's bedside.
She looked down at his handsome, albeit strained, features.
His lips were pressed into a thin, tight line.

"Does it hurt?" she asked softly.

William's dark eyelashes fluttered.

Slowly, he opened his eyes, his gaze meeting hers directly.

"Yes," he admitted, his voice a low rasp.

"Then why did you do it? Why risk yourself like that? What if that pipe had hit your head instead?" The terrifying 'what if' still gripped Elise's heart, the potential consequences too horrifying to fully contemplate.

"Because..." William's voice was hoarse, each word measured. "It would have hurt me far more... if it had struck you."
###Chapter 66

Elise's heart fluttered unexpectedly.

She couldn't deny the surge of emotion coursing through her.

Still, she maintained her composure and spoke softly. "But you'd do the same for anyone, wouldn't you?"

Meaning he would sacrifice himself for any woman in his life!

"Only for you," William stated with absolute seriousness.

"Are you implying I'm special?" Elise questioned.

William raised an eyebrow, clearly recognizing the trap in her question.

Before he could respond, she changed the subject. It was his personal life after all.

She had no right to pry.

"Why did you come to the production plant today?" she asked instead.

"Robert told me you were going. I was concerned about your safety, so I went to check." William answered.

"And I arrived just in time."

The alternative...

William didn't want to imagine the guilt he would feel if Elise was lying in this hospital bed instead. His expression darkened at the thought.

"My father orchestrated this," Elise confessed. "He never wanted me leading Stellar Group. He's been creating obstacles to force me out."

"Do you want my assistance?"

"I'm telling you this because I don't want your interference," Elise said firmly.

As William narrowed his eyes, Elise immediately understood his intentions and her gaze turned equally cold.

"I will absolutely get justice for you! Those responsible will face consequences!"

She had reached her limit with the Scotts—even after their past betrayals, she had never sought revenge because she was still a Scott.

They could have maintained boundaries and left each other alone.

But her compromises only made them bolder.

Their actions were becoming increasingly severe.

And she had no reason to show mercy to people who refused to recognize her goodwill.

"Understood," William said.

He respected her decision and would allow her to handle it, as long as the situation remained within his control.

Elise's phone suddenly vibrated.

She checked the screen, then stepped onto the balcony to answer. "Matthew?"

"Ms. Scott? I've submitted the official statement. The rioters and Gene Larkin—the one who attacked you—are in custody. The police are asking if you want to settle privately."

"Absolutely not, and make it perfectly clear to them," Elise said decisively. "We're pursuing full legal action."

"Understood, Ms. Scott."

After ending the call, Elise pressed a hand against her stomach. The pain was inevitable after not eating all day.

She took a steadying breath before returning to the hospital room, settling into the chair beside William's bed.

"You should rest," Elise told him.

"Will you leave when I fall asleep?" he asked.

Elise pressed her lips together—that had been her plan.

She needed food, and there was still chaos at the plant requiring her attention.

"I'll stay with you," she finally conceded.

At the very least, she owed him her presence for today.

The answer seemed to satisfy William, and he closed his eyes, drifting into peaceful sleep.

Elise's phone buzzed again—she quickly switched it to silent mode, not wanting to disturb William.

She declined the call immediately when she saw Benjamin Scott's name.

She knew exactly why he was calling—he must have learned she refused to settle privately and wanted to negotiate.

But she powered off her phone completely—she wouldn't give him the satisfaction!

"Ms. Scott." Samuel Wilson approached her quietly, his tone respectful.

"Yes?"

"Please, come eat something," he said.

Elise looked surprised, so Samuel explained, "Mr. Levine asked me to prepare this for you."

Elise couldn't help glancing at William, but he remained fast asleep.

He had noticed her stomach pain? But how?

She hadn't shown any outward signs...

Once again, that unfamiliar feeling washed over her.

But ultimately, they came from different worlds. She couldn't afford to blindly trust anyone ever again.

Even when she felt moved, even when her heart softened, she couldn't truly reciprocate his feelings.
###Chapter 67

William was so flawless that Elise sometimes questioned if he was real.

She moved to the small table and ate a warm bowl of oatmeal. It soothed her empty stomach.

Lately, she had been too occupied to rest properly. After finishing her meal, exhaustion washed over her.

She leaned back against the sofa in the hospital room. Sleep claimed her quickly.

When she awoke, darkness had fallen. Someone had draped a blanket over her without her noticing.

"You're awake." William's deep, captivating voice broke the silence, startling Elise. For a moment, she forgot where she was.

William smiled, finding her disoriented expression endearing.

Elise flushed with embarrassment and rose from the sofa, struggling to appear composed. She glanced at the wall clock. She had slept for more than three hours. She was supposed to be caring for the patient, yet she had slept more soundly than him. "Do you need to use the restroom?" she asked, attempting to diffuse the awkwardness... only making it worse. "I mean..."

"Yes," William replied immediately.

Elise stared at him. He returned her gaze with an innocent look. "Well? Aren't you going to help me?"

"N-No, I mean..." Elise shook her head. "How would that work? With a bedpan?"

"Not while I'm lying down."

"Then..."

"I can get up," he stated.

"I'll call a nurse..."

"You can help me stand. I'm not that heavy," William said cheerfully.

Elise was speechless. His weight wasn't the concern!

Gritting her teeth, she carefully helped him sit up. "The doctors said you need bed rest. Are you sure you should be moving?"

"Absolutely."

Elise decided not to argue further. When nature called, it was urgent.

As she helped him to his feet, he let out a low grunt of pain.

"Are you hurt?" she asked anxiously.

"No."

'He's so stubborn,' Elise thought silently.

It took considerable effort to support him to the bathroom.

Once inside, she tried to leave, but he stopped her. "I can't stand on my own."

Meaning he would fall if she let go.

"I'll be fast," he added.

Elise was speechless again. That wasn't the point!

She turned her back to him, allowing him to lean on her for support.

Not heavy?! He felt like a solid wall of muscle!

She nearly buckled under his weight. What were men made of? He wasn't even overweight.

Then she heard the distinct sound of splashing. Her cheeks flushed bright red.

The sound seemed to go on forever. What happened to being quick?

Did he have a different definition of 'quick'?

Finally, the splashing stopped. "Are you done?" Elise asked impatiently.

"Sorry for the wait," William apologized.

"So you knew you were taking forever?!"

"Well, I've been told it's a sign of healthy kidney function."

Elise was rendered speechless once more.

Especially since he had been on IV fluids all day!
###Chapter 68

Elise struggled to help William back to his hospital bed.

He was staying in a luxury suite that resembled a penthouse more than a medical facility. Nurses and physical therapists waited respectfully in the living area, while Elise found herself alone with William in the private bedroom. His assistant Samuel was conspicuously absent.

The atmosphere grew tense with just the two of them.

"Would you like some fruit?" Elise asked, breaking the silence.
"Yes."

She glanced at the fruit basket nearby. "An apple?"

"Perfect."

"Alright."

Elise selected an apple, washed it thoroughly, and began peeling it awkwardly.

She never kept fruit at home, disliking them generally. Between her time abroad, working at Mitchell Corp, and now managing Stellar Group, she barely had time for proper meals, let alone fruit preparation. The apple peel came off in jagged, uneven strips.

She looked up sheepishly, catching the amused glint in William's eyes. "Finished?" he asked.

"If you don't mind the presentation," she said, handing him the poorly peeled apple.

William examined it thoughtfully. "It has character."

Elise couldn't tell if he was complimenting or mocking her.

"Are you going to eat it?"

"Feed me, please."

"Can't you manage yourself?"

"I'm too weak."

But he had just walked to the bathroom unassisted!

Still, Elise held a slice of apple to his lips.

His mouth closed around the fruit, his lips brushing softly against her fingertips.

A shiver ran through her at the warm, moist contact.

As she watched him chew, he asked, "It's surprisingly sweet. Would you like to try some?"

"No, I really don't care for fruit," Elise refused a bit too quickly.

William chuckled softly but continued eating the apple slices she offered.

When he finished, Elise hurried to the bathroom to wash her hands.

Upon returning, William changed the subject to ease the tension. "Do you have stomach issues? My uncle mentioned you had gastrointestinal bleeding before."

"I overdid it with drinking that time," Elise said dismissively.

"Was that the only occurrence?" William pressed.

"...No," Elise admitted reluctantly. "Women face disadvantages in business drinking situations. I drink as much as possible to avoid being taken advantage of."

William's throat moved visibly. "Samuel Mitchell didn't deserve that level of dedication."

"No, he didn't," Elise agreed.

Back then, she had done everything short of compromising her dignity to secure investors for Mitchell Corp.

All that effort, only to be told she was too strong, too independent, and that being with her made him feel inadequate.

Reflecting on Samuel's breakup words now felt deeply ironic.

"Everything I do from now on will be for myself," Elise declared firmly, as much to herself as to him.

William started to respond when Samuel knocked politely on the door. "Mr. Levine?"

William's lips tightened as he turned toward his assistant, who looked appropriately apprehensive about interrupting. "Your dinner has arrived."

The last time William had abandoned him roadside, Samuel had walked three hours to get home.

The memory still haunted him.

Samuel only entered after receiving William's nod, placing the meal containers on the bedside table before preparing to leave.

That's when Elise spoke up. "Mr. Wilson?"

"Yes, Ms. Scott?" Samuel responded humbly.

"Your boss claims he's feeling weak. Could you please feed him?"

Samuel's jaw nearly dropped.

But William had eaten breakfast independently after waking up!

Samuel glanced uncertainly at William, catching the dangerous look in his eyes, and quickly agreed. "Of course."

The room's atmosphere immediately turned peculiar.

While Elise ate her meal with apparent nonchalance, Samuel fed William under the man's intense, displeased glare.
###Chapter 69

Samuel took his time feeding William, finally finishing the task before practically fleeing the room.
He did remember to politely lock the door behind him though.

Elise glanced at the clock, noticing it was already 10 PM—definitely time for her to head home.
Just as she was about to speak, William's voice interrupted her thoughts. "Could you help me with a sponge bath?"

She turned toward him, and he continued, "The doctors said I shouldn't shower yet."

She knew that medical instruction perfectly well—but was it appropriate for her to be the one doing it?!

"I'm not comfortable with male attendants touching me," William explained, and Elise immediately recalled that all the rehabilitation staff were indeed men.

"I don't think I'll be able to sleep feeling this unclean," William added softly. "Please."

Elise drew a deep breath at that moment.
She believed in returning favors, and this time she owed William far more than just a simple favor.

She walked to the bathroom, filled a basin with warm water, and soaked a soft towel before returning to his bedside.

"Close your eyes," Elise instructed. "I'll start with your face."

William complied obediently, feeling only the gentle, warm pressure of the towel moving carefully across his features.

After wringing out the towel, Elise moved to his neck and collarbone area. "Should I continue?" she asked.
"Yes," he responded quietly.

Elise set down the towel, mentally preparing herself before unbuttoning William's hospital gown, revealing his well-defined chest.

It wasn't as if she was unfamiliar with male anatomy—or shirtless men, for that matter.
She'd supervised numerous photoshoots with male models who often worked shirtless.

Yet she felt heat rising to her cheeks as she observed the perfect, tempting muscular contours of William's chest and abdomen.
Even through the towel, she could feel the firm yet resilient texture of his muscles.

She meticulously wiped him down, moving methodically to his arms and legs.
By the time she finished, she was breathing heavily, unsure whether from exertion or... something else entirely.

Just as she picked up the basin to leave, William's voice stopped her. "You missed a spot."

Elise stiffened slightly, her fingers tightening unconsciously around the basin.
Give him an inch, and he'd definitely take a mile!

"I can manage that part myself," William added, but Elise was already clenching her jaw.
No matter how she looked at it, he was clearly doing this intentionally!

"I'll get you fresh water," she said curtly before returning to the washroom for clean water and another towel.

William noticed her reaction, a faint smile touching his lips as he accepted the towel from her.

Elise deliberately looked away, and after what felt like an eternity, he announced, "Done."

She turned back to him but instructed him to drop the towel into the basin rather than taking it directly from his hands.
With that task completed, she returned to the washroom and disposed of the used towel.

When everything was settled, she announced, "I'm leaving now."

"Will you come tomorrow?" William asked.

Elise remained silent—there was overwhelming work waiting for her at Stellar Group.

"That's fine if you can't make it," William continued. "I'll just be here alone, feeling isolated and lonely. No one to keep me company, and this injury does ache quite a bit... but it's nothing serious, really."

Elise was rendered speechless.
How could anyone be so deliberately manipulative?!
###Chapter 70

Elise gave him her word. "I'll come by tomorrow after work."

"Perfect." William's lips curved into a genuine smile, no pretense of politeness.

He was utterly cunning.

After Elise left the hospital, Samuel entered the room, his demeanor respectful. "Mr. Levine."

"Help me to the restroom," William instructed.

Samuel couldn't resist commenting. "But sir, you had the perfect opportunity earlier... Why didn't you ask Mrs. Levine to assist you?"

William shot him a sharp glare.

Samuel immediately corrected himself. "Right, of course. You're always proper, sir. You wouldn't take advantage."

William's expression darkened further. He simply didn't want Elise to think his kidneys were weak from frequent bathroom trips.

As Samuel supported him toward the restroom, William suddenly paused.

"Should I help you with your trousers, sir?" Samuel offered eagerly.

He had definitely fallen out of William's good graces since his boss fell in love.

Still, he knew when to be obsequious, always seizing the chance to please.

"Get out!" William snapped.

Samuel flinched, exiting with a wounded expression. What had he done wrong this time?

It felt like all his efforts were pointless...

Meanwhile, William stared at the towel in the wastebasket.

Just how much did Elise despise him?!

The next morning, Sophia was waiting outside Elise's office when she arrived for work. She followed Elise inside.

"Dad tried calling you all day yesterday. Why didn't you answer?" Sophia demanded, her voice tight with anger.

"I was at the hospital. My phone died," Elise replied coolly.

"You could have called him back! Don't you know how worried he was?"

Elise settled into her chair and fixed Sophia with an icy stare. "Are you certain he was worried?"

Sophia's heart skipped a beat. She realized she couldn't deceive Elise.

"I have a lot of work to do," Elise stated dismissively. "You can leave if there's nothing important."

Clenching her teeth, Sophia had no choice but to exit. She immediately called Benjamin.

Within moments, Elise's phone rang.

It was about time. She'd kept him waiting for an entire day.

"I've had enough of your games, Elise Scott! Why were you ignoring my calls?!"

Elise let out a cold laugh. "Why should I answer? Do you think you're some kind of monarch?"

"Elise Scott...!"

"I'm busy. I'm hanging up if you have nothing meaningful to say."

"Wait!" Benjamin quickly stopped her. "What's this I hear about taking the factory riot to court? What are you thinking?! Don't you realize this will become public knowledge?! Are you trying to destroy Stellar Group?!"

"Even if I am, that's my concern, not yours!" Elise retorted firmly. "That riot gave me PTSD, and I will see this through to the end."

"You wretched...!" Benjamin gnashed his teeth in fury, but Elise remained unmoved.

After all, he was the one who orchestrated the riot at the manufacturing plant.

Once investigations began, he would inevitably be implicated, landing him in serious trouble.
###Chapter 71

Simon struggled to contain his rising anger. "What will it take for you to agree to a private settlement?"

Elise responded with a cold laugh. "Why this sudden urgency to handle things privately? I would have been severely injured if William Levine hadn't intervened and taken that hit for me."

"I understand you've been through an ordeal, but I'm doing this for Stellar Group's reputation," Simon said, his voice softening deliberately. "A public scandal would damage the company, and we're still family, aren't we? After dedicating so many years to Stellar Group, I've developed genuine affection for it. I don't want our numerous employees to suffer because of this incident."

Elise scoffed inwardly. Despite Simon's polished words, she knew his real concern was preventing any connection between the attack and himself.

Nevertheless, she conceded. "Well, since you've framed it that way, I suppose a private settlement isn't entirely out of the question."

"Really?" Simon's voice brightened with evident relief.
