"But I have several non-negotiable conditions."

"What conditions?"

"First, the person responsible must cover all of William's medical expenses. This includes medication costs, hospitalization fees, rehabilitation treatments, and compensation for psychological trauma. He was hospitalized because of this attack, after all."

Simon's face immediately darkened.

The heir to the Levine fortune would naturally receive treatment at the finest hospital with premium care. The costs would be astronomical, and since the actual perpetrator couldn't afford it, Simon would have to foot the bill himself.

Still, he agreed through clenched teeth. "Fine."

"Second, the culprit must offer personal apologies to both William and me. They must also provide written assurance that they won't cause further trouble for Stellar Group."

"Fine," Simon repeated without hesitation.

This condition cost him nothing.

"Third, Jonathan Locke and Patrick Longman must resign from their positions as head of production and plant manager respectively."

Simon immediately refused. "Absolutely not! How could you demand that?!"

If those two were forced out of Stellar Group, they would no longer be able to manipulate company affairs from within. Moreover, how would his other loyal informants perceive this betrayal? He would undoubtedly lose their allegiance!

Elise countered calmly, "Their poor management directly caused the riot in the first place. Demanding their resignations is completely reasonable, isn't it?"

Simon had to restrain his temper once more. "But who would manage production if they resign?"

"I will," Elise declared, emphasizing each word.

"That's impossible!" Simon exclaimed, his agitation rising.

"Nothing is impossible," Elise stated firmly. Not wanting to prolong the discussion, she continued, "Agree to these three conditions, and I'll accept the private settlement. Otherwise, we'll meet in court."

She prepared to end the call.

"Fine! It's a deal!" Simon growled, his teeth grinding in frustration.

How had Elise managed to corner him like this?

They had always controlled her before... now she was threatening them!

As Simon's blood pressure spiked from sheer aggravation, Elise added, "Once all three conditions are met, I'll personally visit the police station to settle matters. But you need to act quickly. The authorities won't wait indefinitely."

With those final words, Elise ended the call. Immediately, several text notifications appeared on her screen.

[My back hurts even more after waking up. The pain medication must have worn off.]

But he hadn't been given any painkillers yesterday!

[I think they changed the apple variety. These aren't sweet at all.]

They were the exact same apples from yesterday!

[Am I interrupting your work? Just trying to distract myself from the discomfort. You don't need to respond if you're busy.]

Elise sighed. The cunning man was at it again.

She remembered that most of her work would remain stalled until Jonathan and Dean resigned, leaving her with some free time.

Without further hesitation, she stood up, collected her laptop, and headed toward William's VVIP suite.

However, she paused at the entrance. Inside, Sophia was flashing her signature charming smile, looking perfectly at ease.

"Hey, sis!" Sophia called out cheerfully, her demeanor appearing genuinely enthusiastic and completely unawkward.

Elise couldn't help but smile to herself. Sophia truly adored her 'belongings'!
###Chapter 72

Sophia dialed Samuel the moment she stepped out of Elise's office. She drove directly to the hospital where William was recovering.

She had been anticipating this opportunity.

Previously, she had no way to get close to William, fearing she might push him away. Now, with his hospitalization, she had the perfect reason to visit.

Men had always been easy for her to charm.

She knocked gently on the hospital room door before entering. A vibrant bouquet was in her hand, and a brilliant smile was plastered on her face.

Seeing the incredibly handsome man lying in the bed, she greeted him sweetly. "Hello, Mr. Levine."

William regarded her with a cool, detached gaze. She quickly introduced herself. "I'm Sophia Mitchell. Elise's sister."

"Half-sister," William corrected flatly.

A flicker of annoyance crossed Sophia's features, but she swiftly masked it. "My sister is incredibly busy, so she asked me to check on you. I also want to apologize on behalf of Stellar Group for the worker who injured you. The entire incident is deeply regrettable…"

William's judgmental stare remained fixed on her. "Elise sent you, you claim?"

"Yes, but please don't be angry with her, Mr. Levine. She just took over Stellar Group recently. The workload is immense. She simply couldn't get away right now," Sophia explained, feigning defense of her sister.

Internally, she was confident. She had stolen Samuel from Elise before. William would be no different.

"Really?" William's tone dripped with sarcasm. He mercilessly exposed her lie. "My memory is quite clear. You stole her fiancé. Are you certain she would send you here after that?"

The verbal slap stung, but Sophia recovered quickly. "That's a misunderstanding, Mr. Levine. The situation between me, my sister, and Samuel is complicated–"

"I have zero interest in you or Samuel Mitchell," William interrupted coldly. "I wish to rest. Since you are irrelevant to me, please stop disturbing my peace."

He was directly dismissing her. Sophia felt a surge of humiliation.

She had taken extra care with her appearance today. Yet, William hadn't even given her a proper look before telling her to leave.

"Very well, Mr. Levine. I won't impose further. I wish you a speedy recovery," she said, swallowing her pride.

She knew she couldn't be too aggressive while William was still infatuated with Elise. She needed to keep things natural.

This strategy had worked perfectly on Samuel. Sophia felt a smug satisfaction that she had used him as her test subject.

"Oh, I brought these flowers for you. I hope you like them." She maintained her coquettish smile as she placed the large bouquet on his bedside table. "I also want to apologize for mistaking you for someone else when we first met. I hope you can forgive that little error."

The door swung open before William could respond. Elise stood in the doorway, having witnessed the entire scene.

"Sis!" Sophia appeared completely composed, though inwardly she was startled by Elise's swift arrival. Elise should have been swamped with work today, especially with the autumn collection launch.

Had someone tipped her off? Had she rushed straight here?

She was getting clever.

"Well, since you're here, I'll be going now." Sophia smiled, as if graciously making way.

She walked past Elise with an air of nonchalance. Suddenly, William's voice stopped her. "Wait."

Elise's eyes widened slightly. Her lips pressed into a thin line. Sophia wasn't a stunning beauty, but she was a master at enchanting men.

Sophia herself was surprised, and delighted.

She had thought William disliked her. Now he was calling her back.

Sophia couldn't resist shooting Elise a fleeting, triumphant glance before turning around.

Her eyes wide with feigned innocence, her tone became even more flirtatious. "Were you calling for me, Mr. Levine?"

"Take your flowers with you. I'm allergic to them," William stated bluntly.

The air in the room seemed to freeze solid.
###Chapter 73

William's words left Sophia frozen in place.

Her face paled instantly before she forced tears to well in her eyes.

"I'm truly sorry," she whispered, voice trembling. "I had no idea you were allergic to lilies. I'll be more careful next time."

"It's not lilies I'm allergic to—it's you." William's voice cut through the air like ice. "Keep your distance from me."

Sophia's composure shattered completely.

No man had ever spoken to her like that. Not since she was a child.

They always fell for her delicate, vulnerable act.

This had to be Elise's doing.

William was only saying this to impress Elise!

Swallowing her humiliation, Sophia gathered the lilies and fled the room.

Elise watched her retreating figure, feeling a strange sense of satisfaction.

Men had always flocked around Sophia since their school days.

Elise never understood the appeal.

Couldn't they smell the hypocrisy radiating from her?

When Sophia stole Samuel from her, it felt like the ultimate betrayal.

Elise could have handled losing Samuel to anyone else. But Sophia? It made her question if any man could resist Sophia's charms.

Until today.

Whatever happened next, this moment felt like sweet revenge.

"Hey." William's voice pulled her from her thoughts.

Elise stepped fully into the room and took a seat beside his bed. "I told you yesterday I'd make it up to you after you recovered."

"And?" he prompted.

"Right now, my father's loyalists are embedded throughout Stellar Group. I need to win them over and separate them from my father. That's why I can't expose the culprit immediately—I lack solid evidence. Plus, with Stellar Group already vulnerable, I can't afford another crisis. Compensation is our only option."

"Compensation?" William raised an eyebrow.

"I know money means nothing to you, but it's all I can offer."

"Actually, you could do much more..."

Elise pressed her lips together. She wasn't naive enough to misunderstand his implication.

"For instance," he continued, "you could take care of me until I'm discharged."

Elise stared at him, surprised.

That wasn't what she expected him to say.

"Disappointed, Ms. Scott?" William's lips curved into that heart-stopping smile.

Her pulse quickened.

She realized he was offering her a more comfortable alternative, sparing them both awkwardness.

If not for her past, she might have been brave enough to take a chance on him—even with his complicated personal life and the uncertain outcome.

But she couldn't gamble again.

She knew she wouldn't survive another loss.

"Alright," Elise agreed.

She owed him this much, after all.
###Chapter 74

Elise remained in William's hospital room, keeping him company while working on her laptop. There was still a substantial amount of work demanding her attention.

She became so deeply absorbed in her tasks that she occasionally missed what William was saying to her.

He didn't interrupt her focus. Instead, he quietly picked up a nearby apple and began peeling it with careful precision.

Samuel Wilson happened to be passing by the room and glimpsed the scene through the doorway. His jaw nearly dropped. Was that really his boss playing the attentive caregiver? He quickly retreated, not wanting to disturb the intimate moment.

Meanwhile, Elise was reviewing several authorization requests that required her approval. Her concentration was broken when she noticed a small plate of meticulously sliced apple pieces beside her laptop. Each slice was neatly speared with a toothpick for easy eating. She absently picked one up and ate it.

It had been ages since she'd last eaten an apple. She'd almost forgotten how satisfyingly crisp and sweet they could be.

After the first slice, she reached for another, her eyes never leaving the computer screen... until a sudden realization dawned on her.

There were only two people in this room.

If she hadn't prepared the apples, then...

She abruptly looked up and found William watching her from his bed, a soft smile playing on his lips.

"You did this?" she asked, gesturing toward the plate.

William shrugged casually. "Who else was going to?"

"But you're the patient here," she reminded him.

"I still have full use of my hands," he countered smoothly.

Hadn't they just agreed that she would be taking care of him? And hadn't he claimed just yesterday that his hands were too weak to function properly?

She remembered how she'd basically shoved chunks of apple into his mouth yesterday, unlike these perfectly sliced pieces he'd prepared so thoughtfully.

No matter how she looked at it, her caregiving efforts seemed downright negligent compared to his.

"I'm not really a fruit person," she explained, attempting to justify her lack of attention to such details.

"I noticed," William nodded, his eyes drifting to the nearly empty plate where only two small apple slices remained.

"This apple tastes different," she tried again, searching for another explanation for her unusual appetite.

"Well, I'm honored that you enjoy..." William grinned before finishing, "...my apples."

That wasn't what she meant at all! She absolutely wasn't suggesting the apple tasted special because he'd peeled it!

She took a deep breath, suddenly realizing she was digging herself deeper with each attempted explanation.

Pursing her lips slightly, she simply turned back to her computer screen, determined to refocus on her work.

"Can I ask you something?" William's voice broke the silence just then.

Elise had more or less completed her immediate tasks. Now she was just waiting for Benjamin to fulfill his part of their arrangement.

"What is it?" she asked, turning to face him again.

"Why did you become so distant after I left on that business trip?" William asked, his tone shifting to complete seriousness.

The question caught Elise completely off guard.

He'd never brought this up before, almost as if it hadn't mattered to him.

And it had happened so long ago—why would he mention it now?
###Chapter 75

Elise remained silent, her gaze lowered.

William's voice cut through the quiet. "Don't pretend you haven't been avoiding me. I can feel it."

His meaning was clear—he wouldn't tolerate any lies from her.

She met his eyes steadily. "I thought you understood why."

"I don't," he stated flatly, refusing to play games.

"I've told you before—I don't believe in love," Elise said, her voice soft but firm. "My rejection stands. This is just your persistence talking."

She wouldn't admit it was because of Elizabeth York.

If he truly didn't want to be with her, there would be no other woman in the picture.

"So you're saying my efforts were actually working?" William pressed, seeking confirmation. "That's why you're pulling away?"

Elise hesitated, then nodded silently.

What other explanation could there be?

William was dangerous territory—she couldn't afford to get too close.

"Then I've failed to make you feel secure," William concluded. "I'll be more restrained from now on."

"Or you could simply let go," Elise suggested seriously. "We can remain friends. James adores me, and I won't deny—I've grown fond of him too. You could even make me his godmother, if you're willing."

"Not a chance, Ms. Scott."

His refusal was immediate and absolute, leaving no room for negotiation.

"You'll either be James's mother, or..." William paused, his eyes locking with hers.

Elise wondered if he meant to say 'or nothing.'

"Or my wife."

She was speechless—it was the same thing!

"Exactly," William said, reading her expression. "You don't get to choose."

"You'll regret this, Mr. Levine," Elise warned, knowing further argument was pointless.

"I already do," he murmured quietly.

"What?"

"I need to use the restroom," William said abruptly.

Elise blinked. Nature's call certainly had interesting timing.

She stayed until William fell asleep before leaving the hospital.

Late that night, Benjamin finally called. Fabian Locke and Dean Longman would submit their resignations tomorrow. He would also bring the culprit to visit William, offering both compensation and an apology.

The next morning, just as William texted her about Benjamin's hospital visit and the substantial compensation payment, Elise sat at her desk.

She picked up the internal phone. "Matthew? Inform the CEO to come to my office. There's a department heads meeting in thirty minutes. Tell them punctuality is mandatory."

"Right away, Ms. Scott."

Soon, Sophia entered Elise's office.

After yesterday's humiliation when she tried to visit William, Sophia had returned home seething.

Fortunately, she had developed thick skin since childhood. While others might call it shamelessness, the Scotts always indulged her because of it.

That resilience allowed her to compose herself even in worse situations. Today, her mood showed no traces of yesterday's embarrassment.

In fact, she was already planning her next move.

Still, she felt apprehensive about Elise summoning her alone.

Elise never forgot a slight. Was this about yesterday? Would she use this opportunity to insult or warn her?

Steeling herself, Sophia sat opposite Elise. "You wanted to see me, sis?"

"I've just accepted Jonathan Locke and Patrick Longman's resignations," Elise said without preamble.

Sophia already knew about this.

Elise's eyes remained fixed on her as she continued, "This leaves our current season's production without leadership, and deadlines are approaching. Given your background in the design team and your connections with production, I'm putting you in complete charge of next season's fashion line. Any problems with that?"
###Chapter 76

Sophia stood frozen, her gaze locked on Elise.

She couldn't decipher what Elise was thinking.

Sophia had been certain Elise wanted to discuss her visit to William's hospital room. She doubted Elise was unaware of her intentions, especially since she hadn't even tried to conceal them.

Of course, Sophia would never admit to anything... but the problem was, Elise hadn't brought it up at all! And Elise definitely took offense!

Elise, of all people, knew men had always flocked around Sophia since they were children. Yet now, all Elise talked about was work.

Most importantly, Elise had gone to great lengths to remove both Jonathan and Patrick. Wasn't that just to ensure she could take control of production herself? Elise obviously knew those men were loyal to Benjamin and would never truly work for her.

And yet, Elise was delegating all production authority to Sophia, who would unquestionably side with Benjamin.

That meant all of Elise's efforts were wasted, didn't it?

Had her brain short-circuited or something?

While Sophia remained silent, worried this might be one of Elise's traps, she still couldn't comprehend this move—would it harm her in any way?

Or perhaps Elise was simply too busy to handle it herself?

After all, Elise must be devoting all her attention to William, having stayed by his side the entire day yesterday at the hospital. If Sophia's intuition was correct, Elise would be right back at his side after the upcoming meeting.

With that thought, Sophia said, "No problem, sis. We're family, and I've always worked here anyway. I certainly can't refuse to share your workload and contribute to Stellar Group."

Elise smiled, but otherwise remained expressionless. "I'm counting on you."

"It's fine, sis."

"Don't forget the meeting later. You may go now."

"Okay."

Sophia began to leave, deliberately pausing for a moment.

But Elise made no mention of yesterday at all.

Did Elise truly not mind, or was she perhaps concerned about upsetting Sophia, since Sophia could stop assisting her in retaliation?

Sophia believed it was the latter.

The conference room at Stellar Group was always noisy before meetings, a deliberate show of disrespect toward Elise.

Even so, Elise never wasted time restoring order—they would quiet down once they heard something that interested them.

"I've just accepted the resignations of Jonathan Locke and Patrick Longman," she announced, getting straight to business. "I'm sure everyone is aware by now."

As expected, everyone fell silent immediately—most of them had indeed noticed the resignations.

"I accepted them because I respect every employee's decision," Elise continued. "However, their departure was sudden, and there are no suitable replacements. Therefore, Sophia will temporarily take charge of next season's production."

Everyone was visibly stunned by this news.

They all knew that removing Jonathan and Patrick would streamline production, since the delays were Benjamin's arrangement.

Elise had to be intelligent enough to recognize that, and yet, she was now appointing Benjamin's most trusted daughter to oversee production.

What was that, if not shooting herself in the foot? She had gone through all that trouble for absolutely nothing!
###Chapter 77

Still, none of the department heads and executives at Stellar Group thought Elise Scott was foolish after working under her leadership for some time.

Before they could indulge in wild theories about Elise’s intentions, she smoothly moved to the next agenda item. “It’s been more than two months since I joined Stellar Group. Thanks to everyone’s support—especially my sister Sophia—I’ve become well-acquainted with the company’s operations and management structure. For that, I sincerely thank you all.”

No one applauded. Not even Sophia, though Elise had clearly acknowledged her contribution.

Unfazed, Elise continued calmly. “As I’ve grown more familiar with the company, I’ve also identified certain inefficiencies and operational gaps. Naturally, these issues require correction. Therefore, I’m announcing a comprehensive reshuffling of positions—affecting everyone in this room.”

Her statement sent a ripple of unrest through the conference room.

A company-wide reshuffle was a major disruption, yet Elise announced it as though it were a minor adjustment.

“I will now read the list of personnel affected by this reshuffle. Of course, if anyone has concerns, you’re welcome to discuss them privately with me or the CEO. Those not on the list are expected to provide reassurance to your teams and coordinate with transferred personnel to ensure a smooth handover of responsibilities, minimizing impact on company operations.”

Elise activated the projector and began reading the reassignment list.

“Christopher Brown is hereby relieved of his position as Director of Human Resources. His deputy will assume the role. Christopher will be reassigned as Supervisor of Transport Logistics—”

“What did you just say?” Christopher snapped, rising from his seat with a look of pure disbelief. “You’re demoting me to oversee vehicle maintenance? Is this some kind of joke?”

“It’s no joke,” Elise replied coolly. “You’re well-suited for this role, and Daniel Watson, your deputy, is better equipped to lead the HR department.”

“I’ve dedicated ten years to this company! You can’t just reassign me on a whim—”

“As I stated earlier,” Elise cut him off firmly, “any concerns should be addressed privately with me or the CEO. We will not debate this during the meeting.” Her tone left no room for argument. “Please do not delay the proceedings further.”

Furious, Christopher shoved his chair back and stormed out, slamming the conference room door behind him.

Elise paid no mind to the disruption and continued announcing the remaining reassignments.

The room remained relatively calm, as only two department heads were directly affected.

Wrapping up, Elise added, “Once again, anyone with issues regarding the reshuffle may speak with me or the CEO privately. Reinstatement to original positions is possible under certain circumstances. However, if you disagree with the changes but choose not to discuss them, you have my full support should you decide to pursue opportunities elsewhere. I would never stand in the way of anyone’s professional growth.”

Turning to Daniel, she said, “Please relay this message to Christopher as well. This meeting is adjourned.”

Elise stood, pausing to place a hand on Sophia’s shoulder before leaving. “We have challenging days ahead.”

She didn’t wait for a response.

All she needed was for everyone to witness the apparent solidarity between her and Sophia.

With that, she returned to her office briefly before leaving the building—leaving a messy situation for Sophia to handle.

Before long, Sophia’s office was flooded with disgruntled employees.

Voices overlapped in a chaotic uproar, each person demanding answers, pushing Sophia to the brink of frustration.

She had no prior knowledge of the reshuffle—Elise had never mentioned it—yet everyone assumed it was a joint decision between the two sisters.

No matter how she explained, no one believed her. They refused to speak directly with Elise, no matter how she urged them.

It took considerable effort for Sophia to extricate herself and head to Elise’s office, only to find her long gone.

In that moment, she realized both she and her father had fallen right into Elise’s carefully laid trap.
###Chapter 78

Sophia was completely overwhelmed.

It had taken immense effort to finally clear out the angry employees crowding her office.

Just as she prepared to leave for the day, Christopher blocked her at the doorway!

He slammed his phone onto her desk, shouting, "Sophia Mitchell! I've been completely loyal, doing everything your father and you ever asked! Now I'm being removed because Elise Scott decided so? You better give me answers, or you're not leaving this office!"

Terrified by Christopher's outburst, Sophia fumbled for her phone to call for help.

Though she managed to unlock it, Christopher snatched it from her hands.

"Please calm down, Mr. Brown!" Sophia pleaded, trying to soothe him. "I genuinely didn't know about your reassignment. My sister never discussed this with me beforehand. Had I known, I would have stopped her immediately-"

"Stop lying! I finally understand now... this is all your family's conspiracy!" Christopher snarled viciously, refusing to listen. "I always believed your family was against Elise, that you and Benjamin were working to overthrow her... How naive I was! You're family, and family always sticks together! You've all been collaborating to restructure Stellar Group!"

"How can you believe that, Mr. Brown? Elise essentially stole Stellar from us," Sophia reasoned desperately. "My father and I genuinely want her removed. We'd never support her because we're fighting to reclaim what's rightfully ours-"

"Enough!" Christopher yelled, cutting her off. "I've been watching you and Elise closely - I should have suspected your collaboration earlier! She summoned you to her office privately, then instructed you to keep your meeting confidential. That was about this reshuffle, wasn't it?! Then there's Jonathan Locke and Patrick Longman - Benjamin himself forced them out, they didn't resign voluntarily! They were his most trusted allies, yet he discarded them so easily! Now I see your family's true colors!"

"Mr. Brown, you've completely misunderstood. This is all Elise's trap. She deliberately created this misunderstanding between everyone and my father and me..."

"Misunderstanding?!" Christopher laughed coldly. "If that's true, why would she appoint you as CEO and give you control over production? She knows how crucial production is at this stage - do you think she'd entrust it to you if she didn't truly trust you?! And she's only twenty-five! Do you believe she could have come this far without Benjamin controlling things behind the scenes?!"

"We didn't anticipate these moves from her either!" Sophia cried out in frustration when Christopher mentioned this.

Elise had masterfully created the impression that she and Sophia were on good terms by appointing Sophia as CEO, even falsely claiming that Sophia had developed Stellar's fashion designs for the upcoming season. Now, after forcing Benjamin to dismiss Jonathan and Patrick, she had given Sophia another critical responsibility!

Every strategic move had been executed flawlessly!

Yet people still failed to recognize Elise's true intention: to drive a wedge between Benjamin and the company's department heads and executives by making them feel they could no longer depend on him!

Then, by making an example of Christopher through his reassignment, everyone would feel pressured to align with Stellar Group's new direction!

In fact, Sophia could now predict that no one would listen to her or her father after this, while Elise would seize complete control over Stellar Group, establishing absolute authority!

Worse still, Sophia and Benjamin would bear all the consequences of Elise's maneuvers... while Elise herself would escape completely unscathed!

"No more excuses, Sophia! This is my final warning - reinstate me as head of HR, or I'll destroy both you and Benjamin even if it costs me everything!"

With that final threat, Christopher kicked the door open and stormed out, leaving Sophia trembling with rage!

Since childhood, she had always been the one bullying Elise, never the other way around!

She would repay every humiliation she suffered today a hundred times over!

The personnel reshuffle at Stellar Group caused minor ripples within their business circles. However, it didn't attract attention from major corporations since Stellar wasn't significant enough in the larger landscape.

Meanwhile, at Crestwood Central Private Hospital...
###Chapter 79

Samuel Wilson was finishing his report on the recent developments at Stellar Group. He couldn't contain his admiration as he concluded. "Mrs. Levine's strategy has been absolutely flawless... I was genuinely concerned when she stormed into Stellar Group alone, but she completely dominated them in less than four months!"

William listened quietly, though the subtle satisfaction was evident beneath his composed exterior.

"We have reliable sources confirming that Christopher Brown has been running around town, badmouthing Benjamin Scott everywhere. He's calling him an ungrateful snake. Jonathan Locke and Patrick Longman are both reconsidering their positions too—in short, Simon's foundation is crumbling. His company's stock prices have started dipping because of it. Most importantly, no one at Stellar trusts him anymore. Everyone just wants to keep their jobs and remain at Stellar, so their loyalty naturally shifts to Mrs. Levine." William took a slow sip of his tea, clearly enjoying the reverent tone in Samuel's voice.

"The only remaining challenge for Mrs. Levine is Sophia Mitchell. She could still cause some trouble within Stellar Group. But there's no question that Sophia would never come out on top against Mrs. Levine. If they ever clash, Mrs. Levine won't be the one who loses."

Samuel had developed complete confidence in Elise. "Also, Mrs. Levine is personally overseeing production for next season's fashion line. Her schedule is incredibly tight, and she can't afford any mistakes. She mentioned she won't be able to visit for the next few days, but she promised to come when you're being discharged."

William's smile vanished instantly, while Samuel struggled to hide his own amusement.

His boss had become surprisingly attached, and Samuel suddenly thought William had real potential as a kept man.

"When can I get discharged?" William demanded.

"In three to five days, assuming no complications," Samuel replied.

"Make it three days," William stated flatly. "I need to be out of here in three days."

"Yes, Mr. Levine," Samuel responded respectfully.

After all, anyone who stood between William and seeing Elise must have a death wish!

Elise glanced at the clock in her office, noticing it was well past 10 PM.

She had only appointed Sophia as production manager for appearance's sake—something this important required her personal attention.

Not only was Elise monitoring their progress daily, but she also visited the production site whenever possible. After all the delays, there was zero room for error.

Because of this, she hadn't visited William for three days, despite her promise to check on him daily.

She had thought today would be smoother and she could leave work early, but unexpected issues kept her until now.

Still, she wondered what William was doing at this moment.

After a moment's hesitation, she typed out a text and sent it. [Are you asleep?]
###Chapter 80

Elise had just pressed send when her phone buzzed immediately.

[No.]

A small smile touched her lips.

It felt like William had been waiting for her message. She could almost picture his expression.

Her heart didn't race, but her lips curved upward out of habit.

She typed back: [It's late. You're a patient. Get some rest.]

After sending it, she called Robert to drive her to the hospital.

Somewhere along the way, she'd stopped being so formal with William.

During the ride, she kept glancing at her silent phone.

He could be so childish sometimes.

Arriving at the hospital, Elise tiptoed into William's private room.

If he was already asleep, she didn't want to wake him.

But the bed was empty.

Frowning, she heard movement from the bathroom just as the door swung open.

There he stood, dripping wet.

Shirtless. Hair soaked. Only a white towel wrapped loosely around his waist.

Elise froze completely.

She never expected to see William like this...

She had to admit he looked better than any model she'd ever worked with.

William seemed equally surprised by her sudden appearance.

After her text, he assumed she wasn't coming. So he'd decided to shower and sleep.

Noticing her staring, he smirked. "See something you like, Ms. Scott?"

Elise snapped out of it, cheeks flushing instantly.

She'd been gaping at his body!

She quickly turned away, though William's mood seemed to brighten.

Casually, he stepped out in wet slippers and immediately lost his footing.

Elise saw him stumble and rushed forward without thinking.

All she could think was that he was already injured—she couldn't let him fall.

She didn't consider that he might be too heavy for her to support.

The next moment, they both tumbled to the floor with a thud.

Elise had tried to cushion his fall, positioning herself beneath him.

But he'd shielded her head and back, taking the impact instead.

"Mr. Levine?!"

Samuel burst through the door loudly.

He stood frozen in the doorway for several seconds before backing out slowly.

Oh my god... The boss had made his move!

Though honestly, after watching them dance around each other for so long, even he was getting impatient.

Sometimes direct action was the best approach.

But Samuel reappeared suddenly, looking pained. "Mr. Levine, the doctors said your kidney injury needs care! Please be moderate!"

"Carry on!" he added before disappearing again.

William's face darkened. His kidneys were perfectly fine!

Underneath him, Elise couldn't help but giggle softly. Samuel could be so clueless sometimes.

Still, his interruption had broken the tension between them.

"Could you get up?" Elise asked. William felt like solid steel, and she could barely breathe.

"Yeah," he grunted, slowly lifting himself off her with visible effort.

As they stood, their close proximity made the air feel thick and heavy.

That's when she saw it clearly—his towel had fallen to the floor, along with... everything else.

William felt the cool air at the same moment he saw Elise cover her mouth.

But instead of screaming, she suddenly turned and rushed into the bathroom.

The sound of retching followed.

It didn't stop, making William question his very existence.

After what felt like forever, Elise finally emerged.

She hadn't meant to react that way, but she couldn't control it.

William was already in bed, wearing his patient gown buttoned all the way up.

It looked uncomfortably tight just from looking at it.

Approaching his bed slowly, she decided to stick with the plan she'd formulated in the bathroom: pretend nothing happened.

"Did you hurt your arm?" she asked casually.

"No," William replied. "Just my pride."

She was momentarily speechless. "It's late, I should go. I have work tomorrow. Stellar Group has been busy lately."

"I'm being discharged in three days," William reminded her.

"That's soon. Hasn't it been less than two weeks? Are the doctors allowing it?" Elise asked.

"I wasn't that badly injured anyway."

Elise narrowed her eyes. Who was the one complaining constantly about the pain on the first day?!

"You'll visit after I'm discharged, right?" William asked.

"Yes, I told Samuel already. Didn't he tell you?"

"Just checking," William said casually. "Your word isn't exactly golden."

Elise pressed her lips together but couldn't argue.

She definitely hadn't been taking care of him every day.

"You should go. Get some rest," William said, unusually willing to let her leave.

It was genuinely late, and he knew how busy Elise had been lately.

He remembered feeling like he might die from overwork when he first took over Levine Holdings' overseas branch.

"Okay, I'm leaving now. Goodbye."

Elise didn't hesitate, turning to leave as if worried about wasting another second.

William watched her retreating back and sighed weakly.

It wasn't like he could change his appearance!
###Chapter 81

The day had finally arrived for Elizabeth York's photoshoot to endorse Stellar Group's upcoming fashion collection.

Elise Scott arrived at the studio before dawn, personally overseeing every detail of Elizabeth's preparation—from wardrobe selection to hair styling and makeup application.

This campaign marked Elise's first major product launch since taking the helm at Stellar Group. Both her professional reputation and the company's future depended on its success, leaving no room for complacency.

She had custom-designed Elizabeth's gown based on precise measurements, yet seeing it actually draped over the actress's frame exceeded even her highest expectations.

"I've admired your sketches, but witnessing the final product on my body is truly breathtaking," Elizabeth marveled, studying her reflection critically. "This might be the most exquisite casualwear I've ever worn—the silhouette is uniquely perfect. You must reward your designer handsomely."

A modest smile touched Elise's lips. "You're too kind."

"I'm simply stating facts," Elizabeth countered bluntly. "If Stellar Fashion doesn't become an overnight sensation with designs like these, then my influence is worthless, not your talent."

Elise had to acknowledge how Elizabeth's unwavering confidence eased her anxieties.

While she never questioned her own creative vision, these designs had never faced market scrutiny. Elizabeth's faith felt like an unexpected gift.

Had she not worked closely with Elizabeth, Elise would never have guessed this glamorous A-list actress possessed such steel determination in private. The woman was effortlessly likable.

"Once we wrap today's session, we'll officially announce you as Stellar Group's new brand ambassador," Elise confirmed. "We'll release curated highlights across YouTube and other platforms to build anticipation. Naturally, all content will undergo your studio's approval before publication."

Elizabeth nodded enthusiastically. "Your team's planning has been impeccable. You'll find me completely cooperative."

In that moment, Elise found Elizabeth's approachability genuinely surprising.

Did the actress truly harbor no resentment about their complicated past?

Especially considering Elise's tangled history with William...

The photoshoot progressed with unexpected smoothness. Elizabeth's professionalism and dedication were so remarkable they concluded a full two hours ahead of schedule—an outcome that left Elise pleasantly stunned. She had mentally prepared for days of shooting, having previously endured artists who dragged simple sessions into week-long ordeals over creative "discomfort."

"You were magnificent," Elise praised, offering Elizabeth a chilled water bottle. "As discussed, the final selects and highlight reels will be delivered to your studio."

Elizabeth accepted the bottle, taking a long swallow. "Are you free for dinner tonight?"

"Pardon?"

"By the time I remove this makeup and recharge, it'll be dinner o'clock," Elizabeth elaborated. "Don't you have other commitments?" Elise inquired.

"I cleared my entire day, uncertain how long this shoot would run."

"Understood. Any culinary preferences? I'll secure reservations," Elise offered.

"Anything works, but prioritize good cocktails." Elizabeth's eyes sparkled mischievously. "Drinking with you is surprisingly entertaining."

"Very well, I'll arrange everything." Elise acquiesced.

By 6 PM, after confirming Elizabeth's tolerance for spice, Elise guided her to an authentic Mariachi eatery.

The restaurant's extensive menu of vibrant Latin dishes paired perfectly with spirited drinks.

Seated in their private booth, Elizabeth's eyes widened after sampling a spicy chorizo bite. "How did you discover this hidden gem?"

"My public relations background," Elise explained, pouring Elizabeth a glass of sangria. "I've practically canvassed every dining establishment across Crestwood to entertain potential investors. Learning their preferences became essential."

"You're nothing like I imagined," Elizabeth confessed between enthusiastic bites.

This was Elise's first encounter with an actress who ate with such unbridled joy, unlike others who maintained almost religious devotion to restrictive diets.

"I initially pegged you as just another pretty face—dependent on men and lacking substance," Elizabeth stated bluntly. "Though you are exceptionally beautiful."

It wasn't the first time Elizabeth had complimented Elise's appearance.
###Chapter 82

Elise's tone dripped with sarcasm. "Samuel Mitchell certainly knows how to take credit for others' accomplishments."

She had poured her soul into Mitchell Corp, only to watch Samuel claim every achievement as his own.

She never minded before, believing their relationship made them partners. Now she saw how foolish she had been.

"How did you two finally break up?" Elizabeth asked curiously. "Don't tell me a simple fire could burn away years of feelings."

"He fell for my stepsister."

"Sophia Mitchell?" Elizabeth's lips curled in disdain. "That two-faced snake."

"You can see through her?"

"Anyone with half a brain can. Unfortunately, most men seem to be missing that particular organ."

Elise smiled—Elizabeth spoke with such refreshing honesty. She felt unexpectedly touched by their connection.

This was only their second meeting, yet they clicked instantly. Even Elise had to acknowledge their rare compatibility.

Growing up, Elise had always been solitary by nature. Friends never came easily to her.

She'd maintained a few close relationships over the years, but everything changed after the scandal eighteen years ago.

People avoided her as if her misfortune were contagious. Gradually, Elise grew accustomed to solitude.

Sometimes she wondered if her loneliness abroad made her vulnerable to Samuel's advances. She had believed their relationship unshakable.

As their conversation flowed, so did the alcohol.

Elise hadn't intended to get drunk, but the camaraderie proved irresistible. She found herself matching Elizabeth drink for drink.

Her phone vibrated on the table. She glanced at the screen before answering. "Yes, Matthew?"

"Ms. Scott, I've sent the official documents, photos, and video highlights from Ms. York's photoshoot to your phone. Please review them. If everything looks good, I'll forward them to PR and Ms. York's management company for final approval. We're scheduled to release across all major platforms at 10:10 PM."

"Understood." Elise ended the call and scrolled through the materials on her phone.

She showed the screen to Elizabeth. "Take a look. These are the highlights and official announcements we'll be publishing soon."

Elizabeth studied the content briefly. "Perfect. I look stunning in these shots. More importantly, the designs are exceptional."

"I'll give the green light then."

"By all means."

Elise sent a quick confirmation text to Matthew before returning to their feast.

By 9:30 PM, two cartons of beer stood empty between them.

Elizabeth was visibly intoxicated, though Elise fared little better.

Occasionally, Elise sensed Elizabeth might be drowning sorrows, though she mostly appeared to simply enjoy drinking.

Elizabeth didn't strike her as someone burdened by sadness.

"Let's get you home," Elise said, helping Elizabeth to her feet.

"No," Elizabeth mumbled, leaning heavily against Elise. "I want to sleep at your place."

Elise blinked in surprise as Elizabeth continued, "We're practically family anyway."

If William weren't in the picture, Elise might have suspected Elizabeth was flirting with her.
###Chapter 83

Elise brought Elizabeth back to her apartment.

Elizabeth was definitely the kind of person who could fall asleep anywhere, anytime. She was out cold the moment she slid into the passenger seat.

Elise couldn't rouse her. She had no idea where Elizabeth lived. What was she supposed to do?

She drove to the parking garage beneath her own building. It took considerable effort to maneuver the sleeping actress out of the car, into the elevator, and finally through her apartment door. She deposited Elizabeth onto her bed.

Elise had always lived alone. While there were two bedrooms, only one had a proper bed.

Elizabeth immediately snuggled into Elise's pillows, rolling over with a contented sigh, completely lost to the world.

Elise briefly considered how easy it would be to call a trafficker. Elizabeth wouldn't have a clue.

Was she just careless, or did she genuinely trust Elise this much?

Elise let out a long, slow breath. She went to the kitchen and prepared a glass of warm honey water, placing it on the nightstand. Then, she headed for a shower.

When she emerged, her phone screen was lit up, vibrating silently on the counter.

She picked it up and stepped out onto the balcony to answer.

"You think you're so clever, don't you, Elise?!" Samuel's sneering voice was the first thing she heard. "Signing Elizabeth York to endorse Stellar Group? What's the matter, do you get a thrill out of sharing a man with her? You're utterly repulsive."

"Calling off our wedding was the luckiest escape of my life," Elise retorted coolly. "Marrying you would have been a lifelong curse."

"Who are you to judge me?!" Samuel snapped, his voice dripping with contempt. "Know your place! You got yourself knocked up at eighteen, and now you're cozying up to another woman's man! You're as cheap as they come."

"And you consider yourself noble?" Elise shot back, refusing to let his words slide. "Have you forgotten how you crawled into bed with Sophia? You're nothing special, Samuel. Don't pretend you're on some moral high ground. You're not."

"If you're going to be ungrateful, there's no point in continuing this—"

"Don't pretend this is about kindness. This is about jealousy." Elise cut him off, laying his true feelings bare.

"Jealous? Why would I be jealous of you?"

"Because Mitchell Corp has been trying to sign Elizabeth York for years. And you failed. It must sting, seeing little Stellar Group succeed where your mighty corporation couldn't."

"You're delusional!" Samuel fired back. "Stellar Group isn't even in the same league as Mitchell Corp! There is no comparison! I don't care what little schemes you run over there. You'll never reach our level, no matter what you do. So what if you got Elizabeth? It's just a cheap publicity stunt!"

"Fine. We'll see," Elise said, and ended the call. Talking to Samuel any longer was a pointless waste of oxygen and time.

On the other end, Samuel's face was a thunderous shade of gray after Elise hung up on him.

He had to admit, seeing the official partnership announcement between Stellar Group and Elizabeth had felt like a punch to the gut.

Mitchell Corp had courted Elizabeth for years. And Elise had secured her with what seemed like relative ease.

The humiliation for Mitchell Corp, once this news spread, would be immense.

Ever since his breakup with Elise, a simmering fury had taken root in his chest.

But he was certain. A day of reckoning would come for Elise. She would realize that leaving him was the single greatest mistake of her life.

Elise returned to her bedroom after the call. She was surprised to find Elizabeth awake.

Elizabeth was sitting up in Elise's bed, though she looked groggy and disoriented.

"Finished your call?" Elizabeth asked, her voice thick with sleep.

"You're awake," Elise noted.

"My head is pounding."

"Drink the honey water."

"I did."

"Then, maybe take a shower... or should I just take you home?" Elise offered.

"I'm staying here." Elizabeth's tone was firm, leaving no room for argument.

The truth was, Elizabeth rarely stayed in Crestwood. Her filming schedule kept her on location most of the time. On the rare occasions she was in the city, she usually just checked into a hotel. The Levine family owned plenty of them.

This time, the only reason she was staying at Levine Manor was due to direct threats from her parents and Henry Levine. They'd promised to pull her out of the entertainment industry and force her to work at Levine Holdings if she refused to stay at the family home.

Elizabeth, a free spirit who chafed under strict rules and expectations, felt like she was losing her mind in that oppressive environment.
###Chapter 84
Elizabeth then remarked, "Your sheets have such a lovely fragrance."
Elise smiled softly.
After her bath, Elizabeth slipped into a pair of Elise's pajamas and began exploring the apartment. "There's only one bedroom..."
"You can still reconsider," Elise said as she prepared coffee at the kitchen island.
Being a habitual night owl, she felt wide awake despite the late hour.
Elizabeth, now fully alert after her unexpected awakening, showed no signs of drowsiness either.
"Reconsider what?" Elizabeth giggled playfully. "It's not like I'm losing anything here."
Elise suddenly fixed her with an intense stare, prompting Elizabeth to quickly clarify, "Relax, I'm completely straight."
With an amused smile, Elise handed her a freshly brewed cup. "I take mine black. Sugar cubes are right there if you prefer it sweeter."
"No need, I usually drink mine black too-especially when I'm exhausted."
They settled comfortably on the sofa as Elise switched on the television.
The screen illuminated to reveal an entertainment channel broadcasting a reality show featuring Elizabeth.
"You really are a fan of mine!" Elizabeth exclaimed with evident pride.
In truth, Elise only watched such programs as casual background entertainment.
Naturally, she kept this thought to herself.
They continued watching until well past midnight when Elizabeth's phone suddenly vibrated.
She glanced at the screen but let it ring through.
Moments later, Elise's phone began ringing instead. She answered promptly upon seeing the caller ID. "Mr. Parker."
Elizabeth seemed to briefly glance in her direction before refocusing on the television.
Ethan Parker's voice came through clearly. "I heard Elizabeth had her endorsement photoshoot at Stellar Group today?"
"Yes," Elise confirmed. "Are you trying to reach her? She's here with me right now."
"Really?" Ethan asked, his tone seeking verification.
"Absolutely," Elise replied. "We had a bit too much to drink, so she decided to stay over."
"Oh, I see," Ethan responded, his voice noticeably relaxing.
"Did you need to speak with her? Should I pass her the phone?"
"No, that's fine-it's just that she didn't answer my call earlier. Please remind her that tomorrow's scene is crucial, and she mustn't be late."
"Understood," Elise said, though she found it peculiar that he'd call so late just for a reminder.
She couldn't shake the feeling that Ethan had sounded genuinely concerned when she first answered, but now seemed completely at ease.
"That's all I needed. Goodnight then."
"Goodnight."
After ending the call, Elise turned to Elizabeth. "Director Parker says not to be late tomorrow since it's an important scene."
"Tch." Elizabeth clicked her tongue dismissively-she knew perfectly well that Ethan's call was really about her not returning to Levine Manor tonight.
Though only five years her senior, he certainly took his role as responsible guardian seriously
###Chapter 85

Elise studied Elizabeth carefully. "Is there something between you and Director Parker?"

"Not at all," Elizabeth replied, her tone deliberately casual.

Still, Elise sensed tension between Elizabeth and Ethan.

Why else would Elizabeth deliberately ignore his calls?

Under normal circumstances, wouldn't most actors feel obligated to answer a director's call?

Unless Elizabeth considered herself more important than the project itself.

The television series was only mid-tier production quality anyway.

With Elizabeth's current star power, she could easily land leading roles in blockbuster films.

But despite her status, Elizabeth didn't strike Elise as the type to throw tantrums. "I'm exhausted." Elizabeth stretched lazily. "I need to get some sleep."

"Of course."

Elise knew better than to pry into others' private matters, especially since she and Elizabeth weren't particularly close.

Yet here they were, sharing a bed like old friends.

The next morning, Elise prepared a simple breakfast for them both.

Elizabeth emerged from the bedroom, rubbing sleep from her eyes. "Don't worry about me—I'm watching my weight anyway."

She spoke with such familiarity that Elise wondered if this was how Elizabeth treated everyone.

"But you ate quite heartily last night..." Elise pointed out gently.

"Exactly why I need to compensate today," Elizabeth declared as if this made perfect sense.

"Skipping breakfast isn't healthy."

"I know, I know. My assistant will bring me boiled eggs and low-fat milk for the car ride."

"Leaving already?" Elise asked.

"Director Parker made it very clear yesterday that punctuality matters. I wouldn't dare test his patience."

Elise's brow furrowed slightly.

But Elizabeth had deliberately ignored his call last night...

"Thanks for letting me crash here," Elizabeth smiled warmly at Elise, even adding a playful wink. "I'll send you an amazing wedding gift."

Elise could only stare blankly. Wedding? Whose wedding was she talking about?

News of Elizabeth York signing an endorsement deal with Stellar Group sent shockwaves through both entertainment and business circles.

No one could understand why an A-list celebrity like Elizabeth would partner with such a mediocre company.

The unexpected nature of the collaboration immediately trended online, generating massive publicity for Stellar Fashion.

Meanwhile, Samuel Mitchell sat scowling in his office.

Was this Elise's plan all along—using the controversy to drive engagement?

But what did it matter?

Stellar Group's fashion line would never actually sell well. This hype would be temporary.

Once the buzz faded, it would all prove to be a waste of resources.

Samuel picked up his phone and dialed. "Amanda? Why aren't you at the studio yet?"

"I have a commercial shoot today, Samuel," Amanda Mitchell replied impatiently. "I'll come tomorrow."

"You couldn't have told me earlier?" Samuel snapped.

"I just found out myself," Amanda retorted. "Anyway, I have to go. They're starting."

She hung up abruptly.

Samuel felt a wave of disappointment.

Their family had invested heavily in Amanda's entertainment career over the years, yet she remained permanently stuck as a B-list actress. If she just applied herself more to her craft, she could have achieved so much more!

But Amanda had been spoiled since childhood, always prioritizing her own whims.

She hadn't actually forgotten about the commercial—she simply didn't consider her family's business important enough to remember!

As it happened, Amanda's commercial shoot was located near the set of Pomp and Circumstance, and the mere thought of Elizabeth stealing her role made her blood boil.

Just then, an assistant director approached her. "Get ready, Amanda. Director Parker will be here shortly to oversee the shooting."

"Wait, Ethan Parker is directing?" Amanda asked, surprised.

"Director Cammy had an emergency, so the company brought in a replacement. Don't worry though—Director Parker is highly respected in the industry."

Amanda's lips tightened. This day just kept getting worse.
###Chapter 86

Amanda didn't care about the director's identity, but she still felt irritated after Samuel ignored her at Henry Levine's birthday celebration.

After what felt like an eternity, Ethan finally arrived on set. After a brief discussion, filming commenced.

"Action!"

Ethan sat before the monitor, but his expression quickly darkened.

"Cut!" he snapped abruptly.

Amanda's face immediately contorted with displeasure at the sudden interruption.

"What's wrong, Director?" the assistant director inquired hastily.

"She's facing the wrong direction and her positioning is completely off. She lacks screen presence, and both her appearance and costume are unsatisfactory," Ethan stated bluntly. "The final result won't achieve the desired impact."

Amanda's scowl deepened at his words—no one had ever dared speak to her like that, given her family's status!

"Props team, fix her look. I won't tolerate that generic internet celebrity aesthetic. As for the actors, review your scripts again to better understand your roles," Ethan finished curtly. "I need to check on the other set. I'll return shortly."

As he turned to leave, Amanda charged toward him, shouting, "Ethan Parker!"

Never in her entire acting career had she received such harsh criticism!

When Ethan paused, she snapped, "Since you have so many opinions about my performance, why don't you demonstrate what you want yourself?!"

"I'm a director, not an actor," Ethan replied flatly. "And while I could certainly show you, I'm pressed for time." With that, he turned to leave again, leaving Amanda fuming.

Fueled by past grievances and fresh humiliation, she strode forward and grabbed Ethan's shirt.

Caught off balance, he stumbled and crashed into a nearby scaffolding.

The structure wasn't built for durability since the shoot was only scheduled for one day, and the steel pipes began collapsing from the impact.

"Watch out!"

While everyone stood frozen in shock, Amanda remained paralyzed, watching as the steel pipes came crashing down toward her.

But at the critical moment, Ethan suddenly lunged forward, pushing her to the ground and shielding her with his body as the steel pipes slammed onto his back...

News of Elizabeth signing an endorsement deal with Stellar Group sent shockwaves through both the entertainment and business worlds.

No one could have predicted that Elizabeth would collaborate with such a mediocre company like Stellar Group.

Precisely because it was so unexpected, it quickly topped trending searches, rapidly generating buzz for Stellar Fashion.

Meanwhile, Samuel sat scowling in his office.

Was this Elise's plan—to use this unprecedented situation to drive click-through rates?!

But even if it was, what did it matter?

Stellar Group's fashion line would never sell anyway, and the hype would only be temporary.

Once it faded, it would all prove to be a waste of time, effort, and money.

Picking up his phone, Samuel made a call. "Amanda? Why aren't you at the studio yet?"

"I have a short film to shoot today, Samuel," Amanda Mitchell replied. "I'll come by tomorrow."

"Couldn't you have mentioned that earlier?" Samuel huffed in frustration.

"I just received the call myself," Amanda responded. "Anyway, I have to go. They're starting soon."

She hung up immediately.

Samuel felt genuinely disappointed in her.

The family had invested heavily in her entertainment career over the years, yet she remained permanently stuck as a B-list actress. She could have achieved so much more if she just put more effort into her acting!

However, the family had spoiled her since childhood, and she only ever followed her own whims.

Naturally, she hadn't been informed at the last minute—she had completely forgotten about the commercial shoot for her own family's business!

As it happened, the short film was being shot near the set of Pomp and Circumstance, and Amanda was still seething about Elizabeth stealing her role.

Just then, an assistant director approached and informed her, "Get ready, Amanda. Director Parker will be here soon to oversee the shooting."

"Wait, Ethan Parker is directing?"

"It was supposed to be Director Cammy, but something came up and the company had to find a replacement. Don't worry though—Director Parker's highly professional, recognized by everyone in the industry."
###Chapter 87

William had been texting Elise nonstop before his hospital discharge.

He never explicitly asked her to visit before he left, but he filled her phone with constant messages about everything else.

He inquired about her schedule. He casually mentioned the doctors clearing him for release. He dropped hints about his recovery progress.

Every word was carefully crafted to lure her back to his side.

To his satisfaction, Elise appeared at the hospital early that morning.

Samuel Wilson was busy handling discharge paperwork and packing William's belongings.

William had changed from his hospital gown into a light blue casual shirt paired with fitted outdoor trousers. The outfit was less structured than his usual business attire, lending him an air of relaxed sophistication that complemented his striking features.

He sat motionless on the couch, his expression stormy.

Until Elise walked in.

Samuel breathed a silent sigh of relief at her arrival—he'd been dreading the consequences if she hadn't shown up.

The moment she appeared, William's icy demeanor melted into summer warmth.

"Finished packing?" Elise asked Samuel.

"Almost done," Samuel replied quickly. "Hendy is waiting in the parking lot. Mr. Levine is ready for discharge."

William gave a slight nod but made no move to stand.

Elise frowned.

Hadn't he just received medical clearance? Why wasn't he getting up?

She waited for Samuel to assist him, but Samuel tactfully pretended not to notice and made a quick exit.

He knew better than to interfere in their dynamic.

Left with no choice, Elise moved to help William stand. He immediately leaned his full weight against her.

He felt impossibly heavy.

Gritting her teeth, she supported him all the way to the waiting Maybach, which then drove them toward Chateau Emporio.

The car ride remained uncomfortably silent. Samuel, sitting in the passenger seat, felt compelled to break the tension after catching William's meaningful glance.

Why did he have to do all the work when William was the one trying to win over his wife?

"Ms. Scott, I saw the news about signing Elizabeth York as your brand ambassador," Samuel ventured.

"Oh, that's right," Elise nodded, though her eyes flickered briefly toward William.

He appeared completely composed. When he turned to meet her gaze, she had already looked away.

"Excellent choice, Ms. Scott. Elizabeth combines beauty with remarkable fashion sense. She's the perfect representative for your brand," Samuel added smoothly.

He was simultaneously complimenting Elise's business judgment and Elizabeth's appeal—skillfully praising the two most important women in William's life.

He mentally congratulated himself on his diplomacy.

"Don't you agree, boss?" he asked, seizing the opportunity to include William.

William glanced at Samuel before commenting, "The York family has good genetics."

Samuel stared at his boss through the rearview mirror, utterly speechless.

Shouldn't he be acknowledging Elise's business acumen first? Shouldn't he be flattering her by saying she outshone Elizabeth? Shouldn't he be boasting about their family's superior genes instead?

Now Samuel understood why William was making such slow progress with his wife—in fact, it would be miraculous if he ever succeeded!

Meanwhile, Elise frowned at William's words. Had he already met Elizabeth's parents?
###Chapter 88

Elise felt an emotion stirring inside her that she couldn't quite define, but she pushed it aside.

The car pulled up to Chateau Emporio. Before she could speak, William told her, "James is waiting for you inside."

She swallowed the words she had been about to say. Just then, Charles approached, greeting them respectfully. "Welcome home, Master William—and welcome, Ms. Scott."

So Charles was here too? Did that mean William had sent him away during her last visit?

"Mommy!"

A small figure came running out of the chateau and threw himself into Elise's arms. "Mommy, I missed you so much! Daddy wouldn't let me visit you at the hospital!"

It was clearly a complaint.

Elise crouched down to James's level. She explained patiently, "Daddy was injured and needed to rest at the hospital. If you had visited, he would have needed to take care of you, and that would have slowed his recovery."

"That's not true," James retorted sharply. "Daddy just didn't want me to have you all to myself. He doesn't know how to share."

Elise couldn't help but smile at his choice of words.

Charles was smiling too, though he silently agreed with William—after all, a man shouldn't have to share his wife.

"James," William growled, his expression darkening.

James immediately fell silent, intimidated by his father's tone. He quickly produced a pair of pink slippers and placed them on the floor. "Try these on, Mommy! Daddy and I spent forever picking them out at the mall."

Elise felt her heart soften.

She wouldn't be visiting often, yet they had gone to the trouble of buying slippers specifically for her.

The memory of William having to buy his own slippers at her place made her feel even worse.

She slipped her feet into the slippers. They were soft, comfortable, and perfectly sized. "They're beautiful."

"I knew you'd like them, Mommy! Come on, I'll show you around our house. It's huge and so pretty!" James said excitedly.

Elise wondered how his little heart would handle learning she had been here before.

"Ms. Scott, I saw on the news that you signed Elizabeth York as your brand ambassador," Charles mentioned.

"Oh, yes," Elise nodded, her eyes flickering briefly toward William.

He appeared completely composed. When he turned to meet her gaze, she had already looked away.

"You have excellent judgment, Ms. Scott. Elizabeth is both beautiful and fashionable. She's the perfect choice for a brand ambassador," Samuel quickly added.

He was simultaneously praising Elise's business sense and Elizabeth's appearance—killing two birds with one stone by complimenting the two most important women in William's life!

He almost wanted to congratulate himself on his diplomatic skills.

"Don't you agree, boss?" he asked, seizing the opportunity to curry favor.

William glanced at Samuel before commenting, "The York family has good genes."

Samuel stared at his boss through the rearview mirror, utterly speechless.

The man should have acknowledged Elise's business acumen first, then flattered her by saying she was more beautiful than Elizabeth—not bragged about his own family's genetics!

Samuel finally understood why William was making so little progress with the lady. In fact, it would be surprising if he ever managed to win her over!

Meanwhile, Elise frowned at William's words. So... he had already met Elizabeth's parents?
###Chapter 89

Elise glanced at William.

She had so much to say, but didn't want to speak in front of James.

As for James, he didn't dare ask more questions under William's watchful gaze.

Still, Elise stayed for lunch and gently persuaded James to take his nap before leaving.

William rode home with her in the backseat, with Hendy driving.

The car remained silent until Elise finally spoke. "William."

"Yes." He turned toward her.

"I may have a complicated past," she said quietly. "But I'm not that kind of woman either."

William said nothing, clearly taken aback by her words.

"I understand you're successful and women naturally gravitate toward you. I won't deny that everyone appreciates beautiful things, and neither you nor she might be entirely at fault - but it goes against my principles. I'm fine with remaining friends, but I won't pursue anything beyond that."

The car happened to pull up outside Elise's apartment building just then. She opened the door and stepped out, leaving William looking completely bewildered.

He turned to Hendy. "What was she talking about?"

Hendy remained silent for a moment, feeling tense - he was too old to understand the complexities of young love.

But judging from what Elise had said...

"Perhaps she's suggesting you're somewhat unpredictable, sir?" Hendy ventured carefully.

"Am I really?" William asked, raising an eyebrow.

"You do give that impression, sir... I mean, with your appearance, women might feel somewhat insecure."

William was speechless.

So everything was his fault!

Whether he kissed her...

Exposed himself in front of her...

And now, even his looks were problematic!

Meanwhile, Elise became extremely busy after things cooled off with William. The Stellar Group's autumn collection was about to launch.

William never approached her after that day, and she didn't know if he understood what she had been trying to tell him.

Their relationship had clearly cooled down.

Still, she felt somewhat nervous as she sat in her office.

They had done everything possible to promote and build excitement for their autumn collection. Elizabeth was particularly enthusiastic too. Beyond the scheduled photoshoots, she frequently wore Stellar-branded clothes during her casual outings, even deliberately posing for paparazzi to capture more images of her in their designs. The media had recently dubbed her the 'most dedicated brand ambassador' in one of their articles.
###Chapter 90

Matthew Johnson arrived, holding a document. "Ms. Scott? The initial sales report is ready."

"Let me see it," Elise replied, her voice steady.

As he handed it over, Matthew couldn't contain his excitement. "The numbers are incredible—far exceeding our projections. Sales launched at 10 AM sharp. The most sought-after designs vanished instantly. Other styles are performing over thirty percent better than forecasted, and the figures keep climbing. Physical store data is still coming in, but nationwide feedback indicates they're overwhelmed. Supplies are struggling to keep up with demand." A smile touched Elise's lips as Matthew produced an iPad, swiping through photos. "Humphrey Wickers—our sales director—is currently at Aurora Plaza, our city's largest mall, conducting an on-site review. These are live shots from our retail locations. Every other brand on our floor is practically deserted. Honestly, the entire mall seems quiet except for our stores."

It was undeniable—Stellar Fashion had made a spectacular debut.

The success wasn't just a shock to Stellar Group; their competitors were utterly blindsided.

In his office, Samuel Mitchell slammed a stack of papers onto his desk.

He never imagined Elise could actually propel Stellar Fashion to such dizzying heights. Meanwhile, Mitchell Corp's seasonal collection had barely reached a third of Stellar's sales. For Crestwood's leading local fashion house, a company that had long dominated the industry, this was a humiliating blow.

Snapping his laptop open, Samuel forced his fury down as he scrutinized Stellar's new designs.

Previously, his focus had been entirely on Elizabeth. He'd paid little attention to the actual collection. Stellar hadn't produced anything remarkable in years. How could Elise have worked miracles so quickly after taking charge?

But now, actually seeing the designs, his face darkened with a scowl.

Even he had to admit—the quality of Stellar's autumn line far surpassed anything Mitchell Corp's top designers had conceived.

His temper erupted, directed at his cowering team. "Why did no one inform me Stellar Fashion had new design talent?! Is basic competitor monitoring beyond you?! Or did none of you sense the threat when their standards skyrocketed?!"

"Our intelligence confirmed no new hires at Stellar, Mr. Mitchell!" the design director quickly interjected. "Ms. Sophia Mitchell was credited with the autumn collection designs. Our source is reliable—Christopher Brown, formerly a key figure at Stellar. Elise even awarded Sophia a significant bonus for her work!"

Samuel's expression turned thunderous.

Sophia designed those? Whose side was she actually on?!

"Get out!" Samuel barked. His team scurried from the room.

Once alone, he dialed Sophia's number. "What were you thinking, Sophia?!" he roared the moment she answered. "Do you have any idea how much damage your designs for Elise have caused me?!"

On the other end, Sophia was seething.

She'd been stunned when Elise presented the final autumn collection templates. But she never anticipated Elise would drive the entire season to such wild, unprecedented success!

Frankly, she hadn't believed Elise's promotional strategy would work at all.

Even Elizabeth had thrown herself into her role as brand ambassador with unexpected fervor. She promoted the line relentlessly to the public and on her private social media, constantly mentioning Stellar Fashion and lavishing it with praise.

With such prominent placement and designs that truly stood out, Stellar became an instant sensation, capturing the market's full attention.

"I had no idea it would be this popular," Sophia said, injecting a tone of wounded pride. "I just threw some ideas together on a whim. Elise kept rejecting my initial concepts, so I retaliated with my best work. I never thought the final result would resonate like this."

"You should have warned me," Samuel growled, his rage barely contained. "You never mentioned Elise was signing Elizabeth York. You said nothing about her overhaul of Stellar Fashion! Understand this, Sophia—I am your real family! Do you honestly think helping Elise this much will get Starstream Group back for you? Are you an idiot?!"

Sophia was no fool—Elise was simply several steps ahead.

A vicious glint flashed in Sophia's eyes. "Actually, Samuel, Stellar Fashion's current popularity might not be entirely a bad thing."

"What are you talking about?!" he snapped, frustration evident.

"The higher they soar," Sophia hissed, each word dripping with venom, "the harder they'll crash."
###Chapter 91

Samuel's anger subsided instantly. "What are you implying?"

Sophia remained visibly irritated, her patience worn thin by Samuel's mockery and outburst.

She had tolerated his temper before because she desired him.

Now that her interest had vanished, his words only fueled her resentment.

"I apologize for my emotional reaction, Sophia," Samuel said, quickly realizing his harshness and softening his tone, sensing she had a strategy. "Please understand—I'm just concerned you're still assisting your sister after how she exploited you."

While Sophia wasn't particularly forgiving, she didn't dwell on pettiness since Samuel remained useful to her. "Consider this: Stellar Fashion is currently at peak popularity, with every media outlet praising them and netizens showering them with admiration. But if a scandal erupts at this critical moment, do you truly believe Stellar Fashion can maintain its appeal? I anticipate a complete reversal of fortune."

Though Samuel grasped the plan quickly, he questioned, "But is there any scandal involving Stellar Group? There was that internal reshuffle, but I doubt it would gain significant traction."

"Stellar Group might not have any scandals... but my sister certainly does."

"What scandal?" Samuel grew cautious and added, "I won't risk my reputation here. It wouldn't be worth the cost!"

Clearly, he valued his public image greatly and worried Sophia might use his past with Elise to create a scandal.

No matter what, he didn't want any dirt attached to his name!

"Relax, Samuel. I won't let your reputation suffer—anyway, do you recall those photos I sent you earlier?" Sophia inquired.

Samuel thought for a moment and soon remembered. "You mean..."

"Once those photos are released, my sister's reputation will be utterly destroyed. Granted, it's never been stellar, but that will only amplify the impact."

Samuel hesitated, aware that releasing those photos would be underhanded.

"Think it over, Samuel. Honestly, I wouldn't have suggested this... if it weren't for Mitchell Corp, especially after all your efforts to revive it," Sophia said sweetly. "Anyway, I should go—"

"It's all Elise's own doing," Samuel suddenly declared.

Sophia smirked maliciously at that moment.

When his personal interests were at stake, Samuel showed no sentimentality, even if he felt some guilt toward Elise.

Still, Sophia advised Samuel, "There's no hurry, Samuel. Let Stellar Fashion enjoy their hype for a few more days."

"Why?" Samuel asked—that would mean Mitchell Corp's sales would suffer even more.

"Given the current situation, Elise will undoubtedly push production to increase output, which drives up costs. But if sales suddenly plummet and they can't cover expenses, Stellar Group's finances will stall, and my sister will have to sell her shares to raise capital. However, once her reputation is ruined, no other company will buy her shares or offer help. When that happens, I can persuade my father to purchase all her shares, seizing the chance to reclaim Stellar."

Samuel's eyes gleamed as he processed Sophia's explanation.

He had always seen her as delicate, so her calculated and thorough planning surprised him.

The thought of Elise losing everything excited him even more, so he eagerly agreed, "Let's proceed with your plan, Sophia. I'll support you in any way I can."

"Okay," Sophia cooed.

"And once this is over, let's announce our engagement," Samuel added sincerely.

He also planned to publicly praise Sophia's cleverness in this scheme.

"That will have to wait. If we want to create a scandal for Elise, we can't mention our relationship at all, or she might use it against us. If that happens, we'd lose far more than we gain."

"Okay," Samuel agreed immediately.
###Chapter 92

After ending the call with Samuel, Sophia's eyes turned vicious.

There was more to her desire to expose Elise than what she had told Samuel.

She wanted William to be utterly repulsed by Elise.

That would give her the perfect opportunity to steal him away.

Following a week of unprecedented sales, Stellar Fashion's autumn collection faced nationwide shortages.

Instead of implementing scarcity marketing, Elise immediately authorized another large-scale production run.

She never anticipated the autumn line would sell out completely.

Her philosophy in fashion mirrored her mother's.

They both believed in making their personally designed clothing accessible to as many people as possible.

However, just as production concluded and the second batch prepared for market release, a scandalous exposé made headlines.

The shocking article was titled: The Shameful Rise of Elise Scott, Owner of Stellar Group.

The details were elaborate and specific.

It claimed Elise had cheated on Samuel with multiple men during their relationship.

According to the piece, her affairs with these men enabled her successful takeover of Stellar Group.

The article also alleged these connections propelled Stellar Fashion's sudden popularity, generating millions in profits.

Public outrage erupted instantly across social media platforms.

[Elise Scott is disgusting! How could she cheat with so many men?]

[Poor Samuel Mitchell deserves so much better.]

[She doesn't appreciate Samuel wanting to marry her despite her history.]

[I was starting to appreciate local designs through Stellar Fashion, but this ruins everything.]

[Let's boycott Stellar Fashion, sisters! No more purchases from them.]

[I've already requested a refund for my order.]

[I worked so hard to get one of their dresses, but I'll never wear it now.]

Simultaneously, Samuel posted an announcement on his social media account.

[Elise Scott and I amicably ended our relationship some time ago. We kept it private to avoid wasting public attention. I have no comment regarding Elise's current situation. Please stop direct messaging me about this.]
