###Chapter 115

Elise continued speaking, her voice steady. "When I was a child, I couldn't protect myself. But things are different now."

She was reassuring William, though she wasn't entirely sure why she felt the need to. Perhaps it was because genuine concern was rare in her world, and his felt real.

"Does it hurt?" William suddenly asked, his gaze fixed on her cheek where the swelling remained visible.

How much force had Margaret used to leave such a mark?

Elise seemed momentarily surprised by his question, then shook her head.

It truly didn't hurt. Not compared to what she'd endured as a child.

The pain that once haunted her heart had faded. She'd come to understand the Scotts didn't deserve her tears. Not a single one.

William's lips tightened. The atmosphere around him grew cold and intense.

He looked intimidating, but Elise felt no fear. She understood the source of his anger.

"I'd like an apple," she said suddenly, deliberately changing the subject.

William blinked at her unexpected request.

"The one you peeled earlier was quite sweet," she added.

The chill in the air softened slightly. He turned toward the fruit plate on the coffee table.

Picking up an apple and the fruit knife, he began peeling it carefully.

His hands were elegant, his fingers long and slender. They looked immaculate.

Elise quickly averted her gaze.

She wasn't ashamed to admit her caution. After everything she'd been through, she couldn't afford to take risks.

She'd rather miss opportunities than make the wrong choice. No exceptions.

William finished peeling and slicing the apple, presenting it to her.

"Thank you," she said, accepting the plate. She began eating slowly.

She'd never particularly liked fruits before, but now she found apples surprisingly pleasant. Crisp, sweet, and satisfying with every bite.

"Is it good?" William asked.

"Yes," she nodded.

"Let me try," he said.

"Of course," she replied, picking up her fork with a slice she'd been about to eat herself.

Meanwhile, Benjamin scoffed. "She needs to take responsibility and apologize."

"Will she actually do it?" Sophia questioned.

"She has no choice," Benjamin said through clenched teeth. "Either she apologizes, or she faces legal consequences."

Back in Elise's hospital room, William returned her phone.

"Thank you," she said quietly as she took it.

He watched her for a moment before approaching her bedside. He picked up the patient file lying nearby.

"Temporary hearing loss in the right ear?" he read aloud.

"It's only temporary," Elise confirmed.

William raised an eyebrow. "How long will it last?"

"If it's like before, about a month."

"Like before?" William had a knack for catching important details.

"The same thing happened when I was younger," Elise explained calmly. "It lasted a month that time too."

William's fingers tightened around the file. His hand trembled slightly despite his controlled demeanor.
###Chapter 116

"It's fine. I'll remain here with you," William responded, as though it were the most natural thing in the world. "Rest."

Elise stared at him. He added, "You frequently visited and looked after me during my previous hospital stay."

"You were injured because of me," Elise pointed out.

"I always repay kindness a hundredfold. There's no need for such formality, Ms. Scott," William stated, his tone resolute.

Elise pressed her lips together. A persistent feeling nagged at her, suggesting she would wound the vulnerable part of him if she refused.

So, she simply turned over, presenting her back to him, and closed her eyes to sleep.

William settled into the chair beside her hospital bed, his gaze fixed on her delicate, small form.

She was truly petite. So very small.

Yet, she possessed a toughness that was almost painful to witness.

William's Adam's apple moved in a slow swallow.

If he had stopped her all those years ago, during her desperate flight…

Would she have learned to trust more easily?

Elise hadn't anticipated actually falling asleep.

She had formulated a perfectly reasonable excuse to make William leave. But as she lay there with her eyes closed, sleep unexpectedly claimed her.

She rarely slept around strangers, her sense of insecurity usually too profound.

She shifted slightly and discovered William was also asleep, sprawled on the small couch.

The couch was far too tiny for his large frame, forcing him into an awkward, cramped position.

She recalled the time he was hospitalized. Their roles were now completely reversed.

Rising quietly, she picked up her blanket and attempted to drape it over him as gently as possible.

The instant she drew near, however, William's eyes snapped open, startling her.

"Sorry, I… Oh!"

Elise gasped as his hand shot out, capturing her wrist in a firm grip.

He pulled firmly.

Her entire body tumbled into his arms, the world seeming to spin around her.

Before she could process it, William flipped their positions, pinning her beneath him on the couch.

They stared into each other's eyes, locked in that compromising pose.

Then, Elise watched as William suddenly leaned down, his face inching closer to hers…

Her heart hammered against her ribs.

Every instinct screamed rejection, and her body tensed in refusal.

Yet, she hesitated for a split second when she met the intense brightness of his gaze.

She bit her lip, watching him draw nearer…

And nearer still.

At the very last possible moment, she squeezed her eyes shut and clenched her fists, as if bracing herself to endure something inevitable.

Her entire body went rigid with tension. Then, suddenly, she relaxed and her eyes fluttered open.

She found herself staring at the top of his head, at his jet-black hair.

He was simply lying on top of her, his weight threatening to suffocate her.

Had he never truly woken up? Was this all just sleepwalking?

Even as he lay heavily upon her, his breathing remained deep and rhythmic. She felt the warmth of his breath against her right ear.

His lips moved slightly. She couldn't tell if he was actually speaking, as she was deaf in that ear.

All she knew for certain was the difficulty of drawing a full breath under his weight.

After a while… perhaps ten minutes, maybe twenty, Elise felt she had reached her limit. She pushed against his shoulder. "Wake up."

Her body stiffened as he stirred. She couldn't look away from his strikingly handsome face as consciousness returned to his features.

William lifted his head, his eyes heavy with sleep as they focused on Elise beneath him. A flicker of surprise crossed his expression.

"What did I do?" he asked.

Good.

At least he was asking about his own actions, and not accusing her of something. She would never have been able to explain this situation otherwise.
###Chapter 117
"Release me."

Elise shifted her body again, attempting to push William away, only to hear him emit a low, sensual groan.

She immediately froze in place. Even with her limited experience, she was an educated adult who understood the implications.

"Just get off," she stated, forcing her voice to sound as icy as possible.

William swallowed hard.

A flicker of intense emotion crossed his eyes, but he quickly regained his composure. He moved off her and immediately retreated to the bathroom.

Elise took a moment to collect herself. William soon returned.

As she turned towards him, her gaze inadvertently drifted downward. A fierce blush instantly heated her cheeks.

She hadn't meant to look.
But her eyes seemed to have a mind of their own!

She quickly averted her eyes to the floor. It was already taking immense effort to calm her racing heart, and now even her ears were burning bright red!

William, however, wore a faint, knowing smile. "I assure you, that was not my intention. Sometimes, the body reacts... independently. I hope I didn't make you too uncomfortable, Ms. Scott."

Elise was rendered speechless.
Uncomfortable?
She hadn't been uncomfortable!

Yet, his deliberately ambiguous phrasing was clearly designed to lead her thoughts down a very specific, very distracting path.

She understood, of course, that William was offering an explanation.
There had been occasions where she found herself in close proximity to other men, but any hint of intimacy made her physically ill. In fact, she suspected William's hasty retreat to the bathroom was precisely to avoid triggering another bout of her nausea.

William smoothly changed the subject. "So? What exactly happened just now?"

He seemed genuinely perplexed as to how he'd woken up with her pinned beneath him.

"You were asleep. I was trying to drape a blanket over you, concerned you might get cold. Then, I believe you began sleepwalking. You pulled me down and trapped me," Elise summarized, carefully avoiding any language that could be misconstrued.

"My apologies." William's tone was contrite, but Elise didn't detect a shred of genuine remorse in his expression.

Who in the world smiled so smugly while offering an apology?!
It was as if he'd accomplished something praiseworthy in his sleep!

"Must have been the dream," he clarified, his gaze turning momentarily distant.

Searching his memory, he recalled fragmented pieces of a dream.
Piecing it together, it was the night of the incident, the night he was violated.

That explained his body's involuntary reaction.
He let out a quiet, weary sigh.

Elise frowned, sensing a shift in his demeanor that felt anything but innocent.
"Does that happen often?" she inquired.

"What?" William blinked, pulled from his thoughts.
Realizing her meaning, he replied, "No. Not often."

Elise was skeptical.
His movements had been so fluid, so practiced. She wondered how many women had been on the receiving end of such nocturnal maneuvers.

"Only with you, Ms. Scott," William stated plainly.

Elise remained doubtful. In her eyes, he was a seasoned charmer, capable of moves she could never hope to counter.

"I've only ever been... physically involved with you, Ms. Scott," William added quietly.

Elise's eyebrow arched. Her heart gave an unexpected, traitorous flutter.
She quickly suppressed it.
Had he forgotten he had a son?
Though, he did have a knack for saying the right thing at times, even if his sweet talk was occasionally clumsy...

A knock on the door interrupted them. Samuel Wilson entered, looking slightly reluctant as he delivered their dinner.
###Chapter 118

Charles was undoubtedly a cunning old fox. He brought dinner and then immediately left, using the excuse that James was waiting for him at home!

He left Samuel with the task of interrupting William and Elise.

"Charles sent dinner. I was concerned it might get cold," Samuel explained cautiously.

When William nodded, Samuel quickly placed the dinner on the overtable and turned to leave immediately.

However, Elise stopped him. "Let's eat together, Samuel. There's too much for just William and me." Samuel was certainly hesitant to agree and looked towards his boss for guidance.

"Don't let it go to waste," Elise added.

William gave a reluctant nod then.

Samuel, who was already quite hungry, felt immensely grateful that Elise was as gracious as she was beautiful.

After they finished dinner, Elise insisted that William return home, expressing concern about James being alone.

He left reluctantly under her persistent urging, and she let out a quiet sigh of relief once he was gone.

His presence always made her feel somewhat unsettled.

Lying back down in the hospital bed, she began making calls to the various department heads.

After clearing her name through the press conference and gaining widespread support from netizens, Stellar Group's autumn collection was selling exceptionally well once again. Even with this success, Elise wanted concrete data and confirmation.

Each department head reported their updates with evident enthusiasm, as everything was progressing far more favorably than anyone had anticipated.

As expected, following the press conference, Stellar Group's autumn collection was flying off the shelves. The suppliers who had been pressuring for early payments had now ceased their demands, even stating that there was no rush and they could wait much longer.

Business people were indeed pragmatic, but at the very least, this indicated that Stellar Group was firmly back on the right track.

After her final call, Elise checked Elizabeth York's Twitter account.

The tweet where Elizabeth declared her support for Elise had suddenly gained an enormous number of likes and replies.

Elizabeth's devoted fans were naturally mocking the other netizens who had previously doubted her character—the power of a dedicated fandom was truly formidable.

In any case, Elise no longer had to worry about Elizabeth's reputation being negatively impacted because of her.

Just as Elise was about to close the Twitter app, Elizabeth posted a new tweet.

[Need birthday gift ideas for a man in his late twenties. Any recommendations, ladies?]

Elise froze.

Late twenties, man, birthday.

There was no doubt that Elizabeth was referring to William.

She swallowed hard, quietly setting her phone down.

Elise was discharged from the hospital the next day.

Robert was driving her as she gazed out at the passing scenes of Crestwood's skyline and its massive shopping centers.

Several times, she considered asking Robert to stop so she could buy a gift as a polite gesture, but she ultimately decided against it.

She resolved instead to have a clear conversation with William that evening.

No matter what her feelings for him were, her principles would not accept him being involved with two women.

Even if she wasn't doing it for herself, she genuinely believed Elizabeth deserved a sincere and devoted heart.

Having made that decision, she no longer felt uncertain or conflicted.

As evening approached, she applied light makeup and wore a simple white dress, letting her soft hair fall loosely over her shoulders—nothing extravagant, but naturally pleasant and approachable. Anyone would find her easygoing and gentle.

Robert was driving her once more. Although William hadn't specified the location, only the time, he must have informed Robert.

Soon, they arrived at the destination, where William was waiting at the entrance.

He was wearing a tuxedo, his tall frame exuding nobility and elegance.

For a moment, Elise didn't want to get out of the car at all.

She felt a pang of awkwardness, feeling underdressed—she hadn't expected William to be dressed so formally!
###Chapter 119

Elise took a deep breath and stepped out of the car.

Her dress wasn't exactly casual, but it certainly wasn't formal enough for this occasion.

Standing beside William, she felt noticeably underdressed.

He didn't seem to mind her attire at all.

He simply took her hand and led her toward the towering skyscraper.

The restaurant occupied the entire twentieth floor.

Naturally, William had chosen one of Crestwood's most exclusive dining establishments.

They entered the elegantly designed glass elevator together.

Suddenly, William removed his dinner jacket and bow tie.

He unfastened the top two buttons of his shirt.

His imposing presence softened into something more approachable.

"Hold these for me," he said, handing her the jacket and tie.

Elise accepted them without question.

She watched as he slowly rolled up his sleeves.

His muscular forearms were now visible.

The transformation was instant—he looked considerably more casual now.

Elise quickly understood what he was doing.

He was adjusting his appearance to match hers.

A pang of guilt shot through her.

He had probably expected her to dress up for his birthday celebration.

She hadn't put in that effort.

Now he was making accommodations for her oversight.

She pushed the guilty feelings aside.

She needed to focus on why she was here.

The elevator reached their floor.

William took back his jacket and bow tie.

A steward greeted them immediately.

They were escorted to a VVIP room.

Two stewards stood outside the double doors.

They opened them simultaneously.

The room beyond was extravagantly appointed.

Elise followed William inside.

She realized they weren't alone.

She recognized Ethan Parker immediately.

Nathan Davis and Andrew Saunders were also present.

Aside from Ethan being adopted into the Levine family, the other two came from old money dynasties.

"William," Nathan greeted enthusiastically. "You brought company."

William nodded. "Let me introduce my friends. You know Ethan Parker, my uncle?"

"Mr. Parker." Elise offered a polite smile.

Ethan returned the greeting with equal courtesy.

"Nathan Davis." William gestured toward the slightly stout man.

Elise studied Nathan briefly.

He wasn't conventionally handsome, but his features grew more appealing upon closer inspection.

Still, he paled in comparison to William.

"We went to the same school, remember?" Nathan said eagerly. "I was your senior in high school."

"Yes, Mr. Davis." Elise maintained her polite demeanor.

Nathan extended his hand for a handshake.

He quickly withdrew it when he caught William's sharp look.

How territorial.

"And that's Andrew Saunders."

Elise turned toward the man standing near the window.

He was the tallest in the room, standing at around six foot two.

His blonde buzzcut gave him a distinctive look.

He carried himself with natural style and confidence.

Her first thought was that he'd make an excellent fashion model.
###Chapter 120

"Hello, Ms. Scott. I've heard so much about you, but you're even more stunning than you appeared during the press conference." Andrew complimented her sincerely.

"You're too kind." Elise smiled gracefully. "You're quite handsome yourself."

Andrew flashed a proud grin and threw William a challenging look, which William completely ignored.

Pulling out a chair for Elise, William explained, "I've known them since childhood—our families have always been close. Though I spent most of my years abroad and rarely returned. This is actually my first birthday celebration here in Crestwood, so they insisted on joining us."

His words subtly conveyed that he would have preferred celebrating alone with her, but his friends had invited themselves along.

"Just relax and be yourself. Don't feel pressured by their presence," William added quietly, showing genuine concern for her comfort.

Elise nodded gracefully.

It was his special day, and she would respect whatever made him happy.

Still, she wondered if she would get the opportunity to share what she had prepared to tell him tonight.

The door suddenly swung open, interrupting her thoughts, and a familiar feminine voice filled the room.

"Starting the party without me? You're all terrible!"

Elise turned to see Elizabeth entering energetically, carrying a beautifully arranged bouquet.

"Who would dare begin without our beloved entertainment queen?" Nathan exclaimed with dramatic flair.

"Good answer." Elizabeth beamed and walked directly to William, wrapping her arms around his neck in an affectionate hug. "Happy birthday, my dear."

William's eyes, however, were fixed on the bouquet with clear disapproval. "This is what you got me after polling your entire Twitter following?"

"Hey, I poured my heart into this," Elizabeth defended passionately. "I personally selected every flower and arranged them myself. It's completely unique, just like my feelings for you."

"Remember, we're not on a movie set here, sweetheart," Nathan teased playfully.

Elizabeth shot him a sharp glare. "This is between William and me. Keep your opinions to yourself!"

Nathan simply shrugged, completely accustomed to their sibling-like banter.

William snorted dismissively. "I bought you that limited edition Hermès Himalayan Birkin for your birthday, and all I get in return is flowers? Your affection for me clearly knows no bounds."

"You never told me what you wanted!" Elizabeth protested, giving him a wounded expression. "Besides, you can buy anything you desire. How could you expect extravagant gifts from a struggling actress like me who lives paycheck to paycheck?"

"Fine, whatever. Take a seat," William grumbled, not wanting to argue further. "And next time, go easier on the perfume. It's overwhelming."

"Stop being so dramatic!" Elizabeth retorted, though she discreetly sniffed her own wrist. "This is an exclusive vintage blend. I wouldn't normally use something this precious on myself, but I made an exception for your birthday. Do you even appreciate fine things?!"

She then naturally settled into the seat beside Elise.

Elise pressed her lips together slightly, calmly observing the playful exchange between William and Elizabeth.

noveldrama

Even though William claimed to dislike flowers, his eyes lingered on the bouquet several times after Elizabeth mentioned she had arranged it personally.

Elizabeth leaned closer to Elise, asking conspiratorially, "Elise, don't you think this fragrance is exquisite?"

"Indeed," Elise replied knowledgeably. "Royal Chamber's signature rose perfume. It's the Hermès of fragrances, and more importantly, it's been discontinued for years. It only appears occasionally at exclusive auctions, making it a true collector's treasure."

"You really know your perfumes. Men understand nothing about these things," Elizabeth praised genuinely.

Elise possessed far more sophistication and depth than she had imagined, and Elizabeth found herself admiring her more with each meeting.

In fact, Elizabeth had been absolutely thrilled watching Elise dismantle Samuel and Sophia during the press conference, cheering and applauding excitedly from her living room. She would have invited Elise out for celebratory drinks immediately if her schedule had allowed!

press

Fortunately, William's birthday provided the perfect opportunity, and Elizabeth planned to enjoy the evening thoroughly.

If she were completely honest, she wouldn't have bothered joining this gathering with "the boys" if Elise hadn't been attending.
###Chapter 121

"Andrew, why isn't Samantha anywhere to be seen?" Elizabeth questioned Andrew at that moment.

Nathan spoke up beside Andrew before he could respond. "Have you ever seen her attend a dinner where he's present?"

"Then why would he marry Samantha?" Elizabeth huffed in frustration.

Nathan shrugged, equally clueless. "You'd have to ask the man himself."

Andrew flashed a casual grin. "It's a political alliance, understand?"

"All men are pigs," Elizabeth snorted with clear disdain.

Though she was friends with these men through her cousin, when it came to Samantha's marriage with Andrew, she firmly stood by Samantha Summer's side.

"Now, that's not entirely accurate," Nathan corrected Elizabeth with mock offense. "We three aren't like him. William may have a son, but he's always kept his relationships clean. Ethan doesn't even have a girlfriend—the man's practically a saint. As for me, while it might appear women constantly surround me... I've actually always maintained my integrity."

"Tch!" Elizabeth clicked her tongue contemptuously.

"You're really testing our patience, you know?" Nathan snapped back.

"It's not like you can outdrink me," Elizabeth challenged with a provoking grin.

"You little..." Nathan's competitive spirit ignited immediately. "Don't blame me if you end up drunk."

"Worry about yourself. You've never beaten me, and now I have Elise backing me up. Just admit defeat already!"

"Elise isn't drinking," William stated firmly.

Elizabeth frowned. "Why not?"

"No means no," William growled with finality.

Elizabeth's expression clearly showed her displeasure.

Elise felt a complex emotion sitting beside William. She could tell Elizabeth shared a close bond with these men, and William must frequently include Elizabeth in his gatherings with friends. Naturally, Elizabeth would feel slighted when William sided with Elise instead.

Quickly, Elise explained, "My ear injury hasn't fully healed. The doctor advised against alcohol."

"Your ear?" Elizabeth sounded genuinely concerned.

"It's nothing serious. I should recover completely in a few days, but I really shouldn't drink right now," Elise apologized. "I still want to thank you for having my back earlier."

"Oh, it was nothing." Elizabeth waved it off with her characteristic magnanimity.

It was evident Elizabeth meant no harm... but was that why she never suspected William's intentions?

Elise struggled to define the tangled emotions swirling within her.

Meanwhile, the atmosphere at the table grew increasingly lively as Elizabeth began drinking contests with Nathan and the others—she was certainly holding nothing back.

Elise thought even if Elizabeth managed to outlast Nathan, she wouldn't fare much better herself.

Just as she prepared to advise Elizabeth to slow down, Ethan caught Nathan's wrist as they reached for another round. "That's enough drinking for tonight."

"But I'm not even drunk yet," Nathan protested excitedly. "I'm determined to beat her tonight, or else...!"

"Or else what? You'll wear a dress and perform tap dance?" Elizabeth taunted smugly.

"You little..." Nathan rose to the bait, grabbing his glass. "Bottoms up!"

"Bottoms up!" Elizabeth echoed, downing her drink in one go.

Ethan pressed his lips together and told Nathan, "If you're such an accomplished drinker, why not challenge me instead?"

"With your tolerance level? Are you sure about that?" Nathan gave Ethan a skeptical look.

"It's my nephew's birthday celebration. Why shouldn't I indulge a little?" Ethan raised an eyebrow.

With that challenge issued, they both began drinking with renewed enthusiasm.

While Nathan was a seasoned drinker, Ethan rarely indulged, usually only taking small sips even when Nathan pressured him. This rare display of enthusiasm from Ethan prompted Nathan to match his intensity drink for drink.

Elizabeth let out a loud burp just then.
###Chapter 122

Elise had consumed too much alcohol with Nathan earlier, and she was clearly feeling unwell.

If she continued drinking, she would collapse soon enough.

"Are you okay?" William asked Elise, handing her a glass of water. "Drink slowly—it's better for your stomach."

"Thank you," Elise replied as she accepted the glass, speaking with emotional sincerity. "I love you, William."

As Elise felt her heart flutter, William continued, "You remember Samantha, don't you?"

"Yes. We all had dinner together," Elise responded.

"That's my best friend, without question," William told Elise. "But I must declare now that I have another best friend too."

Elise pressed her lips together, feeling somewhat helpless in that moment.

She genuinely sensed that William cared for her, and his sincerity once again left her in a difficult position.

Elise glanced at James, who was drinking with the others, and resolved to say what needed to be said tonight.

The atmosphere around the table grew lively after dinner, and Nathan insisted they go to a karaoke bar.

They descended to the sixteenth floor—the entertainment level—and reserved a VIP room for themselves.

The ornately decorated room wasn't large, and with just the six of them, it felt quite crowded.

Nathan, always the life of the party, became even more exhilarated after drinking.

Once he grabbed the microphone, he began singing and dancing without inhibition.

William, also buoyed by alcohol, tried to wrestle the microphone away from Nathan.

Suddenly, they were tussling and playing excitedly like children.

"I can take you home if you're bored," James suddenly told Elise, though he already carried the scent of alcohol.

"No." Elise shook her head.

Everyone was having fun, and James was the center of attention. How would they enjoy themselves if he left with her?

"Then let me know when you want to leave," James said, before adding quietly, "I want you to understand more about my life."

While Elise was taken aback, she watched as Ethan stood up and approached Jay.

They started drinking again, and it was clear Jay had reached his limit.

Even when Elise saw him head to the restroom, he would return looking pale each time.

As someone who frequently socialized with clients over drinks, Elise certainly recognized the signs of someone who kept vomiting and turned pale after excessive drinking.

"Elise, why don't you sing a song since you're not drinking?" William asked, having successfully taken the microphone from Nathan and settling into the seat beside Elise.

"I'm not very good..."

"You'll be bored since you're not drinking either." William seemed genuinely concerned.

"Alright, I'll choose a song. You go enjoy your drinks." Elise had to compromise, adding, "Don't drink too much. You'll regret it tomorrow."

"Okay, okay..." William replied.

But his actions spoke louder than words—he was already moving toward James's side.

As Elise walked over to the karaoke machine, William was leaning against James's shoulder.

James helped William straighten up, but didn't push him away despite showing slight annoyance.

Pursing her lips, Elise turned to select the song titled 'Third Wheel'.
###Chapter 123

The music began to play.

Elise started singing with her graceful, melodious voice.

Everyone had been pouring drinks and chatting.

But they all stopped when they heard her singing.

They were left in shock by her beautiful voice.

Elizabeth was rubbing her ears in disbelief.

"What a shame she's not competing on The Voice," she murmured. "She sings so much better than I do."

"Aren't you an actress?" Nathan pointed out.

"...Can't I explore other career paths?" Elizabeth retorted sharply.

"Well, she sings beautifully, but I wonder..." Nathan turned to William just then. "Is she trying to convey something with those lyrics?"

As William narrowed his eyes, Elizabeth looked utterly speechless.

"What are you implying?!" Elizabeth snapped. "It's just a song! Are you suggesting William is cheating on Elise?!"

"I didn't say that," Nathan quickly defended himself.

Soon, Elise finished her song.

Everyone applauded enthusiastically.

She had been too focused on singing to notice their attentive listening.

The sudden applause made her cheeks flush with embarrassment.

She quickly put down the microphone.

She headed straight for the restroom – she still felt shy about singing in front of relative strangers.

Besides, this might be the last time she'd see them.

Once she cleared things up with William, their paths would likely never cross again.

After taking several moments to compose herself, Elise opened the restroom door.

She found William waiting outside for her.

Seeing her emerge, he said, "I'm taking you home."

He had probably noticed her discomfort.

Nevertheless, Elise gathered her courage and said, "William, we need to talk."

"Yes?" William raised an eyebrow.

His cheeks were flushed from alcohol.

His eyes looked slightly unfocused.

If anything, the alcohol made him seem more approachable than usual.

Even so, Elise spoke as steadily as possible. "Let's end things here."

William's fingers trembled almost imperceptibly at his sides.

Though his reaction wasn't obvious outwardly.

Elise could feel a sudden coldness radiating from him.

However, he didn't lose his temper or appear despondent.

Instead, he asked with remarkable calmness, "Did I do something wrong?"

The question took her by surprise.

What mistake was he referring to?

Did Elise dislike his friends? His lifestyle?

He had been careful not to push too fast.

He'd only introduced his closest friends, agreeing to let Nathan celebrate his birthday together.

He believed this would help her gradually get to know him better.

He wanted her to feel comfortable around him, not resistant...

Had he moved too quickly and upset her, despite his efforts not to scare her away?

A million thoughts raced through William's mind, crushing his heart all at once!

"No," Elise said firmly. "I've told you before – I won't fall in love again. I've rejected you multiple times."

"Elise Scott." William cut her off. "Give me a real reason so I can move on. Otherwise, I won't accept this."

She couldn't recall if he had ever used her full name before.

Yet hearing it now sent a sharp pain through her chest.

It felt like she had genuinely hurt him.

But he was the one who had gotten ahead of himself.

Biting her lip and taking a moment to steady herself, she finally said, "I can't accept how you approach relationships. I know I have a troubled reputation. Men assume I have loose morals because I'm not a virgin... But I'm not that person. Even when I dated Samuel Mitchell, we only held hands and hugged, nothing more. I dislike overly intimate men because I draw the line at premarital intimacy."

She changed her tone and met William's gaze directly. "I've said a lot, but my point is – that's how I view relationships."

William returned her gaze steadily and asked, "So? How has my approach to relationships disappointed you?" He could change, couldn't he?
###Chapter 124

Even then, William remained completely unaware of his mistake.

Elise decided to be blunt. "You're dating Elizabeth, aren't you?"

She realized indirect approaches hadn't worked. Adults usually avoided such direct confrontations to spare each other embarrassment. They weren't enemies, after all.

But after repeated failed attempts at subtlety, directness seemed the only option.

William blinked, surprise flashing in his eyes.

His stunned silence prompted Elise to continue. "I've known Elizabeth long enough to recognize her goodness. She can be bold sometimes, but her heart is kind and innocent. I don't know your reasons for dating her—whether it's genuine or temporary—but she deserves better treatment. If you're truly seeking a family and mother for James, she's actually a wonderful choice."

"She certainly is," William agreed.

That familiar ache returned to Elise's chest, but she dismissed it. She had gracefully accepted that William belonged to someone else now.

After everything she'd endured, emotional pain had become more manageable.

"So you're stepping aside for her?" William asked suddenly.

"No, not specifically for her. I simply believe you two are well-matched," Elise said sincerely. "I genuinely wish you both happiness. We can all remain friends from now on."

"Do I look like I need another friend?" William countered.

"Then we don't have to see each other again," Elise stated, turning to leave.

William swallowed hard, his voice turning icy. "You're truly heartless, Elise Scott."

Elise pressed her lips together, pausing momentarily.

Truthfully, there were things she couldn't easily let go—especially James.

If she had any regret about rejecting William, it was James.

"What kind of monster do you think I am?" William suddenly exclaimed, grabbing her wrist.

The sudden motion caught her off guard, slamming her against his solid chest.

His grip was forceful, almost retaliatory.

Her nose collided painfully with his chest, bringing involuntary tears to her eyes.

As she struggled to free herself, William held her slender frame firmly against him. The scent of fresh pine and alcohol filled her senses.

Then came a sharp pain in her ear—he was biting her earlobe!

Gritting her teeth against the pain, Elise dug her nails into his arms, pressing deep into his flesh to make him release her.

Instead, he tightened his embrace—partly in retaliation, partly from fear of losing her...

Or perhaps from the anxiety of reclaiming what he thought was gone forever.

And then...

"Elizabeth!"

William released Elise's delicate ear, now flushed red from his biting.

Elizabeth stood before them.

She had simply had too much to drink and stepped out to use the restroom—not to intentionally interrupt them!

"Who am I to you?!" William demanded.

The question left Elizabeth speechless.

Had excessive happiness made him lose his mind?!

"Say it!" William barked more severely.

"My... cousin?" Elizabeth answered uncertainly, suddenly feeling like William had become someone else entirely in his euphoric state.
###Chapter 125
Family Ties?!

Elise flinched while she was still in William’s embrace, and he clearly felt her sudden tension.

“Come here,” William then told Elizabeth.

Elizabeth approached hesitantly, her steps unsteady. Though her cheeks were flushed from alcohol, her expression was one of pure apprehension, like a child caught misbehaving.

“Sorry, I didn’t mean to interrupt your moment,” she said with wounded pride. “I just really needed the bathroom.”

Though Elise had been resisting William’s advances earlier, she now buried her face completely in his chest, too mortified to face Elizabeth. William knew exactly how embarrassed Elise felt, but he showed no mercy. He pressed further, “And what do you call my grandfather?”

“William, you’re frightening me.” Elizabeth’s pupils dilated—had kissing somehow drained all his intelligence?!

Still, seeing the serious look in her cousin’s eyes, she answered truthfully. “Grandfather.”

William then looked down at Elise, who remained hidden against him. “So? Do you understand the connection between Elizabeth and me now, Ms. Scott?”

Elise’s ears turned even redder than when he’d nibbled on them earlier. She nodded without lifting her head, keeping her face pressed firmly against his chest.

“Would you like to see my birth certificate as well, Ms. Scott?” William asked pointedly.

Elise shook her head vigorously, while Elizabeth grew even more confused by their exchange.

“What’s going on with you two?” Elizabeth couldn’t help asking.

“Nothing important. Though someone mistakenly believed I was seeing two women at once—”

William paused as he suddenly felt a delicate hand cover his lips.

Elise finally looked up from his chest, her eyes pleading with him to stop.

This was beyond embarrassing—having William know was more than enough!

William’s lips curved into a smile beneath her palm, and she could clearly feel his warmth and the movement of his mouth—as if he were kissing her hand.

Elise quickly withdrew her hand from his lips, while Elizabeth watched their curious interaction with growing bewilderment.

“What are you two doing?”

“Nothing. You can leave now,” William told Elizabeth.

Elizabeth looked genuinely hurt—was she supposed to stay or go?!

“Why are you still here?” William’s tone grew slightly sterner.

Elizabeth had always been somewhat afraid of him, even since childhood.

Though she’d lived at Levine Manor since she was young, her parents rarely disciplined her—nor did Henry Levine, her maternal grandfather and family patriarch. Only her elusive cousin carried such an intimidating presence that kept her from misbehaving around him.

“I still need the washroom,” Elizabeth protested softly.

“Find another one!”

Elizabeth couldn’t argue further and hurried away immediately.

Elise tried to slip away too, but William caught her wrist the moment she moved.

He felt like her slender wrist might break with just a little pressure!

“Don’t you think you owe me an explanation, Ms. Scott?” he asked in his deep, magnetic voice.

Elise twisted the fabric of her dress so nervously it began to wrinkle.

After making such a terrible mistake, she knew the man before her wouldn’t show any mercy!
###Chapter 126

Elise appeared completely guilty under William's intense scrutiny. He studied her carefully, his expression serious. "I'm curious, Ms. Scott," he began, his tone measured. "What exactly made you believe I was dating Elizabeth?"

Despite the gravity of his question, his relaxed brow betrayed his good mood.

Elise bit her lower lip, knowing full well that mentioning Samuel Mitchell's misleading words would only provoke William's ruthless side.

"Even if I were dating Elizabeth," William continued, emphasizing each word deliberately, "it's even more puzzling that you'd find it logical for me to have you both in the same place." His gaze intensified. "How irresponsible do you think I am in a relationship that I would do something like that?"

Elise had no rebuttal.

She should have questioned the situation with her usual calm reasoning. Yet for some inexplicable reason, she had accepted the idea of William dating Elizabeth without doubt.

Now understanding her mistake, she realized how foolish she had been. Had Samuel's foolishness somehow rubbed off on her?

Or perhaps...

As the thought crossed her mind, Elise's heart skipped a beat.

She refused to acknowledge that her judgment had been clouded because she cared too much!

William continued watching her, pressing for an answer since she remained silent. "So this is why you've been distant all this time, Ms. Scott? This is why you've been constantly rejecting me, even refusing to bring me a birthday present?"

Elise's eyes snapped up at that.

He hadn't mentioned the missing birthday present until now, and she had assumed he didn't care.

She was surprised to realize he had been keeping it to himself all along.

His interrogation left her thoroughly awkward.

She took a deep breath, preparing to apologize, but William suddenly stopped his questioning. "Never mind," he said dismissively. "Forget it."

As Elise blinked in confusion, he added, "As long as we've cleared things up."

He even sighed in relief.

Having resolved the misunderstanding, he didn't need her apology—he wasn't upset anyway.

Elise couldn't comprehend how he could be so tolerant, so indulgent toward her.

It was as if he had no reservations about her!

She seriously doubted he could be this infatuated with her so soon after they met.

But if that wasn't the case, why had he fallen for her?

"After all," he suddenly added with a grin, "I discovered something quite pleasing today."

Elise was puzzled.

What could possibly please him?

Because they cleared up the misunderstanding? Because he didn't have to figure out why she had been distant?

She had to admit, he was always remarkably attentive toward her.

"I mean," he clarified, his smile taking on a different meaning, "you and Samuel never actually did it."

His expression now radiated masculine satisfaction and accomplishment!

Elise's guilt quickly transformed into indignation.

All men really were pigs!

"Come on," he said eventually, taking her hand as if it were the most natural thing. "Let's get you home."

Elise's fingers twitched. Her instinct was to pull away, but she restrained herself because of the major misunderstanding she had about him.

At that moment, she realized his strategy—by not demanding an apology, he had made her feel indebted!

Now, whatever he did, she couldn't reject him and had to endure it.

There was no doubt about it—all successful businessmen were cunning!

"Let's stay a little longer," Elise suggested, taking another deep breath. "The others haven't had enough fun yet, and I'm not working tomorrow anyway."

"Okay," William agreed, his seemingly flat reply unable to conceal his glee.
###Chapter 127

Elise and William returned to the private room. Nathan and the others immediately pulled William aside to continue drinking once they were back.

Not wanting William to worry about her feeling out of place, Elise selected a few songs to sing.

The scene in the private room settled into one where the ladies sang while the men indulged in their drinks.

Time slipped by quickly, and it grew late.

After singing lifted her spirits, Elizabeth leaned against Elise, her words slightly slurred from drink. "So? What did my dear William do to you out there?"

"Nothing happened," Elise replied, shaking her head a little too quickly.

"Nothing?" Elizabeth studied Elise's face intently, her expression full of doubt. "He was in a noticeably better mood when you two came back. And you're drinking freely now, though we could barely persuade you before... Are you sure nothing happened between you?"

Under Elizabeth's persistent gaze, Elise began to feel a flush of guilt. A knowing, suggestive smile spread across Elizabeth's face. "I see."

"It's not what you're thinking," Elise hurried to clarify—she didn't need a vivid imagination to guess Elizabeth's implication!

"It's not? I even saw scratch marks on his hand... Tell me the truth, was he good? I've always said that men who look that good usually are..."

Elise's cheeks burned as Elizabeth continued her bold speculation. Just as she gathered her thoughts to explain what really occurred, William's voice cut sharply through the noise. "Elizabeth!"

Elizabeth flinched. Wasn't he busy drinking with Nathan and the others?

But his attention had clearly been fixed on Elise the entire time.

"I need to use the restroom," Elizabeth announced, and made a hasty exit.

Elise couldn't help a small smile at how intimidated Elizabeth was by William... though, thinking about it, his quiet, solemn demeanor could indeed be quite formidable.

"Let's go," William told Elise, his tone softening considerably.

"What about everyone else?" Elise asked, glancing at the group.

"They can stay if they want," he replied flatly. It was past midnight—far too late.

"But if you leave now..."

"It's after twelve. My birthday is over. I'm no longer the guest of honor," William stated, as if it were the most logical thing in the world.

Elise found herself agreeing. Without another word, she left the room with him.

Elizabeth headed for a restroom further down the hall since Nathan was occupying the one attached to their private room.

She had soundly defeated him in their drinking game earlier.

Pausing in the corridor, her steps faltered as she spotted two unmistakable figures ahead—a sight that soured her mood instantly.

Yet, she couldn't look away, her stare fixed on them until they grew self-conscious and broke apart.

"You're utterly despicable, Andrew," Elizabeth snarled through clenched teeth.

Andrew merely chuckled, completely unbothered by her open insult.

"Move. I'm going to the restroom," she snapped.

Andrew stepped aside, and the woman with him did the same.

As Elizabeth brushed past them, she suddenly pulled out her phone, dialed a number, and shouted into the receiver, "Haven't you had enough of Andrew's cheating, Samantha?! He's constantly unfaithful! Should I hire you a male escort? A premium one—complete satisfaction guaranteed!"

Andrew's expression darkened instantly. The woman beside him noticed the change immediately.

She attempted to soothe him. "Oh, Mr. Saunders..."

"Get lost!" he roared.

The woman was taken aback. He had been perfectly pleasant with her moments before, receptive to her advances.

Now, he was discarding her just like that!

It was well-known in the club that Andrew was as handsome as he was generous. Any woman who spent a night with him was guaranteed satisfaction, both physically and materially!

However, the woman could only watch, fuming, as Andrew strode away coolly.

After finishing her call, Elizabeth returned from the restroom to find the private room... now completely empty.

It went without saying that William had taken Elise home, while Andrew was off philandering with another woman.

Nathan had likely made his escape after Elizabeth humiliated him in their drinking contest.

And as for Ethan... he had consumed quite a lot tonight as well.
###Chapter 128
Assuming Ethan had already departed, Elizabeth retrieved her purse from the sofa and prepared to exit the private room.

"Ugh!"

Just then, retching sounds echoed from the private washroom.

Elizabeth paused mid-step, hesitating briefly before pushing the door open.

She found Ethan hunched over the toilet, violently expelling the contents of his stomach.

Elizabeth pressed her lips together. Ethan had always been a lightweight when it came to alcohol, yet tonight he'd been unusually enthusiastic about drinking.

Didn't he have work tomorrow?

She'd been smart enough to request tomorrow off, knowing full well she'd be drinking heavily tonight.

Meanwhile, Ethan was now vomiting bile, questioning humanity's obsession with alcohol.

He'd never touch the stuff unless absolutely necessary!

As he tried to stand, his legs buckled beneath him - numb from prolonged crouching - causing him to stumble sideways...

Just before he hit the floor, a familiar figure caught him.

The slender frame strained under his weight, using every ounce of strength to support him.

Ethan blinked, suddenly assaulted by the thick scent of perfume...

"Achoo!"

Highly sensitive to such fragrances, he sneezed violently.

Elizabeth knew about his sensitivity perfectly well. She'd worn the perfume intentionally, wanting to maintain distance.

Whenever she approached the men during drinking games, he'd always avoided her.

Now, as he began sneezing repeatedly, she quickly released him and stepped back.

"What are you still doing here?" he asked coolly, splashing cold water on his face.

His posture straightened abruptly, transforming back into the composed man he usually was - a far cry from the one who'd been vomiting moments earlier.

"I was just leaving," Elizabeth said.

Ethan frowned. "Didn't Nathan promise to take you home?"

That was why he'd retreated to the washroom - Nathan had assured him he'd handle Elizabeth's transportation.

"He's completely wasted. I should be grateful I don't have to drag him home myself."

Ethan pulled out tissues, wiping his face and damp fingers before stating, "Come on. I'll take you home."

Elizabeth had been about to leave, seeing that Ethan seemed mostly recovered.

Some people bounced back quickly after vomiting, after all.

"Are you sure you're in any condition?" she asked, arching an eyebrow.

"Positive," Ethan replied.

Elizabeth considered refusing, but something made her agree.

It wasn't like she'd be the one suffering consequences anyway.

During the car ride home, Ethan's sneezing fits returned with vengeance. The driver looked increasingly distressed with each explosive sneeze.

"Should I divert to the hospital, sir?" the chauffeur asked anxiously.

"No need," Ethan responded nasally, pinching his nose shut.

"Just drive faster," Elizabeth instructed the driver.

"Safety comes first," Ethan interjected suddenly.

Elizabeth shot him a sideways glance, exasperated.

"I'm fine," he insisted.

"It's your endless sneezing I can't stand," Elizabeth snapped.

Ethan pressed his lips together, remaining silent while fighting to suppress another sneeze.
###Chapter 129

The car finally pulled up at Levine Manor. Ethan practically stumbled out, immediately doubling over and vomiting violently.

Elizabeth stood behind him, watching as his head hung low between his knees.
He had seemed fine during the ride, but now he was retching as if his stomach was turning inside out.

"Should I call the family doctor–" she began.

"Achoo!" He sneezed before she could finish, then continued heaving.
It was a truly dreadful sight.

Elizabeth took a cautious step back. "I'll get the doctor," she said firmly.

She hurried into the manor.
Alexander Johnson, the family physician, eventually helped Ethan inside since Elizabeth lacked the strength to support him.

After taking a shower, she stepped out of her room and noticed Ethan's door was still open, light spilling into the hallway.
She hesitated for a moment before deciding to check on him.

Alexander had hooked Ethan up to an IV. The man lay pale against the pillows, looking less drunk and more genuinely ill.
His eyes were closed, suggesting he was finally asleep.

"Ms. York." Alexander greeted her respectfully.

"How is he?" Elizabeth asked quietly.

"Severe alcohol intoxication. We'll see how he responds to the IV fluids. If there's no improvement by morning, he'll need a gastroscopy at the hospital. We must monitor for any triggered gastric complications."

"Is it really that serious?"

"Mr. Parker has a history of mild gastritis and typically avoids heavy drinking," Alexander explained. "His system simply couldn't handle the amount of alcohol he consumed tonight. Please advise him to be more cautious in the future."

"I will," Elizabeth replied. "How long will the IV take?"

"Approximately two hours."

It was already 1 AM, and Ethan had work in the morning...

"Don't worry, Ms. York. I'll stay with Mr. Parker."

Elizabeth nodded and left, but sleep eluded her as she lay in bed.
Her mind kept replaying how Ethan kept drinking with Nathan, each time intercepting when Nathan tried to drink with her...

She pulled the blanket tighter around herself.
He shouldn't have given her hope!

Under the same moon, William was driving Elise home, though he had fallen asleep during the journey.

Seeing he remained unconscious even after they arrived at her apartment building, Elise gestured to the driver before carefully opening the car door.

Just as she was about to slip out, William's hand shot out and gripped her wrist. In the next instant, she was pulled firmly into his embrace!

He smelled strongly of alcohol, though the scent was somewhat tempered by his usual pine fragrance.
Elise struggled slightly—he was supposed to be asleep!

"Let me go!" she protested. "I'm home. You should head back now."

William blinked, his gaze unfocused as he looked outside. It took him a moment to process that they had indeed reached her apartment.

"I'll walk you upstairs," he insisted.

"It's very late," she refused.

"Exactly why I'm accompanying you. It's unsafe this late," he argued.

Elise was speechless.
He was the primary reason it felt unsafe!

Nevertheless, she alighted with him, watching as he walked ahead with surprising steadiness.
Had she misjudged how drunk he was?

Soon, they reached her door. "This is it. You can go now," Elise said.

"Okay," William replied simply.
###Chapter 130

Elise turned and entered her door passcode. She stepped inside once the door clicked open.

The man who had promised to leave moments ago remained rooted outside her doorway.

"It's getting late. You should go now," she urged him once more.

"Alright," William replied instantly.

Yet his body betrayed his words, refusing to move from her doorstep.

Elise clenched her jaw and shut the door firmly. She activated the door monitor immediately. William still stood there, unmoving.

Exasperated, she swung the door open again. "I thought you were leaving?"

"I just remembered something important," he said, his expression turning serious. "You never gave me a birthday gift."

Elise pressed her lips together. She had assumed this would be their final parting tonight! Any gift would only encourage false hopes and complicate matters further.

"Anything would suffice," William insisted, his meaningful gaze impossible to ignore.

She sighed deeply, realizing he wouldn't leave until satisfied. Resigning herself, she approached him, rose on her toes, and wrapped her arms around his neck. She pressed a quick kiss to his cheek.

William froze completely. When his eyes met hers again, Elise felt heat rushing to her cheeks, her composure crumbling under his intense stare.

"Just go already," she mumbled, looking away.

"I never expected you preferred... physical expressions of affection." A smug smile played on his lips. "I actually thought a simple 'happy birthday' would be enough."

His eyes, which had been clouded with alcohol earlier, now sparkled with clear amusement. He had received more than he anticipated, yet pretended otherwise?!

She desperately wanted to wipe that self-satisfied look off his face right then.

"That being said," he continued, his voice dropping to a deeper, more intimate tone, "I definitely prefer your method of celebration."

Elise felt her cheeks burning crimson.

"Goodnight," he finally said, turning to leave with evident satisfaction.

She slammed the door shut immediately, leaning against it while clutching her flaming cheeks.

Elizabeth's question echoed in her mind - had she asked if William was good?

Yes, he certainly was exceptional!

She couldn't help wondering how many other women had fallen under his spell, dancing to his tune just like this.

Elise woke much later than usual, having struggled to fall asleep. The persistent ringing of her phone finally dragged her from slumber.

She reacted minimally upon seeing the caller ID, answering with unusual calmness.

"Elise Scott!" Benjamin was shouting through the receiver. "Why aren't you at the hospital?!"

"Am I expected to live there permanently?" Elise responded coolly.

Benjamin had no choice but to swallow his frustration.

It had taken him two full days to persuade his mother to apologize to Elise, hoping to settle matters out of court.

They arrived at the hospital only to discover Elise had been discharged.

She was deliberately making this difficult, wasn't she?!

Margaret had been scowling throughout the journey to the hospital. Now her face contorted with pure rage. "She expects me to visit her personally to apologize?! Who does she think she is?! Tell her to come to the family mansion immediately!"

Margaret's voice carried so loudly that Elise heard every word clearly.

Benjamin had obviously brought Margaret to the hospital to apologize, but it appeared Margaret intended to threaten Elise instead.

Seeing no point in continuing the conversation, Elise simply ended the call.

Naturally, Benjamin was furious about being hung up on.

Wheeling to face Margaret, he thundered, "Don't you understand the situation?! Elise wants you behind bars! The only reason you weren't held in police custody longer is because of your age! That doesn't make you innocent or entitled to special treatment! Plus, William Levine is backing Elise - all she needs to do is say the word, and you'll be serving time!"
###Chapter 131

Margaret stood frozen, staring at Benjamin. Her own son had never raised his voice at her like this before!

He always followed her commands without question. But now he was publicly reprimanding her in front of all these medical staff!

The humiliation burned through her. At her age and status, this was utterly unacceptable!

She couldn't bear such disrespect. Her eyes reddened as she pointed a trembling finger back at him. "How dare you speak to me that way—"

But Benjamin was too consumed by rage to care about her wounded pride.

He was terrified that Elise would escalate this situation and ruin Scott Enterprise's reputation, along with his own standing as Crestwood's most prominent heir.

Otherwise, he wouldn't care if Margaret ended up in prison, let alone have to endure Elise's temper!

"Do whatever you want!" he snapped harshly before turning on his heel and storming away.

Margaret's blood pressure spiked watching her own son abandon her at the hospital without a backward glance.

Victoria, who had accompanied them and witnessed the entire confrontation, quickly stepped forward to soothe her. "Margaret, you need to understand Benjamin's position right now. Scott Enterprise is facing serious challenges, yet he still arranged your bail and legal counsel despite his overwhelming workload. Elise has solid evidence against you. If this goes to trial, you could face three years for intentional harm. At your age, you wouldn't survive a month in prison!"

"It can't be that serious," Margaret protested in disbelief.

She had always viewed Elise as insignificant. Having dominated the girl for most of her life, she never imagined Elise would ever fight back effectively.

"It absolutely is that serious!" Victoria insisted firmly. "Just listen to Benjamin—apologize to Elise. She's still your granddaughter. She won't completely disregard family ties."

Margaret still huffed indignantly. The mere thought of yielding to Elise made her stomach churn.

"Think of it this way," Victoria continued persuasively. "Once this blows over, we just need to be more careful not to give her leverage against us. She's still just a young woman—she can't possibly outmaneuver you forever. You'll have plenty of opportunities to settle the score later."

Victoria's words provided some comfort, and Margaret refused to believe she couldn't eventually put Elise in her place!

Meanwhile, Elise found herself unable to fall back asleep after being awakened. Out of habit, she got out of bed and picked up her phone to check the news.

The internet continued to rage against Samuel and Sophia, with the scandal showing no signs of dying down—if anything, it was gaining more momentum.

Elise checked the stock performance of Mitchell Corp and Scott Enterprise, noting both companies' market values had been dropping since the market opened.

Given their substantial market capitalizations, this translated to losses of hundreds of thousands per day. A sense of satisfaction warmed Elise, and she set aside her phone, rising to begin her morning routine.

There was no denying William's crucial role in her complete victory.

She remembered how he had taken the initiative to help her, only for her to dismiss him as a womanizer who treated relationships carelessly. Yesterday had been his birthday, and she hadn't even considered wishing him well...

Except for that kiss.

Elise's cheeks warmed again at the memory.

She and William weren't even dating, yet he had already acquired numerous boyfriend privileges!

What surprised her more? She didn't feel upset about it at all!

In contrast, her relationship with Samuel had been practically chaste.

Probably because they were both young back then, and he would always respect her boundaries.

But by the time they were older, he was already cheating on her with Sophia.

Why would he endure Elise's reluctance when someone else was readily satisfying his physical desires?

Even now, thinking about that man still stirred complicated emotions within her.

She released a deep sigh and picked up her phone to text William.

[Are you free tonight?]

[Yes.]

Another immediate reply.

Elise couldn't help smiling. Despite the single-word response, she could vividly imagine what was happening on the other end.

[Dinner tonight. My place.]

[Okay.]
###Chapter 132

William accepted Elise's invitation without hesitation.

She began typing another message.

[Bring James…]

Then her fingers stilled.

She deleted the draft.

Elise left the bathroom and changed into fresh clothes.

She headed out to purchase ingredients for dinner.

How difficult could cooking be?

After gathering everything she needed, she downloaded a cooking app.

She followed the recipes meticulously.

That evening, the doorbell chimed.

Elise still wore her apron as she hurried to answer.

William stood outside, holding a bouquet.

He looked impeccably formal in his black suit and silver tie.

"I came straight from the office," he explained his attire.

Elise smiled knowingly.

She accepted the flowers and handed him his slippers.

"Please come in."

William slipped them on and entered.

An unusual scent immediately caught his attention.

It smelled like food, yet something was off.

His eyes drifted to the dining table.

Several homemade dishes awaited them.

Grilled mackerel.

Vinegar-glazed ribs.

Fluffy omelets.

Creamy mashed potatoes.

Simmering chicken soup.

Something still sizzled in the kitchen pan.

Elise rushed back to the stove, looking flustered.

William approached her. "Do you need any help—"

"Ah!" Elise yelped as oil spattered violently from the pan.

She jumped back, startled.

William couldn't suppress a smile.

He rarely saw this vulnerable, real side of her.

Usually she remained so composed.

"It's fine," she said quickly. "Just wait in the living room. I'll be done soon."

William glanced around the chaotic kitchen.

He noticed the carefully sliced beef and various condiments.

She was attempting beef tenderloin?

That required precise flame control.

Ambitious choice.

Not wanting to distract her further, he retreated.

The kitchen was too small for her to avoid him.

Elise's heart still raced from the oil explosion.

Somehow, she managed to finish the dish.

The cooking app's instructions seemed questionable.

But she couldn't pinpoint exactly why.

She plated the beef tenderloin and brought it to the table.

She arranged the fork, spoon, and knife neatly.

Then she removed her apron and went to change.

William had dressed formally—she shouldn't be too casual.

When they sat down at the modest dining table, the atmosphere felt perfect.

She poured wine for William.

For herself, she chose sparkling juice.

"Is this for my birthday celebration?" William asked.

"Yes," Elise replied softly. "And an apology."

The smile William tried to conceal was unmistakable.
###Chapter 133

Elise gestured toward the table. "Go ahead."

Her eyes sparkled with hopeful anticipation.

William picked up his knife with practiced grace.

He sliced neatly through the grilled mackerel.

Bringing the piece to his mouth, he chewed thoughtfully.

His expression remained unreadable, giving nothing away.

Elise watched him intently, searching for any reaction.

After swallowing the fish, he sampled the vinegar ribs.

Then he methodically tasted each remaining dish.

Finally, he lifted his wine glass, taking a delicate sip.

"Well?" Elise finally broke the silence. "How is it?"

William dabbed the corner of his mouth with his napkin. "Acceptable. Not terrible."

"Really?" Elise reached for her own cutlery eagerly.

William's hand closed around her wrist, stopping her movement.

She looked at him in surprise.

"One of us suffering food poisoning is sufficient," he stated calmly.

He took another sip of wine, his gaze steady.

Elise understood his meaning but pulled her hand free.

She was determined to taste her first cooking attempt herself.

The moment the vinegar ribs touched her tongue, her face contorted.

She spat the morsel out immediately.

Vinegar ribs shouldn't taste bitter!

William chuckled softly at her reaction.

He handed her wine glass to her.

She accepted it gratefully, washing the taste away.

"How did you eat that?" she demanded, turning to him.

"Your earnest effort deserved appreciation," he replied with an amused smile.

Elise fell silent, slightly embarrassed.

"Your omelets were actually quite good, Ms. Scott," he offered gently.

She bit her lower lip in frustration.

She'd hoped to replicate Elizabeth's cooking skills.

Clearly, she still had much to learn.

Gathering all the dishes from the table, she returned to the kitchen.

She prepared two plates of pasta instead.

She watched William eat, noting how elegant he looked.

Even simple actions seemed refined when he performed them.

His occasional sips of wine complemented the meal perfectly.

"I overestimated my abilities," she admitted awkwardly, staring at her plate.

"Having one competent cook in the household is adequate," he responded bluntly.

Elise had no retort, merely pressing her lips together.

After a moment, she gathered her courage and looked up.

"Do you truly... have feelings for me?" she asked hesitantly.

"I believed I'd made that quite evident," William answered.

"But I don't feel the same way about you," she stated frankly.

"That much is also apparent," he acknowledged calmly, as if expecting her response.

"I've shared my past with you..." she continued uncertainly. "If you can accept limited intimacy for the foreseeable future, and that my schedule will keep us from meeting frequently, and that I require considerable time to develop trust and deeper feelings without making our relationship public..."

William listened intently, his grip tightening on his fork.

"If those conditions are acceptable, we could attempt dating," she finished quickly, lowering her gaze.

She pretended to eat her pasta without actually taking a bite.

Her cheeks flushed despite her attempt at composure.

As a twenty-five-year-old woman, this wasn't her first relationship.

Yet she felt unexpectedly vulnerable.

She wasn't the only one affected.

William swallowed hard, his Adam's apple moving visibly.

Finally, he spoke. "Agreed."

His voice carried a slight tremor!

For him, time wasn't a pressing concern.

Elise glanced up cautiously, finding his gaze resting gently on her face.
###Chapter 134

Their gazes locked, and undeniable emotions began to bloom between them.

Elise truly never imagined that after surviving all her hardships—and more recently Samuel—she would suddenly embark on another relationship.

She wasn't entirely certain about this decision, yet she took that first step anyway.

Was William naturally skilled at charming words, or was he simply not as icy as she had assumed—not as disillusioned with the world as she believed?

After defining their relationship, an unexpected silence fell between them, creating a palpable awkwardness.

"Do you despise the man who slept with you that night?" William asked abruptly.

A flicker of nervousness crossed his typically composed expression, while Elise wondered why he would bring that up.

Perhaps because her aversion to men seemed to stem from that incident?

"I do," she admitted.

William's eyes subtly shifted as Elise continued, "He was fully conscious that night. I remember it vividly—begging him to stop, pleading with him not to touch me... but he never listened."

The memory still carried a pain that lingered stubbornly in her mind.

"But is it possible your body's response differed from what you described?" William murmured.

However, his voice was too soft for Elise to catch, and she asked, "What did you say?"

Her right ear remained deafened at that moment, and her left ear couldn't pick up such quiet words.

"What if you encounter that man again someday? What would you do?" William rephrased his question.

"I won't," Elise stated firmly. "Because I never saw his face throughout the entire ordeal. I don't know his identity, so I'll simply pretend he never existed—even if I'm only deceiving myself!"

William pressed his lips together and reached for her hand.

Her fingers twitched instinctively, and he immediately released her, sensing her discomfort.

Yet just as he withdrew, she suddenly caught his hand, startling him.

"I'll try my best to grow accustomed to you," she promised.

William's lips curved into a gentle smile.

"That's alright," he said softly. "I can be patient."

Elise's heart quickened its pace—she'd be lying if she claimed to feel nothing.

If she hadn't endured so much tragedy, she knew she would love William with every fiber of her being, without hesitation.

But despite her impulses, memories of betrayal and heartbreak surfaced unbidden.

She understood she shouldn't project others' mistakes onto William, yet humans are emotional creatures, far less rational than they pretend to be.

Perhaps time could heal these wounds!

Soon, evening deepened, and Elise walked William to the door.

Now that they were officially together, parting felt unexpectedly difficult.

"Get some rest soon," William finally told her.

"You too," Elise replied.

"Good night."

"Good night."

"Now close the door."

Elise found herself speechless, feeling they might continue this exchange indefinitely at the doorway.

Well, someone had to break the cycle...

However, just as she moved to shut the door, William suddenly reached out to stop her.

While Elise stood frozen in surprise, he leaned down and pressed a soft kiss to her forehead.

Elise stiffened completely.

"Boyfriend privileges." He smiled warmly, then gently closed the door for her.

Heat rushed to Elise's cheeks.

Who was it that claimed he could wait again?!

Honestly, all men were truly impossible!
###Chapter 135

The morning sun filtered through Elise's curtains, casting warm patterns across her bed.

She stirred awake, reaching instinctively for her phone on the nightstand.

A text notification glowed on the screen, sent from William Levine.

[Good morning, beautiful.]

Her lips curved into an unconscious smile as she read the message.

She lingered in bed for a moment longer, the simple words warming her heart.

Then she rose, determined to maintain her usual routine despite the flutter in her chest.

She dressed carefully in her professional attire.

Her makeup was applied with precision, each stroke restoring her corporate armor.

The drive to Stellar Group passed in a blur of morning traffic and scattered thoughts.

But as she approached her office, the familiar figures waiting there snapped her back to reality.

Benjamin Scott stood with Victoria Scott and Margaret Scott, forming an unwelcome welcoming committee.

"Oh, you're here, Elise." Victoria's voice dripped with false warmth that didn't reach her eyes.

Elise gave them a sidelong glance, already understanding their purpose.

She didn't break stride, moving past them into her office sanctuary.

Taking her seat behind the polished desk, she watched as they filed in after her.

"Elise," Benjamin began, his tone carefully neutral. "Your grandmother has come to make amends."

He shot a meaningful look at Margaret, who stood stiffly beside him.

Margaret's expression was tight, her displeasure at having to humble herself evident in every line of her body.

Yet she knew the alternative—facing criminal charges at her age was unthinkable.

Besides, there would be other opportunities to put Elise in her place once this was resolved.

Taking a visible breath, Margaret stepped forward, attempting a grandmotherly expression.

She reached for Elise's hand, but Elise withdrew it before contact could be made.

The rejection hung in the air between them, stark and undeniable.

Margaret recovered quickly, her smile strained but persistent. "Elise, I behaved poorly the other day. My emotions got the better of me, and I struck you. I heard about your ear injury, so I brought some nutritional supplements and fresh fruits to aid your recovery."

She placed several elegantly wrapped packages on the desk.

Elise glanced at them dismissively, showing no particular interest in the offerings.

A flash of irritation crossed Margaret's features before she controlled it again under Benjamin's warning look.

"I recognize my mistake," Margaret continued, her voice taking on a pleading note. "Please don't make this more difficult than it needs to be. You wouldn't want your elderly grandmother kneeling before you, would you? We're family, after all. You can't possibly want to see me imprisoned at my age."

Elise saw through the performance perfectly.

The Scotts were only concerned about their reputation, about avoiding public scandal.

This apology was merely a formality before they launched their next attack.

Still, Elise knew when to pick her battles. "Of course I don't want to see you in prison..."

Ruining her own reputation over the Scotts wasn't worth the cost.

The court of public opinion would never side completely with a granddaughter prosecuting her grandmother.

"But do you really believe a simple apology is sufficient?" Elise finished, her gaze sharp.

Margaret had expected this resistance.

She had prepared for Elise's stubbornness.

Everything could wait until they had secured what they needed from Elise.

"What would you have me do to earn your forgiveness?" Margaret asked, her voice deceptively mild. "Shall I offer you the chance to strike me in return?"

She presented the suggestion with apparent sincerity.

Elise smiled coldly.

If she dared to hit Margaret, her legal case would evaporate instantly.

The Scotts would use that single moment of violence to destroy her completely.

"I wouldn't dare strike you," Elise said bluntly.

Near the door, Victoria concealed her disappointment.

She had hidden a camera in her purse, hoping to capture exactly that moment.

Evidence of Elise attacking her grandmother would have been perfect ammunition.

"Then what would satisfy you?" Margaret asked, maintaining her composed demeanor.
###Chapter 136

Elise let out a soft sigh, her voice laced with resignation. "Well, it seems there's truly nothing I can do to stand against you..."

Margaret's heart swelled with triumph. She was certain Elise was already surrendering.

Youth might have its advantages, but experience always prevailed! Elise never stood a chance against her cunning!

"Since we're family, we shouldn't let grievances fester between us," Margaret said cheerfully, her tone dripping with false warmth. "In that case, you should go to the police station and withdraw your charges-"

"I want fifteen percent of Scott Enterprises' shares." Elise's sharp interruption sliced through Margaret's words.

Benjamin and Victoria's faces immediately darkened. "Don't be so greedy, Elise!" Victoria exclaimed, her voice trembling with agitation. "You're demanding a kingdom when we're offering you a village!"

"An outsider like you has no say in Scott Enterprises' matters!" Elise snapped, her icy gaze freezing Victoria in place. "Who do you think you are, daring to criticize me?!"

Victoria flushed crimson, rendered speechless by Elise's sharp retort.

That was when Benjamin spoke, his voice cold and final. "No. Absolutely not."

His refusal was short, blunt, and left no room for negotiation.

"Then we'll meet in court," Elise replied with nonchalant indifference.

"Elise Scott!" Benjamin roared, his temper exploding as he launched into insults. "You should know your place! Do you really think I'd hand over fifteen percent of my company's shares?! What right do you have to take what's mine?! Keep dreaming!"

While Margaret naturally disagreed with Elise's demand, her son's rigid stance secretly upset her.

The contemptuous look in Elise's eyes only fueled her fury further!

"Those shares are my rightful inheritance," Elise stated coldly.

"Your rightful inheritance?!" Benjamin bellowed, his face turning purple with rage. "What gives you the right-"

"My mother held thirty percent of Scott Enterprises' shares while she was alive," Elise cut him off, enunciating each word with precision. "Her will clearly stated that Stellar Group belongs to me, but mentioned nothing else. However, I've consulted multiple lawyers, and the law dictates that any inheritance must be divided equally between the deceased's spouse and their children. Legally, I'm entitled to fifteen percent of Scott Enterprises' shares."

As she spoke, she swiftly produced a copy of the share certificate proving her mother once possessed thirty percent of Scott Enterprises' shares.

She had prepared this document in advance, anticipating Benjamin would bring Margaret here.

Benjamin snatched the paper from her hands, his expression growing darker with each word he read.

"If you refuse to transfer those shares, we can settle this in court as well," Elise continued neutrally. "I don't mind handling two lawsuits simultaneously. After all, I already have no reputation left in Crestwood. At worst, our entire family will become the city's favorite laughingstock."

"Enough!" Benjamin thundered, his face dark as storm clouds while his body shook with rage.

He never should have sent her abroad–he should have eliminated her on the operating table all those years ago!

"Well, take your time to consider your options," Elise replied, completely unfazed by Benjamin's threatening demeanor. "You have exactly twenty-four hours. Tomorrow morning, I'll be assembling my legal team to discuss both lawsuits-"

"You'll get your shares!" Benjamin agreed through gritted teeth, the words tasting like poison.

"You can't, Benjamin!" Victoria cried out immediately. "You only control half the company's shares! Giving away fifteen percent is-"

She absolutely couldn't accept this–she could sacrifice anything else, but all Scott family assets rightfully belonged to her son!

No one else deserved even a single penny!

"Shut your mouth!" Benjamin barked at her. "Are you going to stand by and watch my mother go to prison?!"

"But that's nearly a third of your controlling interest-"

Smack!

Margaret suddenly slapped Victoria across the face, leaving her daughter-in-law stunned while she shrieked in fury, "You ungrateful wretch! After everything I've done for you–you don't even care what happens to me!"
###Chapter 137
