Tears pooled in Victoria's eyes. She finally understood the stinging pain of Margaret's slap.

She had believed Elise was pretending about her hearing loss. Now, all she could hear was relentless ringing in her ears.

But she lacked Elise's boldness to press charges against Margaret. She had to endure this humiliation silently.

The thought of Elise gaining ten percent of Scott Enterprise shares was unbearable.

Suppressing her fury, Victoria maintained her fragile facade before Benjamin and Margaret. "You misunderstand, Margaret. It's not that I don't care. I simply think Elise has overstepped—"

"Quiet!" Margaret snapped, cutting off Victoria's explanation.

Margaret knew better than anyone how Elise had crossed every line.

What pained her most was her powerlessness against Elise.

Meanwhile, Elise watched them with detached amusement, a faint smile playing on her lips.

Benjamin and Sophia had always stood by when Margaret abused her in the past, sometimes even encouraging it.

How dramatically the situation had reversed!

Seeing Elise's expression twisted Victoria's face with suppressed rage.

Elise was... mocking her?!

This was more than mockery. This was calculated revenge!

Could Elise have discovered that Victoria was the one who provoked Margaret to storm into Elise's office and slap her?!

That same slap had now been returned directly to her face?!

Benjamin restrained his anger. "My lawyer will send you the share transfer agreement tomorrow..."

"No need. I have the agreement right here. You just need to sign it," Elise said, producing the pre-prepared document.

The sight of the papers made Benjamin tremble with renewed fury.

Elise had planned every step meticulously!

He had walked right into her trap and suffered complete humiliation!

He took the agreement, scanning it quickly. "Drop the charges against your grandmother now."

"Sign first," Elise stated firmly.

When Benjamin hesitated, she continued, "You're in no position to negotiate. Those shares belong to me whether I drop the charges or not. But if you transfer them willingly, I might show some leniency toward Margaret."

Gritting his teeth, Benjamin signed the agreement immediately.

"Your fingerprint, please," Elise reminded coolly.

Clenching his jaw, Benjamin pressed his thumb to the document.

With the signed transfer in hand, Elise smiled in satisfaction. "Don't worry," she said. "I'll visit the police station later."

Having suffered utter humiliation, Benjamin turned to leave without another word.

"One more thing," Elise called after him. "Sophia is too embarrassed to remain at Stellar Group. What kind of CEO fabricates scandals against their own company? Even if I were willing to overlook it, the other employees wouldn't. To avoid further embarrassment, have her complete her resignation paperwork with HR."

Benjamin's expression darkened further.

Within months, Elise had systematically removed his family and their allies from Stellar Group, taking complete control of the company's management... and now she had seized ten percent of Scott Enterprise shares!

The thought fueled his rage as he kicked the door open on his way out.

Margaret and Victoria could only follow quickly, making their escape.

Elise watched their retreating figures, her gaze turning icy.

She would reclaim everything her mother had given to the Scotts.

They deserved none of it!

A week later, Sophia and Samuel remained targets of relentless online criticism.
###Chapter 138

The media storm showed no signs of stopping. It felt like someone was deliberately fueling every outlet to keep churning out increasingly scandalous stories about Samuel and Sophia.

No other news could break through the headlines. Their scandal remained firmly planted at the top of every search engine's trending list. The public outrage seemed to intensify with each passing day.

Naturally, the businesses of both families were suffering a continuous, sharp decline. If this continued, both Mitchell Corp and Scott Enterprise would face bankruptcy over a single scandal. It would be an unprecedented catastrophe in corporate history.

During a tense board meeting at Mitchell Corp, Richard Mitchell was shouting at Samuel in front of all the board members and senior executives. "You can't even silence the media on one scandal?! How long has this been going on? Do you intend to drag the entire company down with you?"

Samuel was seething with indignation. He had been certain the scandal would eventually fade from public attention. Yet, it stubbornly remained at the top of search trends after all this time.

Someone was undoubtedly orchestrating this from behind the scenes. He didn't need to guess who it was. The thought infuriated him. To think Elise had so completely enchanted William. The same woman Samuel couldn't even touch had willingly thrown herself into that man's arms.

The more Samuel dwelled on it, the more his rage boiled over. Still, he ground out through clenched teeth, "I'll have it handled by the end of today."

"You'd better not disappoint me!" Richard snapped before storming out of the conference room. The others quickly filed out, leaving Samuel alone.

He immediately pulled out his phone and dialed a number. "Amanda."

"What is it, Samuel?" She sounded distinctly impatient. "I'm in the middle of my makeup session. There's a fashion gala tonight."

Mitchell Corp's troubles were never Amanda Mitchell's concern. Her world revolved around her own happiness and carefree lifestyle.

"Will Elizabeth York be attending the gala?" Samuel asked, his voice tight.

"Why are you asking about her?" Amanda's expression instantly cooled. In Amanda's view, Elizabeth was her arch-nemesis, even though they weren't remotely on the same professional level. Amanda remained utterly convinced Elizabeth's A-list status was solely achieved by sleeping her way to the top. She believed if she had been willing to do the same, she would have surpassed Elizabeth long ago.

"Is she going or not?" Samuel scowled, unwilling to entertain her petty grievances.

"Yes! She's practically the guest of honor!" Amanda snapped irritably. "Just focus on Sophia, Samuel. Elizabeth is nothing but trash."

"Worry about yourself," Samuel retorted coldly and ended the call.

He tapped open his phone's photo gallery, scrolling through the incriminating pictures he had of Elizabeth and William. A scandal involving the star actress would undoubtedly dominate the search trends for quite some time...

At the gala venue, Amanda was meticulously checking her reflection. She had spent hours perfecting her appearance. She hadn't wanted to attend this event at all. She knew all the attention would be on Elizabeth, and she had no desire to be overshadowed.

The only reason she had come was because she'd heard Ethan Parker would be attending. For a chance to see him, she decided to endure the humiliation.

Suddenly, she sprang to her feet. Her manager, Jennifer, quickly interjected, "It's not time for your entrance yet, Amanda."

"I know," Amanda snapped impatiently. "I just need some air. Don't bother me."

Jennifer wanted to say more, but knowing Amanda's volatile temper, she chose silence over verbal abuse.

Amanda swept out of the dressing room and began prowling the hallways. She searched until she found Ethan Parker's designated breakroom.

Without bothering to knock, she pushed the door open and stepped inside. She walked in just as Ethan was in the middle of changing his shirt.

He was shirtless. The defined, chiseled lines of his chest and torso were everything she adored.
###Chapter 139

Ethan's face darkened instantly as his door burst open just as he removed his shirt.

Amanda stood there, draped in an ostentatiously gaudy gown, her face caked with heavy makeup as she appeared in his private space.

"Oh! I'm so sorry. I must have the wrong room..." Amanda exclaimed with her painfully transparent acting, making a show of turning to leave.

Simultaneously, she deliberately stepped on the hem of her own dress, propelling herself directly into his arms.

Ethan instinctively moved forward to catch her, and Amanda seized the opportunity to press her cheek firmly against his chest.

It felt solid, even springy!

Ethan frowned, immediately recognizing her intentions.

But just as he prepared to push her away, his eyes caught Elizabeth standing coolly at the doorway, watching their entangled forms with detached amusement.

A mocking smirk played on her lips.

Were they really this desperate? Or perhaps hotel rooms had become too mundane for their antics?

Nevertheless, she spoke calmly to Ethan. "You forgot your bowtie in my car."

They had both come from the set of Pomp and Circumstance, with Elizabeth's manager eagerly inviting Ethan into their luxury vehicle.

Ethan immediately shoved Amanda away, leaving her fuming with suppressed rage.

She knew it! Something was definitely going on between these two—why else would Ethan's bowtie be in Elizabeth's car?

She even suspected Elizabeth of stealing her role... by seducing Ethan!

The more she thought about it, the more infuriating it became!

As Ethan approached Elizabeth to retrieve his bowtie, his fingers accidentally brushed against hers, and she immediately jerked her hand away as if touching something contaminated.

Ethan's hand hung suspended in the air while Elizabeth stated, "You two can carry on."

With those words, she turned and strode away confidently.

Ethan watched her retreating form, blinking slowly... before noticing from his peripheral vision that Amanda had closed in on him again.

"What's your relationship with Elizabeth, Mr. Parker? Why would your bowtie be in her car?" Amanda asked in her most coquettish, affectionate tone.

"Did you need something specific, Ms. Mitchell?" Ethan asked, taking a deliberate step back to maintain clear distance between them.

Amanda felt irritation flare but suppressed it. "Just paying a social visit, and to thank you for your direction during that commercial—"

"I need to change now, so please leave if there's nothing important!" Ethan suddenly snapped.

His cold demeanor toward her contrasted sharply with how he had reacted to Elizabeth moments earlier!

"I'm willing to play the casting couch game too, Mr. Parker," Amanda declared with serious expression. "I won't demand anything else—"

"Leave immediately if you wish to avoid further humiliation," Ethan growled icily. "Or I can't guarantee the public won't discover how eager you are for the casting couch!"

Amanda paled dramatically, stunned that Ethan would treat her this way.
###Chapter 140

Amanda had no alternative but to depart, clenching her jaw tightly under Ethan's watchful gaze.
Still, she always obtained whatever she desired!
The fashion gala commenced with celebrities appearing on the red carpet according to a predetermined schedule.

Typically, Amanda would be scheduled early in such events, but due to her family's powerful connections, most organizers positioned her later. Since Mitchell Corp served as one of the event's primary sponsors, she secured the second-to-last slot.

When her turn arrived, however, she declined to walk, citing sudden health concerns.

The red carpet remained empty for ten minutes, yet organizers couldn't compel Amanda—nobody dared challenge her family's influence.

Left with no options, the organizers urgently requested Elizabeth to salvage the situation.

Elizabeth maintained no diva reputation and agreed immediately, despite clear disapproval from Linda, her manager.

Just as she prepared to step onto the crimson pathway, Elizabeth overheard a crew member accidentally mention Amanda's plans.

So Amanda intended to appear last?!

Elizabeth immediately halted and informed the crew, "I'll walk the red carpet, but the event must conclude after my appearance—nobody follows me. That was the organizers' original promise!"

The crew hesitated, well aware Amanda feigned illness to secure the final spot.

"This isn't negotiable." Elizabeth stood firm.

Even Linda felt surprised by Elizabeth's determination, though secretly pleased—she'd frequently advocated for Elizabeth, who possessed every right to assert herself.

Her protégé had truly matured.

Consequently, the crew reported Elizabeth's demands to their superiors.

After considerable delay, Linda's phone rang with her boss's call.

"What?!" she exclaimed sharply. "Why should Amanda Mitchell appear after Elizabeth?! Does her name carry any weight in this industry?!"

"The organizers called—Mitchell Corp pressured them. It's unfortunate, but Amanda possesses privileged lineage we can't challenge," her boss explained. "Please calm Elizabeth—I'll treat her to an exquisite dinner afterward, my personal apology!"

Linda couldn't dispute further after her boss's plea, reluctantly ending the call to inform Elizabeth, "The boss insists we proceed."

Elizabeth's expression darkened, while Linda sighed ironically. "Amanda benefits from influential parentage, and rumors swirl about her involvement with Ethan Parker. Even without Levine inheritance claims, he remains family—every industry player grants him deference. With Amanda attached to him, she struts with impunity."

Linda grew increasingly irritated discussing Amanda, despising the woman's theatrical arrogance.

"Incidentally, doesn't Ethan collaborate with you? How did that opportunist Amanda surpass you?!" Linda seethed with frustration. "If only you'd leverage connections, Elizabeth... Amanda possesses nothing compared to you!"

"Let's proceed." Elizabeth refused to hear another word.

She had stubbornly entered the entertainment industry, vowing independence from Levine resources.

Ethan remained the only industry professional aware of her true identity.

Thus, she had no choice but to endure this humiliation.

After Elizabeth completed her red carpet circuit, Amanda materialized at the entrance, smirking triumphantly. Entering the venue, she glided beside Elizabeth, pausing to whisper venomously, "My apologies, Elizabeth. I never intended to claim your position, but I felt unwell. Surely you understand?"

"Completely," Elizabeth smiled faintly. "After all, should I bite back when a bitch attacks first?"
Amanda's face contorted.
Did Elizabeth just label her a bitch?!

Simultaneously, Elizabeth scoffed. "Additionally, you obstruct my path. Please move—well-trained canines understand basic commands."

Amanda's eyes flashed with malice before she suddenly collapsed onto the floor directly before Elizabeth! As spectators watched, Amanda gazed tearfully at Elizabeth. "You tripped me... I never wanted to take your place..."
###Chapter 141

Gasps echoed through the venue as all eyes remained fixed on Amanda and Elizabeth.

The event crew rushed to Amanda's side, their faces etched with concern. They gently helped her up, noticing her pained expression.

Amanda had taken a nasty fall. She winced dramatically with every attempted movement. Her feet seemed completely immobilized.

"Do you need medical attention, Ms. Mitchell?" a crew member asked anxiously.

"The ceremony is about to begin—there's no time for that," Amanda moaned, her voice trembling with feigned pain. "I must return to my seat immediately, but I can't possibly walk..."

"Let us assist you then..."

"No, it's too painful!"

The crew tried to be as gentle as possible, but Amanda's exaggerated reactions left them helpless and confused.

That's when Amanda pointed directly at a nearby seat. "I'll just sit there for now."

It was Elizabeth York's designated spot.

The crew members exchanged horrified glances.

Elizabeth ranked among the top three A-list celebrities present.

Amanda Mitchell barely qualified as B-list. What gave her the right to claim a prime A-list seating position?

"Ms. Mitchell, that's Ms. York's assigned seat," one crew member protested. "You can't possibly—"

"I don't see the issue here," Amanda interrupted sweetly. "She's the one who tripped me. Taking her seat seems only fair compensation."

The surrounding celebrities maintained their professional composure, carefully avoiding commentary.

However, Amanda's paid actress friends immediately understood her strategy.

One of them spoke up sharply. "Wasn't the entire industry praising how certain A-listers never throw their weight around? How they treat newcomers with such fairness? Yet here we are, witnessing petty retaliation over red carpet positioning. How utterly classless!"

"Exactly," another chimed in. "Amanda is clearly injured, and she won't even offer her seat? Disgusting behavior."

"She's just fortunate she landed roles in major films and collected some awards. Anyone with her luck would have achieved more... I wonder how she actually gets those parts!"

"Luck? Don't be naive. Everyone knows about her casting couch arrangements..." The actress let her words hang meaningfully before adding, "She's certainly not like our Amanda, who never leverages her family connections despite her privileged background. Amanda actually works hard instead of stealing opportunities!"

"That reminds me... wasn't there that actress who took Amanda's role through questionable means? Amanda never complained! Now Amanda needs her spot due to injury, and she can't handle it!"

Elizabeth's expression darkened dangerously.

The actresses spoke just loudly enough for nearby attendees to hear every word.

Amanda caught the comments and flashed Elizabeth a venomous smile.

Thank goodness she'd been clever enough to bribe these B and C-list actresses beforehand. Humiliating Elizabeth was going exactly according to plan.

Elizabeth slowly rose from her seat.

Amanda's smile widened triumphantly—Elizabeth wouldn't dare challenge her publicly! That would be giving herself too much importance!

"You want my seat?" Elizabeth's voice was ice cold.

"My leg is injured. I have no other choice," Amanda simpered with false innocence.

"Really?" Elizabeth's sneer could have frozen hell over. The fire in her eyes burned brightly.

She had never been one to tolerate bullying—when truly provoked, she wouldn't hesitate to strike anyone!

She'd even made Nathan Davis cry once before!

"Since you're so determined to play the injured victim," Elizabeth enunciated each word with precision, "I suppose I should help make your performance more convincing. I refuse to be your scapegoat!"

With that declaration, she lifted her foot, aiming the sharp stiletto heel directly at Amanda's already "injured" foot.

There was no one in this world Elizabeth wouldn't confront when provoked!

She kicked forward with all her strength!
###Chapter 142

Amanda could clearly see Elizabeth was about to strike her. She never imagined Elizabeth would do it so publicly.

Just as panic surged through Amanda, someone abruptly pulled her aside. Elizabeth's stiletto heel came down hard on Ethan's leg instead.

Elizabeth knew exactly how much force she had put behind that kick. With heels that sharp, she could vividly imagine Amanda's agony. Instead, she had struck Ethan.

Ethan flinched slightly from the impact but otherwise endured the pain silently.

Elizabeth stared at him, completely stunned.

Amanda was equally shocked that he had come to her rescue again. Her heart began racing wildly, leaving her dazed and mesmerized.

Elizabeth clenched her fists, watching Ethan with an icy glare. He straightened up, scooped Amanda into his arms, and carried her away right before Elizabeth's eyes.

He had fallen for Amanda after all.

Though Elizabeth couldn't see his expression clearly, her eyes welled with tears. She lowered her gaze, fighting desperately to hold them back.

No. She wouldn't cry over Ethan. Her tears weren't that cheap.

She had already cried enough for him.

Slowly, she returned to her seat as if nothing had happened.

Meanwhile, Ethan placed Amanda in her chair and immediately turned to leave.

"Mr. Parker..." Amanda caught his wrist, stopping him.

"Let go unless you want me to turn against you," he growled threateningly.

Amanda could feel his fury radiating from him. She was confused—why had he saved her moments ago only to now seem to despise her?

Gritting her teeth, she released him. He walked back to his seat without a single glance back.

The incident sparked whispers throughout the venue even before the gala officially began.

"I thought those were just rumors, but Ethan Parker really is dating Amanda Mitchell!"

"He doesn't seem that serious about her though... but he's still so protective! That princess carry? So romantic!"

"He's incredibly handsome! When I first worked with him, I actually mistook him for a new actor. That's how good-looking he is!"

"Amanda really hit the jackpot, didn't she? Her family's wealthy and influential, her acting career is taking off, she's不缺钱. And now she has an ideal boyfriend like Ethan... This is too much!"

Elizabeth listened to their conversations, a bitter smile touching her lips as memories of her past with Ethan surfaced.

Her maternal grandfather had brought ten-year-old Ethan into the Levine family.

She was only five at the time and didn't understand much—she just thought Ethan was very handsome.

Before his arrival, she had been the only child in the entire manor. William was usually abroad, and whenever he returned, he would only hang out with Nathan and the others, rarely playing with her. So Ethan became her only companion, and for the longest time, she followed her 'uncle' everywhere.

Yet Ethan had always remained emotionally distant from her.
###Chapter 143

Elizabeth was sitting in the audience at Ethan's university graduation ceremony when the realization struck her like lightning.

She was in love with him.

Every time one of his classmates approached him with affectionate words, a sharp pang of jealousy shot through her heart.

noveldrama

That evening, as the wind howled through the darkness, she stood waiting at the grand entrance of Levine Manor.

Ethan was out having dinner with Nathan and their other friends.

The moment his tall silhouette emerged from the car, Elizabeth threw caution to the wind.

She rushed forward and leaped straight into his arms.

He smelled strongly of alcohol—nothing else. Ethan never wore cologne or deodorant.

Yet even when he was drenched in sweat after playing basketball, she found his natural scent refreshing from head to toe.

"What's wrong? Don't tell me you had a nightmare. You're a grown woman now—" Ethan's soft question was abruptly cut off.

Even Elizabeth couldn't explain where she found such boldness.

She wrapped her arms around his neck, stood on her toes, and pressed her lips firmly against his.

There was tongue involved—the taste of alcohol on his tongue was intense, making her head spin.

Despite the dizziness, she knew exactly what she was doing and why she was doing it.

The moment their lips met, she understood the depth of her feelings for him.

This love had been growing for so long, she couldn't even pinpoint when it began.

He wasn't the most successful man she'd ever known, but loving him made her lose all sense of reason.

The kiss lasted an eternity, yet she was inexperienced—this was her first real kiss.

It was more instinct than skill.

When she finally pulled away, his lips were swollen from her passion.

An awkward silence fell between them.

Elizabeth's heart hammered violently against her chest as she tried to form the words to confess her love.

She couldn't wait any longer, or Ethan might become someone else's.

With his film degree, he would be surrounded by actresses... and the entertainment industry was notoriously messy.

"I—" Elizabeth began, but Ethan leaned in and captured her lips with his.

There was tongue again, but his kiss was much more tender than hers had been.

So tender it made her feel like the luckiest woman in the world.

Her knees turned to jelly beneath his skillful kiss.

Any confession of love died on her lips.

She was far too embarrassed to speak now anyway.

Besides, they had both made their moves—words seemed unnecessary now.

In the end, Ethan had to carry her back to her room, her legs too weak to support her.

That night, she was certain their relationship was now confirmed.

She shared every sweet detail with her best friend, Samantha.

But however blissful that night had been, the following evening brought crushing devastation.

Once again, she waited by the entrance for Ethan to return home.

After graduating, Ethan had immediately started an internship at Windmill Studios, which was affiliated with Levine Holdings.

Interns always worked late, so she had to wait quite a while before his car finally appeared.

She hid in the shadows, planning to surprise him... only to see another woman in the passenger seat of his car.

The woman looked familiar—Elizabeth recognized her as an actress, though not a famous one.

While Elizabeth stood frozen in confusion, she watched Ethan push the woman against the car seat and kiss her fiercely.

The dim yellow streetlights made it difficult to see exactly how entangled they were, but Elizabeth could hear the sound of her own heart shattering.

She couldn't tell how much time had passed when Ethan finally released the actress, who then drove away in his car.

He strode through the front door and found Elizabeth there, sobbing uncontrollably.
###Chapter 144

Ethan showed no concern when he saw Elizabeth. His tone was flat. "It's late. Shouldn't you be asleep? Don't you have class tomorrow?"

"What am I to you, Ethan?" she asked him then.

She was only seventeen. She didn't understand why she was losing her composure like this.

She was strangely calm. Not her usual pampered self. She was even willing to believe Ethan's encounter with that actress was just an accident.

Later, she realized she didn't throw a fit because she didn't want him to hate her.

But that night, Ethan's answer was casual. Utterly without hesitation. "My niece."

"But, last night..." Elizabeth whispered.

"Last night?" Ethan chuckled. His eyes held no warmth. "I was drunk."

"No, you weren't!" Elizabeth cried out. She knew he couldn't handle his liquor. He always avoided getting drunk!

"Fine, I wasn't." His gaze was cool as he watched her tears fall. "I didn't want to take advantage. But I'm twenty-two. A grown man. It's hard to refuse when a woman throws herself at me."

He finished indifferently. "Just forget it happened."

Forget it?!

He couldn't refuse any woman?!

Elizabeth's heart shattered. A miserable sound escaped her. "That was my first kiss..."

"Sorry," Ethan said. As if it meant nothing.

Even then, she dared to ask. "Don't you love me?"

She breathed the words with desperate hope. Her eyes were full of longing.

She had mustered every ounce of her courage to ask.

Ethan's reply was ice. Immediate and final.

"No. I don't. Not even when we were children. I live on charity. All I can do is pretend to smile."

Not even as a child? He lived on charity?!

So her entire childhood had been a lie.

He never loved her. All his kindness was an act.

Elizabeth laughed through her tears. She was a complete joke.

Just like she was now.

How could she ever expect anything from Ethan?

So she listened coolly as everyone discussed Ethan and Amanda. She sat through the fashion gala with detached nonchalance. When the hosts called her onstage, she joked easily. She flashed her signature dazzling smile.

The gala ended at midnight. The usual interview session followed.

Guests began leaving in an orderly stream.

But the journalists erupted the moment Elizabeth appeared. Chaos ensued. They mobbed her instantly.

Every other star was ignored. Including Amanda, who felt a wave of disgust at the press's favoritism.

Amanda knew the press wouldn't care about anyone else if Elizabeth was present. Coming here was just asking for humiliation.

Still, Amanda found the gala worthwhile. Because of what Ethan had done for her. In full public view.

As she slowly passed the crowd of journalists, she heard their excited, probing questions bombarding Elizabeth.

"Elizabeth York! Is the man in those photos your boyfriend? The physical intimacy is undeniable!"

"Who is he? Is he in the industry? Are you two dating?"

"When did the relationship start? How long has this been going on? Weren't you just interviewed claiming you were single and unavailable?"

"The forums are speculating he's your financial backer, Elizabeth. Is he the reason your career has been so smooth?!"

Elizabeth was genuinely perplexed by the journalists' questions. A vague understanding dawned.

Someone must have taken photos of her!
###Chapter 145

Elizabeth had no clue what those photographs contained.

She hadn't been intimate with any man recently. There was nothing scandalous for the paparazzi to exploit.

Making matters worse, her phone wasn't with her. Her manager and assistant were also absent.

"I have no idea what you're referring to," Elizabeth stated coolly. "If I were in a relationship, I wouldn't conceal it."

She attempted to push through the throng of journalists. They immediately tightened their circle around her.

Elizabeth's expression darkened visibly. They were deliberately blocking her exit.

As a prominent figure in the entertainment industry, she had to maintain her composure in front of cameras.

"These photos are absolutely authentic, Ms. York. What are you trying to hide?"

"Why not just confess? Tell us who he is and define your relationship!"

"Don't keep your fans waiting, Ms. York!"

The relentless interrogation was wearing her down. She genuinely didn't understand their accusations.

Just as her composure was about to break, her manager Linda, assistant Penelope, and a team of bodyguards forcefully cleared a path through the crowd. They swiftly escorted Elizabeth into her waiting luxury vehicle, leaving the frustrated journalists behind.

"What's going on?" Elizabeth demanded immediately. Linda handed over her phone.

Elizabeth's brow furrowed as she scanned the screen. Suddenly, she burst into laughter at the scandalous article and accompanying photos.

She was utterly speechless. Linda and Penelope stared at her in complete bewilderment.

How could she laugh when they'd just exposed her having a sugar daddy?!

She had numerous television series, film projects, variety show appearances, and endorsement deals pending! Countless scheduling requests remained unresolved!

Wasn't she concerned about her career being destroyed?!

However, Elizabeth found it hilarious that someone had attempted to create a scandal between her and her own cousin.

Clearly, whoever was behind this feared William enough to blur his face. While most people wouldn't recognize him, Elizabeth remembered that location perfectly—the backyard of the banquet hall where Henry Levine had celebrated his birthday.

She recalled it distinctly because William had snapped at her, ordering her to return home.

Somehow, someone had captured that moment and twisted it into something sordid.

"Who is he, Elizabeth? Is he really your boyfriend?" Linda pressed anxiously.

"Absolutely not," Elizabeth denied instantly.

"Then who is he?" Linda persisted.

Elizabeth hesitated briefly before responding, "I can't tell you."

Revealing his identity would expose her connection to the Levine family.

That would mean immediate retirement from show business and a desk job at Levine Holdings.

She had signed that agreement with her parents and maternal grandfather before entering the entertainment industry. Her word was binding.

"Elizabeth!" Linda snapped, genuinely upset.

She had nurtured Elizabeth's career from its inception to its current success.

Yet Elizabeth was keeping secrets from her...

Elizabeth spoke with solemn determination. "Handle this through PR, Linda. Trust me, it's far better if we manage this ourselves rather than exposing that man's identity."

Linda felt deeply uncomfortable but recognized Elizabeth's resolve. She decided not to push further. "I'll coordinate with PR immediately."

"Thank you," Elizabeth replied.

The luxury vehicle dropped Linda and Penelope at the management office. The scandal meant working overtime.

"Oh, one more thing." Linda paused before exiting, remembering something important. "It was Ethan Parker who alerted me to bring bodyguards and extract you from the gala. That's how we reached you so quickly and saved you from those journalists—make sure to thank him later."

Elizabeth pressed her lips together briefly before responding, "I will."

With that, Linda and Penelope exited the vehicle.

Penelope watched the departing car and couldn't resist commenting, "Have you noticed, Linda? Elizabeth has refused to let us take her home ever since she returned to Crestwood. The luxury vehicle and chauffeur are both privately hired."

"I've noticed," Linda replied thoughtfully.

"Then where does she actually live?" Penelope exclaimed with burning curiosity. "Is there a reason we can't find out? Is she hiding her background because she comes from an important family?"

"Of course not! Why would she work herself to death in show business if she were born into wealth or power?!" Linda immediately dismissed the idea. "But I don't understand it either. Why aren't we permitted to know about her family circumstances? Or does she truly have a powerful backer, like the rumors suggest?!"

Both women stood completely perplexed by the mystery.
###Chapter 146

Elizabeth York sat in her luxury SUV, scrolling through her phone with little focus.

Online articles were multiplying rapidly, nearly eclipsing the scandal surrounding Samuel Mitchell and Sophia Mitchell.

She finally arrived at Levine Manor. Stepping out of the vehicle, she spotted Ethan Parker waiting by the grand entrance.

Elizabeth walked straight past him, pretending he was invisible.

"Elizabeth," he called out softly.

She continued ignoring him.

"Elizabeth," he tried again, reaching for her arm.

She swiftly pulled her wrist away, turning to face him with an icy glare. "Don't touch me. I don't want your filth on me."

Ethan's fingers curled into fists as he lowered his hand. "Have you decided how to handle the media situation?"

"My agency is managing it. Don't worry about your precious television series."

"That's William in the photo, isn't it? From my father's seventieth birthday celebration."

His tone was certain, but Elizabeth offered no confirmation. He continued, "I could speak with him on your behalf. Perhaps he could arrange a media blackout."

Though William disapproved of Elizabeth's acting career and rarely offered assistance, he shared a close bond with Ethan. Being William's uncle, Ethan usually held some influence over him.

But Elizabeth wanted none of it.

"Just stay away from me, Ethan Parker," she snapped.

He swallowed hard, remaining silent.

"I've been doing everything to avoid you," she continued, her voice trembling with suppressed emotion. "I wouldn't even be staying here if William hadn't insisted. I certainly wouldn't have taken a role in your production. Can't you just leave me alone when I'm clearly not fighting back?!"

Tears welled in her eyes despite her composed expression.

For eight long years, she had struggled to forget and restrain her feelings.

The effort had been excruciating, tearing her apart inside.

And here he stood, offering pity and creating more confusion.

"I'm sorry," he murmured.

Another apology.

It was all he ever gave her.

"I thought we—"

"You thought I don't love you anymore?" Elizabeth finished his sentence bluntly.

Ethan pressed his lips together. "I'm not worthy of your love."

"Absolutely not. I feel like such a fool." A bitter smile touched her lips. "But it doesn't matter anymore. After all these years, I've grown accustomed to the pain. Just stop bothering me, and I can pretend I never loved you at all."

As Ethan stood speechless, she brushed past him. "Goodnight, Uncle Ethan."

She walked away calmly, showing no trace of heartbreak or misery—as if she hadn't been the one rejected all those years ago.

She never saw how Ethan was fighting to control himself, his knuckles white from how tightly he clenched his fists.

Meanwhile, hidden in the shadows, a paparazzo slipped away quietly, satisfied with the photos he had captured.
###Chapter 147

Elizabeth York's scandal remained the top trending topic the following day.

Her management team couldn't suppress the media coverage completely—not with Samuel Mitchell secretly pressuring the press behind the scenes.

Samuel felt quite pleased as he watched the news unfold. After some consideration, he decided to call Elise Scott.

Elise had been following the news as well, and she immediately recognized Samuel's handiwork in the situation.

He was clever enough to use one scandal to bury another, all to protect Mitchell Corp from further damage caused by his own controversies.

"Elise... Do you really think William Levine would publicly support Elizabeth York?" Samuel asked contemptuously when she answered.

Elise had no desire to engage with Samuel, especially since telling him that Elizabeth was actually part of the Levine family would likely give him an aneurysm.

"Do you think William would announce his relationship with Elizabeth or quietly make the scandal disappear?" Samuel continued mocking her. "See? You're just one of his many women."

Elise remained silent, which only seemed to fuel his contempt. "I'm genuinely curious... How exactly did you manage to seduce William? Did you throw yourself at him? You used to push my hands away whenever I tried to touch you before, only to turn out this easy!"

"Oh, I threw myself at him and then some," Elise replied, not bothering with explanations. "As for why I wouldn't let you touch me? Let's just say you weren't worth my time."

"Elise Scott!" Samuel snapped angrily.

"What exactly do you have over William Levine, anyway?" Elise continued her scathing response. "Are you more handsome? Wealthier? More influential? Clearly, you're none of those things, so you have no right to criticize my relationship with him! And let me give you some friendly advice—Sophia has always been obsessed with what I have. She can't stand seeing me with anything better than her. Weren't you ever concerned that Sophia might develop feelings for William?"

"She's nothing like you!" Samuel shouted, furious and unable to tolerate Elise's disdain.

"Well, it's your choice whether to take my advice or not," Elise said before ending the call.

Samuel glared at his phone screen displaying 'call ended.'

He had intended to mock Elise, but she had turned it around and highlighted all his inadequacies!

Then he remembered Sophia's increasingly unpredictable behavior toward him recently...

Frustrated, veins bulged prominently on his forehead.

After hanging up on Samuel, Elise couldn't resist texting William.

[Did you see the news about Elizabeth?]

[I did.]

His reply came quickly, but showed clear indifference.

[Aren't you going to help clear Elizabeth's name? Many people are assuming she has a sugar daddy.]

[She hasn't asked for my help.]

Elise didn't know how to respond to that, but William added: [I never supported her acting career anyway. It might be for the best if this scandal makes her retire.]

[Are you even related?!]

Elise couldn't stop herself from snapping at William.

Even though she had only recently met Elizabeth, she could sense the younger woman's passion for acting—more importantly, Elizabeth possessed natural talent and brought every character she played to life.

Nevertheless, William replied: [We're just cousins.]

Elise rolled her eyes, finally understanding why Elizabeth was so intimidated by William—the man was completely unyielding!

[Elizabeth could always return to Levine Holdings to assist me if she leaves acting. There aren't many Levines available to manage our expanding business.]

It sounded like an explanation from William, but Elise wondered if he was actually boasting.

Who in their right mind would describe their family business as 'expanding' so blatantly?!

Still, something puzzled Elise.

[What about Ethan Parker?]

[You certainly pay him extra attention, don't you?]

Elise could practically feel the jealousy radiating through the phone. She smiled as she typed her response.

[He's helped me on multiple occasions.]

She genuinely considered Ethan someone reliable and trustworthy.
###Chapter 148

Elise considered Ethan far more dependable in corporate roles than Elizabeth.

William's response was immediate: [My grandfather forbids it.]

Just as confusion began to cloud Elise's thoughts, he elaborated.

[Since Ethan is adopted, my grandfather wants to prevent any potential power struggles within Levine Holdings that could disrupt family harmony.]

Elise understood the reasoning, yet she couldn't ignore the glaring injustice toward Ethan.

Did anyone ever consider his feelings?

She quickly realized William was redirecting the conversation—a skill he'd perfected.

[So, you're not going to help Elizabeth?]

[She'll manage on her own. If she can't, she'll come to me.]

Fair enough.

Elise reasoned that since Elizabeth was a Levine, the family wouldn't stand by while she faced harassment.

As she closed the chat window and prepared to focus on work, another message appeared.

[That's it?]

Elise frowned—what else was there to discuss?

It was still office hours, and her workload was substantial.

Most importantly, Crestwood Channel (CC) was launching a fashion reality show where celebrities would wear creations from various designers. Elise saw this as a prime opportunity to expand Stellar Fashion's clientele through publicity.

Yet William persisted: [How long has it been since we last saw each other?]

How long?

Wait—it had been over a week!

[We haven't met since we officially started dating.]

William's words carried a sigh, while Elise pressed her lips together—he wasn't wrong.

He teased: [Is this what you meant by dating, Ms. Scott? Virtual romance, specifically?]

Feeling slightly defensive since William had agreed to her terms, she retorted: [I warned you I'd be busy.]

His reply was characteristically unreasonable: [There's always time for love.]

[Love doesn't pay the bills.]

[It nourishes the soul.]

[Have I been starving you?]

[Yes. I have substantial emotional needs.]

The flirtatious tone was unmistakable.

Elise quickly typed: [I haven't seen James in ages. Let's have dinner together—I'll finish work on time.]

[…]

[It's settled then. See you after work.]

[…Fine.]

Reluctant agreement came from the man, while Elise smiled victoriously.

She returned her attention to preparing for the fashion reality show, registering without hesitation.

There would be preliminary selections. Only chosen designers would create dresses for assigned celebrities before presenting them on the runway during recording.

CC's programs consistently drew large audiences, and few fashion brands would decline such free exposure.

Unsurprisingly, renowned designers from major national labels were participating, making competition intense.

Even Sophia noticed the competition after a friend alerted her.

She had remained unemployed at home since Elise dismissed her.

She refused to work at Scott Enterprise either, since she'd merely be serving her younger brother Daniel with no chance of inheriting the company. She'd have to endure his dramatic temper, just like her aunt.

She would never willingly accept such humiliation, making this publicity opportunity particularly appealing.

Wasn't this her chance to restore her reputation and shine?
###Chapter 149

At six o'clock sharp, William arrived with James as promised to pick up Elise from work.

"Mommy!"

That sweet, innocent voice never failed to melt Elise's heart.

She scooped the boy into her arms, settling beside him as they engaged in cheerful conversation.

William frowned, feeling thoroughly ignored.

"Aren't you happy to see Mommy?" James asked seriously, his little brow furrowing in concern.

"Of course I am," William replied coolly.

"But you're making this face," James said, mimicking William's stern expression perfectly.

He had always been a miniature version of his father, and the resemblance was uncanny when he frowned.

Elise burst into laughter, and even the chauffeur couldn't suppress an amused smile.

William's scowl deepened, which only encouraged James further. "Mommy, don't mind Daddy. I think he's having one of his mood swings."

Elise laughed even harder at that—perhaps William had educated their son a bit too well!

Fortunately, the restaurant was nearby, and they arrived quickly.

Any longer, and William might have actually had a stroke!

They settled at a window table in the elegant restaurant, with Elise still joking with James while William placed their order.

Why did he feel like the third wheel here?

"William?" A woman's voice called out, drawing the attention of all three.

"Oh, it really is you!" the woman exclaimed delightedly before introducing herself. "Do you remember me? I'm Isabella Lynd, Victoria Stuart's cousin. We met when you were studying abroad with her."

"Vaguely," William replied, finally placing her.

Isabella seemed undeterred by his aloofness, growing even more enthusiastic. "What a coincidence! I just came from Rockville to meet my boyfriend, so I didn't expect to see you here. By the way, did you hear? My aunt mentioned that Victoria will be returning to the country soon."

"I wasn't aware," William said flatly.

"Victoria didn't tell you?" Isabella appeared genuinely surprised. "You two aren't fighting, are you? When I saw the news about you returning to Crestwood alone, I thought—"

"Ms. Lynd," William interrupted sharply, cutting off her rambling. "We're about to start our meal."

His dismissal was clear, and Isabella understood immediately.

"Alright, I won't disturb you then," she said, visibly chagrined.

As she turned to leave, she cast a subtle glance toward Elise, who was seated beside William.

Elise noticed the look, but when she met Isabella's eyes, the other woman quickly averted her gaze and departed.

Returning to her own table, Isabella immediately texted Victoria.

[Victoria, I just ran into William at a restaurant. He brought his son and a woman who I believe is Elise Scott.]

Having seen Elise's face in various news articles recently, Isabella recognized her immediately.

But why was Elise here with William?

And why would William, who always maintained such privacy, be involved with someone with Elise's complicated past?

Overseas, Victoria's eyes narrowed as she read the message.

She never should have let William return to Zidonia alone. She had underestimated Elise!

Back at the restaurant, William, James, and Elise began their meal.

Elise continued focusing most of her attention on James, helping him with his food.

William finally couldn't resist asking, "Am I invisible to you, Ms. Scott?"

Elise was speechless.

He was a grown man, yet he was jealous of his own son?

Could he be more childish?

Still, seeing that James was managing fine on his own, she finally turned her attention to William. "Victoria Stuart... as in the Stuarts who practically own Rockville?"

William blinked at Elise, momentarily taken aback.
###Chapter 150

William thought to himself that Elise should be focusing on him instead of inquiring about Victoria!

Still, he gave a nod. "Yes."

"What's the connection between you two?" Elise asked directly—after that misunderstanding with William and Elizabeth, she had resolved to clarify everything upfront.

"Our grandfathers fought together in the war and remained close friends. They kept in touch regularly despite living in different cities. My grandfather particularly enjoyed visiting them, so he often brought me along to Stuart Manor in Rockville. As for Victoria Stuart, she's..."

William paused, searching for the right description, and Elise raised an eyebrow. "Your childhood sweetheart?"

"No," William denied immediately. "More like the girl next door."

The girl next door?!

That sounded far more intimate than necessary!

William continued, "Anyway, when my grandfather sent me abroad for studies, Charles Stuart—her grandfather—arranged for her to study overseas too. After we graduated from university, I focused on expanding Levine Holdings in international markets, while she remained abroad to manage Stuart Group. We've always looked out for each other because of our families' long-standing relationship."

Looked out for each other? In what way?!

William's lips curved into a faint smile when he saw Elise's skeptical expression. "Don't worry. There's nothing romantic between us."

"Your history is your own business, and I'm not particularly interested," Elise replied casually, though a slight smile played on her lips.

Her response put William in better spirits, and he remarked, "You're making progress, Ms. Scott."

"What?" Elise looked confused.

"Now you're asking questions directly," he said. "Unlike before, when you'd silently condemn me without a word."

Elise felt her cheeks warm, but she knew she'd never live down suspecting William of having something with Elizabeth.

After dinner, they headed to Elise's apartment in William's Maybach.

With just Elise and James chatting animatedly in the backseat, William once again felt like the odd one out.

Despite this, Elise suddenly felt her fingers tremble as her heartbeat quickened.

In the dim interior of the car, William had quietly taken her hand, his grip firm and sure.

She pressed her lips together slightly, while William turned to gaze out the window after capturing her hand.

She could sense his determination from the set of his shoulders—he had no intention of letting go.

But she didn't pull away either, surprised to find she didn't feel the discomfort she'd expected.

"Mommy?" James called out to her just then.

Elise snapped back to attention and continued her conversation with the boy.

As the luxury car moved through the city, William maintained his hold even though Elise's palm grew damp with nervous sweat. Here she was, a grown woman well past her first experiences with romance, reacting like a teenager!

It wasn't until they reached Elise's apartment building that she flexed her fingers and William reluctantly released her.

Opening the car door, she told James softly, "Goodbye, sweetheart."

"Bye, Mommy," he replied with clear reluctance.

Elise gently patted his cheek before stepping out, and the Maybach didn't pull away until she had entered the elevator.

The car fell into sudden silence, with James sitting quietly in his seat, far less animated than when he'd been with Elise.

"James," William said suddenly.

"Yes, Daddy?" the boy replied formally.

"Would you like to attend a summer camp?" William asked.

When James tilted his head in confusion, William added, "The kind that lasts for a full month."

James immediately pursed his little lips. "You might as well just leave me at an orphanage, Daddy."

William had no response to that.
###Chapter 151

The elevator ascended smoothly toward Elise's apartment floor.

When the doors slid open, she found Elizabeth leaning heavily against her front door.

Elizabeth's face was flushed a deep crimson.

She was clearly intoxicated.

Elise hurried toward her. "Elizabeth?"

Elizabeth's eyes fluttered open, glassy and unfocused.

Tears welled up instantly when she recognized Elise. "You're finally home."

"What are you doing here?" Elise asked with concern. "You're completely drunk."

"Finished filming early today," Elizabeth slurred. "Went out drinking with some friends."

Her breath reeked strongly of alcohol.

How much had she actually consumed?

"With Samantha?" Elise inquired.

"No, she's swamped with work and traveling for business anyway," Elizabeth mumbled. "Went with other friends... Burp! Drinking buddies."

She belched loudly.

Elise knew Samantha wouldn't be so irresponsible.

She quickly unlocked the door and guided Elizabeth inside. "Why didn't you call me if you were waiting out here?"

"Didn't want to interrupt your date with William," Elizabeth slurred, then suddenly gagged.

Elise swiftly steered her toward the bathroom.

Elizabeth collapsed before the toilet, clutching it as she began vomiting violently.

The retching continued for what felt like ages.

Elizabeth clutched her stomach miserably while Elise rubbed soothing circles on her back. "Feeling any better? Should I get you some water?"

"I need a bath," Elizabeth announced abruptly.

"Can you manage on your own?"

"Yeah."

Elise hurried to fill the bathtub with warm water.

She gathered fresh towels and clean clothes for Elizabeth.

After Elizabeth finished bathing, Elise handed her a warm honey drink she'd prepared.

Elizabeth settled into Elise's bed, sipping the sweet beverage with a tired smile. "Knew you'd take good care of me once I got here."

Elise smiled wryly—Elizabeth's face remained pale from vomiting, yet she still managed to beam. "Don't you have plenty of staff at home to look after you?"

"Levine Manor has people I'd rather not see right now."

"Fighting with your parents? Or Henry Levine?" Elise asked gently.

"No." Elizabeth denied it flatly, offering no further explanation.

She simply pulled the blankets up to her chin. "I'm going to sleep now."

Elise never pressed about others' private matters.

As she stood to leave, Elizabeth's phone began ringing insistently.

Elise retrieved it from Elizabeth's bag and handed it over. "It's Linda."

Though clearly annoyed, Elizabeth answered. "Linda…"

"Are you with Ethan Parker right now?" Linda demanded immediately.

Elizabeth's heart jolted violently.

It was ridiculous how her pulse raced anytime that man's name came up.

She maintained a calm tone. "Who's feeding you nonsense this time?"

"Someone photographed you with him!" Linda exclaimed grimly.

Elizabeth bolted upright in bed, startling Elise beside her.

Elise watched as Elizabeth frantically tapped her phone screen, scrolling through breaking news alerts with trembling fingers.
###Chapter 152

The headline screamed in bold letters: [Exclusive: Ethan Parker Spotted with Elizabeth York!]

Elise watched Elizabeth's hands tremble as she pulled out her phone to read the article.

The piece revisited Elizabeth's previous scandal, now confirming her relationship with Ethan. It specifically mentioned how Amanda Mitchell was originally cast as the female lead for Pomp and Circumstance, but the role ultimately went to Elizabeth.

With all the speculation, their relationship appeared confirmed beyond doubt.

Elise clicked on the attached photos showing Elizabeth and Ethan maintaining distance from each other. Though taken from afar, their body language clearly indicated an argument.

The article suggested they were upset about being photographed, connecting it to the previous scandal.

Naturally, the media assumed Ethan was the mosaic-censored man from earlier photos. Comparing silhouettes would lead anyone to that conclusion.

Without seeing the uncensored version, misunderstanding was inevitable.

"Damn it!" Elizabeth cursed, furious after reading the piece.

She hadn't hung up yet, and Linda was ranting on the other end. "Elizabeth, no more secrets now. Talk to Mr. Parker—decide whether to go public or issue a statement... though explaining won't work after being photographed at his mansion. Fans are more accepting of celebrities dating these days, and you two make a striking couple. I'll discuss with management about making it official, especially since we have that romantic commercial coming up..."

"There's nothing between me and Ethan Parker!" Elizabeth growled through clenched teeth.

"Elizabeth..."

"I'll call you back!" Elizabeth hung up immediately, took a deep breath, and dialed Ethan.

The moment he answered, she got straight to the point. "Please issue a statement denying our relationship immediately. I'll also declare we only have a professional connection—I don't want my career ruined because of you."

"Fine," Ethan agreed without hesitation, remaining perfectly calm. "But we need consistent stories. Why were you at my doorstep, after all?"

Elizabeth fell silent, unable to provide an explanation.

She couldn't claim they were discussing the script—that would only make things worse, showing up at his house late at night!

"Let me think about it. I'll call you later," Ethan said before hanging up, apparently without an immediate plan.

Elizabeth's hand shook as she held her phone.

Nothing had gone smoothly since she returned to Crestwood.

Beside her, Elise sensed Elizabeth's distress.

She wondered if she was imagining it, but Elizabeth definitely acted strangely around Ethan.

Weren't they relatives? Why was she so hostile toward him?

"Elizabeth? Is the person you didn't want to see... Ethan Parker?" Elise asked, though she already felt certain.

Elizabeth looked up at Elise and nodded—she didn't want to keep secrets from her.

"But why? He seemed quite decent..."

"He's hypocritical, two-faced, a wolf in sheep's clothing," Elizabeth fired off immediately.

"Really?" Elise hadn't expected Elizabeth to view Ethan so negatively.

"Appearances deceive," Elizabeth said seriously. "That applies to William too. He might have a son, but he's practically celibate. You're the only woman he's allowed close to him in all these years."

Elise was startled—how had the conversation turned to her?

She bit her lip briefly before asking, "Have you ever met James's mother?"

"No," Elizabeth said curtly. "No one in the family has met her, and William never discusses her. Honestly, if James didn't resemble William so much, we'd think he picked him up off the street, given how abstinent the man is."
###Chapter 153

Elise looked up at Elizabeth.
Elizabeth studied her expression carefully.
"You're wondering how William ended up bringing James home, aren't you?"

Elise nodded slowly.
Elizabeth gave her a knowing smirk.
"Of course he never tells you anything important."

In truth, Elise hadn't actually asked William about it.
She preferred to let him share such personal matters voluntarily.

Elizabeth closed her eyes briefly, searching her memories.
"William was twenty-one when James came to live with us.
Our grandfather Henry was the one who brought him home.
William didn't even know he had a son until then."

She paused, choosing her words carefully.
"As for how grandfather found out... let's just say he has his ways.
He always knows everything that happens in our family."

Elizabeth's voice softened with the memory.
"William was still studying abroad when grandfather returned with James.
I overheard him on the phone saying the mother abandoned James at the hospital right after giving birth.
That's why grandfather brought him to us."

A sharp pain pierced Elise's chest.
Though William had mentioned James was abandoned, hearing the details still shocked her.
The thought of a newborn being left behind made her feel suffocated.
It reminded her of her own lost child.

Tears welled in Elise's eyes without warning.
Elizabeth stared at her in alarm.
"What's wrong? Why are you crying?"

"I'm not sure," Elise whispered as tears streamed down her cheeks.
"My chest suddenly hurts so much."

"I felt the same sadness when I first saw James," Elizabeth admitted.
"What kind of woman could abandon her own child?
If I were William, I'd never forgive her!"
She crossed her arms indignantly.
"Actually, I don't think William has forgiven her either."

Elise nodded silently, wiping her tears.
Elizabeth continued with growing frustration.
Seriously, what woman in her right mind would abandon William and his son?
Is he not handsome enough?
Not wealthy enough?
Not powerful enough?
She must be completely blind!"

Elise wondered about that woman too.
Who could sleep with William and then disappear without a trace?

Elizabeth sighed heavily.
"One time Nathan Davis and I got drunk and discussed that woman.
We both agreed she must have been a stranger William met by chance.
She probably didn't know about his family's status and wealth.
Maybe she even thought he was some kind of gigolo because of his looks."

Elise choked slightly, surprised by Elizabeth's blunt words.

"Be honest," Elizabeth insisted.
"What other reason could any woman have for dumping my brother?"
Her pride in William was evident, quite different from her attitude toward Ethan.

Thinking of Ethan reminded Elise of their current situation.
"Elizabeth, shouldn't you be focusing on your scandal with Ethan?"
She was impressed Elizabeth could remain so calm and gossip about William.

Elizabeth remained completely unbothered.
"You get used to these things in show business.
It'll get resolved eventually.
There were rumors before that I slept my way to the top.
This time, at least it's only Ethan Parker..."

Her voice trailed off, a flicker of annoyance crossing her features.

"You could just reveal your identity," Elise suggested.
"That would solve everything naturally."

"Hasn't William told you?" Elizabeth asked.
"Everyone in the Levine family opposes my acting career.
They all want me to help William with the family business.
My parents are the worst about it."
She made a face.
"I have zero interest in business management.
Acting is my only passion.
That's why my parents and grandfather made an agreement—
I can't use the family's influence in my career,
or I have to come home and work in the family business."

Elizabeth sighed wearily.
"If I reveal my identity now,
that counts as using family connections.
Grandfather would drag me back home immediately!"

"That does complicate things," Elise said thoughtfully.
"You and Ethan were photographed right outside Levine Manor.
That's going to be difficult to explain..."

"He said he'll handle it, so I'll let him," Elizabeth said, clearly done thinking about it.
She'd never been good with complicated planning anyway.
Her grades in school had been so poor that Ethan had to tutor her for years...

"I'm going to sleep now," Elizabeth announced suddenly.
"My head hurts from all this thinking."

"Alright, get some rest," Elise told her.
"I'm going to take a shower.
I'll check on you when I'm done."
###Chapter 154

Elise nodded, her expression sincere. "Elizabeth, you're truly wonderful. William is fortunate to have found you."

A faint blush colored Elise's cheeks. In truth, she felt equally blessed to have William in her life.

"Thank goodness James's mother was blind," Elizabeth continued, her voice filled with genuine admiration. "Otherwise, I'd never have such an amazing cousin-in-law."

"Just get some rest," Elise urged with a soft smile before quickly making her way to the bathroom.

As Elise disappeared from view, Elizabeth's smile gradually faded from her face.

She found herself wondering if every woman had to encounter scoundrels before finding their prince. Even someone as remarkable as Elise had crossed paths with someone like Samuel Mitchell.

As for herself, Ethan was more than enough disappointment for one lifetime.

The piercing ringtone of her phone jolted Elizabeth awake early the next morning. Recognizing the caller, she answered with a groggy voice. "Linda, didn't I tell you Ethan Parker would handle everything? We just need to wait, so please stop bothering me already..."

"He just made an official announcement," Linda stated bluntly.

Elizabeth immediately sat upright in bed, all traces of sleep vanishing instantly.

The movement woke Elise, who rose to find Elizabeth visibly agitated. Elizabeth had already opened her news app and found Ethan's statement denying any personal relationship with her, insisting they only had professional ties. He further announced he was now dating Amanda Mitchell and warned media against making baseless speculations.

Elise felt stunned reading the news... Ethan was actually dating Amanda?

It wasn't that she held prejudice against the Mitchell family, but Amanda was definitely not good news.

"Elizabeth!" Linda's sharp voice came through the phone.

"Yeah." Elizabeth quickly composed herself, her demeanor turning surprisingly calm.

"Is there really nothing between you and Ethan Parker?" Linda asked, her tone clearly skeptical of Ethan's announcement.

After all, would Elizabeth have been photographed at Levine Manor if there was nothing between them? That wasn't a place just anyone could access freely!

"Ethan made himself pretty clear, didn't he?" Elizabeth responded with a faint, bitter laugh.

Linda sounded furious. "Honestly, what's wrong with his eyesight? Choosing Amanda over you?"

In reality, Linda had been convinced Elizabeth was dating Ethan. She'd even gotten the company to agree to fully support Elizabeth if they went public with their relationship.

She was absolutely stunned to discover Ethan had such poor judgment!

If that was the case, why would Elizabeth harbor such strong feelings against him?

People don't develop such intense dislike without reason, unless...

Elise glanced at Elizabeth and noticed she had already put away her phone and settled back into bed.

Elise resisted voicing her thoughts—if Elizabeth truly didn't want others to know, she could certainly pretend ignorance.

"I need to get to work. Will you be alright here by yourself?"

"Yeah," Elizabeth murmured sleepily. "The issue is resolved, so I can finally relax in peace."

"Okay. There aren't many dining options around here, so you'll need to order delivery."

"Understood."

With that, Elise got out of bed and prepared to leave for work.

During her commute, she thought for a moment before calling William. "Elizabeth's staying at my place."

"I know. Keep her company for the next few days."

"You were aware, weren't you?" she asked, though his tone made it evident he had been.

"What wouldn't I know when we all live under the same roof? Only Elizabeth thought she was hiding it well, but I never realized she'd hold on for so many years." William admitted, though he acknowledged he hadn't anticipated this outcome. "I shouldn't have pressured her to return to Levine Manor."

Still, Elise couldn't contain her curiosity. "Does Ethan not have feelings for Elizabeth?"

She could clearly see that Elizabeth loved Ethan.

But was Ethan's lack of interest the reason they couldn't be together?
###Chapter 155

"Everything's settled," Elizabeth murmured softly. "I can finally rest here without any worries."

"Alright. The food options around here are limited, so you'll need to order delivery for meals."

"I will."

Elise rose from the bed and prepared to leave for work.

During her commute, she contemplated the situation before dialing William. "Elizabeth is staying at my apartment."

"I know. Please look after her for the next few days."

"You were aware of this all along, weren't you?" she asked, though his tone had already confirmed it.

"How could I not know when we share the same living space? Elizabeth believed she was being discreet, but I never imagined she'd still be holding on after all these years." William admitted, though he hadn't anticipated the situation would escalate this far. "I shouldn't have pressured her to return to Levine Manor."

Elise couldn't suppress her curiosity. "Does Ethan not return Elizabeth's feelings?"

She could clearly see how deeply Elizabeth cared for Ethan.

But was his lack of reciprocation the reason they couldn't be together?

If that were true, why would Ethan sacrifice himself for Elizabeth's sake?

"Their relationship is complicated," William explained carefully. "Out of respect for Ethan as my friend and elder, I can't disclose the details. The fundamental truth remains that they cannot be together."

Elise felt a flicker of irritation at William's deliberate secrecy.

However, William suddenly seemed to reconsider. "Though it's not that I'm completely unwilling to share..."

Elise remained skeptical of William's sudden willingness to cooperate—she knew his cunning nature all too well!

"But since this involves my family's private matters, I'm afraid I can't reveal them to someone outside the family. That being said," he continued smoothly, "if you were to marry me, you would naturally have the right to know everything."

William stated this as if it were the most logical conclusion, leaving Elise momentarily speechless.

She had already formed opinions about Henry Levine's serpent-like cunning, but it appeared the entire Levine family shared similar traits.

"Regardless," William continued, "Elizabeth can stay with you for now. I'll arrange alternative accommodation for her once I've found a suitable place."
###Chapter 156

"Yes," Elise answered, though she secretly wouldn't have minded if Elizabeth stayed longer.

William smoothly changed the subject. "Ms. Scott, have you considered taking a vacation?"

Elise was momentarily speechless. His energy levels were truly remarkable lately.

"I apologize, but my schedule is completely packed for the foreseeable future," she declined immediately, knowing she needed to focus on preparing for the reality competition.

William's expression darkened noticeably. "How long will you be occupied?"

"Approximately three months."

That was if her designs made it to the final rounds, of course.

"Mark your calendar," he declared, his tone commanding yet underlying with tenderness. "After those three months, you can't refuse me anymore!"

Her heart fluttered unexpectedly, but she ultimately nodded in agreement.

A week later, the media frenzy surrounding Ethan had begun to subside—undoubtedly due to the Levines' intervention.

Ethan had played his cards wisely. Even if the Levines had no particular reason to support Elizabeth's entertainment career, they had every reason to protect Ethan. Naturally, they'd enforce a media blackout on any stories that portrayed him negatively.

In reality, the damage wasn't severe. Since Ethan had been the one orchestrating things behind the scenes, Elizabeth's reputation suffered minimal impact—if anything, she was now widely perceived as the victim in the whole affair, garnering waves of public sympathy.

One could almost call it a fortunate misfortune.

Samuel Mitchell's strategy proved clever indeed. By allowing Ethan's story to dominate headlines, he'd effectively overshadowed his own scandal with Sophia Mitchell, however questionable the ethics of that move might be.

Meanwhile, Elise received official notification that she'd advanced to the round of thirty-two in Solo Show, the fashion reality competition she intended to participate in. The notice instructed her to report immediately to the studio where filming would commence.

From this point forward, designers would compete in elimination rounds until only one remained victorious.

The winning designer's brand would receive full sponsorship from one of Crestwood's major corporate sponsors associated with the program.

Though the sponsorship was appealing, it wasn't the primary motivation for most competitors—as the competition intensified, the caliber of remaining designers would become exceptionally high.

Unsurprisingly, Elise encountered Sophia the moment she stepped into the studio.

Her stepsister had always craved the spotlight, and Sophia would never miss an opportunity to showcase herself.

Naturally, Sophia also sought redemption after her recent scandal. It came as no surprise that both Mitchell Corp and Scott Enterprises were quietly backing her participation.

On the other hand, Sophia stared at Elise in utter disbelief.

Did Elise actually believe that owning a fashion company qualified her to compete in a designers' competition?!

How utterly ridiculous!
###Chapter 157

Elise Scott was about to speak with Sophia when Amanda Mitchell arrived.

She was surrounded by a group of assistants.

Amanda immediately sneered at Elise. "What a small world. I can't believe we'd run into you here."

Elise was genuinely surprised to see Amanda involved in this.

Then she remembered. Solo Show could never attract current A-list celebrities.

They only had former stars whose popularity was fading.

Or actresses who were at best B-listers.

The designers were newcomers or even amateurs.

There was no real appeal for genuine stars here.

Naturally, Elise ignored both Sophia and Amanda.

There was nothing to say to them.

She was about to walk away when Amanda laughed scornfully.

"Elise Scott! Anyone can have self-respect, but be careful it doesn't turn into arrogance. Aren't you afraid of humiliating yourself in front of millions nationwide?!"

"Shouldn't you be more concerned about yourself, Ms. Mitchell?" Elise retorted. "I know Ethan Parker well enough to say he doesn't appreciate troublemakers."

Amanda's face darkened immediately.

How dare Elise threaten her with Ethan?

Elise continued as if she had just remembered something. "By the way, I had drinks with Mr. Parker himself during William's birthday. Perhaps he'll bring you along for dinner... if the opportunity arises."

With those words, Elise turned and left.

Amanda watched her, face pale with fury.

Was Elise mocking her?

Despite the official announcement, she and Ethan weren't really in a relationship!

The night everyone speculated about Ethan and Elizabeth, he had called Amanda at 3 AM.

He asked if she would date him.

If she agreed, he would announce it publicly at daybreak.

He admitted her reputation would plummet afterward.

It would more or less affect her career.

Still, Amanda agreed without hesitation.

The damage to her reputation was temporary.

But Ethan was the one she wanted for life!

Once she agreed, Ethan arrived at Mitchell Villa by 4 AM that same night.

He took several intimate photos of them together.

Then he told her to tweet the official announcement after he did.

Yet, Amanda never discovered the connection between Ethan and Elizabeth.

She felt something was going on between them.

And it had been for a while.

But once their possible relationship was revealed, Ethan was willing to cut ties with Elizabeth.

Even if it meant damaging his own reputation.

Amanda didn't pry.

Dating Ethan was enough for her.

She was confident that as long as she was with him, he would fall for her!

But a week after announcing their relationship, Ethan hadn't contacted her at all.

Whenever she texted or called, he said he was busy.

He was basically ignoring her.

She was already upset about that.

Elise's mention of it struck a nerve.

She would have strangled Elise if they weren't in public!

Elise even mentioned William...

Was she using him to intimidate her, especially since William and Ethan were close?!

Beside her, Sophia could feel Amanda's rage.

It had been a while since Elise had walked all over her.

But she was less volatile than Amanda.

She could endure it.

"Calm down, Amanda," she said. "This is still a competition. My sister will be eliminated soon since she's no professional."

"She's such a curse!" Amanda huffed disdainfully.

She told Sophia seriously, "You have to do your best, Sophia. My family has already paid the media for your publicity. Even more than they did for me. You're basically representing Mitchell Corp now."

Sophia certainly knew the Mitchells had paid heavily for her to win.

She had arranged it, after all.
###Chapter 158

Sophia knew she couldn't rely on her own family. Their patriarchal structure was too rigid. They would approve media spending for publicity, but anything beyond their predetermined budget would be immediately rejected.

With the Mitchells involved, however, she could easily exceed her original financial limitations.

That was precisely why she had approached Samuel and competed as the chief fashion designer of Scott Enterprise.

Even though her feelings for Samuel had faded, he remained useful until she achieved the success she desired.

Moreover, Samuel would eagerly agree to her proposals, especially since the expenditure would essentially clear both their names.

If she could win the grand prize representing Mitchell Corp, it would significantly enhance their reputation.

Sophia's eyes narrowed thoughtfully.

She had never fully revealed her capabilities during her time at Stellar Group—not even when Elise pressured her to design templates for their autumn collection. She refused to excel for Elise's benefit.

But once Sophia became serious, she would undoubtedly leave everyone astonished.

The contestants entered the studio and took their assigned seats as directed by the production team.

Since this was the pilot episode filming and opening sequence, their primary task was introducing themselves before the celebrity judges selected their designer partners.

Despite Elise competing as Stellar Group's chief designer, several contemptuous glances were directed her way. Everyone assumed she had claimed the position arbitrarily and only passed the pre-audition due to recent public interest in her personal life. The potential drama of the Scott sisters competing again certainly made for compelling television.

Nevertheless, after Elise introduced herself, none of the celebrity judges chose her.

Public interest was one thing, but this was business—they worried about being eliminated alongside Elise. Solo Show rules stipulated that celebrity partners would be eliminated with their chosen designers, making the selection process particularly delicate.

In contrast, Sophia had numerous impressive titles: fifth place in an international design competition, continental runner-up, former chief designer of Stellar Group, and current chief designer for Scott Enterprise's fashion division.

After her introduction, half of the thirty-two celebrity judges selected her. The others refrained only because they recognized their own limitations and wanted to avoid potential humiliation.

Sophia stood and graciously thanked everyone for their confidence before selecting Amanda Mitchell.

Their partnership was undoubtedly strategic—the Mitchells clearly intended to use Solo Show to elevate both Amanda and Sophia.

After all, who would refuse free publicity?

Ultimately, Elise was paired with Stella Murray, the only celebrity who remained unchosen.

Their combination lacked dramatic appeal, resulting in minimal camera time during the pilot episode.

To everyone's astonishment, when live audience voting occurred, Elise placed fifth—a complete reversal of expectations. Unlike other celebrities present, Stella had no substantial fanbase, meaning their ranking depended entirely on Elise's design abilities.

Even the judges agreed Elise's design deserved a top-three placement.

Naturally, Sophia couldn't accept this outcome—even though she had placed first!
###Chapter 159

The premiere of this season's Solo Show exploded across social media.

Viewership records were shattered overnight.

Public reaction was overwhelmingly positive.

William finally understood Elise's cryptic remark about being busy for three months.

He kept replaying her segments obsessively.

What should have been minutes stretched into half an hour of repetitive viewing.

Samuel Wilson watched his boss with growing concern.

He recognized Elise's stunning beauty—how she outshone even established celebrities.

But William's fixation seemed almost unhealthy.

Samuel stifled a chuckle at the thought.

The sound died in his throat as William's deadly glare pinned him.

"Something amusing you?" William's voice cut like ice.

"Not at all," Samuel quickly recovered. "Ms. Scott looks absolutely radiant."

William's scowl deepened, making Samuel wish he'd kept silent.

What was wrong with complimenting Elise? She was breathtaking!

"Get out," William commanded.

Samuel escaped gratefully.

Alone again, William grabbed his phone.

Elise was mid-meeting when his call came through.

Seeing his name, she sent a quick text: [In meeting. Call you later.]

William's expression darkened.
