His phone buzzed with WhatsApp notifications.

Nathan Davis had posted the same clip William had been obsessing over.

[Nate] tagged William enthusiastically.

[Did you see this, Will? Your girl's on Solo Show! She's trending everywhere!]

[Nate] continued, [Fifth place despite elimination rounds? She's turning this competition upside down!]

William remained silent.

[Nate] then tagged Ethan Parker.

[Your wife's competing too. First place with Sophia Mitchell!]

The conversation attracted Andrew Saunders' attention.

[Andrew] typed, [Elise Scott, Sophia Mitchell, and Amanda Mitchell? This is getting juicy!]

[Nate] couldn't resist adding fuel. [If Elizabeth York joined, we'd have physical fights on live TV. Now that's entertainment...]

Neither William nor Ethan responded.

William's phone rang.

He answered immediately.

"Just left my meeting," Elise said. "Everything okay?"

William rarely called during work hours.

"If I relied on news feeds, I'd never know my girlfriend was becoming public property," he said coolly.

Elise fell silent.

Public property? What an exaggeration!

"It's a design competition," she explained patiently. "Three months maximum."

"Why wasn't I informed?" William pressed.

Elise hesitated—they were still navigating their relationship boundaries.

Weren't they just testing the waters?

"Or do you doubt your boyfriend's influence?" William arched an eyebrow.

"What?" Elise sounded genuinely confused.

"If Samuel Mitchell can manipulate media coverage, you think I can't?"

Understanding dawned—he was upset about Sophia overshadowing her.

Yet she'd been questioning his involvement.

Guilt and warmth warred within her.

A smile touched her lips. "Your girlfriend is perfectly capable. No need to waste your resources."

"Money opens doors," William reminded sternly.
###Chapter 160

Elise still shot back defiantly, "Don't you dare underestimate what your girlfriend is capable of."

"Even so," William countered smoothly, "you should never forget that your boyfriend possesses a mountain of solid gold bars. Hard. Reliable. Dazzling to the eye."

Elise couldn't suppress a laugh at his vivid self-description.

Changing topics, William then inquired, "Are you free tonight?"

"...Yes." She'd been practically ignoring him for nearly two weeks. Even if work wasn't finished tonight, she decided she absolutely had to see him.

William made the arrangements. "I'll pick you up after work. We're having dinner at your place."

Elise did a double take, certain she'd misheard. "My place? There's practically no food there."

"You can make pasta, can't you?" he asked simply.

"That's all you want?" Elise questioned, surprised.

"Yep," he replied, a happy note in his voice.

Elise didn't refuse.

Just then, Matthew Johnson entered with a report for her. "I need to get back to work now," she said into the phone. "See you tonight."

"Alright."

With that, Elise ended the call and plunged back into her work, fully focused.

It wasn't until half past seven that Elise suddenly remembered her date with William. She quickly snatched her phone and called him.

"I was beginning to think you'd forgotten all about me, Ms. Scott," his melodious, magnetic voice chided from the other end.

"Where are you?"

"Look down," William instructed.

Elise walked to the glass wall and peered down. There it was—that impossibly conspicuous black Maybach. And leaning against it was the man himself, looking up and waving casually at her.

It was a nonchalant gesture, yet he managed to look incredibly cool doing it.

"I'll be right down," Elise said hurriedly. She shoved her phone into her bag and practically flew out of her office, her mind racing, wondering just how long he'd been waiting there for her.

At the main entrance, William was still leaning against his car as Elise rushed up to him. "I'm so sorry. You should've called me, you know—my mind goes blank after back-to-back meetings."

"As long as you remember me eventually," William shrugged, unfazed.

She wasn't usually that unreliable—just overwhelmingly busy.

He opened the passenger door for her. "Get in."

Elise slid inside, and they drove off toward her apartment.

The moment they stepped inside, Elise didn't pause. She immediately tied an apron around her waist and got started on cooking.

While she was focused on the task, she suddenly felt strong arms wrap around her from behind.

His tall, powerful frame easily enveloped her, pulling her snugly against his chest. Her heart stuttered in her chest.

"Consider this compensation," William murmured, his voice a low rumble near her ear as he nuzzled the sensitive skin of her neck. His lips brushed against her earlobe.

He seemed to have a particular tendency to nibble there—the memory of the last time sent a phantom ache echoing.

"Then stop moving," Elise said, forcing herself to stay still.

For a split second, her instinct was to push him away. But that impulse cooled, soothed by the unique, clean scent of pine that clung to him.

"Okay," William agreed, though his hot, steamy breath continued to scorch the shell of her ear.

"Your lips too," Elise demanded firmly.

William remained silent.

She could certainly be bossy when she wanted to be.

A silence settled over them then… though it wasn't truly silent. The frantic beating of their hearts seemed audible in the quiet kitchen.

Ba-dump.

Elise could feel the powerful, rhythmic thumping of William's heart against her back. It seemed to resonate with her own erratic pulse, both completely out of sync.

Finally, she finished cooking the pasta. But as she was draining the boiling water, she accidentally splashed some on her hand.

"Ah!" she yelped involuntarily.

In an instant, William scooped her up as if she weighed nothing, depositing her neatly on the nearby kitchen counter. He immediately took her hand in his, his expression turning serious.

"Where did it burn you? Show me," he demanded, his voice utterly bewitching and full of concern.
###Chapter 161
Elise shook her head. “I’m okay.”

It was just a minor burn from the hot water, a fleeting sting—nothing she couldn't handle.

But the real threat was William moving closer.

He was already towering, and even seated on the kitchen counter, her petite frame didn't reach his height.

He seemed to ignore her protest, his focus entirely on inspecting her slender fingers for any injury. After confirming there were no visible red marks, he finally spoke, “Ms. Scott, you're always so accident-prone, aren't you?”

His words were critical, yet his tone held an unexpected softness.

“I am not!” Elise retorted, though her conviction wavered immediately.

She suddenly realized how often she made mistakes whenever she cooked for William at her apartment.

Meeting his meaningful gaze, she conceded, “It's only around you.”

She never fumbled like this with anyone else.

“Is that so?” William's lips curved into a smile, clearly pleased with her admission. “I'm honored you feel so comfortable with me, Ms. Scott.”

Elise huffed in frustration. Why did he always twist her words?!

She shifted, pointing out, “The pasta is going to get soggy.”

She attempted to slide off the counter, but William stopped her. “Stay put.”

“It'll be ruined if it turns mushy,” she argued.

“I'll handle it,” William insisted. “You just stay right there, Ms. Scott.”

Elise bit her lip, feeling like a child being pampered, much like how James might feel, though James would probably swear his father never doted on him so attentively.

Nevertheless, Elise remained seated on the counter, watching as William carefully drained the pasta and plated it with broccoli, eggs, and ham, then carried the dishes to the dining table.

Once done, he lifted her from the counter and they began their meal.

It was a simple dish, yet it carried a subtle flavor of contentment.

After dinner, William showed no intention of leaving. He lounged comfortably on her living room sofa, casually flipping through TV channels with the remote.

Elise felt a twinge of helplessness; she had planned to work on some designs tonight.

Her days were consumed by responsibilities at Stellar Group, leaving her only the nights for creative work.

But with William settled in…

“Relax, Ms. Scott. No need to feel uneasy on my account,” William said, as if he had a sixth sense for her thoughts while still watching TV.

His comment irked her. Whose home was this anyway?!

Yet, her heart fluttered momentarily.

Had he chosen to dine here because he knew she was busy, wanting to save her time?

Touched by the possibility, she turned and headed toward her study.

The walls were made of glass, positioned behind the sofa, allowing her a clear view of the living room—and vice versa.

Elise powered up her laptop and immersed herself in work. Though not formally trained as a fashion designer, her mother had privately mentored her since childhood. Her mother had always kept her identity as a world-renowned fashion designer secret, a fact known only to Elise.

Just like Elise was doing now.

Once absorbed in her designs, she became oblivious to her surroundings.

The theme for the next round was summer, and she had settled on a style but was deliberating over the color palette.

Should she evoke passion or embody a maiden's purity?

Elise visualized Stella Murray's appearance, knowing that designs should complement the model's features.

Exiting her design software, she intended to search for photos of Stella online but accidentally clicked on an unfamiliar video file on her desktop.

She froze, stunned, unable to process what she was seeing.

Since when did she have explicit content on her computer?!

Then she remembered Elizabeth borrowing her laptop recently… could it be hers?!

Flustered, Elise hurriedly tried to close the video…
###Chapter 162

In that exact moment, a deep masculine voice spoke from directly behind her. "I didn't know you had such interests, Ms. Scott."

Elise's heart nearly leaped from her chest.

She spun around to find William standing there, having entered the study without her noticing.

She hadn't heard him come in at all, though she'd been too absorbed in her research to pay attention.

That wasn't the worst part. The woman in the video continued moaning shamelessly.

Elise frantically turned back to close the video, but her trembling fingers couldn't properly control the mouse. She fumbled desperately, beads of sweat forming on her forehead.

Then a large, capable hand covered hers, guiding the mouse to close the offending window with a single click.

Silence descended upon the study, and Elise wished the floor would swallow her whole.

She had repeatedly claimed she disliked physical intimacy with men, that it made her uncomfortable... and now William had caught her watching this?

There was no reasonable explanation for this.

As she hung her head, blushing crimson to the roots of her hair, William spoke again. "Look at me, Ms. Scott."

She was practically burying her head in her chest like an ostrich.

Elise bit her lip, knowing she had to offer some explanation or this misunderstanding would linger.

She released a heavy sigh and lifted her gaze, only to have William lean in unexpectedly.

Their lips met in that instant, and both their bodies jolted with surprise.

The contact was completely unexpected, catching them both off guard... yet it sent her pulse racing wildly.

The very air seemed to thin around them as everything else faded into stillness.

Their eyes locked intensely, and Elise found herself nervously twisting the fabric of her sleeves.

She knew perfectly well that if she didn't pull away, William would take things further.

And if she did retreat... he would respect her boundaries.

Her lips pursed instinctively, the slight movement almost like an invitation.

William's Adam's apple bobbed enticingly. Both his eyes and lips seemed to burn with intensity, as if consumed by fire.

His large hand came up to cradle the back of her head. Even with her limited experience, Elise knew exactly what was coming next...

Her heart hammered uncontrollably against her ribs. Her breathing grew ragged and uneven.

She closed her eyes just as his tongue began to trace the seam of her lips, and...

BANG!

The front door slammed shut with startling force somewhere in the house.

The sound was loud enough to make both of them jump.

Elizabeth immediately regretted her dramatic entrance.

She could feel the murderous glare directed her way even before she turned around.

Fear made her want to flee immediately.

Their positions made it perfectly clear what she'd interrupted... and she absolutely hadn't meant to return at this particular moment!

If she'd known William was visiting tonight, nothing could have made her come home.

She watched helplessly as her cousin released Elise and began striding toward her.

Was there time to dig a hole and bury herself alive?!
###Chapter 163

William loomed in the doorway, his voice dangerously low. "What are you doing here?!"

Elizabeth froze, unable to form words. She had never moved out of Elise's apartment, despite William arranging alternative accommodation for her.

Meanwhile, Elise took a deep breath before emerging from her study. She couldn't bear seeing Elizabeth trembling like a frightened kitten.

"It's getting late. You should head home," Elise told William.

William's lips pressed into a thin line—he could still feel the ghost of Elise's kiss on his mouth.

But he hadn't enjoyed it!

His scowl deepened as he growled, "I asked what you're doing here?"

He clearly needed to vent his frustration before he could calm down.

"..." Elizabeth remained speechless, casting a pleading look toward Elise.

"I invited her to stay," Elise explained calmly. "I was concerned about her being alone, so I offered her my spare room."

"Why would she need company? She's been independent since she was eighteen!" William snapped through clenched teeth.

"Well, if you're so concerned, why don't you move in with Elise instead? If you do, I'll gladly move out!" Elizabeth suddenly fired back, mustering all her courage with a defiant glare.

William's face darkened instantly, while Elise found herself in an awkward position.

If these two actually started fighting, would she really need to play mediator?

"Anyway, I'm exhausted from shooting all day and need a bath. Feel free to continue your discussion. I won't interrupt," Elizabeth declared, making a quick escape.

Staying any longer risked William actually losing his temper.

Even as she retreated, William shouted after her, "And delete that inappropriate content from Elise's laptop!"

Elizabeth paused, then realization dawned.

Linda—her manager—had sent those videos for research purposes, and she'd simply left them on Elise's computer.

Her manager had been worried about Elizabeth's lack of experience for an upcoming intimate scene. She'd provided reference materials, concerned Elizabeth might struggle with the performance.

Elizabeth appreciated her manager's dedication, but couldn't she learn these things naturally?!

Meanwhile, Elise was taken aback by William's assumption, sneaking a sideways glance at him.

Why was he so certain those videos belonged to Elizabeth and not her?!

She felt unexpectedly touched, sensing his complete and unconditional trust in her.

But did she deserve such faith?!

She wasn't sure...

"It's not inappropriate! It's educational material!" Elizabeth retorted as if stating the obvious—they were all adults, after all. Still, she quickly surrendered under William's murderous glare. "Fine, whatever. I'll delete them."

Though internally, she couldn't help complaining.

Would he have ever kissed Elise without her so-called "inappropriate" help?

Where was the gratitude?

Marching into the study, she deleted the videos while Elise continued urging William to leave at the door. "You have work tomorrow, and James is home alone. You should go."

"Right." William finally agreed, but shot another warning look toward Elizabeth in the study, his expression clearly displeased.

Elise couldn't suppress a small smile—she'd learned enough about him to know he could be quite petty.

"Goodnight," she said, holding the door open for him.

But as William stepped out, his eyes drifted to her lips again, making her cheeks burn.

She quickly closed the door, nearly hitting William in the face.

He let out a heavy sigh as the door clicked shut.
###Chapter 164

William's heart continued to race wildly.

He could still feel the lingering sensation of Elise's lips.

Back at Elise's apartment, Elizabeth emerged from the study after deleting the videos. "Is he gone?"

Elise quickly composed herself and returned to the study. "Yes."

"Was I being too intrusive?" Elizabeth asked, feeling slightly guilty.

"Not at all." Elise shook her head.

Elizabeth studied her friend's expression before teasing, "That wasn't your first kiss with him, was it?"

Elise paused momentarily, then shook her head. "No."

The previous time, William had taken her completely by surprise.

She had felt only shock and anger then.

This time was different—she had given her silent consent.

The experience felt entirely new and distinct.

"He's quite skilled, isn't he?" Elizabeth couldn't resist teasing further when she saw Elise's thoughtful expression.

Elise simply stared back, uncertain how to respond.

Both kisses had been brief and gentle—hardly enough to judge technique.

"Well, that man certainly knows what he's doing." Elizabeth acknowledged William's appeal since Elise wasn't providing details.

His handsome features alone were enough to captivate anyone.

"By the way, I was surprised to learn you joined a reality show," Elizabeth changed the subject. "I wouldn't have known if Penelope hadn't mentioned it. So that's why you've been so busy lately."

"I'm using it as a platform to promote Stellar Group," Elise explained. "Your work as brand ambassador has given us great exposure, but overall, the company hasn't reached the standard I envisioned."

"You need to surpass Mitchell Corp," Elizabeth said supportively. "Seeing Samuel Mitchell act so arrogantly disgusts me."

Elise's ambitions extended beyond simply surpassing Mitchell Corp—if Stellar Group reached the heights she desired, crushing Mitchell Corp would be inevitable.

Her true goal was fulfilling her late mother's dream of elevating Stellar Group to new heights. Her mother had founded the company but passed away in an accident before seeing it flourish. Given her mother's talent, she would have undoubtedly brought Stellar Group to the pinnacle of the fashion industry.

"You should be careful around Sophia Mitchell and Amanda Mitchell," Elizabeth added with concern. "A two-faced witch and a cunning schemer? They're definitely plotting something—don't fall for their tricks."

"I know." Elise nodded.

She had anticipated trouble when she discovered they were also participating in Solo Show.

However, defeating them wouldn't be difficult—she simply needed to ensure they didn't perceive her as a threat.

During the first round, she had unintentionally stood out despite trying to remain low-key, having been unfamiliar with the other designers' capabilities.

Now that she understood the competition's standards, she knew how to approach the remaining rounds.

Elizabeth considered this and felt somewhat relieved. "Actually, with William around, you probably don't need to worry about being bullied too much."

Elise's heart fluttered momentarily. There was no denying that William provided a sense of security.

Yet during the second round, Elise clearly faced harassment.

Not only did she receive minimal screen time—the script assigned to her was deliberately unfavorable.

She knew Sophia and Amanda were behind it, unable to accept her fifth-place ranking in the first round.

In their minds, she should have been eliminated immediately.

William was right—money talked.

But this situation suited her purposes perfectly, because thorough preparation was essential for success!

Consequently, she placed twelfth in the second round.

It was a significant drop from her previous ranking, narrowly avoiding elimination.
###Chapter 165

Sophia and Amanda were positively gleeful after Elise's ranking plummeted.

In their minds, this humiliation-and eventual elimination-was all Elise deserved.

Her first-round success had been pure luck, and she had barely scraped through this time.

Next round would definitely be her last!

Elise, however, appeared completely unfazed by her dropping rank or the public criticism directed at her.

After completing the second round, she poured all her energy into her work.

During her spare moments, she focused entirely on preparing her design for the upcoming challenge.

She was deep in concentration in her office when her phone abruptly rang.

Distracted by work, she answered without checking the caller ID.

"You don't have to keep up this charade, Elise." Samuel's mocking voice came through immediately.

Elise sighed inwardly. Men with fragile egos always became arrogant over the smallest things.

Meanwhile, Samuel continued with a cold laugh. "Don't think I'm fooled. You needed Stellar Group's designers for that first-round dress. But without Sophia, they're useless-they only got you through round one. I heard you're already falling behind in the second round... If I were you, I'd quit now rather than embarrass myself further."

"And why exactly do you care?" Elise responded coolly. "Shouldn't you be watching your precious Sophia more closely? Now that she's becoming famous, she might discard you in a heartbeat!"

"Sophia would never do that!" Samuel exploded, his temper ignited by Elise's words. "Stop trying to drive us apart! Our relationship is perfect, and we're getting married as soon as she wins this competition. You'll never have me, Elise!"

"Where did you get the idea that I want you?" Elise laughed, genuinely amused.

There was a time when his words would have hurt her, when she had genuinely loved him.

Now, everything about him repulsed her, and any lingering feelings had completely evaporated.

In fact, she now regretted ever falling for him, allowing him to hurt her so deeply that she found it difficult to open her heart again.

Yet Samuel continued gloating triumphantly. "It's good that you know your place, Elise. Don't think you're special just because you're involved with William Levine. I might as well tell you-he already has a fiancée, though it hasn't been publicly announced."

He had been on a business trip to Rockville recently and had accidentally overheard some interesting gossip.

"And this fiancée?" Samuel continued viciously. "You're not even worthy of shining her shoes. Stop being so naive. You're just making a complete fool of yourself-"

Elise ended the call abruptly.

She had already made the mistake of trusting him once.

She wouldn't believe a single word he said now.

Somehow, Elise managed to survive the following rounds despite consistently ranking last.

Each episode seemed like it would be her last, yet she always advanced to the next.

Samuel, Sophia, and Amanda were left with a frustrating sense of powerlessness.

It felt like every blow they aimed at her landed on something soft and unresponsive, leaving them increasingly frustrated as they watched Elise advance to the final round.

This time, it would be broadcast live.
###Chapter 166

The design theme remained undisclosed.

Contestants would receive their challenge prompt live on stage.

They had to create original designs using only materials supplied by the production.

Partners would be randomly reassigned for this final round.

Previously, designers had chosen their own models, fostering established teamwork and understanding.

This sudden shift clearly aimed to intensify competition difficulty.

In Mitchell Corp's executive suite, Amanda was furious. "What ridiculous rule is this? Changing partners for the finale?"

Samuel and Sophia appeared equally startled by the announcement.

Amanda continued her tirade. "Samuel, you know Sophia must win this competition. But if I'm not modeling her designs, her victory means nothing! Can't you pressure the producers to reverse this?"

"I already contacted them. They claim the script leaked and changes are impossible now."

"I don't accept that!" Amanda snapped, crossing her arms.

"The decision stands. Besides, you're not the crucial element here—Sophia is," Samuel retorted, turning to his fiancée. "My concern is whether you're prepared for any surprises in this round."

Sophia shared his apprehension, primarily regarding Elise's inexplicable advancement.

Somehow, Elise kept surviving elimination rounds through apparent luck.

Yet she consistently secured enough audience votes to proceed.

Suspicion gnawed at Sophia—was Elise deliberately concealing her abilities?

She shouldn't possess resources to bribe production for such an elaborate scheme, lulling everyone into complacency while she reached the finals.

But if true... Elise had been hiding formidable skills all along.

Sophia suddenly recalled Stellar Group's wildly popular design templates. She knew they weren't her creations, and the design team lacked such talent.

Could Elise truly be the mastermind?

The possibility made Sophia increasingly anxious.

She couldn't allow Elise to defeat her in the final round!

Regret washed over her for not eliminating Elise earlier, deceived into believing she'd naturally fail in subsequent rounds.

The Scotts and Mitchells had successfully pressured production to sabotage Elise before, but live broadcasts complicated manipulation.

Still, she maintained her devoted facade. "Samuel, we can't let Elise win. Her victory would humiliate you and Mitchell Corp. We've invested too much to fail now."

"Wait, shouldn't you feel confident?" Amanda asked, dismissing Elise entirely.

"I believe in my designs, but you know about my sister's connection with William Levine. His slightest assistance could jeopardize our victory," Sophia explained, providing the perfect justification.

Both Samuel and Amanda darkened at the mention.

"How did that woman ensnare William?" Amanda sneered.

"He's merely amusing himself with her. He's engaged," Samuel stated contemptuously.

"Really? To whom?" Amanda asked eagerly.

Nearby, Sophia grew restless.

"It's confirmed, but someone beyond our reach. I won't disclose details," Samuel said. "Knowing you, you'd cause trouble for our family. Still, Sophia's concern is valid—William remains involved with Elise currently, and his minimal intervention could change everything."
###Chapter 167

Sophia hesitated for a moment before speaking. "I... I might have a plan."

Samuel and Amanda both leaned forward, their interest instantly piqued. "What is it?" they asked in near unison.

Sophia bit her lip, appearing deeply troubled. "But it wouldn't be fair to my sister... It feels wrong."

"Fair to Elise?" Amanda cried out indignantly. "When has she ever been fair to you? She humiliated both you and Samuel at that press conference without a second thought! She doesn't consider you family, so why are you protecting her?"

"Alright," Sophia said reluctantly, her voice barely above a whisper. "I'll do whatever it takes for Samuel."

When she finally revealed her scheme, both Samuel and Amanda were genuinely stunned by its audacity.

Could they actually pull that off?

Sophia maintained her troubled expression. "Don't you think... this might be going too far?"

"Too far?" Amanda quickly countered. "Elise started this by cheating first! If she's playing dirty, we're just giving her a taste of her own medicine! It's only fair!"

Sophia then turned to Samuel, who nodded in agreement. "She brought this upon herself. We wouldn't resort to such measures if she hadn't pushed us to this point."

"Yes," Sophia murmured solemnly, her innocent appearance belying the cunning nature of her elaborate scheme.

Her heart had grown dark indeed.

If Elise wanted to play games with her, she would only end up utterly humiliated.

And yet...

Did William really have a fiancée?

While the thought of Elise being toyed with by William gave her some satisfaction, it also meant that Sophia herself had no chance with him!

Sophia bit her lip, deciding to wait and see how events would unfold.

The final round of Solo Show was finally here, and the studio buzzed with palpable tension since it was broadcasting live.

Only three pairs would emerge victorious in this ultimate competition.

The competing designers were Elise, Sophia, and a mixed-ancestry fashion designer named Andal Scott, while their celebrity partners were Amanda, Stella Murray, and Kathy Barnes.

Among the three celebrities, Stella had the smallest following while Amanda was the most popular.

Moreover, Mitchell Corp had spared no expense in buying popularity for Sophia and Amanda. The moment the program began, searches and discussions about them skyrocketed across the internet.

Amanda, in particular, had skillfully used her down-to-earth, straightforward girl-next-door persona on camera to elevate herself among B-list celebrities.

The final round of Solo Show commenced at 8 PM, the prime time for reality television.

As the host officially announced the start of the event, the thousand-strong audience and three judges erupted in applause. There was an additional judge—Maron, an internationally renowned fashion designer whose name carried immense weight in the industry, and who had previously judged Sheeny Cup, the world's most prestigious design competition.

In the CEO office at Levine Holdings headquarters, William's phone kept buzzing incessantly even as his eyes remained fixed on the livestream.

Naturally, it was from his four-person chat group, with Nathan Davis persistently pinging him.

[It's starting! The showdown begins! @ethanparker @williamlevine]

[Elise looks absolutely stunning tonight! @williamlevine]

[Amanda and Elise in the same competition?! This is going to be interesting! @ethanparker @williamlevine]

[Do you think Amanda might actually walk off the stage? @ethanparker]

William quickly exited WhatsApp since the notifications were distracting him, but that meant he had to watch the livestream's comment section, which was being flooded endlessly.

[Elise is such a jinx, barely scraping through to the finals.]

[Why are we even watching? Sophia Mitchell has this in the bag. She designed Stellar Group's incredibly popular autumn collection, which was absolutely perfect for my taste.]

[It's rigged if Sophia doesn't win!]

[I'm rooting for Sophia too.]

[#SophiaWin]

William's scowl deepened with each passing comment. "How do I turn off these live comments?"

"It's more entertaining that way..." Samuel Wilson began, but he promptly disabled the comments when his boss shot him a sharp look.

Though without the comments, the viewing experience felt only half as engaging.

"That said, I'm genuinely impressed with Mrs. Levine," Samuel couldn't help remarking at that moment.
###Chapter 168

William murmured softly, "Hmm?"

His gaze remained fixed on Elise displayed on the screen.

Beneath the brilliant stage lights, she looked breathtakingly beautiful.

"Reaching the finals is one thing," Samuel Wilson remarked at that moment. "But maintaining last place with such precise calculation? That's truly divine artistry!"

Having worked for William for years and eventually rising to become his special assistant, Samuel was undoubtedly highly capable.

He was almost certain Elise had intentionally held her ranking just above elimination, skillfully avoiding attacks from financially-backed competitors.

One could even say she ascended to the pinnacle with minimal effort, effectively slapping the Mitchells' faces against the floor and grinding them into dust.

William's lips curved into a slight smile—Elise was definitely far more clever than he had anticipated.

At the studio, the three designers exchanged their celebrity partners.

Since Elise had the least popularity, she was paired with Amanda, the most popular celebrity.

Andal Scott remained partnered with Kathy Barnes, while Sophia was paired with Stella Murray.

This round also consisted of two phases.

The first phase would determine the third-place winner, with the ultimate victor decided in the second phase.

As the first phase commenced, designers had thirty minutes to create a dress using fabrics available in the studio.

There was no specific theme—scores would be determined solely by the judges' and audience reactions.

Elise quickly scanned the available fabrics and immediately began working without hesitation.

Sophia shot her a brief glance before starting on her own design.

Thanks to Amanda's connections with the production team, they had prior knowledge of today's challenge and had even prepared a design in advance.

Naturally, Sophia finished first, instructing Stella to change into the completed dress.

When Stella emerged, the audience erupted in enthusiastic approval, clearly impressed by Sophia's creation.

Still, Sophia maintained her humility. After all, she couldn't reveal that she not only knew about the competition details beforehand but had subtly consulted an internationally renowned designer for this stunning work.

The design was indeed perfect, and she realized she needn't worry about Elise surpassing her.

Perhaps she had been overestimating Elise all along!

But then the crowd roared again, this time even louder than before.

Sophia turned to see Elise emerging from backstage with Amanda.

Amanda wore a pure-white gown that appeared to be fastened simply with safety pins, yet it perfectly accentuated her figure.

What truly set it apart was the sparkling dust covering the white fabric. Under the stage lights overhead, it glimmered with a faint, dreamlike radiance. It resembled an ocean of stars manifesting and twinkling across Amanda's body, shining vividly against the darkness.

It was a visual feast, leaving everyone in the studio utterly astonished by the spectacle before them.

Sophia's design suddenly seemed dull by comparison.

'No!!!'

Sophia's panic became evident.

Elise shouldn't have been capable of this level. Something was wrong.

It must be William helping her behind the scenes!

This couldn't possibly be Elise's own design!
###Chapter 169

Elise had clearly dominated this round with overwhelming public support, securing her place in the next phase.

The audience's cheers were thunderous.

However, the judges rated Elise's design poorly, dismissing it as lacking uniqueness, only acknowledging its overall aesthetic appeal. Fortunately, their scores accounted for just twenty percent of the total, and Samuel's earlier sabotage had ultimately failed.

Meanwhile, Sophia's face was pale with panic.

At this rate, even if she had a sudden burst of inspiration in the final round, she stood no chance against Elise.

She glanced toward Amanda, who was visibly pleased with Elise's creation.

In fact, Amanda had never felt more beautiful in her life!

If anyone else had designed that dress, she would have immediately hired them as her personal stylist.

But it had to be Elise... and she couldn't even bring herself to hate the design!

Naturally, she noticed Sophia's desperate look.

Personal preferences aside, Amanda would never allow Elise to surpass them.

She gave Sophia a subtle, understanding nod, which instantly boosted Sophia's confidence.

Meanwhile, at Levine Holdings headquarters, William instructed Samuel Wilson to reopen the live comment feed while calmly checking his WhatsApp.

Messages flooded in, all from Nathan Davis.

[Elise is incredible, starting off with a bang! @williamlevine]

[What's wrong with those judges?! How is Elise's design simple?! Don't they understand that less is more?! They have worse taste than I do! @williamlevine]

[Even though Amanda has always been somewhat attractive, and I might have noticed years ago, I have to admit she looks absolutely stunning tonight. It's all thanks to Elise's design. @williamlevine @ethanparker]

Nathan continued his enthusiastic rambling, but no one responded.

William had already switched his focus to the live comments.

[Doesn't anyone think Elise Scott's design was pure genius? How is that just luck?]

[I've always felt her designs had depth... Now comparing hers to Sophia's, the difference in skill is obvious.]

[Are the judges blind?! That dress has so many intricate details, and they completely dismissed it!]

[I still think Sophia has more potential. She's probably holding back to avoid humiliating the competition.]

The comments became a heated debate between Elise and Sophia's supporters, but approval for Elise was clearly growing!

Meanwhile, the final round moved into its second phase—the ultimate showdown between Elise and Sophia.

The theme was 'pinnacle'.

In reality, the theme was mostly for dramatic effect—any design could work as long as the narrative was well-executed.

After the theme announcement, Elise and Sophia began working intently.

The atmosphere grew increasingly tense.

Once again, Sophia finished first, sending Stella onto the stage wearing her creation.

It was a bold, flamboyant dress with vibrant colors and intricate detailing, exuding both regal elegance and playful charm.

One might describe it as a fusion of queenly grace and seductive allure.

It immediately captured everyone's attention, and the audience erupted in applause.

Sophia had always craved validation.

Her troubled upbringing meant she was often looked down upon as a child, but as she grew older, she learned how to manipulate others with strategic flattery. Though she usually got what she wanted, people never failed to mention her humble origins.

That was why she constantly compared herself to Elise and harassed her relentlessly. Anything Elise possessed, Sophia had to take—or acquire something even better.
###Chapter 170

Sophia waited with absolute confidence for Elise to present her designs.

In truth, Sophia was certain she wouldn't even need to scheme against Elise to secure victory this time.

Suddenly, applause erupted through the venue.

Sophia turned to see Elise stepping onto the stage.

Amanda was still back in the changing room, so the audience remained relatively subdued.

Several minutes later, the curtains parted once more.

The crowd roared with renewed excitement as Amanda made her entrance, instantly commanding everyone's attention.

She wore a breathtaking red gown, intricately adorned with vibrant crimson roses from neckline to hem. The design was anything but vulgar. It felt both refreshingly original and stunningly alluring.

Sophia's own design had emphasized aesthetic appeal, something an audience might admire from a distance. Elise's creation, however, sparked an immediate, visceral desire. People longed to own it, to wear it, to become the center of attention themselves.

Naturally, Elise's design was the more breathtaking of the two.

This was exactly what Sophia had anticipated.

To be precise, it was what she had orchestrated.

Sophia's eyes briefly met Amanda's across the stage.

To any observer, it was a simple glance between close friends.

Only the two of them understood it was a silent confirmation of their cunning plan's success.

Meanwhile, Elise pressed her lips together, her gaze fixed on the flawless gown Amanda wore.

She could feel the audience's palpable delight.

Barring any unforeseen disasters, this victory should have been hers.

But the harsh reality was this: the gown Amanda was showcasing… was not the one Elise had designed.

Elise had personally helped Amanda into her original creation in the dressing room. Once finished, she had exited first, as the script required.

But alarm bells had started ringing when Amanda failed to appear after several minutes.

Once she was out, Elise couldn't simply return backstage.

She had anticipated sabotage—perhaps a torn seam. She was prepared to mend it quickly, blaming an accident. A few minor deduction points were manageable; the overall victory would remain secure.

She never expected such a brazen, complete switch.

This had to be a premeditated act. The gown Amanda now wore hadn't been hastily assembled; it must have been smuggled backstage beforehand. The moment Elise left, one of the assistants must have helped Amanda change.

It was a bold theory, but the lack of cameras in the dressing room made it terrifyingly plausible.

Despite the turmoil inside, Elise's expression remained a mask of perfect composure.

Staying calm, she realized the dress she had actually designed was likely already removed by the backstage crew, who were undoubtedly in on the scheme.

She had no evidence to expose the plot. Furthermore, the designers had worked in a sealed room from the very beginning.

Adjudicators had been present in that room, but it went without saying that Samuel had already ensured their cooperation.

After being so vigilant against Sophia and Samuel for so many rounds… to finally fall into their trap at the last moment!

How deeply did they resent her success that they would sink to such despicable depths?

The competition proceeded to the final voting.

Once again, Elise won by an overwhelming majority, claiming the ultimate victory.

Confetti rained down. Poppers cracked, filling the air with colorful streamers.

Sophia walked over to Elise, a brilliant smile on her face, and enveloped her in a warm, seemingly heartfelt hug. She offered sincere, effusive congratulations.

There was no doubt about it. The gesture generated a massive wave of goodwill from the live audience and the millions watching on television screens.
###Chapter 171

The audience erupted in applause for Elise Scott, praising her for living up to expectations.

But they were also cheering for Sophia Mitchell's gracious acceptance of defeat.

Sophia had been leading in both points and popularity throughout the competition. Many found it difficult to accept her loss.

Yet there she was, sincerely congratulating Elise.

[I thought they hated each other, but maybe I was wrong. Sisters shouldn't fight over a man!]

[I never realized Sophia was so mature about these things. She looks happier than Elise about the win.]

[I rewatched the previous episodes. Sophia looked so nervous right before they announced Elise's results. More nervous than Elise herself.]

[Maybe we misjudged her. Perhaps she genuinely cares about Samuel Mitchell.]

Meanwhile, William Levine frowned at the screen. Why was Sophia receiving all this praise when Elise was the winner?

His phone buzzed with messages from Nathan in their WhatsApp group.

[Did Sophia have a change of heart? Or did she pay off the production team to improve her image? @williamlevine]

[But honestly, Elise's fashion design skills are incredible. Should I ask her to design something for me? @williamlevine]

Andrew retorted: [You're too bulky for anyone to design anything fitting. Don't make things difficult for Mrs. Levine.]

Nathan and Andrew began arguing while William ignored them.

William kept his eyes glued to the screen. The show was almost over.

Just as Elise prepared to give her winner's speech, a voice shouted from the audience. "Elise's winning design was plagiarized!"

The accusation echoed through the suddenly silent studio.

"I recognized it too! That design was stolen from Lovely's work! Lovely is an internationally famous fashion designer. That piece was published in a fashion magazine last year!"

"Yes! I knew it looked familiar! It's definitely plagiarized! I've been following Lovely since she won first prize in that international competition!"

The audience grew louder, more restless.

The host tried repeatedly to calm them, but failed.

"This isn't fair! Elise should be disqualified!"

"We can't accept this! Do over! Do over!"

Chaos erupted in the studio. Audience members began throwing bottles at Elise. Security rushed forward to prevent violence.

This was unprecedented in reality television history.

Yet viewership numbers skyrocketed during the incident.

[The production team better not cut the feed now.]

[Yes! If they go off air, we'll boycott Crestwood Channel's variety shows forever!]

[We demand answers! No behind-the-scenes manipulation!]

After quick consultation with executives, the production team decided to stay on air.

The host returned, attempting damage control. "To everyone here and watching at home, our management has made several decisions. First, we will remain on air with all of you as our witnesses. Second, we ask the studio audience to remain calm and maintain order. Your safety is our priority. Finally, we promise a fair investigation into this competition's outcome."

The audience quieted somewhat at the host's words.

The judges returned to their seats.

The head judge spoke firmly. "We have re-examined Elise Scott's design, comparing it to Lovely's 'Eternity' from last year. The designs are virtually identical. We therefore determine this to be plagiarism."

The crowd roared in vindication.
###Chapter 172

Sophia appeared completely stunned, though she certainly didn't look upset at all—not with dozens of cameras pointed directly at her.

Secretly, her scheming heart felt absolutely triumphant watching Elise suffer!

After tonight, Elise's reputation would be completely destroyed, and she would drag Stellar Group down with her too!

The more Sophia thought about it, the more delighted she became, struggling to contain her laughter.

Of course, the audience interpreted her twisted expression as genuine sorrow.

"Disqualify Elise! Recalculate the rankings!" someone shouted from the crowd.

Others quickly joined in, showing their support.

Livestream viewers were tearing Elise apart in the comments section.

[This is disgusting. Absolutely unforgivable! Elise plagiarized!]

[I can't believe I supported her. It makes me sick.]

[Now we see the truth—Sophia is far superior to Elise. I think Sophia and Samuel are truly in love.]

[Maybe Samuel was right to change his mind.]

Meanwhile, William was tapping his fingers rhythmically on his desk.

Having worked with him for years, Samuel Wilson could tell his boss was deadly serious.

When William got serious, things turned dangerous—the Mitchells really shouldn't have challenged him.

The WhatsApp group had gone silent too.

Even Nathan didn't know what to say, aware that one wrong word would incur William's wrath.

And the man certainly knew how to hold a grudge!

William dialed a number and commanded coolly, "Acquire Mitchell Corp."

"...Mitchell Corp, sir?" The voice on the other end sounded confused. "But there's no value there—they're barely hanging on..."

"They're an eyesore."

"Understood, Mr. Levine," the voice replied respectfully.

After hanging up, William rose to his feet, and Samuel Wilson knew he was going to rescue Elise!

Yet the situation in the studio was shifting...

Maron, the specially invited designer, suddenly spoke up. "Actually, Elise's design wasn't entirely plagiarized."

His words stunned the entire studio, while the live comments section froze for several seconds.

There was no way a designer of such international prestige would speak nonsense.

Wait, was he sleeping with Elise?!

When the comments resumed, they immediately exploded with outrage.

Another judge snapped at Maron, "How can you say that, Mr. Maron? Elise's design is identical to Lovely's... This isn't even inspiration—it's blatant plagiarism!"

"I'm not denying their designs are identical. What I'm saying is that Elise is Lovely," Maron said calmly. "Can you plagiarize your own work?"
###Chapter 173
Maron's declaration sent shockwaves through the entire studio.
Elise was actually Lovely, the internationally acclaimed design prodigy?!
Her work at the Sheeny Cup had completely overshadowed every other designer.
She had captured global attention with her groundbreaking collection.

Since the competition was held online, no one had ever seen Lovely's face.
She consistently refused all interviews and public appearances.
After her rise to fame, all her creations were featured in top fashion magazines.
They commanded astronomical prices in the market.
Critics hailed her as the most brilliant fashion designer of this generation.
Her achievements made her the pride of the entire continent.

Given her mysterious nature, numerous rumors circulated about Lovely.
Some claimed she was physically disabled.
Others insisted she was too unattractive to show her face.
There were even theories that Lovely was actually a design collective.
Despite all the speculation, Lovely never broke her silence.
The fashion world eventually accepted her enigmatic persona.

Meanwhile, Sophia had built her entire career on being the Sheeny Cup's continental runner-up.
She placed fifth worldwide in the same competition.
Sophia genuinely believed Lovely was insignificant.
She bought into all the rumors about Lovely's appearance.
In her mind, no one could possibly be both talented and beautiful.
She maintained this superiority complex for years.

Now Maron was announcing to the entire nation that Lovely was actually Elise.
Sophia's mind reeled with disbelief.
It couldn't be Elise!
If Elise were really Lovely, she would have bragged about it constantly!
Would she really have kept such an achievement secret?

Amanda stood equally stunned.
Everyone in fashion knew about Lovely's legendary status.
Even with her limited output, every piece Lovely created became an instant classic.
Celebrities and socialites desperately sought her custom designs.
None had ever succeeded in commissioning her work.
Now Maron claimed Elise was this elusive design genius?
The idea seemed utterly absurd.

A voice rang out from the front row of the audience.
"Why should we believe Elise is Lovely?"
The speaker demanded concrete evidence from Maron.

His challenge sparked a wave of skepticism throughout the studio.
The crowd grew increasingly agitated and vocal.
"Lovely has never shown her face!"
"Anyone could claim to be Lovely!"
"Why should we trust your word?"

Maron remained calm amidst the growing chaos.
"I was personally responsible for publishing Lovely's designs."
He revealed his unique connection to the mysterious designer.
"She only agreed to meet me just before returning to Zidonia."
"That was when I discovered the truth about her identity."
"I learned she was actually this stunning foreign beauty."

Even Maron admitted his own previous doubts about Lovely's appearance.
He had suspected she might be hiding something about her looks.
Now he stood before them, completely vindicated in his changed perspective.
###Chapter 174

Maron couldn't help but glance at Elise, who had remained perfectly composed from the very beginning.

He had been genuinely surprised to encounter her at the studio today. Years ago, he had done everything in his power to convince Elise to stay in Astonia. He could have propelled her to the very top of the international fashion scene. The nation's royalty had even reached out, specifically requesting Lovely's personal designs.

Yet she had refused, claiming she was choosing something far more important than fame and recognition.

Before returning to Zidonia, she had agreed to meet with Maron one last time to thank him for his years of unwavering support. She mentioned she might retire from fashion design entirely and focus on business instead. He had naturally pleaded with her not to abandon her craft, insisting he would never demand a yearly quota of designs from her. All he wanted was for her not to give up on her own dreams, because he could feel the burning passion she held for fashion design.

Fortunately, she had listened to his counsel. That was why, over the last few years, her breathtaking designs had occasionally surfaced in the market.

Even so, the audience remained deeply skeptical.

"Those are just empty words!"

Maron scowled. A public figure of his international stature should not have to endure such relentless, repeated questioning on behalf of another.

With a solemn expression and a voice that resonated with authority, he declared, "Elise is Lovely! I give you my personal guarantee on this, staking my authority and reputation as an editor of the world's most renowned fashion magazine and as a chief fashion designer!"

In reality, when a public figure of his caliber steps forward to vouch for someone, even putting their own hard-earned reputation on the line, it should be proof enough.

There was no rational reason to question his integrity. However, the audience members who had been bribed by Samuel were determined to swing public sentiment against Elise at any cost.

"I can prove my own identity," Elise stated bluntly at that moment, unwilling to let Maron face further harassment on her account.

This man had discovered her, mentored her, and become a true friend.

Elise had always been aloof. Her abusive family had left her paranoid and deeply distrustful of everyone. It was why she had refused to reveal her face as Lovely. This decision naturally put her at odds with media outlets from multiple nations, who almost succeeded in canceling her career entirely.

That was when Maron had stepped in. He took on the role of her referee, championing her designs until they were accepted and celebrated across the globe.

Her living conditions improved dramatically as a result. But just as her fashion career was reaching its pinnacle, she gave it all up and returned to Zidonia for Samuel's sake.

She had been convinced that it was rare to find someone who didn't despise her for her past, someone who could truly accept her. She told herself she must cherish him and learn to be grateful.

That was why she sacrificed her career, engaging in endless social drinking to attract sponsors and secure contracts, all to help Samuel pull Mitchell Corp back from the brink of collapse. There was a time when the alcohol seemed to drown her senses, leaving her utterly fatigued with design. She found no joy in it anymore, let alone any creative inspiration. If Maron had not continuously encouraged her and helped her rediscover her original spark, she would never have returned to fashion design at all.

Despite sacrificing so much for Samuel, she never told him, convinced he already knew.

The outcome was bitterly ironic. The man to whom she had given her heart was now doing everything in his power to discredit her and strip her of everything she had.

Pushing those turbulent emotions deep down, Elise turned to the host. "May I ask a crew member to bring me my phone from the dressing room?"

"Of course," the host nodded. A crew member swiftly retrieved Elise's phone.

With the phone in hand, she calmly tapped the screen and spoke clearly into the microphone. "Now, I would like everyone to check Lovely's official Twitter and Facebook pages for the most recent update."

Immediately, the live audience whipped out their phones. The livestream viewers simultaneously logged into their respective social media accounts to check.

There it was, displayed on both of Lovely's official social network pages for all to see: [Greetings. My name is Elise Scott!]
###Chapter 175

The truth was undeniable now.

With the confirmation from an internationally acclaimed authority and Elise's own social media presence, she was indeed Lovely!

The studio audience, who had been murmuring discontent about the competition results moments earlier, fell completely silent.

Viewers watching the live broadcast were equally stunned, the comment section freezing for several seconds.

Meanwhile, in William's WhatsApp group, Nathan couldn't contain his excitement.

[Unbelievable! Elise is Lovely?! This is incredible! The pride of our continent! The prodigious fashion designer!!!]

[She's as brilliant as she is beautiful! Who wouldn't be captivated by her?!]

[What did you eat to develop such sharp instincts, William?!]

[And Samuel must be regretting everything right now. Ethan, no offense to your brother-in-law, but he totally deserves this!]

Across town, Samuel was indeed seething with rage.

He had spent years with Elise without ever discovering she was Lovely—when they met, she was just a street performer! If not for his financial support, she would have starved overseas!

He suddenly remembered—after they started dating, Elise had mentioned promising career opportunities abroad.

But that was precisely when Mitchell Corp faced a crisis, and his parents pressured him to return to Crestwood. He naturally dismissed Elise's career prospects, assuming whatever she pursued would barely cover her living expenses at best.

He could sense her reluctance, but for him, Mitchell Corp took priority.

He had to admit he'd been genuinely infatuated with her back then, and keeping her by his side had been a selfish decision.

He never expected she would go to such lengths for him, becoming Mitchell Corp's public relations officer and securing numerous sponsors for the company.

Initially, he felt touched by her sacrifice and intended to repay her... until Sophia entered the picture.

Sophia was Elise's complete opposite, always attuned to his feelings. She seemed to worship him unconditionally, constantly boosting his masculine ego.

In contrast, Elise would challenge Samuel's decisions during executive meetings, and even his own executives would side with her. In his own family company, he felt emasculated and stressed!

Naturally, he grew attached to Sophia given the stark contrast, and one night, they stumbled into intimacy after drinking too much.

Moreover, he had barely held Elise's hand even after their relationship was established for so long. Sophia, however, was willing to be with him secretly without any official commitment, and he naturally developed a soft spot for her.

Still, he couldn't bring himself to break up with Elise, since he had genuine feelings for her and was reluctant to let go. It wasn't until their wedding day fire, when he chose to save Sophia and their affair was exposed, that he had to end things with Elise.

But even after all that, a small part of him felt he owed Elise because she was undoubtedly the reason Mitchell Corp survived. He told himself he could still look after her after their breakup... if she hadn't moved on to another man in the blink of an eye!

He always believed Elise loved only him and could never accept another man, which was why he harassed her relentlessly, determined to make her regret her disloyalty! He grew even more bitter when Elise threatened to destroy his financial foundation, vowing to retaliate by discrediting her and making her regret her very existence!

Yet, all he received was one humiliating slap after another!

To make matters worse, this latest attempt only served to reveal to the entire nation that she possessed another identity—one of unshakeable fame and integrity!

He had spent fortunes bribing production crew members and preparing scripts designed to win sympathy for Sophia and Amanda's on-screen personas—including a victory speech for when Sophia claimed first place, since they planned to have Elise disqualified as soon as initial results were announced.

Yet, despite pushing Sophia to the summit through all their efforts, Elise only turned around and delivered a lethal blow that shattered their entire scheme to pieces!
###Chapter 176

Everyone in the fashion industry who understood the inner workings knew the truth.

Samuel’s scheme was destined for a spectacular downfall.

He kept facing humiliation, repeatedly outmaneuvered by Elise.

All he could do now was clench his fists, his furious gaze fixed on Elise through the screen.

The host’s voice cut through the tension. "We are all now convinced that Elise is indeed Lovely. We are deeply honored she chose to participate in this competition. However, the rules explicitly state that all designs must be created on the spot. Any form of imitation is strictly forbidden. The judges and production team have reached a unanimous decision. Even if Elise used one of her own previous designs, it still constitutes plagiarism. Therefore, she will be disqualified from the competition—"

"But I did create a new dress," Elise stated firmly, interrupting him.

The host was visibly startled.

Elise repeated her claim, her voice clear and resonant. "I designed and constructed a dress here, live. Someone switched it."

The studio erupted into chaos once more.

This was a reality show, not a scripted drama! The endless twists were overwhelming.

Yet, the audience was electrified, their excited cheers filling the air. The live comment section exploded.

[My heart can't handle this suspense!]

[This is a total bloodbath! The drama is unreal!]

[I'm literally on the edge of my seat! I just wanted to watch a show, not have a heart attack!]

[This is the most authentic reality TV has ever been! No way this is scripted! Go, Elise, go!]

The host quickly regained his composure after a moment's pause. "Ms. Scott, are you asserting that you designed and made a dress, but Amanda ended up wearing a different one?"

Amanda’s face drained of color at the sudden accusation.

Initially, Amanda had been furious that Elise revealed another of her hidden talents. She had always been intensely jealous of women more accomplished than herself. Elise’s remarkable success at Mitchell Corp only highlighted Amanda’s own inadequacies.

Amanda had despised Elise for a long time. When Sophia expressed interest in Samuel, Amanda eagerly facilitated their connection.

Sophia had been Amanda’s sycophantic friend for years, constantly showering her with praise and favors. Amanda relished that adoration.

A woman like Elise, who treated her with mere neutrality, deserved to be discarded by William.

But now, panic set in as she was publicly implicated in a plot against Elise.

She wanted to defend herself, but didn't know how. Lashing out on camera would only make things worse.

Elise, however, was brutally direct. "If my suspicion is correct, my backstage assistant, Nana, was the one who switched the dresses."

Nana was standing near the backstage entrance, on standby.

Every camera in the studio instantly swiveled toward her.

Instinctively, she threw her hands up to cover her face—a clear, guilty reaction captured for all to see.
###Chapter 177

Every gaze in the room locked onto Nana.

She began to shake uncontrollably, panic seizing her.

Standing exposed before the host, the studio audience, and the rolling cameras, Nana had no choice.

She confessed that she had taken the gown and brought it back to the studio.

The truth was out—Elise had been deliberately framed.

Yet, everyone sensed Nana wasn't the true mastermind. She had no real motive against Elise.

All attention shifted to Amanda, still on stage. Her face turned ghostly pale.

She had no defense.

The host snarled, "Why did you switch the gown Elise made? Why set her up?"

Amanda pressed her lips together, silent.

No matter her connections, her career in showbiz was over. Exposed under the bright lights, in front of the entire nation.

She shook her head, instinctively turning to Sophia, her eyes pleading.

She begged Sophia to cover for her, to offer some explanation, to save her from this isolation.

But Sophia was more enraged than panicked.

She felt Amanda's desperate look, but she wouldn't let her own image and integrity be tarnished.

She had planned for this. If things went south, Amanda would be the fall girl.

So, Sophia's expression twisted into pained disbelief. "How could you, Amanda?" she cried. "I know you didn't want my sister to win for Mitchell Corp, but to frame her? You were my best friend! I can't believe you'd do this..."

She broke down, covering her face with her hands, seemingly devastated.

Amanda stared, utterly stunned.

How could Sophia push all the blame onto her? Why did she have to take the fall?

It was Sophia's plan to frame Elise from the start! And now she was being blamed?

Amanda couldn't accept it. But before she could protest, Sophia cut her off.

Turning to the judges and audience, Sophia sobbed miserably, "I never imagined it would come to this. It's so regrettable... My sister on one side, my best friend on the other. I forfeit my place. The winner is my sister, Elise. She deserves this victory. I'm content with that."

With those words, Sophia turned and hurried off stage, dragging a shell-shocked Amanda with her.

Sophia was clever. She ended the drama here, emerging unscathed, appearing like a saint.

Elise watched, a cold sneer forming as she saw Amanda's face contort in anguish.

Amanda never saw the betrayal coming.

She had trusted Sophia completely. Sophia always listened to her. She had even helped Sophia get close to her own brother.

She probably never anticipated... that Sophia would be so ungrateful.

Elise spoke up firmly. "A competition is a competition. I don't want concessions. I won't accept a win that wasn't earned. I don't want anyone saying I won by luck."

Her words were a clear jab at Sophia's ulterior motive—trying to appear gracious in defeat when she never stood a chance against Elise.

The live comments exploded.
###Chapter 178

[Why does Sophia suddenly seem so insincere?!]
[That's what you get from an illegitimate child. Such poor sportsmanship.]
[Sophia is leaving a terrible taste in my mouth. Just accept your defeat gracefully!]

Meanwhile, Elise spoke with unwavering conviction. "Honorable judges, I sincerely hope this panel will deliver a completely impartial evaluation to properly conclude this competition, entirely free from outside influences. I firmly believe every viewer in this studio and watching live deserves a fair and justified outcome."

The studio audience was buzzing with anticipation to see Elise's design. After all, every creation from Lovely had been extraordinary, and witnessing another would be a visual feast!

Several audience members began chanting loudly. "We want to see Elise's work!"
"Justice for Elise!"
"Continue the competition! We demand a legitimate result!"

As the chants grew into a roaring crescendo, the live stream comments overwhelmingly supported Elise.

William had been unusually quiet in the WhatsApp group, seemingly stunned by the dramatic revelation.

Nathan texted the group: [Holy crap! This is insane! Amanda actually went that far?! Ethan, buddy, your woman is definitely not the simple type, huh? You better watch your back. @ethanparker]

Andrew then commented: [I suspect Samuel was the mastermind behind this. Amanda was just the pawn.]
[Yeah, probably. Amanda has the curves but not the cunning... Not to insult her, but she really isn't on your level. @ethanparker]

Andrew immediately scolded Nathan. [That's inappropriate.]
[Fine, my bad. Anyway, forget Amanda - Sophia genuinely disgusts me! I've never met a more two-faced woman! Do men actually fall for her type? Is Samuel actually blind?!]

While Nathan vented his frustration, Andrew joked: [Would William even stand a chance if Samuel had any brains?]

The two friends continued their lively banter while the two 'husbands' remained silent observers.

Back in the studio, after urgent on-site discussions, the producers decided the competition would proceed.

Sophia's expression darkened imperceptibly. If they had canceled the competition, she could have claimed she voluntarily forfeited.

But they insisted on continuing, and she knew her chances of winning had evaporated now that everyone knew Elise was Lovely.

Forced to comply with public sentiment, she flashed a sweet, apologetic smile. "I merely thought my sister would win first place regardless, since she already defeated me in the Sheeny Cup. I didn't want to waste everyone's precious time... But since everyone feels so strongly, I'm also eager to see my sister's magnificent design!"

She appeared utterly innocent and harmless - a wasted talent for not pursuing acting, given her natural flair for performance.

Her demeanor starkly contrasted with Amanda, who stood beside her scowling, completely unable to conceal her bitter disappointment.

Elise then announced, "I wish to model the gown myself."

The judges and production team immediately approved her request.

After the recent sabotage attempt, no one would trust the person who just tried to frame her with modeling duties.

"I need five minutes to adjust the measurements," Elise added.

"Of course," the head judge responded supportively.

"Thank you," Elise replied gracefully. She carried the gown back into the dressing room to change.

Soon, a vision of breathtaking beauty emerged on stage, leaving the entire audience spellbound by her majestic presence.
###Chapter 179

The entire studio fell into a breathless silence.

It was as if any sound would shatter the breathtaking vision before them.

No creation could have better captured the grace of the woman on stage.

Even without her striking beauty and captivating presence, the blue diamond gown alone commanded awe.

Did the dress enhance her radiance, or did she elevate the gown's elegance?

The distinction blurred into irrelevance.

They were one—a perfect, inseparable fusion of artistry and allure.

The live chat exploded with fervent admiration.

[I'm literally holding my breath!]

[Elise is divine! Why isn't she in movies? I need to see her on screen every day!]

[Someone please make that gown a permanent part of her!]

In William's WhatsApp group, Nathan was spamming excited emojis.

[If I see this again, I might get dangerous ideas. @williamlevine]

[Will, you should really keep that temptress hidden. @williamlevine]

[Just remembered Samuel again. What a fool! Ethan, next time you see your brother-in-law, ask him how he could be so blind! @ethanparker]

Nathan's scorn was palpable even through the screen.

Back in the studio, the audience gradually emerged from their stunned silence.

Thunderous applause erupted, celebrating both Elise and the masterpiece she wore.

Beside her, Sophia's smile twisted bitterly.

Not only had she failed to ruin Elise, but she had handed her another triumphant moment.

She could already envision Elise's rising fame and her own impending humiliation.

Since childhood, Sophia had never accepted Elise's superiority.

Whenever Elise outshone her, Sophia would orchestrate her downfall.

At eighteen, Elise earned a place at a prestigious university while Sophia settled for a mediocre college.

So Sophia drugged Elise and left her in a stranger's bed to create a scandal.

But the plan backfired—the stranger never touched Elise, and Sophia never got the incriminating video.

Fate, however, seemed to smirk at Sophia when Elise turned up pregnant.

Sophia leaked news of Elise's hospital visit for an abortion, shredding her reputation.

The scandal forced the university to expel Elise, her baby was stillborn, and her family exiled her abroad.

The satisfaction had been exquisite—which made Elise's glorious return all the more unbearable.

Now, undeniable and devastating, Elise's design was universally acclaimed.

The clear winner, triumphantly crushing Sophia into the dust.

As Elise accepted the winner's trophy once more, the crowd eagerly awaited her words.

She remained composed, as always.

Whether receiving praise, facing false accusations, or basking in adoration, she wore the same unshakable calm.

"First," she began, "I want to thank everyone for your support. My gratitude to the judges and the production team for clearing my name, allowing me to claim this trophy with integrity. This honor is as much yours as it is mine. Thank you."

She bowed gracefully, her sincerity evident, her humility genuine.

The studio roared with applause.

"And finally," Elise added, her voice clear and sharp, "to certain individuals—remember, no wicked deed ever goes unpunished."

Beside her, Sophia and Amanda felt the sting of her words, forced to swallow their rising fury.

The livestream chat mirrored her triumph.

[Elise is flawless! I love her zero-tolerance for bullies! Two-faced snakes deserve everything they get!]

[I totally misjudged her before. I thought she was timid, but she's an absolute queen!]

[I worship Elise!]
###Chapter 180

[Cancel Amanda already. She doesn't deserve to be a celebrity.]

[Didn't she break multiple laws? The authorities should investigate and penalize her properly.]

The competition had finally concluded. What was scheduled as a two-hour program stretched into four, yet viewership numbers soared to unprecedented heights.

With the recording finished, everyone began exiting the studio.

Elise made her way backstage to thank Maron before departing, escorted by studio personnel.

However, a massive crowd of journalists clogged the exit, swarming Amanda and Sophia, trapping them.

"Amanda, why did you frame Elise? Were you afraid she would win and damage Mitchell Corp?"

"Don't you find such behavior utterly despicable?"

"Do you expect to face consequences for your actions?"

"Amanda, say something!"

The aggressive questions pushed Amanda to her breaking point. She lost control, shrieking, "Enough! All of you, stop!"

The journalists remained relentless, capturing her meltdown with flashing cameras.

"Move! I'm leaving! Get out of my way!" Amanda yelled through her tears.

Beside her, Sophia continued her act of false sympathy. "Calm down, Amanda. They're just doing their jobs—don't make this harder!"

"Sophia Mitchell!" Amanda had finally reached her limit. She had wanted to confront Sophia on stage earlier but held back to avoid escalating the situation.

Now, Sophia's two-faced performance only made Amanda appear more unhinged and arrogant.

Enraged, Amanda slapped Sophia hard across the face.

Tears instantly welled in Sophia's eyes. She stared at Amanda with wounded vulnerability. "Amanda…"

"Amanda! How could you hit someone? That's vile!"

"My god… I've never seen such barbaric behavior! Were you raised without manners?"

"Sophia was trying to help you, and you attack her? You're a monster…"

The journalists' verbal assault shattered what remained of Amanda's composure.

Nothing she said mattered. No one would believe her.

She just wanted to escape, to flee, but she couldn't. Her manager, assistant, and bodyguards were nowhere to be seen.

It felt like the world had conspired to humiliate her indefinitely.

Then, a man emerged, pushing through the throng of journalists. He reached Amanda's side and swiftly escorted her away.

The journalists were momentarily stunned by his sudden appearance. By the time they recovered, Amanda was already inside a sleek black luxury car speeding off.

Nearby, Elise witnessed the entire scene. She wasn't mistaken—it was Ethan…

Ultimately, he was the one who betrayed Elizabeth's unwavering loyalty for him.
###Chapter 181

Sophia was taken aback that Ethan would help Amanda escape.

She knew they weren't particularly close. They hadn't even gone on a single date since announcing their relationship.

Yet when Amanda was desperate, he came to her rescue without caring about appearances. He was like Prince Charming arriving on a white horse to save his beloved!

Any woman would envy such devotion and wish for a boyfriend like that.

Sophia felt a twinge of jealousy herself, especially since Samuel hadn't shown up at all.

At least she had only lost the competition—her reputation remained intact.

With Amanda gone, she could still win the journalists' sympathy. But just as she prepared to speak, the reporters abandoned her completely. Sophia watched helplessly as they flocked toward Elise like moths to flame. Gritting her teeth, she left reluctantly.

"Ms. Scott, how do you feel about being framed? Isn't this treatment completely unacceptable?"

"Aren't you disappointed? Amanda is your ex-boyfriend's sister, and this is how she treats you?!"

"Ms. Scott, why did you hide your identity as Lovely? Is there a special reason?"

"What's your opinion of Sophia's design?!"

Despite the journalists' agitated questions, Elise remained calm. "I don't think I'm the one suffering here. The truth is out, and it doesn't affect me personally. As for disappointment? I believe there are others feeling much more disappointed than I am."

She was clearly referring to Amanda.

After being betrayed by her so-called sister, Amanda probably wanted to murder Sophia.

"I have personal reasons for keeping my Lovely identity secret, so I can't share them. Please understand." Elise attempted to leave.

The journalists persistently blocked her path.

"Ms. Scott, don't you think Samuel might be connected to Amanda's actions?"

"He repeatedly tried to sabotage you despite your past relationship. Don't you have anything to say to him?"

"We heard you gave up an opportunity to study abroad back then. Do you think it was worth it now?"

Fatigue finally showed on Elise's face.

It was already late, and she had been overworking herself for this competition. All she wanted was for it to end so she could get some proper sleep.

"Samuel and I are over. There's nothing between us anymore." Elise gathered her strength to respond. "If he chooses to lie and deceive, that's his responsibility and has nothing to do with me." She added firmly, "Please let me through. It's late, and I need to rest."

"Ms. Scott—"

The journalists continued their badgering until a helicopter suddenly descended from overhead. Its powerful rotors kicked up a fearsome gust as it bellowed through the night air.

Everyone turned to watch as it steadily landed. The door slid open.

A man in a black suit emerged, his upright figure radiating nobility and elegance.

His presence alone inspired both fear and awe, though his face remained concealed by a luxurious black umbrella.

His commanding presence and towering frame suggested both exceptional looks and extraordinary status.

He strode purposefully toward the crowd of journalists, his bodyguards following closely behind.

The six bodyguards were equally imposing, their intimidating presence keeping everyone in line, afraid to offend such an important figure.

With his security clearing a path, the man easily approached Elise and extended his hand toward her.

His hand was elegant with fair skin and chiseled fingers. The expensive watch on his wrist worth a king's ransom complemented his every movement, projecting indescribable nobility.

Elise certainly recognized who it was—she just never expected William would come personally to retrieve her.

He always seemed to appear whenever she found herself in trouble and needed help!
###Chapter 182

Elise was completely taken by surprise, her heart fluttering wildly. After a moment's hesitation, she finally placed her hand in William's waiting palm.

His perfectly sculpted lips curved into a satisfied smile as he guided her through the parting crowd.

All eyes followed their departure, captivated by the scene unfolding before them.

Elise still wore that breathtaking diamond-blue gown that made her look divine. Standing beside the impeccably dressed man, they appeared like a scene from a romantic movie—a wealthy husband reclaiming his runaway wife. Except this was no screenplay; this was reality unfolding in real time.

The sight was so mesmerizing that journalists couldn't stop capturing the moment, their camera flashes creating a continuous strobe effect. Even as the helicopter ascended, they kept shooting, unsatisfied with the footage they'd already obtained.

Inside the helicopter, Elise watched through the window as the ground grew increasingly distant.

She desperately wanted to tell William that such dramatic measures weren't necessary to retrieve her.

He could have simply followed Ethan's approach—using a car!

Heaven only knew how much media coverage this spectacle would generate.
