Despite her practical concerns, she couldn't deny the thrill coursing through her veins. Her heart raced with undeniable excitement.

"Congratulations, Ms. Scott," William's magnetic voice murmured close to her ear.

Elise turned to face his handsome features, finding him almost unbearably attractive in that moment.

As she blinked, a beautiful bouquet of flowers suddenly appeared before her.

He certainly knew how to create magical surprises.

Accepting the flowers, she smiled warmly. "Thank you."

"Mr. Levine personally arranged those flowers himself. It took him all morning," Samuel chimed in from behind them.

Elise started, surprised to realize they weren't alone in the helicopter.

She glanced between the vibrant red roses and William, who looked more captivating than the flowers themselves.

Overcome by sudden impulse, she leaned forward and pressed a soft kiss against his cheek.

William's entire body went rigid with surprise. Elise immediately regretted her boldness as he stared intently at her, making her feel completely self-conscious.

She'd momentarily forgotten about Samuel's presence behind William... But in that moment, she'd been unable to resist the overwhelming urge to kiss him.

Biting her lip in silent embarrassment, she noticed William was also struggling to find words.

He swallowed hard before speaking coolly, "Samuel, have you completed your parachuting certification?"

Samuel remained speechless.

If it were possible, he would have grown wings and flown away right then and there!

Fortunately, the helicopter soon began its descent toward a private airfield. Elise frowned as she studied the scene outside.

"Are you traveling somewhere?" she asked, assuming William was heading for another business trip.

"Not me—we," William corrected sharply. "We're going to the Ayro Islands."

Elise was rendered speechless.

Now she understood why he'd brought a helicopter.

He'd been planning this all along.

"Ms. Scott, you promised me three months ago that you'd make time for me. You're not going back on your word, are you?"

Even if she tried to break her promise, he would simply bundle her onto the plane regardless.

"I didn't pack any luggage," she protested, making one final attempt to escape the situation.

"I've already packed everything for you, including your passport," he added smoothly.

It appeared nothing she said would change his plans!
###Chapter 183

Elizabeth stepped into her luxury SUV and departed the film location.

They had just finished shooting an intense night sequence, and she remained deeply immersed in her character's emotional state.

Slowly emerging from her acting trance, she leaned back against the plush leather seat and closed her eyes.

Her manager Linda sat nearby, engaged in quiet conversation with her assistant Penelope.

"Did you watch the Solo Show finale, Linda?" Penelope inquired.

"I caught some clips while heading to the set," Linda responded.

"Me too! That confrontation between Elise and Sophia was absolutely explosive!" Penelope exclaimed with genuine excitement. "The twists were more dramatic than any scripted series! News outlets are flooded with articles about it. It's more thrilling than any historical epic!"

Elizabeth's eyelids fluttered open.

She had nearly forgotten today was Elise's competition finale—her filming schedule had been too demanding to follow events.

Opening her eyes fully, Elizabeth asked, "Did Elise win?"

"Yes, and it was spectacular! I absolutely admire her now!" Penelope gushed enthusiastically before quickly adding, "Though you'll always be my favorite, Elizabeth!"

Such flattery.

Still, Elizabeth felt pleased hearing about Elise's victory.

If Sophia had won, who knows how that witch would have gloated!

Penelope continued excitedly, "You probably haven't seen the news yet, Elizabeth, but Amanda Mitchell tried to sabotage Elise during the competition tonight. Elise exposed her right on stage! You can't imagine how satisfying it was to watch! That poetic justice was absolutely magnificent!"

"Really?" Elizabeth naturally enjoyed gossip too, sitting up straighter at Penelope's revelation.

Though exhausted, Elizabeth couldn't resist checking her phone for updates, immediately finding numerous articles about the competition trending.

The more she read, the more delighted she became. She had to give credit to Elise.

Exactly what she'd expect from the woman her brother admired. Even Elizabeth herself felt like worshipping at Elise's feet!

Just then, another new article appeared.

[Knight in Shining Armor: Ethan Parker rescues girlfriend Amanda Mitchell amid scandal!]

Elizabeth's fingers hesitated momentarily before she finally tapped the headline.

The article contained extensive text, which Elizabeth skimmed without full attention.

Her focus remained on the striking photographs showing a commanding Ethan cutting through the crowd, shielding a sobbing Amanda beneath his protective embrace as he guided her to his vehicle.

Elizabeth scrolled down to read the comments.

[I don't like Amanda and don't think she deserves Ethan, but the man is simply incredible! Love him!]

[I envy her so much for having a boyfriend like Ethan. When will I find someone so handsome and chivalrous?]

Burning desire?!
How dare William say that!
And who exactly packed that scandalous bikini in her suitcase anyway?
Truthfully, it was the only swimsuit she could find after thoroughly searching the entire closet!
Elise vividly recalled Elizabeth being the one who gifted it to her, claiming it was from a sponsor after some celebrity event. Elizabeth had insisted that as a public figure, she couldn't wear anything so revealing or the paparazzi would have a field day if they captured photos.
Still, Elizabeth argued it was too beautiful to waste, pressing it into Elise's hands while complimenting her incredible figure that would do it justice.
Not wanting to reject Elizabeth's enthusiastic gift, Elise accepted it but never intended to actually wear it.
And now William had deliberately packed it as her only swimwear option!
"Did you instruct Charles to pack my luggage?" she demanded irritably.
"Would Charles have such exquisite taste?" William countered, his tone dripping with smugness.
He maintained his infuriating smile despite her glare. "You can swim, can't you? How about a race?"
"Absolutely not," Elise stated flatly—she wasn't foolish enough to play his games.
"I'll give you a half-lap head start," William offered. "Winner gets any wish from the loser."
Elise measured the pool's length with her eyes.
Half a lap? His arrogance knew no bounds!
Feigning reluctant acceptance, she asked, "No backing out?"
"Of course not," he confirmed.
"Any wish at all?"
"Naturally," William replied with complete confidence.
"Fine," Elise agreed, sounding thoroughly put upon.
They both moved to the pool's edge.
"Alright, I start first. You only begin when I reach the midpoint," Elise clarified once more.
"Agreed," William nodded.
With that, Elise dove into the water, starting with a leisurely breaststroke.
She moved quite slowly, and William's amused smile was unmistakable.
When Elise reached the halfway point, he was still casually stretching before his tall frame launched gracefully into the water, covering significant distance before switching to freestyle.
He was nearly at the opposite end when he glanced up, only to find her already waiting there, seemingly catching her breath.
William was genuinely astonished—there was no way she could have beaten him with that breaststroke. Unless...
Swimming quickly to join her, he remarked, "You're full of surprises, Ms. Scott."
Elise simply shrugged. "I merely forgot to mention that I would've been a national-level swimmer if my father hadn't sent me overseas."
While William pressed his lips together, she beamed triumphantly. "All's fair in love and war!"
With that, she executed an elegant dive back into the pool, speeding away.
"Cheeky minx," William murmured with affectionate amusement before giving chase.
They played in the water a while longer before heading to breakfast together.
Taking a bite of his croissant, William said, "You won, Ms. Scott. As promised, I'll grant your wish."
Elise was momentarily startled—she hadn't actually considered asking anything from William, having grown accustomed to achieving everything through her own efforts.
Nevertheless, she found herself saying, "Never lie to me."
As William held her gaze intently, she spoke with crystal clarity. "Be direct with me—no matter what it is, even if you fall out of love with me. Just don't deceive me, ever, under any circumstances."
If Samuel had been honest when he developed feelings for Sophia, their relationship wouldn't have ended so disastrously.
Similarly, she wouldn't have been so thoroughly wounded, and she absolutely refused to repeat that experience!
William swallowed hard before responding, "You have my word."
Unaware of the emotions William was suppressing, Elise then asked, "So? What's planned for today?"
"Nothing particular," William replied.
Elise frowned—was this entire trip just about sleeping and scrolling through phones in a different location?
Quite the 'zoomer' vacation indeed...
Then William flashed a mischievous grin. "No need to rush, Ms. Scott. Rest today, because I'm concerned your stamina might not handle the excitement I have planned."
Elise remained thoroughly skeptical.
###Chapter 194

Elise finally understood what William meant by 'fun' on the third day of their vacation.

He scooped her out of bed before dawn, while she was still deep in sleep.

Carrying her gently, he brought her to a luxurious yacht already waiting, and they set sail into the open ocean.

When she finally blinked awake, they were surrounded by nothing but water, the yacht rocking softly on the gentle swells.

"Here," William said, descending from the deck. "Let me put some sunscreen on you."

"It's fine," Elise murmured, snuggling deeper into the plush beach chair. "I'm staying right here... Oh!"

She pursed her lips as William firmly held her in place. His long, skilled fingers began applying sunscreen to her back, spreading the lotion in slow, deliberate circles.

He covered every inch meticulously. She'd doubt anyone who claimed he didn't have OCD.

She considered protesting a few times, but resistance was clearly pointless. Better to save her energy and just relax into his touch.

"Should I do the front too?" William offered, a playful glint in his eyes.

"No!" she snapped, snatching the tube from him to handle that part herself.

William chuckled softly, pulling out another tube for himself.

Once he noticed Elise was finished, he turned to her. "Ms. Scott? Would you return the favor and help me with mine?"

Elise sighed but took the tube, applying the sunscreen with brisk, efficient strokes.

Her movements gradually slowed as she noticed his skin. It was a healthy, sun-kissed wheat color, neither too pale nor overly tanned.

Like his chest, the muscles of his back were sharply defined. She could feel the firmness of his skin and the powerful contours beneath her fingers.

"How does it feel?" William asked, as if reading her thoughts.

His sudden question startled her, making her heart skip a beat.

She merely hummed noncommittally. "Hmm."

Despite her lack of verbal response, he added, "Yours too."

Elise rolled her eyes. She hadn't asked for his opinion, and he'd offered it unsolicited.

Finally, they were both properly protected from the sun. William looked at her expectantly. "Do you know how to ride a jet ski?"

"No," Elise replied honestly.

"Really?" he asked, his tone laced with healthy skepticism after her previous 'average swimmer' deception.

"No, I really don't," she insisted. "This is my first time at the ocean since I became an adult."

William did a double take, then gave her a sympathetic pat on the head. "I'll bring you out more often from now on."

Elise sighed inwardly. She wasn't sure she wanted more of these exhilarating experiences. They were dangerously addictive.

Eventually, she found herself clinging to William on a jet ski, whooping with pure joy as they sliced through the crystal-clear water.

She couldn't remember the last time she'd felt so free, so unburdened. Her life had been nothing but weighty responsibilities for far too long.

Later...

"Want to try surfing?"

"No."

"I can teach you."

After that...

"Want to try scuba diving?"

"No."

"It's alright. I'll be right beside you the whole time."

And when they emerged from the underwater world...

"Would you like-"

"No! Absolutely not!"

"A drink?" William finished, holding out a chilled bottle of vitamin water.

Elise pursed her lips, a pang of guilt hitting her for misjudging his intentions.

That was when he casually added, "Anyway, we're skydiving from a helicopter tomorrow." Elise stared at him, wondering why she'd ever felt guilty in the first place.
###Chapter 195

Night had fallen when they finally returned to the villa. Elise was completely drained, her body refusing to cooperate any further.

Yet the very next morning, William was already pulling her from bed. He guided her sleep-heavy form toward the waiting helicopter.

The flight started peacefully. They circled the entire island, taking in the breathtaking views from above. It was serene, almost meditative.

Then William produced the parachute.

Could she say no?!

"We're jumping together. I'll have you secured to me the entire time," William assured her.

That wasn't her concern! She was terrified of heights!

Ignoring her silent protests, William fastened their harnesses together. He led her firmly to the helicopter's open door.

"William..." she whispered, her voice barely audible over the rotor blades.

"Yes?"

Elise squeezed her eyes shut, surrendering to the inevitable. "If I die today, I'll haunt your dreams forever."

"That's exactly what I'm counting on." His voice, deep and magnetic, vibrated against her ear. Then they were falling.

She kept her eyes tightly closed. The wind roared past, a deafening torrent. The sudden, gut-wrenching sensation of freefall nearly stopped her heart.

"Open your eyes, Ms. Scott!" William shouted over the rushing air.

"No!"

"Be good now!"

"Absolutely not!"

"Or I'll kiss you right here!"

Elise gritted her teeth. His threats always found a way to disarm her.

Reluctantly, she forced her eyes open. The vast expanse of cerulean sky and fluffy, cotton-like clouds filled her vision.

"Look down," William instructed softly.

She obeyed. The endless azure ocean stretched beneath them. The island appeared as a beautiful, intricate jewel dotted with curious shapes.

The sheer, breathtaking majesty of nature washed over her. All her fear melted away in an instant. A profound sense of peace began mending her weary spirit.

William deployed the parachute. Their descent slowed dramatically. Elise finally allowed herself to relax.

She unclenched her fingers from his clothing. Spreading her arms wide, she embraced the sensation of floating through the immense sky. It was pure, unadulterated freedom.

"Extreme sports are remarkably effective for stress relief," William commented. "I used to do this frequently."

Elise turned her head to look at him. "Were you under a lot of stress?"

"Being the heir to Levine Holdings is no simple task." He offered a faint smile.

She nodded, understanding his meaning without needing further explanation.

He continued, "My grandfather fell ill the year my parents died. The grief of burying his own children was too much for him. My aunt and uncle couldn't stabilize the company. My cousin was barred from taking any executive role."

"The entire weight of our family's legacy landed squarely on my shoulders. I was just twenty. I was grappling with my own profound loss while being forced to lead Levine Holdings and maintain order. When the pressure became unbearable, I'd escape for a while. I'd engage in extreme sports like this. I'm living proof of its effectiveness."

So that was his intention in bringing her here. He wanted her to forget all her troubles. He wanted her to find complete release.

It wasn't merely about being playful.

Elise's heart pounded fiercely in her chest. She was overwhelmed by his thoughtfulness, by how deeply he cared for her.

On the fifth day of their trip, they went shopping.

The island boasted several luxury shopping malls. One housed the largest outlet for high-end brands.

Compared to extreme sports, Elise felt confident in her shopping prowess. Once again, she had underestimated William.

His capacity for extravagance far exceeded her imagination!

In every store they entered, he would simply flash his credit card. He instructed the attendants to pack anything she so much as glanced at. He never inquired about prices. He never asked if she actually desired the items.

"I didn't say anything!" Elise protested.

"I like it. That's reason enough," William replied smoothly.

"Aren't you worried about going bankrupt?" she challenged.

"Don't underestimate your boyfriend's financial capabilities," he stated with utter seriousness. "This is mere pocket change. You have nothing to concern yourself with, Ms. Scott."

"What, by your definition, isn't pocket change then?!" she huffed in disbelief. Buying the entire mall?

"Well..." William considered her question carefully. "The moment you sign our marriage certificate."
###Chapter 196

Elise frowned as William continued solemnly, "And when that happens, half my fortune will be yours."

Her heart skipped a beat.

They had only known each other for six months.

Their relationship had been official for just four months.

And he was already talking about marriage?

No, he had probably planned their entire future together already!

"Let's go," he said, taking her hand and leading her casually to the next boutique.

Worried that William might purchase the entire shopping district, she quickly said, "I'm exhausted. Let's sit down over there or grab something cold."

"Alright," he agreed, guiding her toward a refreshment stand.

Meanwhile, all their purchases were being handled by hotel staff, coming and going in continuous streams.

Elise wanted ice cream, but as she contemplated flavors, William told the attendant, "One of each flavor, please."

The attendant, accustomed to wealthy tourists on Ayro Island, remained professionally composed.

Before the attendant could proceed, Elise intervened. "Just one chocolate sundae, please. I can't eat that much, and excessive cold food isn't healthy."

The attendant glanced at William for confirmation.

After receiving a nod, the attendant began preparing the order.

Elise suddenly remembered something. "You didn't order anything."

"Weren't you just saying too much cold food is unhealthy?" William countered.

Elise frowned—since when did he follow her advice so readily?

The attendant soon presented Elise with a sundae cone.

She took a delicate bite.

The creamy texture melted luxuriously on her tongue.

It was perfectly sweet without being overwhelming.

"Is it good?" William asked.

"Yes, delicious," she replied.

As she prepared for another bite, William suddenly leaned in and took a taste from the same spot.

Elise pursed her lips in surprise.

She watched as he calmly savored the bite. "It's really good."

"Why don't you get your own?" she asked.

"Too much cold food isn't healthy," he repeated.

Elise felt speechless. If only he would always listen this well!

She stared at the spot where he had bitten and took another taste herself.

The temptation was simply too strong.

Before she could enjoy another bite, William leaned in again.

"William," she protested, clearly annoyed.

"Too much cold food isn't healthy," he maintained.

"Then stop eating mine!" Elise snapped.

"No, I need to eat more so you'll eat less."

Elise was rendered speechless again.

Could she take back what she said about healthy eating?

They continued sharing the ice cream, taking alternating bites.

Snap!

The sudden shutter sound made them both turn.

A man with a professional camera stood before them.

Noticing Elise's displeasure, he quickly explained, "Hello! I'm a street photographer. I was passing by and saw you two sharing that sundae. The scene was so beautiful I couldn't resist capturing it. I'll delete it if you object, but I'd love to send it to you—it's truly a wonderful photo."

"Send it to me," William said, appearing completely unbothered.

Elise was surprised—since when did William not mind unauthorized photographs?
###Chapter 197

The street photographer transferred the photo to William via Bluetooth. William stared at his phone screen after receiving it, completely captivated.

How perfect was this picture that he couldn't look away?

Elise hadn't been interested initially, but curiosity was getting the better of her.

Finally, she couldn't resist asking, "Let me see."

William turned his attention from his phone and handed it to her.

She looked at the screen to see William taking a bite of her ice cream sundae. His profile was flawless, every feature perfectly sculpted.

What surprised her was that she was captured staring at William's face. She had thought she was just watching her sundae, worried he might finish it all.

But the undeniable truth was in her eyes. They held a soft, sentimental gaze as she looked at William.

She suddenly realized this would be difficult to explain.

"Finished looking?" William asked.

"Yes," Elise replied, trying to sound casual as she returned his phone.

He chuckled softly. "Let's go."

"Where?" she asked.

"Bungee jumping."

Elise was speechless. Couldn't they just have a relaxing day for once?

This was their sixth and final day on Ayro Island. They would be returning to Crestwood early tomorrow morning.

Elise had been reluctant to come here initially. Now she found herself equally reluctant to leave.

It had been years since she felt such genuine affection for a place.

Whether it was her homes in Zidonia or rented apartments abroad, those were merely places to stay.

But Ayro Island held truly beautiful memories for her. This place genuinely allowed her to forget her troubles.

However, William seemed to have vanished today.

On previous days, he would always burst into her room before she woke up, scoop her into his arms, and carry her out of the villa.

So where had he gone today?

Eventually, she gave in and called him.

He answered, saying he had to handle some business matters. He promised to return before dinner.

So she was being ignored for work.

And yet...

Fine, work was important!

Besides, she enjoyed having some downtime. At least she didn't have to worry about William dragging her off to some potentially dangerous activity.

She lounged around the villa, alternating between eating and sleeping. She even went for a swim in the private pool.

But William still hadn't returned as darkness fell. She wondered if he had forgotten about her and was having dinner with business partners instead.

She hesitated for a moment but ultimately didn't call him. She didn't want to disturb him during important meetings. Instead, she stayed in the living room, watching television and occasionally checking her phone, killing time while waiting for him.

He called at nine o'clock. Elise's first thought was that he would say he wasn't coming back for dinner.

But though she braced herself for disappointment, his familiar voice said, "Come to the window, Ms. Scott."

Elise frowned as she walked to the window with her phone.

As soon as she reached it, endless fireworks shot into the sky. They burst in brilliant colors, illuminating the night.

Any woman would be swept off her feet by such a romantic sight, and Elise was no exception.

She stared outside at the dazzling display painting the sky. It definitely struck a chord deep within her heart.

"Would you please come outside, Ms. Scott?" William's voice came through the phone again.

Elise did as he asked. When she stepped outside, she found the beach had been exquisitely decorated. Flashing colored lights were everywhere, shining brightly against the night sky.

The villa they stayed in was connected to land by a long wooden bridge. As she walked toward it, lights on both sides illuminated her path, creating a grand atmosphere.

Elise eventually stopped at the beach and stared at the dining table before her.

It was adorned with candles and roses. Glasses of red wine and expensive steaks were already prepared.

Beneath it, more colored lights were arranged in the shape of a perfect heart.
###Chapter 198

His deep, magnetic voice resonated behind her. “Ms. Scott.”

Elise’s heart stuttered.

She hadn’t noticed his approach, and a flicker of apprehension kept her from turning.

Her pulse was racing, a frantic drumbeat against her ribs.

She took a deliberate breath, willing her heart to slow, before finally facing him.

He stood there, bathed in the twilight, a vision in his tailored tuxedo. A massive bouquet of crimson roses rested in his arms, the scene so perfect it felt surreal.

Her carefully regained composure shattered instantly. Her heart hammered wildly against her chest.

“My apologies for the wait.” His smile was devastating, a work of art that stole the air from her lungs.

Elise dropped her gaze, a faint smile touching her lips. “So, this entire day was for my surprise? I thought you were in business meetings.”

“Who says this isn’t business?” William chuckled, the sound low and intimate. “Haven’t I been diligently negotiating an acquisition?”

She let out a soft, incredulous laugh. An acquisition? Was that how he saw her?

He gently took her hand, his fingers lacing with hers, and guided her to the elegantly set table.

The tropical night on Ayro Island was cool, a gentle sea breeze caressing their skin as they sat. It was utterly relaxing.

As they dined, Elise marveled at this side of him. She had never pegged William Levine as a man capable of such profound romance.

She had assumed their trip would conclude with a simple, straightforward departure. This… this was a storybook ending.

A memory she knew would be etched into her soul forever. A night where her heartbeat refused to settle.

Then, the poignant notes of a violin drifted from the shore.

Elise watched, mesmerized, as William set down his cutlery. He moved around the table, his posture elegant, and extended his hand to her. “A dance, Ms. Scott?”

Her breath hitched. Slowly, she placed her palm in his.

They began to sway, a slow, intimate movement synced to the violin’s melody. Their breaths mingled, each soft exhale audible in the space between them.

“Has this trip met your expectations, Ms. Scott?” William’s voice broke the delicate, charged silence.

“It has,” she whispered, nodding slightly.

“But it hasn’t met mine,” he stated bluntly.

Her brow furrowed. “What? Is there something else you wanted to do?” They had experienced so much already. She felt she had given everything, played her part completely.

Then he leaned close, his warm breath ghosting her ear as he whispered, “There’s still you.”

Elise fell silent. That single word rendered her speechless, stirring a deep, quiet longing.

Their gentle swaying ceased. Her fingers tightened their grip on the fabric of his tuxedo jacket.

His gaze was intense, tracing the lines of her face—flawless even without makeup. It drifted over her features before finally settling, with heavy intent, on her parted, scarlet lips.

“May I, Ms. Scott?” he asked, his voice a husky murmur.

Nervousness gripped her. Could she? Would she instinctively push him away?

And yet…

She rose onto her toes, closing the small distance between them. Her soft lips met his in a tentative, decisive kiss.

Both their hearts skipped a beat in unison. Elise closed her eyes, surrendering to the moment.

Her tongue tentatively sought entry, a shy exploration that met his.

She wanted this. She needed to try. Perhaps William held the key to helping her forget the painful past she was desperate to leave behind.

Or perhaps he could be the one to help her finally make peace with its complicated, messy weight.
###Chapter 199

The night was exquisite, warm, and filled with tenderness.

Two shadows merged into one under the moonlight, their lips meeting in a passionate embrace.

Even William needed a moment to collect himself.

For what felt like an eternity, he let Elise explore his mouth with her lips and tongue, her movements hesitant and clearly inexperienced.

He swallowed hard, his large hands slowly rising to pull her delicate, warm frame firmly against his.

Then he leaned down, capturing her mouth with even greater intensity.

In that instant, everything else faded into insignificance. Their minds and bodies became one, completely lost in each other.

Elise's knees began to buckle, actually growing weak from William's kiss.

His powerful arms immediately lifted her from the beach as if she weighed nothing, and before she could process what was happening, he had placed her entire body on the dining table.

He leaned in again, his lips claiming hers relentlessly, leaving her no room to breathe.

The night seemed to grow darker as William swept everything else off the table, pressing her soft, petite form flat against the surface. The breathtaking woman now lay alone on the wide table, her devilish curves and thoroughly flushed cheeks after their kissing creating an irresistible allure.

Desire burned in William's eyes, igniting into a fierce conflagration that threatened to consume her completely...

Between adults, a single spark can set everything ablaze. Elise was definitely nervous, her breathing becoming rapid and shallow, her chest rising and falling dramatically.

That moment only made William even more captivated. He thought to himself that she had no idea how tempting that look made her to a man!

How difficult it was to restrain that overwhelming impulse.

William began to lean toward her gradually, moving inch by inch.

Elise pressed her lips together, acutely aware of his raging desire.

She couldn't help clenching her fists, suddenly remembering the nightmare she had experienced at eighteen.

A flash of panic appeared in her eyes, but just as William reached her, she chose to close them.

Her knuckles tightened even more, yet she made no move to push him away.

As if she was accepting him.

Or trying to...

Nevertheless, a long time passed.

What she anticipated somehow didn't happen, and the pressure above her disappeared.

When she opened her eyes again, she saw William had already jumped into the ocean.

His tall figure showed no hesitation as he dove into the water, creating violent splashes and waves.

Once submerged, he began swimming in frantic circles as if possessed.

Naturally, he was forcing himself to calm down completely.

Watching him, Elise felt a strange quiver in her chest.

She had no idea what would become of their relationship if they had actually gone through with it.

Maybe she would have accepted it—or forced herself to accept it—telling herself repeatedly that William was the man destined to spend the rest of her life with.

Even so, when she saw William earnestly suppressing his desire for her sake, she breathed a great sigh of relief.

She actually wasn't as brave or fearless as she thought. She had never truly escaped her trauma, let alone opened her heart enough to fully accept an intimate relationship. She was uncertain whether she would regret being with William, but she knew for sure her mind would have been burdened.

Nevertheless, William didn't force her to accept it.

As for the days she spent abroad with Samuel, even though he respected her, they never went this far or lost control like this. He was a restrained young man, and while there was genuine affection in their relationship, it was bound by strict morals. They were never intimate, and once they returned to Zidonia, it became impossible with Sophia getting involved.

Getting off the dining table, Elise walked toward the ocean.

William had been in there for so long she was worried he might drown.
###Chapter 200

Elise stood on the shore, calling out, "William!"

William broke through the water's surface moments later. He had already discarded his tuxedo jacket. The white dress shirt beneath was completely soaked, clinging tightly to his sculpted physique.

"Can you come out now?" Elise asked.

"Almost there," he responded.

"Take my hand," she offered, extending her arm to help him.

However, he declined, "You shouldn't touch me right now, Ms. Scott."

Elise's fingers twitched slightly, understanding his implication.

"I can manage on my own," he added, stepping out of the ocean.

The night air carried a chill, and William's body visibly shivered as he reached the beach.

A cool breeze swept over him, causing him to sneeze. He looked thoroughly disheveled in that moment.

Elise couldn't suppress a giggle at his appearance.

He shot her a grumpy look. "I wonder who's responsible for this?"

"Come on. Let's get you inside," she said, her mood noticeably brighter.

She followed him back toward the ocean villa.

He was heading straight to his room for a warm bath when she called out, "William."

He turned to find her smiling warmly. "Thank you."

She felt genuinely moved by everything he had done for her that evening.

William returned her smile, though it seemed weary. "I'd actually prefer if you scolded me right now, Ms. Scott..."

Elise frowned. Did he have some hidden masochistic tendencies?

"Something like calling me an idiot or worse," he finished before turning away and closing his bedroom door.

A faint smile touched Elise's lips.

She understood exactly what William meant.

Perhaps, just perhaps... things might work out better next time!

Returning to her own room, she took a quick shower before slipping into bed.

The trip had exhausted her so much that she usually fell asleep instantly these past few days.

But tonight, for some unknown reason, sleep completely eluded her. She found herself staring at the starry sky through her window.

She bit her lip unconsciously. Though some time had passed, her lips still felt slightly swollen.

The memory of that fiery, passionate kiss with William made her cheeks burn with embarrassment.

Yet when she recalled it, she felt no disgust whatsoever.

Their previous two kisses had been mere gentle brushes compared to this one...

Elise buried herself under the blankets - she really shouldn't dwell on this any longer!

The next morning, Elise emerged from her room with dark circles under her eyes. Her lack of sleep was clearly showing.

William sat across from her during breakfast. He too sported dark circles and looked equally tired.

"Ms. Scott, could it be that you were..." he began, deliberately letting his words trail off.

"No, I just napped too much yesterday and couldn't sleep at night," she denied quickly.

He grinned knowingly. "How did you know what I was going to say? Why so quick to deny it?"

Elise felt speechless and seriously considered cursing at him.

"We'll be leaving for the airport after breakfast," William murmured, his tone betraying his reluctance.

Elise actually shared his sentiment.

They were still the same people, but she couldn't shake the feeling that everything would change once they returned home.
###Chapter 201

Elise and William finally boarded their flight back to Crestwood, touching down at 3 PM local time.

Neither had slept properly the night before, so they spent the entire journey dozing on the private jet.

By the time they disembarked, both felt completely refreshed.

William's black Maybach was already waiting beside the aircraft when they landed.

Samuel Wilson stood respectfully beside the vehicle, ready to receive them.

He opened the car door smoothly. "Welcome back, Mr. and Mrs. Levine."

Mrs. Levine...

Elise felt her cheeks warm, but she followed William into the car anyway.

Samuel took the front passenger seat and began reviewing William's schedule. "Sir, since we postponed three dinner meetings during your seven-day trip, I've rescheduled them for tomorrow evening and the following two nights as instructed."

"Excellent," William replied.

Samuel continued, "Also, for tonight's Levine Holdings 60th anniversary gala, we're expecting many important guests. Your grandfather specifically asked me to remind you to be punctual. Your custom-tailored suit is ready, and you can change immediately. Mrs. Levine's evening gown has also been prepared according to your specifications."

Elise looked surprised. "I'm attending too?"

"Of course," William said smoothly. "As the future Mrs. Levine, it's only natural you should be there to oversee the event."

Elise felt another blush coming on—Samuel was right there listening!

"Mrs. Levine," Samuel said respectfully, turning to her, "I've scheduled a meeting tomorrow at 10 AM to finalize Stellar Group's partnership with Crestwood Channel. They've agreed to most terms already. Here's the draft agreement for your review. If everything meets your approval, you can proceed to their offices tomorrow to sign."

Elise was genuinely impressed when Samuel produced the contract and handed it to her.

Even after being away for a week, her work hadn't fallen behind at all.

As she scanned the document, she couldn't help asking, "Samuel, exactly how much does your boss pay you monthly?"

Before Samuel could respond, William answered for him. "You probably can't afford to steal him away."

Elise shot him an annoyed look—he was so arrogant!

"The only way to get Samuel working for you is by marrying me," William added with a smirk. "Then everything I have becomes yours."

He never missed an opportunity to flirt, did he?

They proceeded to the luxury boutique at Aurora Plaza.

William had his hair styled before changing into his custom suit, then left immediately—his busy schedule was evident.

Yet he'd still made time to take her abroad. The thought warmed Elise's heart.

She then changed into the gown William had selected for her. As always, he knew exactly what suited her. Every dress he chose was stunning.

After more than three hours of makeup, hairstyling, and preparations, she entered Robert's car and headed to the venue.

Levine Holdings' 60th anniversary gala was being held in a magnificent three-story banquet hall.

Henry Levine's 70th birthday had been spectacular enough, but this anniversary celebration promised to be even more impressive.

While business partners had been invited to the birthday, it had been a relatively conservative affair. This anniversary, however, was being celebrated with full grandeur.

Elise felt a flutter of nervousness as she entered the imposing venue.

There seemed to be even more guests than before. For a moment, she wondered if every notable figure in Crestwood's business community had gathered here.

As she stepped into the main hall, she saw everyone holding champagne glasses and engaging in lively conversation.
###Chapter 202

Minor disruptions in the grand ballroom rarely captured widespread attention. Elise Scott's entrance caused only fleeting glances. The glittering crowd quickly returned to their conversations. Their focus remained on networking with Crestwood's elite.

Elise moved toward a secluded corner. She scanned the room while remaining unobtrusive. Some faces looked familiar from business events. Many were strangers, likely not from Crestwood. This confirmed the event's national significance.

Her primary concern remained locating James. Separation had intensified her maternal longing. Her eyes continuously searched the shifting crowd.

An unfamiliar male voice interrupted her thoughts. "Elise Scott?"

She turned toward the speaker. His face sparked vague recognition, but specifics eluded her.

He noticed her hesitation and scowled. "Brandon Miller." Annoyance sharpened his tone. He hadn't anticipated being forgotten.

Elise remained perplexed. She mentally reviewed business contacts without success. Her meticulous nature ensured she remembered every professional acquaintance. This man didn't fit any category.

Brandon observed her genuine confusion with growing irritation. "Elise Scott! Most men appreciate subtle games, but this is excessive!"

She considered offering polite greetings despite her uncertainty. Social decorum mattered at William Levine's events. But respect required reciprocity.

Without another word, she turned to leave.

"Elise Scott!" Brandon's surprise at her dismissal made him grasp her arm.

"Release me immediately unless you want a public scene," she warned coldly.

He reluctantly let go, remembering his precarious position. Being included among Levine Holdings employees was privilege enough. "We met at Scott Manor. Your father Benjamin introduced us."

Recognition finally dawned. This was the man her father had tried to matchmake. Her thorough rejection had erased him from memory.

"Satisfied?" Brandon sneered.

"Unimportant people don't occupy my memory," she retorted without hesitation.

"You're incredibly arrogant!" he snapped.

"My character is none of your concern, Mr. Miller." She attempted departure again.

"Elise Scott! Do you understand who you're dismissing?!" His voice rose, drawing nearby glances.

After their initial meeting, he'd repeatedly expressed interest to Benjamin Scott. Elise had ignored all overtures. William Levine's demanding standards had consumed his time recently.

Now that he'd finally found her, surrender wasn't an option.
###Chapter 203

Elise Scott stared coldly at Brandon Miller.

She viewed him as nothing more than a casual acquaintance and hadn't anticipated he'd be such a creep.

"Everyone attending Levine Holdings' anniversary gala tonight is either wealthy or influential!" Brandon declared smugly.

"And?" Elise countered.

"That means I'm superior to everyone else!" Brandon snapped, his voice rising with anger.

Elise smiled sardonically—this was genuinely amusing.

"Am I not here?" she challenged.

Brandon faltered momentarily, recognizing her mockery, then quickly tried to bolster his ego. "You're only here because of your connection to Scott Enterprise. Meanwhile, I received a personal invitation."

"In that case, do you see any member of the Scott family present?" Elise inquired.

It wasn't just the Scotts either—the Mitchells were conspicuously absent, and given their status in Crestwood, they certainly warranted invitations.

If they had been invited, they would have arrived early, not be missing entirely.

Yet no representatives from either prominent family could be spotted in the banquet hall. They definitely wouldn't be upstairs either, since most guests remained on the first floor where socializing was optimal.

Would those two social-climbing families really miss such a prime opportunity?

The only logical explanation was they hadn't been invited at all.

As for why?

Elise felt certain William had personally intervened.

The thought warmed her heart.

Meanwhile, Elise's observation made Brandon realize he hadn't seen any Scotts all evening.

He'd been circulating for quite some time and couldn't possibly have missed them.

Still, he refused to back down, continuing to manufacture excuses while demeaning Elise. "What are you so arrogant about?! You merely gained temporary fame from winning Solo Show. That's why Levine Holdings reluctantly invited you—once your fifteen minutes end, you're nothing!"

"You're quite the hypocrite, Mr. Miller." Elise sneered. "You boast incessantly as if it's your birthright, while accusing me of arrogance when I've said nothing. Have you never received validation? Are you truly that desperate for admiration?!"

"Elise Scott!!!"

"I don't have time to waste on you. Do as you please, Mr. Miller," Elise concluded coolly, stepping around him.

Brandon's face contorted with rage from her mockery, and he immediately moved to block her path again.

This time, Elise scowled, her patience clearly wearing thin.

Undeterred, Brandon continued his grandstanding. "Let me make this clear, Elise Scott—I'm an executive at Levine Holdings, and my position carries significant weight. If you possess any intelligence, you'd know how many Crestwood companies desperately seek partnerships with Levine Holdings, and they must show us respect to conduct business. A insignificant company like your Stellar Group must demonstrate proper deference if you want to establish yourself here!"

"You mean, show you deference," Elise laughed, genuinely amused now as she spotted a familiar figure approaching behind Brandon.

"We happen to know each other and have distant connections," Brandon reasoned smugly. "I'm not completely heartless—be nice and show me respect, and I can ensure you operate comfortably in Crestwood."

"And what exactly do you propose?" Elise asked, her smile widening as the man behind Brandon drew closer.

"Well, our initial meeting was pleasant. I've been too busy to schedule another date, which is my oversight. However, fate has reunited us today, so I suggest we start dating. If you prove suitable, I might even consider marriage," Brandon declared, as if bestowing a great honor.

Elise remained silent, maintaining her enigmatic smile as William Levine finally reached them.
###Chapter 204

Brandon could see the disdain in Elise's eyes. His expression darkened as he continued speaking. "You need to understand something, Elise. This current attention you're getting is only temporary. Your past remains problematic. Once the hype dies down, people will go back to looking down on you. You should be grateful that I'm willing to overlook your history."

"Mr. Miller, are you suggesting that if I date you, you'll use your influence to ensure my company faces no obstacles in Crestwood?" Elise asked directly.

A smug smile spread across Brandon's face. "Exactly. You're getting the better end of this arrangement if we start seeing each other-"

The man standing behind him finally spoke up. "Mr. Miller."

Brandon immediately turned to find Samuel Wilson standing there. His expression transformed instantly - from arrogant superiority to fawning obsequiousness.

"Oh, Mr. Wilson! What a pleasant surprise!" Brandon exclaimed, eagerly extending his hand.

Everyone at Levine Holdings knew Samuel served as William's personal assistant and essentially acted as his second-in-command.

Nobody dared disrespect him.

Samuel glanced briefly at Brandon's outstretched hand before pointedly ignoring it.

Brandon awkwardly withdrew his hand, his face flushing with embarrassment.

"Since when did you gain the authority to make decisions for Levine Holdings, Mr. Miller?" Samuel asked calmly, though his words carried clear mockery.

Panic immediately gripped Brandon. "This is all a misunderstanding, Mr. Wilson! I was just...you see, this woman was arranged to marry me through family connections. She was trying to ask for favors for her personal business. Of course I wouldn't abuse my position for a woman's sake. I merely needed to put her in her place..."

Samuel raised an eyebrow skeptically. "Are you certain she was the one seeking your help?"

"Oh, they're all like that. Women always try to use their looks to get special treatment-"

"Brandon Miller - she's not someone you get to disrespect," Samuel stated coldly, cutting him off mid-sentence.

Brandon stared in disbelief before quickly recovering. "Mr. Wilson, she's no saint. You know how complicated some people's personal lives can be. You shouldn't let yourself be deceived by her appearance alone..."

"I certainly know my place," Samuel replied bluntly.

Brandon looked even more confused now.

"Report to Finance tomorrow to collect any outstanding wages," Samuel said, clearly unwilling to waste more time on someone so insignificant. "You're dismissed from Levine Holdings effective immediately."

"What?!" Brandon thought he must have misheard. Then agitation took over. "You're just William Levine's assistant! You don't have the authority to fire anyone!"

"You're correct - I don't," Samuel acknowledged. "But you're still fired."

"What kind of nonsense is this?!" Brandon shouted.

"You can figure that out yourself," Samuel said coolly before signaling to the nearby security guards. "Escort him out."

Within moments, several imposing security guards stood menacingly before Brandon, forcing him to leave while he cut a pathetic figure.

Only after being marched out of the banquet hall did realization dawn on him.

Was Samuel implying that William himself had ordered his termination?

And that Elise was William's woman?!

His legs turned to jelly the moment this understanding struck him.

Back inside the banquet hall, Samuel was offering apologies. "Ms. Scott, I hope your evening wasn't ruined by such an insignificant person from our company."

Insignificant?

Elise smiled, understanding more clearly why William always kept Samuel by his side.

His use of "insignificant" provided such perfect contrast to Brandon's earlier smug arrogance!

It definitely earned her appreciation.

Her gaze then drifted upward to the second floor, where a familiar figure leaned casually against the handrail, smiling tenderly down at her before turning to disappear into the shadows.
###Chapter 205

Samuel Wilson was acting on William Levine's direct orders to assist Elise Scott.
William was occupied with other pressing matters, unable to handle the situation personally.
Still, he observed from a distance until everything was resolved.
Just as he departed, Elise blinked—the woman standing beside William turned her gaze toward her.
Her expression held neither animosity nor warmth, merely a casual, fleeting glance.
Yet, Elise's intuition prickled with an inexplicable tension.

The banquet hall grew increasingly vibrant, and at precisely 8 PM, the guest of honor made his entrance.
Henry Levine had narrowly survived the tragic loss of his son and daughter-in-law, but the ordeal left him paraplegic, confined to a wheelchair.
Elise suddenly recalled what William had shared during their trip to Ayro Island. He had spoken with detached calm about the family tragedy and the immense pressure that followed.
It must have been an unbearable burden for him then, and her heart ached at the thought.

Then she spotted William, impeccably dressed in a tailored suit, his presence commanding as he wheeled Henry into the elevator on the second floor.
They descended toward the central stage, bathed in brilliant floodlights.
Accompanying them were William's aunt and uncle, Nancy Levine and Ivan York.
James was notably absent, and Elise surmised it might be inappropriate for a child to attend such a formal celebration.
Elizabeth was also not with William and the others.

Elise glanced upward and found Elizabeth leaning against the handrail, champagne flute in hand, offering a subtle toast.
It was as if she sensed Elise seeking her out.
Though they had only recently met, Elise felt an inexplicable connection between them.
She returned Elizabeth's gesture, taking a delicate sip from her own glass.
Then, Elizabeth motioned for Elise to focus on the stage, perhaps concerned she might miss her cousin's defining moment.
Elizabeth had always idolized her older cousin, after all.

Onstage, William approached the podium while the rest of the Levine family remained in the shadows.
As he spoke in his resonant, captivating voice, the massive LED screen behind him chronicled Levine Holdings' sixty-year evolution.
It showcased their numerous accolades, remarkable achievements, and profound contributions to society.
The hall fell into a hushed reverence for thirty minutes—a masterful public relations display, yet every moment felt essential, holding the audience spellbound.

While the crowd marveled, captivated by the presentation, Elise quietly watched William at the podium.
This was her first time witnessing him in his professional element.
His tall stature, confident posture, handsome features, and solemn expression… Men truly appear most attractive when fully immersed in their work.

Once William concluded his address, thunderous applause erupted throughout the hall.
He then handed the microphone to Henry, who struggled to contain his proud smile.
Henry had always known his grandson was exceptional, but William had spent much of his youth abroad.
Henry had seen his academic records but never witnessed his business prowess firsthand.
Since William's return, Henry had observed his grandson's strategic brilliance up close, filling him with immense pride.

After expressing his gratitude, Henry declared, "Today marks Levine Holdings' 60th anniversary… I recall my father entrusting this company to me when I was forty. Thirty years have passed in the blink of an eye. Now at seventy, it is time for me to step down! I introduced William at my birthday celebration, but tonight, I formally announce that he will assume full management of Levine Holdings!"

The moment he made the declaration, celebratory crackers exploded overhead.
A breathtaking spectacle unfolded as endless streams of confetti showered the applauding crowd.
Amid the roaring applause, Henry continued, his voice trembling with exhilaration, "Lastly, I have one more crucial announcement to make!"

He was visibly shaking with overwhelming excitement!
###Chapter 206

A man like Henry, who had faced countless storms in his life, would never normally tremble with such visible excitement.

The guests were all captivated, watching as he spoke with unwavering determination. "My grandson will be formally engaged to Victoria Stuart, the heiress of Stuart Group!"

Every person in the room was stunned—this was undoubtedly a monumental announcement.

It was already assumed that William would inherit Levine Holdings, though no one expected the transition to happen so soon.

What truly shocked everyone was William's engagement to Victoria Stuart, the heiress to Rockville's most influential family!

Since William's return to Zidonia, numerous society heiresses had been vying for his attention, hoping for a chance to marry him... only to have their hopes dashed by this sudden development?

Meanwhile, Victoria was gracefully ascending the stage at Henry's warm invitation.

She wore an elegant pink gown that was both alluring and sophisticated. The tiny diamonds sewn into the fabric scattered brilliant light across the floor, making her fair skin appear to glow.

Few women could wear pink while maintaining such an air of natural superiority.

She walked confidently to stand beside William, who coincidentally wore a tailored suit with a pink shirt collar. At first glance, it seemed like a unique fashion choice reflecting his personality, a festive touch for the celebration.

But when Victoria stood beside him, the matching hues made them look like a perfectly coordinated couple.

The message became crystal clear to everyone present.

Though all the guests were wealthy and powerful in their own right, they couldn't help but feel both admiration and envy for this union.

The Levines and Stuarts were perfectly matched in wealth and status, while William and Victoria appeared to be heaven-sent partners.

It was disappointing for those who had hoped to secure William for themselves, but they could only offer congratulations since competing with the Stuarts was impossible—even though William was currently scowling.

More observant guests noticed Henry gripping William's hand tightly. Those unaware might interpret it as a grandfather's affectionate gesture toward his grandson.

Only Henry knew the real reason for his firm grasp!

Elise stood among the crowd, watching the dramatic scene unfold on stage.

How could she possibly describe what she was feeling? The emotions were too complex, too painful.

She vividly remembered William assuring her that nothing existed between him and Victoria.

So why this sudden engagement announcement?

Elise turned to leave.

Some people develop higher tolerance for emotional pain because they've experienced so much of it.

And Elise was accustomed to abandonment, deception, and being used as a pawn in others' games.

That's why she could maintain her composure no matter how devastating the circumstances.

Her heart had built impenetrable walls long ago.

However, William noticed her departure and forcefully shook off Henry's grip, rushing down from the stage.

The guests grew restless—they could all clearly see the fury in William's expression.

Could this engagement be one-sided?

All eyes turned toward Victoria at that moment.

After all, the Stuarts were nearly equal to the Levines in influence—some would argue the Stuarts had even greater political backing.

If Victoria expressed any dissatisfaction with the Levines' handling of the situation, no one in the Levine family would dare object. Yet she showed no agitation toward William's abrupt departure. Though disappointment and slight embarrassment flickered in her eyes, her impeccable upbringing kept a pleasant smile firmly in place.

She truly embodied grace and poise under pressure!
###Chapter 207

Alan's gaze from the stage was a silent command.

The black-clad bodyguards positioned throughout the hall immediately converged on William.

He had no opportunity to resist.

Strong hands seized his arms, forcing him away.

He watched Elise vanish from his sight.

His eyes burned with frustration.

His knuckles turned white from clenching his fists too tightly.

Elise exited the banquet hall with remarkable composure.

There was no panic in her movements.

She walked steadily, lifting the hem of her gown gracefully.

Her pace was measured, neither hurried nor slow.

She raised her hand in a casual gesture.

A sleek black luxury sedan pulled up before her outside the venue.

She had sent Robert home earlier, anticipating a late night.

The chauffeur stepped out and opened the rear door for her.

She slid inside without a backward glance.

Looking back would only invite disappointment.

Where hope died, disappointment couldn't flourish.

She leaned back against the plush leather seat.

Her eyes traced the glittering nightscape of Crestwood through the window.

A faint, ironic smile touched her lips.

Her instincts had been correct.

Everything felt different since her return to Crestwood.

Her smile faded as she noticed their route.

"This isn't the way to my apartment," she stated, her voice cool.

The chauffeur's response was crisp and clear. "No, Ms. Scott. Master Henry Levine instructed me to bring you to Levine Manor."

His tone was respectful, yet carried an undeniable edge of authority.

It brooked no argument.

Elise's fingers tightened instinctively.

She considered reaching for her phone to call for help.

The chauffeur anticipated her. "Master Levine means you no harm. However, resistance might… complicate matters."

His words were a cool, deliberate warning.

Elise's heart gave a sharp, nervous thud.

She weighed her options.

In Crestwood, the Levine family could make anyone disappear without a trace.

She wasn't willing to gamble with her life.

The car eventually glided through imposing wrought-iron gates.

Levine Manor sprawled before them, a testament to immense wealth.

The front yard was vast, more a private park than a garden.

Such opulence was staggering in a city where land was worth a fortune.

The car halted before the grand entrance.

A servant awaited her, ushering her inside.

They passed through a magnificent lobby, its scale intimidating.

They ascended a sweeping staircase to the second floor.

She was shown into a lavish study.

The servant gestured for her to wait. "Master Levine will be with you shortly, Ms. Scott."

"Very well," Elise replied, her voice even.

The servant departed, returning moments later with a tray.

It held a delicate porcelain teapot, a cup, and an assortment of fine confectionery.

Elise didn't refuse.

Formal banquets often left one starving.

She ate a little, the sweet pastries a small comfort.

She waited.

The clock on the mantelpiece ticked steadily towards 10 PM.

Finally, noise echoed from the main entrance below.

She rose from her chair, smoothing her gown.

The study door opened.

Henry Levine was wheeled inside by his butler, Charles.

Charles closed the door softly, leaving the three of them in the spacious room.

He assisted Henry into the high-backed chair behind the massive mahogany desk.

Charles then took his place, standing silently behind his master.

For a long, heavy moment, both men simply stared at her.

Their combined gaze was intense, unnerving.

Elise pressed her lips together.

Some individuals possessed a natural, commanding aura that was impossible to ignore.

"Please sit, Ms. Scott," Henry finally said, his voice breaking the prolonged silence.
###Chapter 208

Elise Scott settled into her seat gracefully. She met Henry Levine's gaze without hesitation. "Thank you for having me, Mr. Levine."

"I'm certain you understand why I've requested your presence here today." Henry's voice was direct and businesslike. "We're both people who value efficiency, so I'll be straightforward. I want you to end your relationship with my grandson."

"Relationships concern only the two people involved." Elise's response was equally direct. "I don't believe it's appropriate for family to interfere in personal matters."

She maintained her composure perfectly, showing no sign of intimidation despite the pressure Henry was projecting. Her posture remained elegant and confident.

The truth was, she could walk away from William. Their relationship hadn't reached the point where she would choose him over his family. Not after everything she'd been through.

Henry's public announcement of William's engagement to Victoria had essentially sealed their fate. That engagement was now set in stone.

Still, Elise preferred to end things properly with William herself, rather than having others dictate the terms. She had her pride and principles to maintain.

"I must say, I admire your attitude, Ms. Scott." Henry's tone shifted unexpectedly.

Elise blinked, surprised by the sudden change.

"I expected you to be more intimidated," he continued. "But your composure has proven me wrong."

He paused thoughtfully before continuing. "I'm aware of your past circumstances. I've also followed your recent accomplishments. After all, no ordinary woman could capture my grandson's heart."

His eyes studied her intently, but Elise met his gaze steadily. She didn't mistake his words for acceptance.

"However," Henry continued firmly, "not just anyone can become the wife of the Levine heir. There are certain requirements that must be met. Family background being one of them."

Elise raised an eyebrow. "Are you suggesting an alternative within your family?"

"Ethan Parker," Henry stated plainly.

"Marriage isn't something you choose like picking from a menu," Elise responded succinctly.

"It's not your character that makes you unsuitable for William," Henry insisted. "It's your birth. Any relationship without family blessing is doomed to fail. I believe you're intelligent enough to recognize when to cut your losses."

Elise pressed her lips together.

Now she understood where William got his stubborn, domineering nature. The apple certainly didn't fall far from the tree.

"I won't fight you about ending things with William," she said calmly. "My past has taught me well about the pain of not being accepted. I won't make that mistake again."

She leaned forward slightly. "However, you don't get to unilaterally decide how William and I end our relationship. I want to hear from him directly. I believe I deserve that much courtesy."

Henry studied her determined expression. This was exactly how she had ended things with Samuel - cleanly and decisively, leaving no loose ends.

But...

"What if I refuse?" Henry challenged.

Elise frowned slightly. How much clearer did she need to be?

She was already agreeing to break up with William. She simply wanted to do it properly.

Even though this was their first meeting, she hadn't pegged Henry as completely unreasonable.

Yet he stated clearly, "I don't want an amicable breakup. I want you to leave him of your own accord, without any explanation from him."

Elise's expression cooled. "Then we have nothing more to discuss. We've reached an impasse, and I wouldn't want to waste any more of your valuable time."

A sharp glint appeared in Henry's eyes. "Perhaps we should discuss terms instead."

Elise watched him warily as he continued.

"My first offer: name your price. Any amount you desire. I understand you've accumulated considerable wealth as a renowned fashion designer overseas, but one never complains about having too much money."

He paused for effect before continuing. "My second offer: Scott Enterprises. Your mother built that company from nothing, and the Scotts took it as their own. I can return it to you immediately with a single word."

"Third: Mitchell Corp. You poured your blood and sweat into that company, yet Samuel and his family showed no gratitude, instead trying to destroy you at every turn. Your desire for revenge is justified, and I can make them lose everything overnight."

"Choose one."
###Chapter 209

Elise remained silent, deep in thought.

William waited patiently. Businessmen understood the value of time in negotiations.

She didn't take long to respond. "Your offers are certainly appealing, Mr. Levine. However, I'm capable of earning my own money. Accepting something for nothing feels wrong, regardless of the amount. As for the Mitchells and the Scotts, I'm confident they can't defeat me. Their downfall is inevitable."

Henry sipped his tea, his expression unreadable.

He slowly placed the cup down. "I can give you all three."

Meaning she wouldn't have to choose.

"I've made myself clear—I don't want any of them," Elise stated firmly.

"Have you considered that I could permanently withhold what I've offered and ensure you lose even more?" Henry's voice turned cold, the atmosphere chilling instantly.

"You want me to leave William voluntarily because you're afraid I won't break up with him," Elise exposed his intentions. "But why would I fear your threats when I have William as my advantage?"

Henry scowled, surprised she saw through him.

"So you've chosen to challenge me?" he growled.

"I'm too young and insignificant to challenge you, Mr. Levine," Elise replied. "All I seek is fairness—"

"I'll give you fairness!" Henry roared, cutting her off. "You will leave William, whether willingly or by force!"

Elise's gaze turned icy.

Meanwhile, Elizabeth was rushing back to Levine Manor.

The shock from Levine Holdings' 60th anniversary celebration had finally worn off.

She'd been worried the moment she spotted Victoria at the banquet.

That woman was no saint. Elizabeth refused to believe anyone could be that perfect!

Victoria's ability to fool so many people proved she was more dangerous than she appeared.

Unsurprisingly, she'd orchestrated the engagement announcement with William!

It was utterly despicable!

Elizabeth's heart broke watching Elise quietly slip away through the crowd downstairs.

She understood too well the pain of being hurt by someone you love. She was certain William knew nothing about being engaged to Victoria.

After years of Victoria hovering around him, both abroad and locally, William would have fallen for her already if he had any feelings. The sudden announcement was clearly forced, and even Elizabeth could see William's fury when they pulled that stunt!

As she prepared to follow Elise, Levine bodyguards stopped her, stating no one could leave before the banquet ended.

Elizabeth gritted her teeth but maintained composure—she knew better than to cause a scene at such an important event.

She called Elise, but Elise immediately hung up—understandable. If she were in Elise's position, she wouldn't want to talk either.

Elizabeth hadn't even told her best friend Samantha about her own heartbreaking rejection by Ethan.

When you're truly hurting, words become impossible.

So Elizabeth endured the remaining banquet, distracted and anxious to leave early to comfort Elise. Even if Elise didn't want to talk, Elizabeth could offer a shoulder to cry on.

It felt like forever before Henry finally departed, and about half the bodyguards left the hall, allowing Elizabeth to slip out.

Just as she reached her car, Ethan called from behind, "Elizabeth!"

He sounded urgent, clearly having run after her.

Elizabeth turned sharply, lips pressed together.

"If my instincts are correct, your grandfather has taken Elise back to Levine Manor," Ethan said quickly.

"How do you know?" Elizabeth asked.
###Chapter 210
'That cunning old fox! What is he doing to Elise?!'
'He could have handed her over to traffickers and no one would ever know!'
The more Elizabeth thought about it, the more terrified she became. She quickly jumped into her car, determined to rescue Elise from Levine Manor.
"Wait."
Ethan grabbed her wrist, but she violently shook him off.
The rejection was instant, creating a tense silence between them.
Still, Ethan spoke calmly. "When you see Elise, tell her to question everything your grandfather says. She shouldn't make any rash decisions. Tell her to wait for William to explain everything."
"What are you talking about? Was William keeping secrets from her?" Elizabeth asked, her intuition sharp as ever.
It wasn't that she lacked intelligence—she simply preferred not to apply it to things like academics.
But Ethan offered no further explanation. "Just stay with Elise for a few days. These things hurt deeply, so try to comfort her. I'll reschedule your filming dates so you don't need to worry about the set."
Elizabeth didn't press for details. It was pointless—Ethan never revealed anything he didn't want to share.
Her priority was getting to Elise quickly.
During the drive, Elizabeth urged her driver to speed. When the car finally stopped outside the manor, she rushed straight to her grandfather's second-floor study.
Henry always conducted important conversations in his study.
The moment Elizabeth opened the door, she saw Elise sitting inside, looking small and vulnerable.
"Where are your manners? You're an adult now!" Henry scolded as Elizabeth barged in, though his tone held no real anger.
It was merely a perfunctory reprimand from an elder about a younger person's abrupt behavior.
Elizabeth immediately moved to shield Elise. "You're much older, Grandfather, yet you'd bully a young woman. People will laugh if they find out."
Elise felt a surge of emotion watching Elizabeth defend her so fiercely.
She was surprised Elizabeth had come directly for her. She had ended their call earlier specifically to avoid involving Elizabeth further.
"As I said, I'm far too old to wrestle with youngsters like you. I was simply discussing business with Ms. Scott. Isn't that right?" Henry said righteously.
"Hmm," Elise murmured, offering no real cooperation.
Henry didn't seem bothered.
But Elizabeth wasn't about to trust her grandfather—every word from a businessman needed careful scrutiny.
Whatever the truth, her first priority was getting Elise out of there.
"Are you finished? I'm taking Elise home if you're done," Elizabeth stated firmly.
"We are." Henry nodded.
"Alright, let's go." Elizabeth took Elise's hand, ready to leave immediately.
"Elizabeth," Henry called out suddenly.
When she turned, he told her, "You're not a child anymore. You've been wandering aimlessly for years. It's time to come home and assist with the family business."
"We had an agreement," Elizabeth reminded him.
"Businessmen are never gentlemen."
Elizabeth said nothing.
But that was precisely why she despised businesspeople!
###Chapter 211

Elise didn't waste time arguing with her grandfather.

She had lived in that house long enough to know how his circular arguments drained the life from her.

Grabbing Elizabeth's hand, she pulled her out of Levine Manor and into the waiting car that would take them back to Elise's apartment.

The entire ride was filled with Elizabeth's silence, so profound she might as well have been invisible.

Elizabeth had never been good at reading people—if she were, she wouldn't have been so thoroughly broken by her own failed relationship.

She couldn't decipher Elise's impassive expression. Was she genuinely heartbroken, or was she truly as composed as she appeared?

But once they arrived home, Elise moved through her evening routine with practiced ease—showering, changing, preparing for bed.

It was as if the evening's events never happened, as if she hadn't been betrayed, traumatized, or confronted by Elizabeth's grandfather.

Unable to bear it any longer, Elizabeth dropped to her knees beside the bed. "Just cry, Elise! You'll feel better—I've done it countless times myself. Keeping all that pain inside will destroy you."

Elise turned to meet Elizabeth's worried gaze.

A faint smile touched her lips.

It wasn't that she didn't want to cry, but tears changed nothing. The situation would remain exactly the same.

After everything she'd endured, she'd become numb to suffering. No tragedy, no matter how devastating, could break through that barrier.

Today was no exception.

Elizabeth grew more agitated seeing Elise's calm demeanor. "Don't smile like that! It'll only hurt more later!"

"I'm fine, Elizabeth," Elise responded evenly, her voice steady and clear.

Her eyes reflected genuine tranquility, with no hidden pain beneath the surface—as if she truly didn't care.

But Elizabeth knew better. Even if Elise didn't love William, no one could remain unaffected by such deception.

What horrors had Elise experienced to make her heart so resilient?

Years after Ethan first shattered Elizabeth's heart, the pain still haunted her!

"Please don't worry about me," Elise added softly.

A terrible thought struck Elizabeth. "Are you keeping your distance because I'm a Levine?"

"No." Elise's smile warmed slightly—she genuinely didn't feel that way, even if her opinion of the Levine family had plummeted after tonight.

Elizabeth was different, though Elise couldn't explain why she trusted her so instinctively.

Perhaps after encountering so many villains in her life, not every person she met would be another one!

She refused to believe fate could be that cruel.

"God, Elise—I don't even know why I care about you so much," Elizabeth confessed earnestly. "But I promise you this: even if you and William become enemies, you'll always be my best friend!"

"I believe you." Elise nodded. "I won't hold your family name against you either. I've learned to accept things as they come. Nothing hurts when you truly embrace what life gives you."

Elizabeth stared in disbelief while Elise continued calmly, "It's getting late. You should rest—don't you have filming tomorrow? I don't want to keep you up."

Elise's rationality was heartbreaking!

"Ethan rescheduled my scenes," Elizabeth revealed. "He told me to stay with you tonight. He also had some messages for you."
###Chapter 212

Elise was stunned. "What did he say?"

Elizabeth repeated Ethan's message. "He told me to warn you not to trust anything my grandfather says. Don't make any rash decisions. And you should wait for William to explain everything to you."

Elise pressed her lips together.

So Ethan knew everything. That explained why he had been so helpful all these years.

She had honestly believed he was just being kind.

Still, Elizabeth couldn't resist defending William.

"Listen, Elise," Elizabeth said urgently. "My brother would never fall for Victoria Stuart. The elders must have arranged this engagement without his knowledge. If William knew about it, he would never have brought you to the banquet hall. Even if he doesn't love you, he wouldn't humiliate himself like that or create unnecessary trouble. You have to trust William!"

Elise responded calmly, "Elizabeth, what do you think will happen now that your grandfather has publicly announced William's engagement to Victoria? Even if William refuses to cooperate?"

Elizabeth was speechless. She hadn't considered that far ahead. All she cared about was that William was being forced into this.

Elise continued reasoning with her. "What-ifs don't matter when the outcome can't be changed."

Elizabeth felt confused. Since when was Elise the logical one here?

But Elizabeth argued anyway, blinded by her devotion to her brother. "No, Elise... What I mean is you should still have faith in William. I agree my grandfather made the first move to trap him, but William never accepts defeat. He'll find a way to break off the engagement with Victoria. Please don't give up on him?"

Elise didn't want to crush Elizabeth's hopes completely.

The Stuarts ruled Rockville and ranked among the top five most powerful dynasties in Zidonia. They had a reputation to maintain. They wouldn't easily annul an engagement once it was publicly announced.

In fact, the announcement had already pushed them past the point of no return.

"I'm not giving up on him," Elise said.

Before Elizabeth could smile, Elise added, "All good things must come to an end."

Elizabeth was speechless.

Was there really no chance to turn this around?

Elise clearly had feelings for William, even if she hid them well. Why else would she tolerate William's advances, especially after being betrayed by a scoundrel like Samuel?

Did people in love really end things so simply?

Elizabeth suddenly remembered what Ethan said—that he had anticipated what Elise would believe, and what she would ultimately decide.

How did that man know something Elizabeth didn't?

"Elizabeth, no matter what happens between William and me, our friendship won't change," Elise assured her.

Elizabeth pressed her lips together.

She wasn't worried about their friendship being affected... She just thought it was such a shame that Elise and William were splitting up like this!

There was so much she wanted to say, but Elizabeth ultimately remained silent. She decided it was better to wait for William to properly explain things to Elise.

When Elizabeth woke the next morning, Elise was already gone.

Elizabeth had struggled to fall asleep until very late.

She didn't know if Elise had actually slept, though she had remained motionless all night.

Still, she wasn't surprised to find Elise gone when she checked the clock and saw it was already 9 AM.

A workaholic like Elise would be working even if the world was ending.

After washing up, Elizabeth went to the living room and saw breakfast prepared on the dining table. A note beside it read: [Everything is sugar-free and low fat. You have nothing to worry about.]

Tears welled in Elizabeth's eyes.

Elise had still made breakfast for her despite everything?

Elizabeth's heart ached for Elise. She couldn't help wanting to dedicate her heart and soul to her!
###Chapter 213

Elise sat at the dining table, picking up her fork to eat breakfast. She quickly sent a text to Elizabeth. [Heading back to work today?]

Elizabeth's reply came almost instantly. [Yes. I have a meeting at Crestwood Channel to discuss a potential collaboration.]

[Okay, I won't disturb you at the office then, sweetie. Breakfast was delicious, love you.]

[Love you too.]

With that, Elise closed WhatsApp and opened her news app.

As expected, William and Victoria's engagement dominated every headline. Various media outlets had published photos of them standing together during the announcement, surrounded by cheering guests.

The comment section was even more absurd.

[A perfect match! Most importantly, a union between the Levines and the Stuarts is absolutely ideal!]

[I heard they grew up together. Maybe James Levine's real mother is actually Victoria?]

[Now that I look closely, James does resemble Victoria quite a bit.]
