[Beautiful people always have beautiful children.]

[Wait, who are the Stuarts exactly?]

That last comment had numerous replies.

[Just google Charles Stuart and you'll understand.]

[Let me put it this way - there are only a handful of people in this country who have received the Victoria Medal, and Charles Stuart is one of them.]

[Half the Stuarts are politicians, the other half are business leaders. And they're all top players in their fields - isn't that just frustrating?]

The more Elise read, the more irritated she became. She soon closed the app entirely.

She knew the Stuarts would use their influence to claim William!

After finishing breakfast, Elise called Linda to rearrange her schedule. She wanted to film all her remaining scenes as quickly as possible. Since Elizabeth had already left for work, Elise didn't need to stay with her anymore.

Still, she wondered if Elizabeth would feel upset when she saw the news.

In reality, Elizabeth wasn't particularly bothered. She had expected this outcome.

If anything, she felt slightly disgusted reading comments speculating that James might be Victoria's child.

But then again, that was how the media operated.

They would write whatever attracted attention and glorified their subjects!

Putting her phone away, Elizabeth gazed out the window.

The car taking her to Crestwood Channel was a company vehicle - she probably wouldn't be having Robert drive her around anymore anyway.

Her phone rang with an incoming call. Elizabeth glanced at the screen before answering.

Samuel immediately scoffed at her. "See, Elise? Did you think I was lying? I told you William has a fiancée. How does it feel to get your face slapped?"

"You were engaged to me when you started seeing Sophia, weren't you?" Elizabeth retorted. "And Sophia still came out on top, didn't she?"

"How can you be so shameless?!" Samuel snapped, instantly infuriated by her response.

He never knew when it started, but Elizabeth had become so articulate that he could never win an argument with her!

"Just learning from Sophia," Elizabeth said flatly.

"She and I truly love each other."

"And you think William and I don't?" Elizabeth laughed coldly. "If that weren't the case, why weren't your family or the Scotts invited to Levine Holdings' 60th anniversary celebration last night? You probably already know why, don't you?!"
###Chapter 214

Samuel was left clenching his jaw. "Don't get too cocky, Elise!"

Still, he had become the joke of Crestwood's elite circles after being denied an invitation to Levine Holdings' anniversary celebration. Amanda's exposure for trying to frame Elise had completely destroyed his family's reputation. No one in Crestwood's business community took the Mitchells seriously anymore.

To make matters worse, the investors Elise had brought to Mitchell Corp were now threatening to pull their funding.

If this continued, Mitchell Corp would face bankruptcy just like they had years ago.

Despite this, Samuel only grew more vicious toward Elise. "Victoria is a thousand times better than you. You know prominent families care about lineage, and she and William share genuine feelings. Not to mention, William's son is hers! What exactly do you have that's better than Victoria?"

"I don't need to compete with her. All that matters is knowing William would stand against your family and the Stuarts for me," Elise replied calmly.

Samuel was left speechless with rage, though he had to admit the truth—even if William wouldn't marry Elise, he could easily make the Stuarts and Mitchells suffer if he cared about Elise at all.

"Everyone has their limits, Samuel. Stop humiliating yourself like this!" Elise was about to hang up when Samuel shouted desperately, "Wait!"

Though Elise pressed her lips together, she kept the phone to her ear.

"Why did you hide being Lovely from me?" Samuel demanded, unable to keep the tremor from his voice.

He couldn't accept that Elise had completely deceived him.

If he had known Elise was Lovely, he would never have ended their relationship.

If he hadn't broken up with her, Elise wouldn't have driven him to this desperate situation!

He had to admit he'd been impressed when Sophia placed fifth in that international fashion design competition. Mitchell Corp ran a fashion brand, after all—they needed famous designers.

He thought he was being clever and strategic, but it had backfired spectacularly.

He would be lying if he said he didn't regret his choices, but he'd never admit it, even on his deathbed!

Despite his demanding tone, Elise countered coldly, "Does it even matter now?"

"Were you ever sincere with me, Elise?!" Samuel growled through gritted teeth. "Would you have hidden so much if you truly cared?!"

"You're questioning my sincerity after everything you did?!" Elise retorted sharply. "I won't explain myself to a man who cheated on me. You have no right to question my past feelings for you! No matter what I felt, it doesn't change that you betrayed me or your countless attempts to ruin my reputation!"

Samuel's hand trembled as he held the phone.

He had no defense against Elise's accusations.

He knew he could never win against her, yet he couldn't stop himself from calling.

He just needed to vent the frustration that had been building inside him, and...

Clenching his jaw again, he snarled viciously, "One last warning, Elise—break things off with William now if you know what's good for you. Don't fool yourself into thinking you'll end up with him. Victoria isn't someone you can—"

Elise simply hung up mid-sentence.

She certainly didn't need Samuel's reminders—she understood her position better than he did.

Elizabeth ended up visiting the set of Pomp and Circumstance.

Staying home alone was boring anyway, and she was eager to finish filming her scenes.

When she arrived, Penelope informed her they were shooting the bed scene today.

It seemed Ethan had already found a body double for Elizabeth. She only needed to film a few close-up shots.

She smirked, genuinely curious about how attractive the double Ethan had chosen would be!
###Chapter 215

The set was perfectly arranged for the intimate scene. Everything was in position, waiting only for Ethan's command to begin filming.

But Ethan hadn't given the signal yet.

Amanda was visiting the set.

She had brought lavish boxes of gourmet treats for the entire crew. Everyone, including Ethan, was busy indulging in the delicious offerings. Naturally, Amanda stayed close to Ethan, linking her arm through his with evident delight.

Despite her typically arrogant demeanor, Amanda transformed into a gentle, affectionate presence around Ethan.

Once the treats were finished, the crew quickly resumed their positions to continue filming.

Amanda feigned surprise when she noticed Elizabeth. "Elizabeth! When did you arrive? I'm so sorry, I didn't see you earlier. I brought some food—would you like some?"

Though her words sounded friendly, she sighed with exaggerated regret before Elizabeth could respond. "Oh, what a shame—we just finished everything. I'll definitely bring extra for you next time."

Elizabeth merely offered a cold smirk and walked right past Amanda without another word.

Ethan was already seated behind the monitors, preparing to direct the bedroom scene. Elizabeth positioned herself directly beside him.

Ethan glanced at her briefly but remained silent, focusing on the upcoming shot.

Through the monitor, Elizabeth watched her body double. Only the woman's back was visible on screen—slender, with fair, flawless skin. While it wasn't divine perfection, and Elizabeth would never claim such for herself, she had seen Elise's bare back before, which far surpassed this double's.

As Elizabeth continued watching the screen with sardonic amusement, she thought to herself that Ethan certainly had peculiar tastes.

The filming of the bedroom scene took considerable time. Finally, it was time for Elizabeth's close-ups.

When she entered the set and came face-to-face with her double, Elizabeth couldn't hide her astonishment.

The double smiled politely at her. "Good afternoon, Ms. York."

The resemblance between them was strikingly accurate!

While Elizabeth had felt contemptuous earlier judging the double's body proportions from afar, seeing the woman's face made her admit Ethan had chosen well.

In fact, she felt somewhat impressed, realizing this double could easily pass for her in scenes that didn't require close-ups.

"Good work," Elizabeth replied, maintaining her composure.

The entertainment industry always needed convincing lookalikes for actors. She'd just never encountered one who resembled her so perfectly.

"Thank you," the double responded before quickly exiting under the crew's direction.

The close-up shots were straightforward, and Elizabeth completed them efficiently before leaving the set.

She headed to her dressing room to remove her makeup.

Her phone rang. Seeing the caller ID, she answered with a bright smile, her voice turning noticeably sweeter. "Hello, Your Majesty. To what do I owe this honor? Finally missing your precious princess?"

"Enough with the theatrics! Tell me the truth! Did you snap at your grandfather last night?!" The voice on the other end was stern and unyielding.

"Oh, please don't tell me you're scolding me about that?" Elizabeth adopted a wounded tone. "He was bullying a young woman! I was merely standing up for justice!"

"Standing up for justice?! Do you even know who makes the decisions in this family?!" Nancy Levine snapped. "Or perhaps you're tired of acting and ready to come help with the family business?!"

"Stop it, Mother. Grandpa already threatened me with that."

"So you do know how to feel fear?" Nancy grumbled.

Nancy had always spoiled Elizabeth, her only daughter, doing everything possible to fulfill Elizabeth's desires.

Indeed, Nancy's support had been crucial to Elizabeth's successful acting career.

Naturally, Elizabeth quickly softened her tone. "Please, just put in a good word for me. I love acting, and I'm not ready to come home yet..."

"I'm just telling you to be careful," Nancy warned. "Your grandfather will be too busy dealing with William to bother with you right now."
###Chapter 216

Elizabeth exhaled slowly, feeling a wave of relief wash over her. But it was short-lived. Anxiety quickly tightened its grip around her heart once more. "Wait, what is Grandfather planning for William? William would never agree to this engagement."

"I called you specifically to warn you about that," Nancy snapped sharply. "Do not interfere with William's engagement to Victoria! I know you're close friends with Elise, and I won't stop your friendship. But certain matters are beyond your reach—and you have no power to change them!"

"Mother…" Elizabeth groaned in frustration.

"And your grandfather has ordered you home for dinner tonight," Nancy added, her tone leaving no room for refusal.

"But why?" Elizabeth questioned, confused.

Henry had grown increasingly reclusive over the past few years. Family dinners were rare occurrences. He avoided social gatherings unless absolutely necessary.

It wasn't a holiday. Not Christmas. Not Thanksgiving. So why the sudden summons?!

"Victoria's grandfather is joining us for dinner. They'll be discussing William's engagement to her."

"What does that have to do with me?" Elizabeth pressed.

"Just be quiet and come home for dinner!" Nancy's voice was laced with impatience and anger.

"Fine," Elizabeth surrendered immediately. She didn't dare refuse. Offending her mother could jeopardize her entire acting career.

Then, a thought struck her. "Wait, will William be at dinner too?"

"What do you think?"

Elizabeth could vividly picture her mother's exasperated eye-roll through the phone.

"I mean, I haven't been able to contact William since last night. Grandfather has him confined, doesn't he? And if Grandfather is worried about me causing trouble…"

"Don't concern yourself. Henry never acts without certainty," Nancy stated assuredly.

Elizabeth wanted to say more, but Nancy cut her off. "Be home on time, or I'll break your legs. Let's see you land any roles after that!"

Elizabeth fell silent. She ended the call and quickly texted Elise.

[I won't be home for dinner tonight. Don't wait for me.]

[Okay.]

Elise's reply was instant. It worked out anyway, since Elise would be working late.

Elizabeth typed another message: [Dinner is at Levine Manor. I should see William there. Grandfather must have him confined; that's why I can't reach him. I'll relay anything he says once I see him.]

Elise didn't respond this time. Elizabeth pursed her lips thoughtfully.

No matter what, she needed to discover William's true feelings tonight.

After removing her stage makeup, Elizabeth hurried from her dressing room. She was eager to see William.

But as she opened the door, she came face-to-face with Amanda.

They locked eyes for a tense moment. Elizabeth moved to leave, but Amanda spoke first, boasting, "Ethan and I are truly in love. He protects me, even with all my scandals."

"And your point is?" Elizabeth turned back, scoffing dismissively. "That you're both snakes in the same pit? That you deserve each other because you're equally terrible?"

"Stop being bitter. There's no need to pretend. You have feelings for Ethan, don't you?" Amanda said with overconfidence. "You stole my role by sleeping with him, right?"

Elizabeth shrugged indifferently. "Why assume everyone is as blind as you? Or that they'd trade their dignity for a role?"

"Shut your mouth!" Amanda snapped, her anger flaring. "I'm warning you—stay away from Ethan! I can easily remove insignificant nobodies like you from the industry! And mark my words: I'm visiting his parents at his family home soon. Our marriage is practically arranged!"
###Chapter 217

Elizabeth was completely stunned.

She never questioned Ethan and Amanda's connection. After all, when Elise had publicly shamed Amanda on live television, Ethan hadn't batted an eye at Amanda's obvious guilt. He'd shielded her, protecting her from the aggressive journalists. That told her everything she needed to know about their bond.

But she never anticipated Ethan would bring Amanda home to meet his parents this quickly.

Even if Ethan wasn't Henry's biological son, he'd always remained loyal to the strict patriarch. Amanda, however, had a reputation in tatters, her career in entertainment utterly ruined.

The Mitchell family, mired in their own scandals, hadn't even received an invitation to the prestigious Levine Holdings' 60th-anniversary gala.

Was Ethan truly not concerned that Henry would slam the door in Amanda's face the moment she arrived?

As these thoughts raced through Elizabeth's mind, Amanda misinterpreted her silence for bitter jealousy.

Truthfully, Amanda herself was shocked by Ethan's invitation to dine at Levine Manor.

His demeanor towards her was, at best, detached. She never imagined he would take her to his family home.

However, his protection during her most vulnerable moment was merely repayment. He had used her to clear his name after that messy scandal with Elizabeth.

He had been brutally clear about that that night.

No matter how pathetically she had pleaded or how many tears she had shed, he had deposited her at her doorstep and left without a backward glance.

Weeks had passed, and their only physical contact remained the staged photos for their relationship announcement. She hadn't even managed to hold his hand since.

Then, today, he called. He informed her she was accompanying him to Levine Manor for dinner.

Even though he stated it was strictly to meet his parents, a flicker of happiness ignited within her.

That's why she was here now, playing the part of the devoted girlfriend. She'd brought lavish treats for the crew, deliberately ensuring Elizabeth found out.

Seeing Elizabeth's reaction was deeply satisfying. Amanda sneered, "I suppose you understand your position now."

"Ha!"

Elizabeth let out a sharp, disdainful laugh. Without another word, she turned and strode away, her posture impeccable.

Elizabeth's beauty was undeniable. The elegant line of her back and the confident sway of her hips embodied a true leading lady.

Watching her walk away fueled Amanda's fury.

What right did she have to be so arrogant?!

Once Amanda solidified her relationship with Ethan and secured the power of the Levine name, her first order of business would be to destroy Elizabeth's career.

Elizabeth and Penelope headed straight for her private van, utterly indifferent to Amanda's seething glare.

As they passed a crew lounge, however, they couldn't avoid overhearing gossip.

Elizabeth, ever curious about chatter, especially when it involved her, paused to listen.

Two crew members were inside. One, munching on the expensive pastries Amanda had brought, said, "I always thought Elizabeth was prettier than Amanda, but you have to admit, Amanda's generous. This stuff is from the most exclusive tearoom in Crestwood. Must've cost a fortune. It's good to be rich."

"The wealthy stick with the wealthy," the other commented with a shrug. "Pity Elizabeth lost that ticket."

"Lost? Did she ever even want it? Have you seen how sweet and adoring Amanda is around Ethan? You can't fake that look in your eyes. Elizabeth? She barely glances his way unless it's scripted. A woman that proud is too much work. Honestly, Ethan and Amanda seem perfectly suited."

The other crew member chuckled. "Those pastries really bought your loyalty, huh?"

"I call it like I see it…"

Penelope, standing beside Elizabeth, had heard enough. She was ready to storm in and give them a piece of her mind.

But a glance at Elizabeth showed her completely unruffled, as if the conversation was about a stranger.

Before Penelope could charge forward, Elizabeth shot her a sharp, silencing look.

They continued walking. Penelope marveled at Elizabeth's composure, wondering how she managed to remain so unaffected.
###Chapter 218

Elizabeth sat composedly in her luxury SUV while Penelope continued her agitated rant beside her. "Those ungrateful hypocrites, switching loyalties the moment Amanda bought them some cheap treats! Have they forgotten you've been treating them to afternoon tea every other day? The total cost far exceeds Amanda's petty offerings! And why won't you let me confront them, Elizabeth? Gossiping behind our backs... it's absolutely infuriating!"

"They have their own mouths. They can speak whatever they wish," Elizabeth responded calmly.

Penelope pouted dramatically. "But they're also questioning your relationship with Ethan..."

"And they're correct," Elizabeth stated flatly—she never made direct eye contact with Ethan unless absolutely necessary.

Penelope opened her mouth to protest further, but Elizabeth cut her off. "I need to leave early today, so I'll drop you at the intersection ahead. Take a cab home—you can expense the fare through Linda."

"Alright." Penelope nodded, genuinely appreciating Elizabeth's kindness in that moment.

After all, what other celebrity would personally drive their assistant home?!

In truth, Elizabeth always ensured Penelope reached her doorstep safely when time permitted. And in special circumstances like today, she authorized taxi fare reimbursement.

Honestly, how could anyone call Elizabeth arrogant?!

Elizabeth returned to Levine Manor unusually early, finding only her grandfather and parents in the grand living room.

She wanted to inquire about the dinner arrangements multiple times but remained silent under her mother's warning glances.

Yet, sitting idly felt both tedious and uncomfortable.

After brief consideration, Elizabeth rose gracefully. "Shouldn't I wait for William at the entrance? It would demonstrate proper respect."

Before Nancy could respond, Henry nodded, offering rare approval. "Proceed."

Elizabeth exited the living room cheerfully, though her true motivation was checking her phone away from her grandfather's watchful eyes.

Family etiquette prohibited phone usage in elders' presence.

Thus, she positioned herself at the main entrance, retrieved her phone, and began scrolling while awaiting William's arrival.

After some time, a familiar black luxury sedan pulled up outside.

Ethan emerged first, circling the vehicle to open the passenger door with gentlemanly precision.

Once his passenger disembarked, Ethan instructed her to wait outside while he parked in the garage.

However, Amanda's expression darkened immediately upon spotting Elizabeth casually using her phone at the entrance as if she belonged there.

Amanda thought her eyes deceived her... but it truly was Elizabeth!

What possible right did Elizabeth have to be here?!

Was Elizabeth so offended by Amanda mentioning her visit that she decided to crash the dinner?!

Was Elizabeth attempting to escalate the situation?!

How utterly shameless could she be?!

Yet Amanda refused to let Elizabeth ruin her introduction to William's parents.

With determined strides, Amanda approached Elizabeth and delivered a sharp, stinging slap across her face!

The unexpected blow left Elizabeth completely stunned and speechless!
###Chapter 219

Elizabeth's cheek stung with a fiery pain.

She had anticipated Amanda might be shocked to find her here. Perhaps even offended that someone dared to challenge her perceived superiority.

She never imagined Amanda would resort to violence so immediately. The slap left Elizabeth momentarily frozen, her mind blank.

Even the security guards at the entrance stood paralyzed, unsure how to respond.

"Have you no shame, Elizabeth York?!" Amanda shrieked, her voice trembling with raw fury. "How dare you show your face here?! Are you really this desperate to sabotage my meeting with Ethan's parents?! Did my warning this afternoon mean nothing to you?!"

Elizabeth slowly regained her composure, her fingers gently probing her swollen cheek.

In truth, Elizabeth had faced few humiliations in her life. Most of her losses were connected to Ethan.

And she could accept losing to him.

But anyone else…

Elizabeth's eyes narrowed dangerously. She raised her hand, ready to deliver a retaliatory strike.

However, a strong hand caught her wrist mid-air. Elizabeth turned to find Ethan had appeared seemingly from nowhere.

Amanda spotted him too and instantly ducked behind his broad frame, her demeanor shifting to one of feigned vulnerability. "Ethan, she was going to hit me…" she whimpered.

"Release me!" Elizabeth commanded, her gaze locked on Ethan with an icy intensity that could freeze hell.

The depth of her fury was palpable to anyone watching.

"Don't cause a scene. This is Levine Manor," Ethan reminded her calmly.

"So I'm supposed to endure this humiliation in my own home?!" Elizabeth's voice rang out, clear and sharp.

Ethan pressed his lips together while Amanda, emboldened by his presence, softened her stance—a stark contrast to the arrogant woman who had struck Elizabeth moments before.

"What do you mean, your home?" Amanda asked with feigned innocence. "Look, Elizabeth, I know you have feelings for Ethan, but he's with me now. We're discussing marriage prospects. You really shouldn't interfere, or this could end badly for everyone involved… It would be such a shame if your acting career suffered because of this unfortunate obsession!"

"Let… me… go!" Elizabeth's burning eyes remained fixed on Ethan, completely ignoring Amanda's taunts.

"I owe you for that slap," Ethan stated flatly.

Elizabeth arched an eyebrow. "Are you suggesting I should hit you instead?"

"Yes."

"How chivalrous." Elizabeth's smile was cold, belying the storm raging inside her.

Ethan said nothing. She struggled against his iron grip, which only tightened painfully. "How exactly am I supposed to hit you if you won't release me?"

Ethan's lips thinned, and he slowly loosened his hold.

But the instant her wrist was free—

CRACK!

Elizabeth's palm connected squarely with Amanda's cheek, the force behind the blow far exceeding the one she had received.

Amanda's face immediately reddened and swelled, tears springing to her eyes from the sheer pain.

She never expected Elizabeth would strike her, though she wouldn't have tolerated Elizabeth hitting Ethan either.

Elizabeth had simply moved too swiftly for anyone to react.

"Elizabeth York…" Amanda growled, her voice thick with rage.

Having been pampered her entire life, she was utterly unaccustomed to such indignity.

She raised her hand, intent on returning the slap with equal force.
###Chapter 220
Ethan abruptly pulled Amanda into his embrace, preventing her from striking Elizabeth again. "That's enough!"

Amanda fought against his restraint, her expression fierce. "She has no right to be near you! How shameless to show up at Levine Manor! Who does she think she is?!"

"I'm not just anyone. I'm the granddaughter of Henry Levine!" Elizabeth declared immediately.

As Amanda stood speechless, Elizabeth continued, "Doesn't sound familiar? Well, he's not that famous in Crestwood... But what if I told you he's the chairman of Levine Holdings?"

Amanda's heart raced as understanding dawned.

But she refused to believe it. This couldn't be possible!

There was no way Elizabeth could be Henry's granddaughter!

If she were, why would she struggle so much in her acting career? With Levine Holdings owning numerous studios, Elizabeth would have been treated like royalty everywhere! Amanda had personally witnessed a furious director screaming at Elizabeth when she was just starting out because she ruined a scene!

Amanda knew Henry had a granddaughter, but the girl had always been kept out of the public eye. Rumors said the Levines protected her privacy fiercely, not wanting her life disrupted. Some even claimed Henry spoiled her rotten and raised her himself.

Amanda turned to Ethan, her eyes questioning if this was true.

Ethan nodded, and Amanda felt her world tilt.

Her entire body began trembling uncontrollably... the person she'd been tormenting was actually a Levine family member!

So Ethan's kindness toward Elizabeth was because she was his niece, not what Amanda had suspected?!

And Elizabeth was at Levine Manor because it was her home, not because she was chasing Ethan?!

Amanda's face turned deathly pale as these realizations struck.

She'd never felt this terrified before, not even when Elise exposed her on live television, because she hadn't feared Elise's retaliation.

But Elizabeth being Henry's granddaughter changed everything. Revenge would be effortless for her.

She could even complain to Henry and stop Ethan from dating her...

Frozen with fear, Amanda couldn't form words.

Elizabeth completely ignored Amanda's shock and turned toward the man beside her, though she hated looking at him.

Some situations, however, were unavoidable.

"Ethan Parker, you should remember your position," Elizabeth stated coldly. "You're merely my grandfather's adopted son. Our family blood doesn't run through your veins, just as you have no right to inherit the family business. To true Levines like us, you're nothing but an outsider, an insignificant presence. That's why you have no right to make demands of me, including who I choose to slap!"

They knew each other too well, understanding exactly which words would inflict the most pain.

"Yes, I'll be more careful next time," Ethan replied calmly, accepting Elizabeth's verbal assault without emotion.

She felt little toward him now—even hatred seemed excessive.

Ethan knew precisely how to act around her or wound her deeply.

Having grown accustomed to it, Elizabeth cared much less these days.

Just then, several Bentleys pulled through the main gates.

Elizabeth paused and turned toward the arriving vehicles.

Simultaneously, Henry was being wheeled out by his butler Lawrence, with Nancy and Ivan following closely behind.

Naturally, anyone warranting such reception from the Levines couldn't be ordinary!
###Chapter 221

Three sleek Bentleys stood gleaming outside Levine Manor's grand entrance. Security guards rushed to open the doors with practiced precision.

William Levine emerged first from the lead vehicle. His tall frame was accentuated by a perfectly tailored black suit that radiated authority. Every movement carried weight and purpose.

He turned and offered his hand to Victoria Stuart with ceremonial grace. She accepted with a demure smile that didn't quite reach her eyes.

"Thank you, William."

"My pleasure," he responded, his tone polite yet distant.

Victoria maintained her composed expression, seemingly unaffected by his formal demeanor. They moved in synchronized steps toward the adjacent vehicle.

Charles Stuart emerged next, assisted by his personal butler Lawrence. Though his hair shone silver-white and his years numbered over seventy, he stood with military straightness that defied his age.

From the final Bentley came Andrew Stuart and Wendy Stone, followed by their son James Stuart. The Stuart family procession began moving toward the manor entrance.

Simultaneously, Henry Levine's wheelchair was being guided toward the gathering. The two patriarchs had been friends for decades, though their meetings had grown scarce with declining health.

Their reunion sparked visible emotion in both men. Charles bent to embrace his old friend.

"Henry, it's been too long."

"Indeed it has, Charles," Henry replied, his voice thick with feeling.

They immediately fell into quiet conversation, catching up on lost years while their families stood in respectful silence around them.

Amanda Mitchell watched the scene unfold with wide eyes, intimidated by the sheer presence surrounding her. She barely dared to breathe.

When news of William and Victoria's engagement had broken, she'd felt triumphant. This confirmed everything she'd suspected about Elise Scott's true status.

The fact that William had never publicly acknowledged Elise spoke volumes. How could Elise possibly compete with someone like Victoria?

Amanda had made sure to research Victoria's background thoroughly. The more she learned, the more she realized how outmatched Elise truly was.

Victoria's life appeared flawless - a pedigree so impeccable that even Amanda, who rarely acknowledged other women's superiority, had to concede defeat.

She risked another glance at Victoria, who seemed to sense the attention and turned slightly. Amanda quickly averted her eyes, suddenly feeling like an insignificant observer in this world of old money and power.

This unfamiliar insecurity unsettled her deeply. She'd never experienced such overwhelming inadequacy.

Her gaze drifted to Elizabeth York standing nearby, who appeared completely at ease in this environment. Ethan Parker stood equally composed beside her.

They moved through this world of wealth and influence as if born to it. Amanda felt like an imposter in comparison.

Resentment simmered within her - partly at her own visceral reaction to the social hierarchy unfolding before her, partly at their effortless belonging.

She'd been raised believing herself special, yet this single family gathering exposed her as utterly ordinary in their world.

The realization stung more than she cared to admit.
###Chapter 222

She had believed she would be the center of attention at tonight's banquet.

After all, she was here to meet the family formally.

Yet the Levines barely glanced her way.

Being ignored like this was utterly intolerable.

Still, she couldn't show her frustration.

For the first time in her life, she felt completely insignificant among these people.

After Henry and Charles exchanged greetings, they moved into the grand hall of Levine Manor.

They proceeded directly toward the opulent dining room.

Everyone found their seats around the massive table according to their status.

Servants stood attentively nearby, ready to assist with the meal.

The conversation flowed mainly between Henry and Charles.

They reminisced about old times and shared recent news.

"I intended to attend Levine Holdings' 60th anniversary celebration," Charles expressed regretfully. "But urgent business called me to the capital. My apologies, Henry."

"No need for apologies between us," Henry responded graciously. "Though I hope you don't mind that we announced William and Victoria's engagement in your absence."

"Not at all," Charles assured him. "We agreed upon this match long ago. Having their engagement announced during such an important Levine family occasion honors Victoria."

Henry smiled warmly. "It's William who's honored to marry Victoria. And our family is equally honored by this union."

They continued exchanging pleasantries about their families.

Elizabeth glanced toward her brother. With all the elders present, she hadn't found a private moment to speak with William. His expression remained unreadable. She couldn't tell if he truly accepted this engagement with the Stuarts. The uncertainty made her anxious.

"Since your family rarely visits Crestwood," Henry suggested kindly, "why not stay a few extra days? William can show you around. The city has developed rapidly in recent years. Much has changed."

"Fortunately, my schedule is clear this week," Charles agreed. "I only need to travel to the capital next week. Though my health prevents extensive sightseeing, I'd enjoy sharing tea and chess games with you."

"Excellent," Henry approved. "As you can see, my mobility is limited to this wheelchair. While we elders remain indoors, the young people should explore the city together. They've spent considerable time abroad, and William has been back only six months. They might appreciate reconnecting."

Henry turned to William. "Starting tomorrow, spend time with Victoria. Samuel can manage your office responsibilities."

"Of course," William acquiesced.

His response held neither enthusiasm nor resistance.

"Victoria," Henry addressed her.

"Grandfather," she responded cordially, setting down her spoon with perfect poise.

Her manners reflected her upper-class upbringing flawlessly.

"Enjoy tomorrow with William. Tell him where you'd like to go—anywhere you choose. If he dares mistreat you, inform me immediately," Henry told her benevolently.

"Thank you, Grandfather," Victoria replied with a light laugh. "Though William would never treat me poorly. He always took excellent care of me when we lived abroad."

"Splendid." Henry nodded, visibly pleased.

"Incidentally," Victoria inquired suddenly, "where is James tonight? I used to see him daily. Now that he and William have returned home, I haven't seen him in ages. I miss him terribly."

"James has evening classes now that he's in grade school," William explained.

"Does James require extra tutoring?" Victoria sounded surprised. "He's such a bright child!"

"His spelling needs improvement," William clarified.

Victoria couldn't suppress an amused chuckle.

James had grown up overseas. That he spoke English so well was accomplishment enough.

"I considered offering to tutor James if you're too busy," Victoria offered. "But my own writing skills aren't exceptional. Like you, I spent much of my youth abroad."

She expressed this with charming helplessness.

"That won't be necessary," William stated evenly. "We've engaged a tutor for him."

His tone matched his general demeanor toward Victoria—neither eager nor opposed.

Elizabeth watched, frustration simmering within her.

That infuriating man.
###Chapter 223

The dinner continued with William and Victoria making pleasant conversation. Both families watched them interact with visible satisfaction.

Elizabeth was the only one struggling to maintain her composure. Her knuckles turned white around her silverware.

Thinking of Elise... Elizabeth took a deep breath. She needed to stay calm.

"How old are you now, Elizabeth?" Charles suddenly addressed her.

Elizabeth's heart skipped a beat. This couldn't be good. She forced a polite smile and set down her spoon.

"I'm turning twenty-five this year, Mr. Stuart."

"You've grown into such a lovely young woman. I hear you're making quite a name for yourself in the entertainment industry?"

"I wasn't cut out for academics," Elizabeth said lightly. "Acting seemed the natural path."

Charles chuckled warmly. "Plenty of people can study. Not everyone can captivate an audience."

"Then perhaps you could convince my grandfather. He keeps insisting I leave the entertainment world."

"I'll have a word with that stubborn old man," Charles promised, eyes twinkling.

The table's atmosphere remained cheerful.

Amanda pressed her lips together tightly.

She had always dismissed Elizabeth as someone who slept her way to success.

Yet here Elizabeth was, chatting effortlessly with the patriarch of Rockville's wealthiest family, even making him laugh.

Amanda burned with jealousy.

"Tell me, are you seeing anyone?" Charles asked Elizabeth.

"Who would want me?" Elizabeth replied with a practiced smile.

Ethan's grip tightened on his cutlery.

"Now you're just being modest," Charles said kindly.

"I'm quite serious," Elizabeth insisted. "No one seems interested in someone like me."

"Perhaps I could introduce you to someone?" Charles suggested.

Elizabeth froze.

"My grandson, James," Charles continued. "He's thirty, a bit older than you, but with excellent character. What do you think?"

He gestured toward James, who sat nearby wearing rimless glasses, looking every bit the refined gentleman.

"Oh, no, that's quite all right," Elizabeth refused hastily.

"Elizabeth," Henry warned.

"It's not that James isn't wonderful," Elizabeth explained quickly. "But he watched me grow up. He feels like an older brother. The idea seems almost... improper."

"Besides," she added, turning to James, "I'm sure you prefer quieter women. Isn't that right, James?"

James adjusted his glasses. "Not necessarily."

Elizabeth's eyes widened.

Victoria joined in. "I adore your energy, Elizabeth. You always brighten any room. My brother often mentions you and never misses any of your films or shows."

Elizabeth stared at James. "You... actually like me?"

James gave her a warm smile. He reached over and ruffled her hair affectionately. "I've always admired your spirit. I never said I was in love with you."

"But since we're both unattached," he continued, "if you're willing, I wouldn't mind exploring the possibility..."

"I'm not interested," Elizabeth stated flatly. "The industry doesn't allow casual relationships. Besides, with William and Victoria's engagement, things would become too complicated."

"Not at all," Charles said cheerfully. "It would only bring our families closer."

"Grandfather, you're making Elizabeth uncomfortable," James defended her.

"Elizabeth has a point," he added. "William and Victoria's wedding should be our priority."

"Their marriage is settled," Charles said airily. "Just a matter of choosing the date."

"You're not getting any younger," he told James. "Don't let opportunities slip away."

"I understand," James nodded, effectively ending the discussion about him and Elizabeth.

After dinner, the families gathered in the drawing room. Some enjoyed tea while others played chess.

No one showed any intention of leaving.

Seeing her chance, Elizabeth finally managed to catch William alone for a private conversation.
###Chapter 224

Elizabeth dragged her brother forcefully to a secluded corner in the manor's shadowy garden.

"William, what's this nonsense about your engagement to Victoria?!"

She was furious but kept her voice low. Her eyes nervously scanned the mansion, terrified someone might overhear.

"How is she?"

William's voice was deep and measured. He was asking about Elise, of course.

"You dare ask about her?!"

Elizabeth snapped angrily. "You disappeared for two days without a trace. No calls, no messages. How do you think she feels? She's putting on a brave face, obviously. Did you expect her to collapse in tears?"

"Grandfather confiscated my phone," William explained calmly. "He's monitoring my every move."

"That sly old fox," Elizabeth muttered. "So what's your plan? Are you actually going to marry Victoria?"

She paused, then added suspiciously, "Or were you just playing Elise all along..."

William's sharp glare silenced her immediately. Her brother had always been honorable. He wouldn't do something so deceitful.

"Here, take my phone and call Elise," Elizabeth offered, pulling her device from her pocket.

Before she could hand it over, a figure emerged from the darkness.

"Young Master." Elizabeth jumped in fright. She turned to see a black-suited bodyguard standing unnervingly close.

When had he approached so silently?

"The Old Master has ordered no private communications until Ms. Stuart leaves Crestwood," the bodyguard stated respectfully.

"This is insane!" Elizabeth cursed under her breath.

How could their grandfather be so controlling? He'd always ruled the Levine family with absolute authority, but this was beyond extreme. William was a grown man, not some pet on a leash.

Did he deserve no freedom at all?

"I'm simply following orders. Young Master, Miss, please understand," the bodyguard bowed apologetically.

Elizabeth looked desperately at William.

What now? Her eyes pleaded for guidance. If Victoria decided to stay in Crestwood permanently, would William remain a prisoner forever?

"Tell her to wait for me," William said firmly.

"For how long?" Elizabeth pressed.

"Young Master." The bodyguard stepped forward warningly, ready to intervene if the conversation continued.

William turned and walked away without another word.

Elizabeth stood frozen, watching his retreating figure.

'Damn it! Is William just going to accept this?' The injustice burned within her. If she felt this frustrated, how must Elise be suffering?

Elizabeth returned to the mansion, muttering curses under her breath as she endured the remaining hours of the torturous banquet.

Finally, at 10 PM, the two elderly patriarchs retired to their rooms. Only then were the other guests permitted to leave.

Elizabeth had planned to return to her own apartment, but her mother's persistent nagging forced her to stay the night at Levine Manor.

As she walked toward her bedroom, she passed Ethan's room and spotted Amanda inside...

If Elizabeth were honest, she'd never seen Amanda so subdued. The usually vibrant woman had become almost invisible, blending into the background like mere air.

Elizabeth had nearly forgotten Amanda was attending dinner at Levine Manor tonight.
###Chapter 225

Elizabeth spotted Amanda at the exact same moment.

Henry had summoned Ethan, who then instructed Amanda to wait in the room so he could escort her back later. She questioned her own judgment in coming here tonight at all.

From the very start, no one in the Levine household had acknowledged her presence, let alone anyone from the Stuart family.

Running into Elizabeth now felt profoundly awkward.

Remembering how she used to mock, despise, and speak arrogantly to Elizabeth filled her with burning shame. She dreaded imagining the ridicule and attacks she would face if Elizabeth's true identity ever became public knowledge.

Elizabeth, of course, sensed Amanda's discomfort. A cold sneer formed on her lips.

A woman like Amanda deserved no sympathy from her, not even a shred.

Elizabeth walked past, her posture radiating a subtle but unmistakable haughtiness.

Amanda seethed with indignation.

In the past, she had been the one treating Elizabeth with such contempt.

Now, not only was she on the receiving end of Elizabeth's arrogance, but she was also forced to endure it silently.

The worst part was knowing she would have to keep swallowing this bitterness indefinitely!

Just as Elizabeth reached her own door, she saw Ethan walking down the corridor from the opposite end.

Their eyes met and held for a tense moment.

Elizabeth turned towards her room.

"Elizabeth," Ethan called out.

She paused, her hand on the doorknob.

"Not spending the night at Elise's?" he inquired.

"I'll make sure my windows and doors are securely locked. That way, I won't have to overhear anything from your room with Amanda next door," Elizabeth retorted, her voice dripping with sarcasm.

Ethan said nothing.

Elizabeth felt the chasm between them was now insurmountable.

noveldrama

Even maintaining a familial relationship seemed like an impossible task.

She moved to enter her room again.

"James is a decent man," Ethan remarked from behind her.

Elizabeth let out a sharp, bitter laugh.

Had he stopped her just to push her into a relationship, to ensure she stopped fantasizing about him?!

"I'm aware," Elizabeth said, turning back to face him. "But matters of the heart are my own to decide. I wouldn't dare trouble you with my concerns. And rest assured, I have absolutely no expectations regarding you. I won't interfere in your relationship. As for my issues with Amanda, they are between her and me. They have nothing to do with you. Whether she is your girlfriend or not doesn't change the fact that I despise her. Naturally, I'll maintain appearances in front of the Levines, and I will do my utmost not to disrupt your marital plans with her."

"That wasn't my intention..."

"I have no interest in your intentions," Elizabeth cut him off mid-sentence. "I've stated my position clearly, hoping you will refrain from bothering me in the future. Good night, Mr. Parker."

She slammed the door shut with finality.

Whether any lingering affection remained, Ethan was firmly in her past.

Ethan stared at the solid, unyielding wood of the closed door.

Elizabeth made no effort to conceal her disdain, her utter disgust towards him.

Perhaps that was for the best.

He turned and walked back to his own room.

Amanda immediately painted a sweet, welcoming smile on her face.

"Ethan."

"My father wishes to see you," he stated flatly in response.

Amanda was taken aback.

A moment later, her shock transformed into a flutter of gratification.

It was understandable, of course. The Stuarts and Levines were important guests, demanding their full attention. Her neglect was inevitable under the circumstances.

Now that the Stuarts were properly settled, they finally had time to grant her a proper audience.

Joy bubbled within her, but she carefully maintained a veneer of demure nervousness on the surface.

"Ethan, is your father approachable? Will he be difficult? I feel so anxious."

"He won't deliberately make things hard for you," Ethan replied mildly. "Let's go."

Amanda quickly looped her arm through his, making herself appear delicate and dependent.

Ethan did not pull away.
###Chapter 226

Ethan stopped walking when they reached the door to Henry's study.
"My father is waiting for you inside," he told her.

"Aren't you coming in with me?" Amanda asked, her voice trembling slightly.

"He specifically asked to see you alone."

"Ethan..."

Amanda's nerves were getting the better of her.

"Just be honest with him," Ethan reassured her.
"He won't bite your head off."

Amanda drew a deep, steadying breath.
Though this was her first visit to Levine Manor, she understood their strict household protocols couldn't be challenged.
She had no option but to gather her courage and knock firmly on the heavy wooden door.

As she stepped inside the study, she found Henry seated behind his large desk, sipping tea elegantly.
The door clicked shut behind her, amplifying her anxiety.
She looked cautiously at Henry and offered a respectful greeting.
"Mr. Levine."

"Take a seat," Henry instructed, his eyes briefly scanning her.

Amanda immediately moved to obey.
In her nervous state, her foot accidentally struck the leg of the mahogany chair as she sat down.
The chair scraped loudly against the polished floor, creating a sharp, grating sound.

"My apologies..." Amanda hurriedly said, her face flushing with embarrassment.
She looked completely flustered and uncertain.

Henry observed every subtle shift in Amanda's expression.
The last woman who had sat in that same chair speaking with him had been Elise Scott.
The contrast between the two women was striking.

As Crestwood's most accomplished businessman, Henry had mastered the art of concealing his emotions.
"Ms. Mitchell," he began, "during your visit today, you've had the opportunity to observe our family's circumstances firsthand."

"I have," Amanda responded immediately, clearly striving to make a favorable impression.

"Then you should possess enough self-awareness to recognize that the Levine family is beyond your reach." Henry's words were brutally direct, showing no concern for preserving Amanda's dignity.

Amanda's face paled instantly.
She could scarcely believe what she was hearing.
She had assumed this meeting with Ethan's parents implied their acceptance of her as his partner.
Never had she anticipated being told to step aside!

"The Mitchell Corp of previous years might have qualified for a marriage alliance with our family," Henry continued, "but since Elise Scott departed, I see no promising future for Mitchell Corp.
Moreover, the numerous scandals that have recently emerged have caused Mitchell Corp's stock value to plummet dramatically.
Combined with your own considerable share of controversies, our household could never accept you.
As compensation, I'm willing to grant you one request."

Henry got straight to the point, unwilling to waste time on pleasantries.

"Ethan and I share genuine love..."

"Not exactly," Henry interrupted sharply.
"This has been largely one-sided on your part.
Ethan is simply too kind-hearted to reject you outright, given how you've assisted him.
As his father, I must play the villain in this scenario."

Amanda found herself speechless.
Firstly, Henry's commanding presence was so overwhelming she couldn't possibly contradict him.
Secondly, it was true that Ethan's feelings for her weren't particularly deep.

"Ms. Mitchell, I'm presenting you with three options," Henry stated calmly.
"First, you leave Ethan voluntarily.
Part on good terms, and you might remain friends.
Second, accept what I'm offering you and leave Ethan.
Naturally, I won't inform Ethan about our arrangement, so his perception of you remains unaffected.
Third, I approach your father directly and have him intervene to end your relationship with Ethan."

Amanda bit her lip hard.
This was the first time anyone had backed her into such a tight corner, and the sensation was utterly dreadful.

"Time is passing, Ms. Mitchell.
I suggest you make your decision promptly," Henry urged, his patience visibly thinning.

"I want the lead female role in a major blockbuster film," Amanda finally declared through gritted teeth, her eyes red-rimmed with unshed tears.

"Agreed," Henry responded without hesitation.

He didn't particularly despise Amanda for choosing personal gain from this situation.
As a seasoned businessman, he was well accustomed to transactional arrangements.
However, Amanda's aspirations struck him as disappointingly limited.
He had clearly outlined Mitchell Corp's impending crisis, yet she remained focused solely on advancing her own career.
Such a narrow-minded woman had no place in the Levine family.
###Chapter 227

An agreement was finally reached.

Amanda stepped out of Henry's study.

Henry took a slow sip of his tea, then released a heavy sigh.

"I've ended two relationships in just two days."

"It was unavoidable with Ms. Scott, but Ms. Mitchell... that was different."

Lawrence, having served Henry for decades, understood his master's thoughts perfectly.

"Sir, you summoned Ms. Mitchell to test her character, not to deliberately separate them. She simply failed to meet your expectations."

Ultimately, it had little to do with Mitchell Corp or her past scandals.

Henry gave a dry chuckle.

Lawrence was correct, as always.

If Elise and Amanda had switched places, the outcome would have been entirely different.

The moment Amanda left the study and saw Ethan, her composure shattered, and tears streamed down her face.

Ethan knew exactly what his father had done.

His father had always been clear: anyone wishing to enter the Levine family must first pass his scrutiny.

Amanda had clearly failed.

In truth, Ethan had anticipated this result even before she entered the study.

He hadn't objected because this... would finally bring closure to their complicated situation.

Their relationship had begun as a facade anyway, but Amanda was the one who kept demanding more.

"Let's go. I'll drive you home," Ethan said, taking Amanda's arm and leading her toward the estate's gates.

Amanda suddenly stopped walking.

Ethan turned to face her.

"Ethan Parker, you knew your father would never accept me, didn't you? You deliberately brought me here today to humiliate me, right?"

Amanda jerked her arm away, her voice trembling with accusation. Even she, in her naivety, had finally pieced it together.

Ethan remained silent. His silence was answer enough.

Slap! Amanda's hand connected sharply with Ethan's cheek.

Overwhelming humiliation gave way to uncontrollable emotion. All the indignities she'd suffered throughout the day came pouring out.

Meanwhile, on the balcony of her room, Elizabeth lit a cigarette.

She wasn't a heavy smoker, but she occasionally indulged when feeling frustrated or troubled. She had been considering calling Elise to chat once she felt better when the explosive scene below caught her eye. She watched the two of them arguing as if enjoying a theatrical performance.

Of course, she couldn't hear their words.

Elizabeth wondered if Amanda, unable to swallow the slap she had received earlier, had decided to return the favor to Ethan.

See? How clever.

If she had slapped Amanda, it might not have truly taught her a lesson.

But slapping Amanda meant Ethan would be implicated too. She had killed two birds with one stone. She definitely held the upper hand this time.

Back at the entrance, Amanda's tears continued to fall.

"If you wanted to break up, you could have just told me directly. Why did you have to humiliate me like this?"

"I've told you before. Did you listen?" Ethan countered coldly.

Amanda bit her lip.

Ethan had repeatedly reminded her that they were only pretending to be a couple.

Once their public popularity faded, their relationship would naturally dissolve. She was the one who insisted on pushing for more, finding every excuse to cling to him and refusing to let go.

"It's getting late."

Ethan had no desire to continue this conversation.

It wasn't so much that he didn't want to pursue the matter, but rather that his patience with her had worn dangerously thin.

Amanda eventually got into Ethan's car. The interior was eerily silent.

"Can we still be friends after this?"

Amanda's voice broke the quiet.

Ethan's eyes flickered slightly, but he said nothing.

"Your father said if we break up amicably, we can still be friends," Amanda continued, looking at him pleadingly. "Regardless of everything, I was always there for you when you needed help. Can't we remain friends on that basis alone?"

"Yes, we can," Ethan finally nodded.

"If you need help in the future, feel free to come to me."

"I'll hold you to that."

"Of course," Ethan promised.

That seemed to offer Amanda a small measure of comfort.
###Chapter 228

'Everything will be fine,' she whispered to herself.

'There's still plenty of time.'

Just because they had separated now didn't mean they couldn't reunite later. Her agreement to the breakup, and the benefits she gained from it, were merely strategic delays. She had never intended to give up Ethan Parker so easily!

The car pulled up outside Mitchell Villa.

The moment Amanda stepped out, Ethan spoke.

"I need a favor from you." Amanda was startled.

Ethan needed her help? "Please keep Elizabeth's identity confidential."

Amanda felt a surge of irritation. She harbored a deep-seated animosity toward Elizabeth, and even hearing her name was enough to annoy her.

"Revealing who she is won't benefit you." Ethan was direct.

"Do you have feelings for Elizabeth?"

Amanda stared straight into Ethan's eyes. Women were infinitely sharper when it came to romantic rivals.

"I don't."

"Then why are you so protective of her?"

"We're all part of the Levine family."

"Elizabeth doesn't treat you like family. Did you forget how she mocked you earlier?!"

"She's always been like that, ever since we were young. Blunt in her manner and speech." Ethan defended Elizabeth.

"Ethan, are you denying your feelings for her because Henry doesn't approve of your relationship?"

Amanda's voice was filled with conviction. Initially, she had attributed the subtle nuances in Ethan and Elizabeth's dynamic to their familial ties.

But now, thinking calmly, she recognized the tell-tale signs. Moreover, having faced Henry personally and witnessed his domineering presence, she knew no one could defy his decisions.

Ethan couldn't possibly go against Henry! "No. Stop speculating."

Ethan had no intention of continuing the conversation.

"Just remember what I said."

With that, he drove off without a goodbye. Amanda bit her lip hard.

Whatever the truth was, whether he had feelings for her or not, she would never let Ethan be with anyone else!

In the courtyard of Levine Manor, Elizabeth watched the black car disappear into the distance as she took a drag from her cigarette. She pulled out her phone and made a call.

"Elise."

Her tone was light and casual, a stark contrast to her brooding silence moments ago while smoking.

"Elizabeth."

Elise sounded equally calm and ordinary. Though if one listened closely, a trace of fatigue laced her voice.

"I'm not coming back tonight. My mother won't let me leave," Elizabeth complained.

"Sure."

"What are you up to?"

"Still working."

"Are you that busy?" Elizabeth's brows furrowed. It was past 11 PM.

"I did take a week off," Elise said lightly.

It didn't sound like she was avoiding what happened between her and William. Such composure was only possible if she had truly moved on.

"I saw my brother today."

Elizabeth decided to say it after a moment's hesitation.

Elise's fingers, which had been tapping on her keyboard, finally stilled. She held her phone, picked up the Americano on her desk, and stood up, walking toward the floor-to-ceiling windows.

After sitting all day, she needed to stretch her stiff joints.

"The Stuarts joined us for dinner today," Elizabeth continued, "My brother is under my grandfather's surveillance. He can't call or meet you. He wants you to wait for him."

"Okay," Elise responded.

Elizabeth thought she had misheard. She repeated Elise's reply in disbelief.

"You're willing to wait?"
###Chapter 229

Elise took a slow sip of her coffee, gazing out at the city of Crestwood swallowed by the night.

Once more, she had chosen to stay in Crestwood.

How vast could one city truly be? Encounters were inevitable, and she had no real place to conceal herself anyway.

If William wished to find her, it would be effortless.

What was the use in resisting the inevitable?

"I'll tell my brother."

Elizabeth's face immediately blossomed into a wide, radiant smile.

Elise genuinely valued Elizabeth's character.

Elizabeth was unburdened, vibrant, and full of life. Spending time with her always infused Elise with a greater sense of optimism.

"It's late. You should get some rest," Elise reminded her.

"You too. You work relentlessly; it will take a toll on your health. Have you eaten dinner?"

"Yes, I have."

"Please, take good care of yourself. I'll return tomorrow to keep you company."

"Alright."

Elizabeth ended the call happily and went directly to William's room.

A line of stern bodyguards stood guard outside his door, their postures rigid, ready to intervene at the slightest provocation.

Elizabeth feigned ignorance and reached for the door handle.

"Miss."

One of the bodyguards moved to block her path.

"I need to speak with my brother."

"The Young Master is asleep."

"Then I'll wake him."

"The Old Master has given explicit instructions. No one is to see the Young Master alone."

"Seriously..."

Elizabeth felt her temper beginning to flare.

"Especially you, Miss," the bodyguard added, his tone respectful yet firm.

Her grandfather was certainly taking William's engagement to Victoria with grave seriousness. A flicker of worry ignited within her.

Would William have any room to maneuver against this? Elizabeth gritted her teeth in frustration. There was nothing she could do here but retreat.

Later, well past midnight, Elizabeth concluded that Ethan probably wasn't coming back.

Since Amanda wasn't staying at the manor overnight, they could be anywhere else.

After a fight, wasn't sharing a bed often the best way to reconcile? Elizabeth turned away from Ethan's bedroom door.

As she took a step, she saw Ethan ascending the staircase.

"Looking for me?" Ethan asked, his demeanor cool and composed.

"My brother asked me to relay a message to Elise, to wait for him. I called Elise just now, and she agreed. I was concerned my brother might be worrying, so I wanted to tell him, but the guards refused me entry. Grandfather probably knows I'm close to Elise and specifically ordered them to prevent me from seeing William alone. Could you please deliver the message for me?" Elizabeth asked politely.

"Of course," Ethan nodded.

"Thank you."

Elizabeth expressed her gratitude before turning to leave.

Ethan simply watched her retreating figure. He saw her door close once again, a barrier he could seemingly never cross.

For the past several days, the headlines of every major news outlet in Crestwood had been saturated with coverage of William and Victoria.

There were fresh developments daily.

On the first day, William escorted Victoria to renowned sightseeing spots across Crestwood. Hashtags like #LevineCoupleGetaway and #SweetWhispersLove began trending online.

The news pictures didn't explicitly show them holding hands, but the two were clearly captured in intimate conversation, leaning close, laughing and chatting with apparent joy.

The second day, William took Victoria to Crestwood's most luxurious shopping mall. #LevineSpendsBigToWooWife started trending.

Photographs depicted William laden with numerous shopping bags, dutifully accompanying Victoria.

The third day, William brought Victoria to the offices of Levine Holdings. #MrsStuartDotesAtHubbyWork began circulating online.

Pictures showed William opening the car door and offering his hand to assist Victoria out of the vehicle at the main entrance of the Levine Holdings building.

The fourth day, and the fifth day...
###Chapter 230

Elise was feeling incredibly bored.
She decided to call Charles.

"Hello Charles, it's Elise Scott."

"Good afternoon, Ms. Scott. I have your number saved. How may I assist you?"

Charles maintained his usual respectful tone.

"It's the weekend. Is James at home?"

"Yes, he is."

"May I take him to my place for an overnight stay?" Elise inquired.

Elizabeth was away for the weekend, attending an event in another city.

"Ms. Scott, please hold while I check," Charles said politely.

"Are you asking William? He wouldn't be available to speak with you," Elise stated directly.

"No. Mr. Levine previously instructed that you may take Young Master James anytime. I'm simply checking with Young Master James himself. He's currently playing with his toys and doesn't appreciate interruptions when he's focused."

"Of course. Thank you then," Elise responded courteously, choosing to ignore certain implications in Charles's words.

After a brief pause, Charles returned to the phone.

"Young Master James is quite excited about you coming to pick him up, Ms. Scott."

"I'll be there in approximately thirty minutes."

"Very well."

After ending the call, Elise drove directly to Chateau Emporio where James resided.

The elevator opened directly into the apartment's entrance, and her eyes widened in surprise.

Through the open doorway, she spotted a familiar figure standing in the center of the living room.
She hadn't expected to encounter Victoria here.

Victoria heard the elevator's arrival and turned to see Elise.

Their eyes met across the space.

While no visible hostility existed between them, neither woman initiated conversation.

Elise couldn't determine if William was present.
Standing in the hallway, she could only see a partial view of the living room.

"Mommy!"

James clearly expressed his excitement at seeing Elise.
He rushed from the living room straight into her arms.

Elise knelt down to embrace him properly, holding him tightly.

Emotions swelled within her, though she worked hard to conceal them.

"Mommy, why haven't you visited me for so long? Daddy disappeared a few days ago and I couldn't even reach him by phone. Did Daddy hide you somewhere so I couldn't see you? Daddy's being so selfish not wanting to share you," James complained with a pout.

Children certainly say the most unexpected things.
Elise wondered what Victoria must be thinking hearing James's words.

She focused entirely on James, missing the expression in Victoria's eyes.

She comforted him, saying, "I won't let this happen again from now on, alright?"

'From this point forward, we'll be going our separate ways.'

"Ms. Scott."

Victoria's voice reached her ears, gentle and polite.

Elise released James and stood up, meeting Victoria's gaze directly.

Both women stood at similar heights, with Victoria having a slight advantage of a couple inches.

"I didn't anticipate seeing you here."

Victoria extended her hand first.

"I'm Victoria Stuart."

"Hello, Ms. Stuart."

Elise reciprocated the handshake.

She shouldn't be surprised Victoria knew her identity.
Still, it felt surreal how calmly this encounter between romantic rivals was unfolding.

They shook hands briefly before Victoria continued, "It's the weekend.

William and I planned to take James to Levine Manor, but we just heard Charles mention you want to take James to your residence."

"I want to be with Mommy." James made his preference clear immediately.

"Alright."

Victoria crouched down to James's eye level.
She spoke in a friendly manner, "You can go wherever you wish. Since you were little, have I ever denied you anything you wanted?"

She affectionately ruffled James's hair.

"But Daddy..."

James began to whine.

At that precise moment, Elise watched William emerge from the living room into the hallway.
###Chapter 231

Elise had anticipated crossing paths with William again eventually, but she never imagined it would happen like this.

She had assumed William and Victoria would be too occupied to take James out recently, given their hectic schedules. That was precisely why she chose this time to spend with her son.

If she had known this encounter was inevitable, she might have reconsidered, despite how much she missed James.

She glanced casually at William, careful to maintain neutral eye contact—neither lingering too long nor avoiding him completely. She treated him like any other stranger, with polite detachment.

"Daddy, can I go home with Mommy?" James looked up at William with pleading eyes.

William swallowed hard, tearing his gaze away from Elise.

He nodded at James. "Of course."

"Thank you, Daddy!" James couldn't contain his excitement.

He tugged at Elise's sleeve eagerly. "Mommy, Daddy said yes! I can come home with you!"

Elise gave James a warm smile before turning to William. "Thank you."

William's fist tightened almost imperceptibly.

"Let's go." Elise didn't prolong the moment, taking James's hand to leave.

"Jamie." Victoria's voice stopped them.

James turned to look at her.

Victoria crouched down again, leaning close to him. "Jamie, I'll be returning to Rockville in a few days. Could we have dinner together before I leave?"

"Um..." James hesitated visibly.

"Ms. Stuart, just let me know when and I'll bring James over," Elise offered.

"Really?" Victoria sounded surprised.

"Yes." Elise nodded.

"Thank you."

"You're welcome." The exchange between the two women was excessively polite.

"Jamie, I'll be waiting for you at home." Victoria kissed James's cheek.

Disgust flashed across James's face as he quickly wiped his cheek.

Victoria didn't seem offended. In fact, her smile widened. "You're still the same as when you were little, hating kisses. What will you do when you have a girlfriend someday?"

She seemed to be deliberately teasing him.

James covered his face with both hands, remaining silent.

"We should get going." Elise took James's hand and led him away.

It was clear James wasn't comfortable with Victoria's close proximity.

"Take care," Victoria called after them warmly.

Throughout the entire exchange, William remained cold and distant, like an outsider.

Elise drove James back to her apartment in the car she'd purchased just days before.

She was still getting used to the vehicle and drove cautiously, especially with James in the passenger seat.

Just then, she spotted a familiar Maybach passing her car.

They were probably heading back after failing to pick up James.

"Jamie."

"Mommy." James sat obediently in his child seat.

"Would you like to go to the amusement park?" Elise asked.

"Yes!" James exclaimed excitedly. "I'll go anywhere with you, Mommy!"

"Okay." The corners of Elise's lips curved upward.

Her eyes began to redden with unshed tears.

She focused on calming herself as she navigated toward Crestwood's largest amusement park.

Elise held James's hand as they approached the ticket counter.

The weekend crowd filled the park with energy and excitement.

"Mommy, this is actually my first time at an amusement park! I really want to ride that one, and that one, and that one too..." James pointed enthusiastically at various rides, his face glowing with happiness.

"Has your daddy never brought you here before?" Elise frowned slightly.

"Daddy was always too busy to take me out for fun."

Elise's heart ached.

She squeezed James's small hand tighter. "Today, Mommy will take you on every ride you want, as long as you meet the height requirements."

"Yay!" James jumped with joy.

Hand in hand, they hurried into the bustling amusement park.
###Chapter 232

They remained completely unaware of William, Victoria, and the swarm of black-suited bodyguards trailing them.

Their visit to the amusement park had been scheduled for today.

The original plan included James, but Elise had taken him away unexpectedly. So, the two of them proceeded with their initial arrangement and came here. Surprisingly, they encountered Elise and James.

Victoria's gaze flickered.

James had always been reserved since childhood, never the exuberant type. He possessed a maturity that defied his tender age.

Yet, around Elise, he was transformed entirely.

Victoria glanced at William, noticing how his eyes remained fixed on the pair ahead until they disappeared into the bustling crowd.

"Let's proceed." William withdrew his gaze, his expression neutral as he looked at Victoria.

A subtle smile lingered on Victoria's lips as she walked beside William.

Surrounding them were not only bodyguards but also strategically placed reporters.

Their purpose was to provide live updates and reports on their every move.

"What ride would you prefer?" William inquired, turning to Victoria.

"Anything is fine with me."

"Something thrilling, or perhaps something more relaxed?"

"Maybe something thrilling," Victoria replied. "Opportunities like this to truly let loose are rare. I want to release some pent-up energy."

"Understood," William responded.

He led Victoria to the Extreme Thrills Zone, where they experienced numerous rides—roller coasters, the 360-degree mega swing, the drop tower, the pirate ship….

James was too young for all these intense attractions, eliminating any possibility of an accidental encounter.

Elise stayed with James in the Children's Wonderland, gently navigating a small boat across the serene lake.

Her phone suddenly vibrated.

Seeing James completely absorbed in the boat ride, Elise checked her phone. A new friend request appeared on her social media.

She tapped the notification. It was from Victoria.

Elise's finger hesitated slightly.

She decided to accept the request.

"Mommy." James called out to her, his voice filled with excitement.

Elise immediately set her phone aside.

"Look, tiny fish!" James exclaimed with delight, "Over there! So many of them!"

"Let's steer the boat closer," Elise suggested.

"Okay!"

The mother and son duo were thoroughly enjoying themselves.

After the boat ride, they explored many other attractions.

The massive crowds and endlessly long lines consumed their time, and soon, evening descended.

"Mommy, I want to ride that!" James pointed enthusiastically at the giant Ferris wheel.

Elise contemplated taking James home.

She was genuinely tired, and they had only eaten a light lunch earlier. She worried James might be hungry.

But seeing his boundless energy, she couldn't bear to cut his fun short. Taking his hand, she walked toward the Ferris wheel.

The Ferris wheel was even more popular at night, with a winding, serpentine queue stretching far from its base.

They had just joined the line when someone called out to Elise.

"Ms. Scott?"

Elise turned around.

To her surprise, she saw Victoria and William.

Clearly, they were also queuing for the Ferris wheel.

Elise pressed her lips together.

What incredibly coincidental timing.

"James, what a lovely surprise," Victoria said with a warm smile.

"Oh," James responded coolly.

Victoria feigned a pout, scrunching her nose. "So aloof, just like your father. If you keep this up, no girls will like you when you're older."

"I don't need girls to like me. Having Mommy like me is enough." With that, James tightened his grip on Elise's arm.

"You little ingrate! I took care of you since you were a baby! Now that you have your Mommy, you don't want anything to do with me anymore?! Have you forgotten how I fed you and changed your diapers?! You even promised you'd take care of me when I'm old!"

Victoria pretended to be deeply hurt.
###Chapter 233

James pouted unhappily.

He had never once mentioned wanting to take care of Victoria, his so-called 'godmother', in her old age.

Even that godmother title had been forced upon him against his will.

Elise remained silent, simply observing the exchange between James and Victoria.

She didn't speak, and neither did William.

Only Victoria and James' voices could be heard in the queue, with Victoria mostly initiating the teasing and James reluctantly playing along.

After what felt like an eternity, their turn finally arrived.

"Should we all ride together?" Victoria suggested enthusiastically.

The Ferris wheel carriage was spacious enough to accommodate eight people comfortably.

"That won't be necessary," Elise declined immediately. "We wouldn't want to interrupt your private time."

"Oh, it's perfectly fine," Victoria said generously.

"There are reporters everywhere," Elise stated bluntly. "It wouldn't look good if they photographed us together."

She had clearly noticed the swarm of media personnel surrounding them.

Victoria glanced toward William, as if seeking his opinion.

"Let's ride separately," he decided.

"Oh," Victoria responded, her expression turning disappointed though she didn't argue. "I just wanted to share the carriage with Jimmy."

"You two go ahead," Elise offered. "James and I can ride by ourselves."

"I want to ride with Mommy," James insisted firmly.

"You ungrateful little thing, after how much I dote on you," Victoria said with affectionate exasperation. "Fine, fine. Ride with whoever you want. When have I ever forced you to do anything?"

James looked thoroughly displeased.

He wasn't being ungrateful—he would never act this way toward someone he genuinely cared about.

Elise took James' hand as they stepped into their carriage, while William and Victoria entered the one directly behind theirs.

The massive wheel began its slow ascent.

The glittering nightscape of Crestwood gradually unfolded before them.

"Mommy, it's so beautiful," James breathed, pressing against the glass window in awe.

Elise smiled and nodded in agreement.

She remembered visiting amusement parks with her own mother when she was young, but the facilities then were nowhere near as advanced, and the city skyline hadn't been nearly as spectacular.

"Mommy, are you..." James suddenly turned with a serious expression. "Are you upset?"

"What?" Elise seemed startled by the question.

"Because of Victoria being here," James whispered softly.

"Not at all." Elise gave a light chuckle.

"I heard that Victoria is going to marry Daddy."

"Yes, she is." Elise confirmed with a nod.

"Doesn't that make you sad, Mommy?" James looked up at her, his eyes filled with concern.

The city's twinkling lights reflected in James' bright, innocent eyes, their pure intensity tugging at Elise's heartstrings.

She couldn't help but pull James into her arms.

She held him tightly against her chest.

James seemed slightly surprised by the sudden embrace.

For some reason, he sensed that Mommy was acting differently today than she usually did.

Was Mommy feeling emotional because Daddy was about to marry someone else?
###Chapter 234

He kept noticing something else in his Mommy's gaze whenever she looked at him—a flicker of guilt mixed with sorrow.

It was clearly Daddy who had broken Mommy's heart.

James remained still in Elise's embrace, not resisting at all. He let her hold him tightly, as if she'd lost him once before and couldn't bear to let him out of her sight for even a moment.

The Ferris wheel carriage rose steadily higher, climbing toward the sky's peak.

Elise finally composed herself and answered James's earlier question. "Having you with me makes everything better."

"Mommy." James reached up and gently wiped the tears from her cheeks.

Only then did Elise realize she was crying.

These were emotions she had bottled up for far too long.

Today, she allowed just a little of that pain to escape.

Elise said tenderly, "Jamie, I'm so grateful to have you in my life."

"No matter who Daddy chooses to marry, I'll always stay by your side," James promised solemnly.

"Okay." Elise stroked James's hair affectionately, a soft smile gracing her lips.

"Actually..." James looked up at Elise with earnest eyes, "Daddy doesn't have feelings for Victoria."

Elise shook her head gently.

It wasn't that she doubted James's words, but she simply didn't care anymore.

"He really doesn't. When we lived overseas, even though Victoria visited often, Daddy always kept his distance. It's you he cares about, Mommy. It's Great-Grandpa who keeps pushing for their union," James continued passionately. "Daddy won't actually marry Victoria. He told me once that the only person he'd ever—"

"Jamie," Elise interrupted softly.

James looked at her with his large, dark eyes full of concern.

"Children shouldn't worry about adult matters." Elise didn't want James burdened with complications beyond his years. "Just remember that no matter what happens between Daddy, Mommy, or even Victoria, we all love you deeply. Nothing will ever change that."

"Okay." James nodded seriously.

"Let's take some pictures." Elise pulled out her phone and began taking selfies with James.

She refused to let unhappy thoughts ruin this moment.

In the adjacent Ferris wheel carriage, Victoria gazed out the window, her face radiant with happiness.

"William, Crestwood has developed so rapidly. When I visited with Grandfather as a child, I never imagined it would become this magnificent. The city's growth these past few years has likely surpassed even Rockville's development." Victoria praised the city enthusiastically.

"Hmm." William responded noncommittally.
