Elise was far more cunning and complex than she appeared. Every encounter left Victoria struggling to read her—she couldn't even discern what mood Elise was in.

How simple could a woman be if she managed to capture William's attention?

Victoria's lips curled into a cold smirk.

In all these years, she had never faced a worthy rival. She was genuinely curious to discover just how remarkable Elise truly was.
###Chapter 239

Elise left Crestwood on Monday morning. Every media outlet was flooded with images of William seeing her off at the airport, their parting painted as heartbreakingly reluctant.

Elise wondered if there was truly no other newsworthy event in Crestwood. Had the entertainment industry run dry of scandal? For an entire week, William and Victoria's images dominated every headline, their faces unavoidable no matter which platform she browsed.

She set her phone aside and entered the executive conference room. The meeting focused on Stellar Group's launch of a new luxury brand line.

Since her identity as 'Lovely' had been revealed, countless businesses were knocking on their door, seeking collaboration. This presented Stellar Group with a significant opportunity. With their current limited resources, expanding marketing through strategic partnerships and increased financing could create a win-win scenario.

The challenge now was selecting the ideal partner.

Elise spent the entire afternoon in back-to-back meetings. She engaged in lengthy discussions with the senior management team.

Her phone remained on silent throughout. Only when she finally returned to her office did she notice the missed calls.

Some were from William. Others were from Samuel.

She chose to ignore them and dove straight back into her work.

The next time she looked up, it was past 9 PM.

They had finally shortlisted three promising potential partners. The team agreed to proceed with a tender process to make the final selection.

Elise stretched her arms, feeling the day's tension. She grabbed her bag and car keys, ready to head home.

The parking lot was her next stop.

Most employees had long since departed. The vast space was eerily quiet and nearly empty.

Elise pressed the unlock button on her key fob. She reached for the car door handle.

A figure lunged from the shadows. A rough hand clamped over her mouth before she could scream. She fought back, struggling against her attacker's iron grip. Her efforts seemed futile against his strength.

She twisted violently. Her muffled cries were swallowed by his palm.

He dragged her sideways, toward a waiting van. He intended to force her inside.

Suddenly, another man charged forward. A powerful kick landed on her attacker, forcing him to release her.

Terror seized Elise. Her only coherent thought was escape.

The two men immediately erupted into a vicious brawl.

Once her initial shock subsided, Elise finally got a clear look at the combatants. The man who had attacked her was Brandon. The one who had saved her… was Samuel.

Elise swiftly called the police.

By the time officers arrived, both men were battered and bruised. Samuel appeared to have sustained more injuries.

Everyone was taken to the Crestwood Police Station to give their statements.

Brandon was detained. Elise and Samuel were eventually released.

It was already 1 AM.

The streets outside were cold and deserted.

A chilly October wind whipped through the air. Elise shivered, sneezing.

"Are you cold?" Samuel immediately shrugged off his jacket, offering it to her.

"I'm fine," Elise declined instantly, her tone firm.

Sensing her clear resistance, Samuel didn't press further. He simply nodded, pulling his jacket back on.
###Chapter 240

"Let me take you to the hospital," Elise insisted.

She had driven her own car to the police station while Samuel had arrived in the patrol car.

From every angle she considered it, giving him a ride was the right thing to do.

"It's not necessary. These are just surface wounds. They'll heal in a few days." Samuel shook his head. "It's late. You should go home."

"You need to get checked out for peace of mind," she pressed.

She simply wanted to avoid any complications.

"Thank you." After a brief hesitation, Samuel finally agreed.

They both got into the car.

Elise drove while Samuel sat in the passenger seat.

"I didn't know you could drive," Samuel remarked. "I've never seen you behind the wheel before."

"I never had the time before," Elise stated frankly.

Samuel looked down, guilt flashing in his eyes. "I'm sorry, Elise. Back then, I..."

"I don't want to talk about the past." Elise cut him off sharply.

She didn't believe Samuel had truly changed his character, and she had no energy for his performance.

"Why were you looking for me today?" Elise got straight to the point.

Samuel pressed his lips together.

Looking at Elise now, without prejudice, he had to admit she was an incredibly sharp woman. Lying to her was futile.

He sighed. "You probably know about Mitchell Corp's current situation."

Of course she did. They were in the same industry.

Previously, Mitchell Corp had tried to boost brand recognition through heavy media exposure. They spent fortunes on advertising, which not only failed but backfired. Their stock plummeted sharply, and with several major investors pulling out, Mitchell Corp was teetering on the edge of bankruptcy.

"I came to you not to ask for money—I know running Stellar Group single-handedly isn't easy, and your funds are limited too. I heard Stellar plans to launch brand collaborations. Mitchell Corp is an established name in fashion. Having worked there so long, you know better than anyone that, putting everything else aside, Mitchell Corp has very mature operational and management systems..."

Samuel spoke at length, then glanced at Elise's cold expression. "I want to collaborate with you."

"I won't consider working with Mitchell Corp." Elise flatly refused.

"I can offer you the best terms..."

"It's precisely because I worked at Mitchell Corp that I know its management system isn't mature—it's outdated and conservative. It can't keep up with modern marketing strategies. Furthermore, Mitchell Corp's current brand design doesn't meet my standards. Brand collaborations are for mutual benefit. Working with you would only be a burden for me." Elise's words were merciless.

"I can change," Samuel said urgently. "When you were at Mitchell Corp, didn't you suggest reforms? I was too arrogant and stubborn back then. Now I finally see I was going the wrong way. I can change immediately. Our design department is weaker, but we can build support. I'll do everything to meet your needs."

"Samuel, if you really understood me, you'd know I would never choose to work with you. What happened between us ended with our breakup. There will be no further interactions." Elise stopped the car at the hospital entrance and turned to him. "We're here."

Samuel clenched his fists.

Elise was terrifyingly cold-blooded.

He knew she was tough, but he hadn't expected her to be completely unmoved.

He was practically begging, yet Elise didn't yield an inch.

Gritting his teeth, knowing he was no match for Elise, he had no choice but to open the door and get out.

Just then, he suddenly winced in pain.

He had clearly aggravated his injury.

Elise pretended not to notice.

She turned and walked into the emergency department, staying with Samuel through his full check-up. She paid his medical bills, waited until all his wounds were treated, and ensured he had no internal injuries before leaving the hospital with him.

As they stepped outside, they saw William standing bathed in moonlight.
###Chapter 241

Elise stared at William standing before her.

He had called her repeatedly throughout the day. Multiple WhatsApp messages remained unread on her phone. She hadn't intended to ignore him. The day had been relentlessly demanding. After work ended, she faced Brandon's retaliatory confrontation. Breathing room felt like a distant luxury.

Elise assumed William would wait until tomorrow to reconnect. Yet here he stood. Three in the morning. Appearing as if from thin air.

She didn't understand how he always located her. Given William's formidable resources, he could probably find her even if she hid underground.

Samuel observed William's sudden arrival. Surprise flickered across his features.

Hadn't William publicly confirmed his relationship with Victoria? Victoria departed mere moments ago. Now William sought out Elise immediately after? At this ungodly hour?

Elise never explicitly denied their affair during their last phone call. Samuel knew her character well. She would never consent to being anyone's secret mistress.

Samuel clenched his jaw. He decided to observe the unfolding scene.

William strode directly toward them. He completely disregarded Samuel. His intense gaze locked solely on Elise. "Finished here?" he asked.

"Yes," Elise replied softly.

"Let's go." William reached out. His hand closed around hers, pulling her close.

A seamless understanding flowed between them. Elise offered no resistance despite his engagement. William showed no anger finding her with Samuel.

Their mutual trust appeared unshakable. External circumstances couldn't undermine their profound connection.

Samuel watched them walk away together. They treated him as invisible. As insignificant as air.

His fists clenched involuntarily. Tightened until his knuckles turned white.

Elise was a woman of dignity and pride. Would she truly degrade herself as William's mistress?

Fury simmered in his chest. Nowhere to release the building pressure. His phone chose that moment to ring.

"Sophia," Samuel answered immediately.

"How did it go, Samuel? I waited all night for your call. I was so worried." Sophia's anxious voice carried through the receiver.

Her genuine concern soothed Samuel's raging emotions. His turbulent heart calmed slightly.

Remembering Sophia's unwavering support comforted him. Last night, she called him late, tears choking her words. She lamented her inability to assist him properly.

It took considerable patience to extract the full story. Benjamin refused to invest additional capital into their family company. Sophia pleaded with her father unsuccessfully. Their argument grew heated. Overwhelmed by Mitchell Corp's precarious situation, she broke down completely.

Samuel's mood already teetered on edge. Constant discussions about Mitchell Corp's decline worsened it. Listening to Sophia's endless weeping tested his limits momentarily. He lost control and snapped at her.

Instead of anger, Sophia blamed herself entirely. Finally, she suggested Elise could restore Mitchell Corp to its former glory.

That's how Samuel learned about Stellar Group's new brand collaboration initiative. Given Elise's current influence, any partnership with her guaranteed profitability. Securing that project would save Mitchell Corp.

Samuel understood Elise's nature perfectly. She wouldn't willingly help Mitchell Corp. Yet Sophia persistently encouraged him to appease Elise into cooperation.

Desperation drove Samuel to attempt anything. He learned about the conflict between Brandon and Elise. Knowing Brandon's character, retaliation seemed inevitable. By positioning himself as Elise's rescuer, perhaps she would feel indebted. Maybe her heart would soften toward him again.
###Chapter 242

Sophia had also pointed out that William had abandoned Elise, leaving her emotionally fragile. If Samuel made the first move, there was a chance Elise might let go of their past conflicts. Sophia insisted he should overlook her own feelings, emphasizing that getting Mitchell Corp through this crisis was the top priority.

Sophia truly believed their bond was unbreakable and steady.

Her resolve touched Samuel deeply, making him grateful to have such a devoted woman supporting him.

“Handling Elise won’t be that straightforward,” Samuel responded, then recounted everything that had transpired.

Sophia let out a cold laugh after hearing the details. She knew Elise inside out. Even if Samuel begged on his knees or sacrificed himself, Elise would remain unmoved.

That woman wanted Samuel to destroy himself.

“It’s probably just a matter of time. You hurt my sister so deeply before, so it’s natural she’s being cautious,” Sophia reassured him.

“But time isn’t on our side. I heard Elise is starting her bids this week, and Mitchell Corp can’t hold out much longer. The stock market is plummeting too rapidly. If we don’t secure funds soon, bankruptcy is inevitable,” Samuel said anxiously.

“What can we do then?” Sophia’s voice trembled, tears welling in her eyes. “Isn’t there any way to force her to cooperate with Mitchell Corp?”

“I don’t have any leverage over her right now…” Samuel’s voice trailed off, his mind racing.

A method to compel Elise to work with Mitchell Corp?

Samuel suddenly straightened, a sinister gleam flashing in his eyes.

“Sophia, do you trust what we have?”

“Of course I do. If I didn’t, I wouldn’t have encouraged you to pursue my sister despite knowing your history. I just want you and Mitchell Corp to survive. For that, I can endure anything right now,” Sophia declared firmly.

“That’s all I needed to hear,” Samuel replied. “I promise you now, no matter what happens, you’re the only one I want as my wife.”

“I understand. I’ll always be here waiting for you,” Sophia said obediently.

They exchanged a few more sentimental vows before ending the call.

After hanging up, Sophia’s smile twisted into something dark. If Samuel wasn’t a fool, he would know exactly what he needed to do to Elise. Once he made his move, Sophia would have the perfect opportunity to betray him. Not only could she sever ties with Samuel and play the victim, but she could also ruin both Samuel and Elise’s reputations.

It was a perfect two-for-one strike!

She never engaged in deals where she came out losing.

Night had fallen. William and Elise stood at the hospital entrance, and William took the car keys from Elise. Glancing at the understated Lexus, he asked, “Your car?”

“Yes,” Elise replied.

William opened the driver’s door and got in. Elise pressed her lips together but quietly took the passenger seat.

The drive to Elise’s residence at Crestwood Heights was silent, arriving at three-ten in the morning.

She had a crucial bidding meeting at eight-thirty the next morning, requiring discussions with senior management. By the time she got home, showered, and got into bed, it would be at least four AM. She needed to be up by seven-thirty to make the meeting on time, leaving her with just three and a half hours of sleep.

Elise gazed out the window at the passing night scenery, then said calmly, “William, if you have something to say, now’s the time.”

She didn’t want to waste what little sleep time she had left.
###Chapter 243

Elise's words hung in the air, met only by the hum of the engine. William gave no indication he had heard her. His focus remained entirely on the road ahead, his expression unreadable.

She forced her turbulent emotions down, maintaining a calm facade. "I have a crucial meeting first thing tomorrow morning," she stated evenly. "And it's already extremely late."

"I'm aware," William replied.

Elise waited for him to continue, but he fell silent again.

Frustration bubbled within her. She bit her lower lip, suppressing the urge to say more.

Since he seemed determined to remain silent, she leaned back against the leather seat. Her gaze drifted to the window, watching the city lights blur past.

Soon, the car descended into the underground parking garage of Crestwood Heights.

Elise unfastened her seatbelt but made no move to exit. She turned to face him fully. "Go on, then. Say what you need to say."

Perhaps he couldn't properly articulate his thoughts while navigating traffic. That was understandable. She valued her safety too much to demand his attention while driving.

"It's late," William said, his tone flat. "You should go upstairs and rest. We can talk tomorrow."

"I prefer directness, William. Let's not dance around this. Just say it. I don't have endless time to devote to this situation. My schedule is packed." Her voice was cool and matter-of-fact.

She had no desire to prolong this agony. She had always been straightforward, even with Samuel in the past.

William's knuckles whitened as his grip tightened on the steering wheel. He finally turned his head, his intense gaze meeting hers.

Despite her seemingly compliant demeanor tonight, he could feel the immense distance she had placed between them.

"I had no prior knowledge of the engagement with Victoria," he finally confessed, the words rushing out. "My grandfather announced it publicly without consulting me."

He had been waiting outside her apartment for hours. When she hadn't returned by the early hours, he had mobilized all his resources to search the entire city for her. The moment he learned she was at the hospital, he had sped there immediately.

Seeing her walk out of the hospital unharmed had only slightly eased the tightness in his chest.

His initial intention had been simply to ensure she got home safely and then leave, not wanting to disturb her rest.

But Elise clearly had little patience left for him. He understood that.

Anyone in her position would feel the same.

"My grandfather has been monitoring my movements closely," he continued. "He confiscated my phone. I had no way to contact you—"

"I know. Elizabeth warned me about that," Elise interjected, cutting him off.

Lengthy explanations were unnecessary. All they needed was a clear resolution.

William's lips pressed into a thin line. He kept his eyes locked on hers. "I have no intention of marrying Victoria. I just need some time."

"How much time?" Elise asked, her voice devoid of emotion.

"Six months."

"And what if, after six months, you're still forced to go through with the marriage?"

"That won't happen."

"How can you be so certain?" A bitter, humorless smile touched her lips. Her gaze was cold and unwavering. "Your grandfather can strip you of your freedom. He can isolate you from the outside world entirely. What makes you believe you can successfully defy him in just six months?"

"It was a sudden ambush. I had no time to prepare any countermeasures," William explained, his voice low. "Furthermore, my grandfather promised to restore my freedom after Victoria's departure. She's only in Crestwood for a week. I didn't want to make any rash moves that would alert him to my resistance."

So, he had chosen to endure the situation temporarily, biding his time.

"I don't trust you," Elise stated slowly, each word deliberate.

William swallowed hard, the muscles in his throat working as he fought to control the emotions threatening to surface.

He had mentally prepared himself for this conversation, but being rejected so bluntly by the woman he loved still sent a sharp, dull ache through his heart.

"Furthermore," she continued, her steely gaze never wavering, her resolve terrifyingly firm, "I have no desire to become entangled in the complex power struggles and grudges between your family and the Stuarts. I lack the patience to navigate the politics of two powerful families." She took a slow breath. "I fought hard for the peace I have now. If forced to choose, I would not hesitate to walk away from you."
###Chapter 244

"Is what we share...so easily broken?" William's voice was strained.

"Between us, there's nothing left to discuss," Elise responded, her tone icy. "Have you forgotten, Mr. Levine? I agreed to give this relationship a trial period. It's clear now it wasn't meant to be."

"What if I promised to keep you separate from my engagement with Victoria? Would you wait for me then?"

"No," Elise refused instantly, without a moment's hesitation.

"Am I truly so undeserving of your faith?" William's knuckles were white, his eyes tinged with crimson.

"Yes," Elise confirmed with a nod.

William let out a bitter, hoarse laugh, laced with self-mockery.

A heavy silence fell between them, thick and suffocating.

They sat without speaking for what felt like an eternity.

It seemed their conversation had reached its conclusion.

Elise moved to open the car door, but William's hand shot out, gripping her wrist with bruising force.

She didn't cry out or flinch.

She simply endured his desperate grasp.

"I don't believe you feel nothing for me," William insisted, his gaze piercing. "What we have is real. I know it is."

Elise remained silent, offering no reply.

"Nor do I believe you'd surrender so easily," William continued, his voice low and intense. "You wouldn't just walk away because the Levines and Stuarts pressured you."

"William, I'm only human. Not invincible. Facing powerful families like them...it's terrifying."

"Yet you've stood against both families with your own strength! I refuse to believe you'd capitulate so quickly!"

Elise bit her lip. It seemed she could hide nothing from William. He saw straight through her defenses.

"What did my grandfather say to you?" William asked, his voice now controlled, emotions forcibly restrained.

So William knew about Henry's private meeting with her. He'd even guessed it influenced her decision.

"He asked me to leave you voluntarily," Elise admitted honestly, seeing no point in concealment.

There was no need for secrets now.

If they were ending this, it should be with complete transparency.

...So neither would be left wondering what might have been.

"Did you agree?"

"Yes."

"Did he threaten you?" William's gaze hardened.

"Not exactly," Elise replied truthfully.

William narrowed his eyes at her response, but Elise continued, "He simply helped me see things more clearly. It was...enlightening."

William's hands trembled slightly. He probably knew exactly what Henry had said. Watching his expression, Elise murmured, "And everything he said appears to be true, doesn't it?"

William pressed his lips together, remaining silent.

"Remember that promise you made me on Ayro Island?" Elise asked calmly.

She wasn't indifferent to their situation. She was simply accepting reality.

When facing impossible choices, she'd always maintained a calm exterior, projecting invincibility.

"You told me not to lie to you. That even if I didn't love you, I should never deceive you." Elise met William's gaze, a faint, ambiguous smile touching her lips. "Yet from beginning to end, it was all one elaborate deception, wasn't it?"

"If I said it wasn't, would you believe me?" William's voice was uncharacteristically small, almost timid.

As if he were praying through the night for her trust.
###Chapter 245

Elise remained silent.

Sometimes, silence truly spoke volumes.

"I never intended to conceal that I was the man from your eighteenth year," William confessed. "I asked you once if you hated him, and you said yes. I planned to tell you when our relationship felt more secure, when you no longer flinched from my touch. Perhaps... just perhaps, you might have learned to accept me."

Elise listened without interruption, her expression unreadable. Encouraged by her silence, William continued.

"That night, I had just returned from overseas and was drinking with Nathan and some friends. At the bar, I saw you—delirious and vulnerable. When another man approached you, and you weakly tried to push him away, I intervened. You collapsed against me, your skin burning with fever. I admit I took you to a hotel, but where else could I have taken you? You were incoherent, burning up. I asked for your address, but you couldn't form words. Then, at the hotel, you were the one who pushed me onto the bed, and..." William's voice trailed off as he watched Elise's reaction.

Elise pressed her lips together. After all these years, the blurred memories of that night began sharpening into focus.

"You claimed you begged me not to touch you, but you have no recollection of how desperately you clung to me that night. Had you not vanished before I woke, you would have seen the scratches and bruises covering my body."

Elise's fists clenched involuntarily. William's casual recounting of the past she'd tried so hard to bury ignited fresh anger within her.

"It's not that I didn't try to find you afterward. But pride... I was young, reckless. After what happened between us, then you disappearing... I felt used. I returned abroad briefly, but within a month, I came back searching for you. That's when my parents' accident happened."

William paused, clearing his throat before continuing slowly. "My entire family plunged into mourning. My grandfather fell ill from grief. Suddenly, I carried the weight of our family legacy. I buried myself in work, traveling constantly between countries. Finding you became... impossible. Later, my grandfather brought James home, claiming... you had abandoned him."

Elise felt her heart constrict painfully.

How could she have abandoned James?

The doctors and nurses had clearly told her the baby was stillborn. The grief had been so overwhelming she couldn't bear to look at the tiny body.

She had accepted the heartbreaking loss.

"So is this your revenge?" Elise asked, bypassing explanations. What was the point, when everything seemed so convoluted?

"I'll admit when I first saw James, anger consumed me. Revenge crossed my mind. But after冷静下来, I chose understanding. You weren't even nineteen—still practically a child yourself. And the shame you would have faced as an unwed mother..."

He wasn't wrong.

So William had believed she would abandon James. He'd never considered other possibilities—that there might have been misunderstandings.

Just as Elise never believed William's feelings could be genuine, assuming he sought revenge for her perceived irresponsibility.

"After that, I did try finding you again," William said. "In Astonia... But you were already with Samuel. I saw you walking together at sunset, and I couldn't bring myself to approach you."

Elise remained quiet, uncertain whether to trust William's words.

"Initially, I tried forgetting you. But when I returned to Crestwood to take over Levine Holdings, there you were again. Radiant in white on your engagement day. I stood so close, yet you never once glanced my way."

During Elise's engagement party with Samuel, her thoughts had been filled only with him. There had been no room for anyone else in her heart that day.
###Chapter 246

"After the fire started, you were trapped inside. I saw Samuel rescuing Sophia and abandoning you," William said, his lips pressed tightly together. "I rushed into the flames and carried you out."

Elise was stunned by this revelation. She had always assumed a firefighter had saved her, as her hazy memories included a figure in firefighter gear.

"I snatched a helmet from a firefighter nearby. The smoke was thick—I feared I wouldn't reach you in time," William explained, as if reading her thoughts.

Elise looked down. Suddenly, she remembered seeing William in the hospital for the first time, his wrists heavily bandaged.

"You've explained a lot..." Elise turned to face William, her eyes returning to their usual icy demeanor. "Why were you so kind to me? We were strangers who met by chance, and I've done many things to hurt you. Why would you care for me?"

Elise couldn't bring herself to believe him.

Trust didn't come easily to her.

She let out a soft, bitter laugh. "Don't tell me it was love at first sight. You've encountered countless beautiful and accomplished women, Mr. Levine. How could you possibly fall for me at first sight... or rather, that first night? Why would you be so determined to have me?"

"That night was unforgettable," William stated bluntly.

Elise bit her lip.

"And yes, it was my first time," William added.

"I never took you for the conservative type," Elise retorted.

"Turns out I am," William replied, his gaze unwavering.

Elise's heart fluttered unexpectedly.

"Maybe it started years ago—" William began, but Elise cut him off.

"I don't want to hear any more of your so-called explanations. Your words may or may not be true. To me, everything ended when your grandfather announced your engagement. Anything else is pointless."

"Is it really pointless?" William's eyes seemed to redden slightly.

"Yes."

Hearing his explanations would only prolong this painful charade rather than provide closure.

Nothing would change the inevitable outcome between them.

"Perhaps this sounds selfish, but logic tells me that you and Victoria grew up together. Your engagement and eventual marriage are natural progressions. I was merely the woman who forced you into intimacy, abandoned your son, and left. Now you're simply getting revenge for my irresponsibility," Elise stated frankly.

"I told you I would break off my engagement with Victoria," William said, a tremor in his voice. "It will only take six months. Is that too much to ask?"

He was growing frustrated. Elise's stubbornness was testing his patience.

But after everything she had endured, Elise found it impossible to trust anyone with her heart.

She would rather walk away than risk being hurt again.

"It's not about whether it's too much to ask. I'm not obligated to wait for you. Maybe you're right about me—I don't accept defeat easily, and I'll fight against any force that tries to crush me. But our connection isn't deep enough for me to be fearless for your sake. You've given me no reason to trust you unconditionally."

Elise pulled her hand from William's grasp and took out her phone. Scrolling through news articles, she saw endless coverage of William and Victoria's public displays of affection.

"Look at this! All of this erodes any trust I might have in you!"
###Chapter 247

"I had no other choice given the circumstances!" William argued, his voice edged with frustration.

My defiance only makes everything worse, especially when I can't stand up to my grandfather's authority.

"Then what makes you believe you can convince me everything will be different in six months?"

Her sharp response left William speechless. He had no words that could persuade her, particularly when his actions couldn't back up his promises.

"Maybe my doubts aren't about your capabilities," Elise said coldly, "but about my own feelings. I simply don't love you enough. We should end this now before we get in too deep."

Elise reached for the door handle once more.

Enough of this emotional turmoil.

She wanted nothing more to do with him.

"Keep the car. Just have Robert return it whenever he's available."

Elise stepped out of the vehicle and walked away with determined strides. As far as she was concerned, this was the end for her and William.

But as she entered the elevator, William suddenly forced the doors open. Before she could react, he rushed inside and pinned her against the wall, crushing his lips against hers in a desperate kiss.

Elise froze for a moment before struggling violently. She pushed against his chest with both hands, but he remained immovable.

Her resistance only fueled his intensity. He kissed her with bruising force, as if trying to prove something through physical connection. His body pressed so tightly against hers that not even air could come between them.

Elise's eyes narrowed dangerously. She bit down hard on his lip.

William flinched from the pain but didn't pull away. Instead, she tasted blood as he deepened the kiss with renewed desperation.

Eventually, Elise's clenched fists relaxed. She stopped fighting, surrendering to the numbness that washed over her.

After what felt like an eternity, William finally broke the kiss.

Perhaps it was the salty taste of her tears that stopped him.

He stiffened and slowly pulled back, his eyes widening at the sight of her swollen lips. Panic flashed across his face as he reached up with trembling fingers to wipe her tears. "I'm sorry, I just—"

"It's fine," Elise replied in a flat, emotionless tone.

William's expression tightened. He watched as tears continued to stream down her face while she maintained that icy composure. Then, to his horror, she began unbuttoning her blazer.

The tailored garment fell to the floor in a heap. Her fingers moved to the buttons of her white shirt, revealing the lace of her blue bra beneath.

"Elise!" William grabbed her wrists, stopping her from undressing further.

"That first time was amazing, wasn't it?" Elise said calmly. "And now you're regretting not getting another chance?"

Rage ignited in William's eyes, turning them bloodshot with fury.

"How many times do you need?" Elise continued in that same detached voice. "I'll satisfy whatever you want. Then can we be done with this?"

BANG!

William's fist slammed into the elevator wall beside her head with violent force. His knuckles bled as he glared at her, veins bulging with anger. Elise didn't even flinch, calmly accepting that the blow could have landed on her instead.

After all, she had deliberately provoked him.

Just then, the elevator doors slid open.
###Chapter 248

Elise hadn't pressed any elevator buttons either, leaving it frozen on the underground parking level.

Elizabeth stood frozen in shock when the doors slid open.

Returning in the dead of night was terrifying enough, and the sudden metallic clang of the elevator nearly sent her fleeing back to her car.

She stared at the couple inside, completely petrified.

Only when the elevator emitted a sharp beep did Elizabeth snap back to reality. She stumbled backward, stammering, "I-I'll take the next one! Didn't see anything!"

Then the doors sealed shut.

That was absolutely terrifying!

William and Elise were actually in that elevator, and...

After catching her breath, Elizabeth suddenly registered that Elise had been crying. Had William... forced himself on her?!

With that horrifying thought, she frantically pressed the elevator button again, watching the descending numbers with growing anxiety.

Inside the elevator, William roughly tugged Elise's clothing back into place. The harsh treatment stung, but Elise bit her lip and endured in silence.

When the elevator reached their floor, William dragged Elise to her apartment door, punching in the security code with furious precision. The door swung open and he shoved her inside.

Elise stood in her living room, watching William remain framed in the doorway with no indication he intended to enter.

So... His urgency to get her inside wasn't about sleeping with her...?

Elise had already been mentally preparing to change her security codes after tonight.

"Elise." William's voice was strained, tight with emotion. "Don't let go. I desperately want a future with you. More than anything."

After speaking, he pulled the door closed.

In that final sliver of closing space, Elise thought she saw a tear tracing down his cheek.

Or maybe it was just a trick of the light, because her own vision was completely blurred by tears.

She'd always comforted herself that their relationship hadn't reached profound depths yet, that ending it wouldn't shatter her completely.

She'd been so certain she could walk away intact.

So why... Why did this hurt so excruciatingly?

Why did the memory of William's bloodshot eyes before the door closed feel like a physical wound?

Elizabeth burst into the ascending elevator, heart pounding as she watched the floor numbers climb. When the doors opened, she rushed out to rescue Elise only to freeze mid-step.

William was leaning against the doorframe.

Alone.

His head hung low, shadow concealing his expression.

Was that... a tear?

Elizabeth must have imagined it.

William never cried!

He hadn't shed tears when his parents died, though she knew he'd mourned.

Otherwise, his eyes wouldn't have been so swollen at their funeral.

She'd once believed William incapable of tears outside life-or-death situations, yet here he was, crying over Elise?

Thinking deeper, she understood. William and Elise must have fought bitterly over their impossible situation. The lavish engagement announcement between William and Victoria had been everywhere; no woman could remain unaffected, no matter how rational or detached.

If Elizabeth guessed correctly, Elise must have suggested ending their relationship.

William's heart was breaking.

Though they hadn't been together long, she knew William genuinely loved Elise.

Why else would such a stubborn bachelor fall so completely?

After meeting Elise, William had transformed into someone approachable, someone kind.

Elizabeth's heart ached for her cousin. Seeing his suffering melted her anger, making her forget her original intention to confront him about potentially assaulting Elise.

With gentle concern, she approached him cautiously. "William," she whispered softly, "would you like to get a drink with me? Talk it out?"
###Chapter 249

William kept his gaze fixed on the table, refusing to meet Elizabeth's eyes for what felt like an eternity. The silence stretched so long that Elizabeth wondered if he'd drifted off. It was four in the morning, after all.

She had just returned from a promotional event out of town and exhaustion clung to her like a second skin.

Just as she considered giving up, William slowly lifted his head. His voice was rough when he finally spoke. "Let's go."

Wait, seriously? She had only offered out of politeness. More than anything, she wanted to crawl beneath her warm duvet and surrender to sleep.

Despite her weariness, Elizabeth followed William out into the night.

They found a small street vendor still open. The silver lining was the complete absence of other customers at this ungodly hour. With her heavy disguise, nobody recognized her.

"William, maybe you should slow down," Elizabeth suggested gently, watching him pour another drink.

She couldn't just sit by while her cousin drowned himself in alcohol. He hadn't touched any of the food she'd ordered since they sat down.

The way he was drinking on an empty stomach worried her. Alcohol poisoning seemed like a real possibility.

William ignored her completely, downing another shot as if she hadn't spoken.

"Did you have a fight with Elise?" Elizabeth asked, frustration creeping into her voice when he remained silent.

William paused mid-drink but said nothing.

Elise wouldn't fight with him. She remained perpetually calm and rational, always seeking solutions.

Sometimes William wished she would scream at him. At least that would prove she cared enough to get angry.

"What's really going on between you, Elise, and Victoria?" Elizabeth pressed impatiently when William refused to answer.

With William under constant surveillance recently, she hadn't been able to get any answers from him. Now that they finally had a chance to talk properly, he chose to remain silent.

"I'm not marrying Victoria," William declared, defiance flashing in his bloodshot eyes.

"The entire country thinks you are," Elizabeth countered, her tone heavy with concern. "The Levines and the Stuarts issued a joint announcement. How could there be any mistake? Backing out now would be a public slap to their faces."

William's jaw tightened visibly.

She was right—it would be incredibly difficult.

When the engagement announcement dropped, he'd stood frozen in shock for minutes. He never expected his grandfather to agree to it. Henry had always respected his decisions.

But nobody could force William to do something he didn't want.

He would not marry Victoria. Never.

He just needed time to find a way out of this mess.

"Never mind that Elise is the victim here who probably won't forgive you," Elizabeth said seriously. "Even I, your cousin who usually supports you blindly, don't see how you can get out of this marriage. It's completely understandable why Elise is rejecting you right now."

She leaned forward across the small table. "She has no reason to trust you, even if you go to her now. Whatever you say, however sincere you are—it won't matter. Besides, Elise has been through so much pain already. Her family made it hard for her to open up because of how they treated her growing up. Then she met that bastard Samuel!"

The more Elizabeth spoke, the angrier she became.

She poured herself a glass and downed it quickly. Thinking about everything Elise had endured made her both furious and heartbroken.

Compared to Elise's struggles, Elizabeth's life had been smooth sailing. Born into wealth and privilege, spoiled by her family, successful in her career—her only struggle was relationships. She sometimes wondered if God gave her romantic troubles to balance out her otherwise charmed life.

"How can you expect her to trust anyone unconditionally after everything she's been through?" Elizabeth challenged William. "If you really want to be with her, the most important thing you need to do right now is cancel that engagement immediately. If you don't, and you still pursue Elise, she'll end up being the other woman. Do you really think she'd accept that title? Could you bear to have millions of people scorn her? Could you accept Elise losing her dignity to Victoria? Seriously, that wouldn't be fair to her at all!"
###Chapter 250

William's knuckles turned white as he clenched his wine glass.

Elizabeth's words echoed in his mind.

"To be honest, even though you're my cousin, I truly believe you don't deserve Elise. She's endured so much tragedy in her past. When she finally found the courage to love again, you delivered the final blow. I can't even imagine her pain right now! I'm terrified she might never open her heart to anyone again," Elizabeth confessed, her voice heavy with concern.

Why did fate keep testing such an incredible woman?

One devastating blow after another threatened to break her spirit.

William set down his glass with a heavy sigh.

His shoulders slumped in defeat.

"I was only..." he whispered, voice barely audible. "Afraid she would leave me."

The fear consumed him daily. After everything she'd been through, what if she closed her heart completely? What if she walked away and never looked back? The possibilities haunted his every waking moment.

He knew the solution was simple in theory.

He needed to end his engagement with Victoria.

But reality was far more complicated.

He couldn't act recklessly without considering his family's safety. He couldn't sever ties with his grandfather completely, regardless of their strained relationship. Doing so would only put Elise in greater danger.

If William lost his wealth, influence, and reputation, how could he protect Elise from those who wished her harm?

Ending the engagement required careful planning and precise timing.

Every move had to be calculated.

His greatest fear was that Elise would drift too far away before he could make things right. He wasn't even sure he could win her back once she made up her mind.

When Elise decided something was over, it stayed over.

Just like with Samuel.

Even though Samuel had been with Elise tonight, William didn't need to investigate whether they'd reconnected. Elise's character ensured she never looked back once she moved forward.

He had to reach her now.

He had to convince her to wait for him.

He couldn't let her disappointment in him become permanent.

Elizabeth listened to her cousin's confession, her heart aching for both of them.

William was always so strong and reserved. Hearing the raw pain in his voice broke her heart.

Only true love could make someone this afraid of loss. It was the kind of love that made pride irrelevant.

Elizabeth found herself at a loss for words.

Neither of them was truly at fault. They were simply victims of cruel circumstances.

She downed several glasses of wine before speaking again.

"Listen," she said firmly. "Since I live with Elise, I can keep an eye on her. I'll talk you up when I get the chance. If I notice anything important, I'll let you know immediately. You focus on ending your engagement, and leave Elise to me for now."

William looked up, his expression unreadable.

"Aren't you glad you have a cousin like me?" Elizabeth smiled proudly, patting her own shoulder.

"...Yes," William admitted softly, his usual stern demeanor melting away.

Elizabeth nearly choked on her wine. Her cousin had never praised her before. When he'd helped with her homework as a child, he'd even questioned whether she was really a Levine!

This rare moment of vulnerability from William meant more than she could express.
###Chapter 251

Elizabeth stared at the empty bottles scattered across the floor. "Are you really going to keep drinking?"

She knew William had an impressive alcohol tolerance, but his current desperate consumption worried her.

Her head throbbed intensely.

She wasn't certain if it was from lack of sleep, but she knew continuing would lead to certain drunkenness.

"I'm waiting..." William murmured.

"For who?" Elizabeth asked, confused.

"The driver."

It took Elizabeth a moment to recall they'd both been drinking and arrived in Elise's car.

Even thoroughly intoxicated, William maintained some rationality. Elizabeth couldn't decide if this was fortunate or unfortunate.

Rational people rarely made mistakes.

But such people also struggled to truly vent and relax.

With nothing else to do, Elizabeth settled beside William as he continued drinking.

He drank steadily.

Without pause.

Suddenly, William asked, "Do you still have feelings for Ethan?"

Elizabeth choked on her wine at the unexpected question.

Seeing William's disgusted expression, she quickly grabbed tissues to clean the spilled wine from the table. After wiping her mouth, she stammered, "What are you talking about?"

She would never admit it, even under threat.

William's intense gaze made Elizabeth fidget uncomfortably. His piercing eyes seemed to see right through her.

She felt like a small rabbit cornered by a predator.

"...I don't like him anymore," Elizabeth denied, though her words implied past affection.

"I suggest you abandon those thoughts completely," William said seriously.

Was he really her cousin? Why did he treat her so differently than she treated him during his heartbreak?

She'd drunk with him, comforted his fears, even advised him on winning back his girlfriend.

Yet when it came to her situation, he showed no consideration, bluntly telling her to give up.

She seemed to be on the losing end of this familial relationship.

Though that was somewhat accurate.

Couldn't William at least offer some comfort?

Eight years of unrequited love! Without strong mental fortitude, how could she have endured it?

"You and Ethan are impossible," William stated, unknowingly twisting the knife in Elizabeth's heart.

"Yes, I understand," Elizabeth replied emotionally. "Just like you and Elise are impossible. Apparently, I don't deserve love!"

William noticed the redness around Elizabeth's eyes and pursed his lips. He knew about her long-standing affection for Ethan but rarely saw her cry, always mistaking her cheerful demeanor for emotional flippancy. He'd assumed she hadn't moved on simply because she hadn't found anyone better.

He suddenly realized he didn't understand Elizabeth as well as he thought.

"You'll find someone better eventually," William said, his voice noticeably softer.

"We'll leave it to fate," Elizabeth responded calmly, blinking away her tears. The alcohol made her emotions more volatile than usual. Mentioning Ethan unleashed a tidal wave of feelings she couldn't control.

William didn't press further. His gaze shifted to something behind her. Elizabeth assumed the driver had arrived and stood to help her cousin. She rose too quickly, dizziness overwhelming her, and she nearly collapsed until a strong arm steadied her.

"Thank—" Elizabeth looked up into Ethan's face.

Hadn't William said he was waiting for the driver?

Why was Ethan here instead?

Elizabeth pulled her arm back gently, and Ethan released her. "Be careful," he said softly.

Elizabeth couldn't help but clench her fist tightly.
###Chapter 252

Ethan finally let Elizabeth go.
He didn't stay.
He went straight to William.

"Can you walk?" he asked.
It was obvious William had consumed a significant amount of alcohol.

"Yes."
"Let's go," Ethan said.

William stood.
He swayed slightly.
Ethan had to step forward and support him, guiding him to the passenger seat of the car.
Elizabeth trailed behind them.
She hesitated, unsure if she should get in the car as well.

"William mentioned this car belongs to Elise," Ethan said, answering her unspoken question. "I need to return it. I'll drive you back."

Elizabeth nodded.
She slipped into the back seat.

The drive was smooth.
Despite the late hour and the empty roads, Ethan maintained a slow, steady speed.
He was always a gentle man.
Perhaps it was because he grew up relying on the charity of others; she rarely, if ever, saw him lose his temper.
Elizabeth wasn't entirely sure what had drawn her to him in the first place.
She couldn't even recall the specific reason she'd fallen in love with Ethan.
Among her social circle, there were men more handsome, more accomplished, and certainly wealthier than him.
She'd come to accept it as fate—a twist of destiny arranged by God, something unavoidable.

Suddenly, the car slowed and pulled over to the curb.

"What are you doing?" Elizabeth asked, frowning.

"William asked me to pick up some medication," Ethan replied. "Wait here a moment. I won't be long."

Elizabeth was surprised.
Who was hurt? Was it serious enough to require an immediate stop for medicine?
She hadn't noticed any visible injuries on William earlier.
Wait… his hand. The back of his hand had looked particularly red and swollen.
But the streetlights were dim, and she hadn't gotten a clear look.

Ethan returned with a small pharmacy bag.
He drove to Chateau Emporio first.

"Give me a moment. I'll just see William inside."
"Of course."

Elizabeth was exhausted.
She couldn't stop yawning.
By the time Ethan returned from escorting William to his door, she had fallen asleep in the back seat.
The soft, rhythmic sound of her breathing indicated a deep sleep.

Ethan didn't disturb her.
He drove with extra care, ensuring the ride was smooth and wouldn't jostle her awake.

Arriving at the underground parking garage, Ethan parked the car and walked to the back seat.
He found Elizabeth had shifted position, now lying across the seat.
Her mouth was slightly agape, and a tiny trickle of drool escaped the corner of her lips.
She was deeply, profoundly asleep.

The corner of Ethan's mouth quirked upwards in a faint smile.
It reminded him of when they were younger, of how she would sleep soundly on his shoulder back then.
Everything had seemed so simple, so perfect, in those days.

Ethan bent down and carefully gathered Elizabeth into his arms.
She had always been a heavy sleeper, even as a child.
He remembered nights with thunderstorms, when he would wake up worried she might be frightened by the noise.
He'd tiptoe into her room, only to find her sprawled across the bed, completely dead to the world, utterly unbothered by the crashing thunder outside.
She hadn't changed.
Being picked up didn't even stir her; she continued her soft, gentle snoring.

It was fortunate there were no paparazzi around to capture her in this undignified state.

Ethan carried Elizabeth to the elevator and rode up to Elise's floor.
He rang the doorbell.

Elise answered almost immediately.
She didn't look like she'd been sleeping.
Perhaps she wasn't as unaffected by her encounter with William as Ethan had initially assumed.

"Mr. Parker?" Elise was clearly surprised to find Ethan at her door, holding a sleeping Elizabeth.
She had assumed Elizabeth had forgotten the security code again—a frequent occurrence.
Periodically, Elizabeth would ask her to repeat it.
Furthermore, Elizabeth's hands were often dry, and her skin prone to peeling, making fingerprint recognition an unreliable option for her.
###Chapter 253

"My apologies for the intrusion," Ethan said, his voice soft. "Elizabeth fell asleep in the car."

"It's quite alright," Elise began, then hesitated. "Would you mind carrying her inside?"

Ethan didn't question her further. He simply followed Elise through the quiet house, carefully placing Elizabeth in Elise's bed. The sleeping woman didn't stir throughout the entire process.

After settling Elizabeth, Ethan stepped out of the room with Elise close behind.

In the living room, he handed her a small paper bag. "William asked me to give this to you."

Elise's eyes widened slightly in recognition. She remembered the faint bruise forming on her neck from Brandon's rough handling earlier that evening. She hadn't realized William had noticed.

"Thank you," she said, accepting the package.

She took it not because she wanted William's concern, but because she didn't wish to make things awkward for Ethan or create unnecessary drama.

Ethan understood exactly what she was thinking. Her calm acceptance spoke volumes about her emotional distance. He seriously doubted William would ever win her back at this rate.

"There are some hangover pills in there too," Ethan added. "If Elizabeth wakes up with a headache tomorrow, make sure she takes them."

"Of course," Elise promised. Then, after a thoughtful pause, she added, "If you can't commit to a future with her, please keep your distance."

Ethan's breath caught in his throat.

"Hope only leads to disappointment when there's no possibility of fulfillment," Elise stated bluntly.

"You're right," Ethan acknowledged with a nod.

He understood exactly what she meant. "I should be going now."

"Goodnight," Elise replied with a polite smile.

They were practically strangers, having only met a handful of times. Tonight's encounter felt more strained than any previous meeting.

Perhaps because of Elizabeth.

Or perhaps because of William.

Either way, Ethan made a quick exit. Elise returned to her bedroom to find Elizabeth still sleeping soundly.

In fact, her snoring had grown even louder.

Elise felt a pang of envy at Elizabeth's carefree nature. Even with a broken heart, she could sleep so deeply, completely undisturbed by the world's troubles.

The next day, Elizabeth woke with a pounding headache. She knew immediately this would be a difficult day—not enough sleep combined with last night's excessive drinking had taken its toll.

She checked her phone and groaned. It was already three in the afternoon.

She vaguely remembered coming back to talk to Elise, to comfort her friend and maybe put in a good word for William. But she had no memory of how she actually got home.

Elizabeth dragged herself out of bed, her eyes puffy as she stumbled into the living room. Scrolling through her phone messages, she headed to the kitchen for some cold water. As she reached the refrigerator, she spotted a note stuck to it.

The message read: Didn't know when you'd wake up, so I didn't prepare breakfast. Please order food when you're up. If you have a headache, take the hangover pills on the table. Your period is due soon—avoid cold drinks.

Elise always knew how to touch Elizabeth's heart.

Elizabeth thought if Elise were a man, or if she herself were one, she would have married Elise without hesitation.

Turning away from the fridge, she grabbed a glass, filled it with warm water from the tap, and swallowed one of the pills from the table. Then she texted Elise. [What time did you leave this morning?]

[Seven.]

[That means you barely slept!] Elizabeth typed, concerned.

[I got some rest. Make sure you eat something. I have a meeting starting soon.]

[Take care of yourself first.] Elizabeth admonished.

[I will.]
###Chapter 254

Elise wasn't physically beside her, but Elizabeth could practically sense the overwhelming workload Elise was buried under.

Elizabeth released a heavy sigh.

Perhaps drowning herself in work was Elise's way of forgetting all the distressing matters. She had always been like this, ever since those earlier days. Her career had consumed her completely, leaving little room for anything else.

Elise set her phone aside and made her way to the conference room. Today's meeting was focused on the upcoming bid, and the afternoon would be dedicated to planning the launch of their new brand line.

A business must constantly innovate to thrive.

By the time the meeting concluded, office hours had ended. Yet, Elise remained at her desk, steadily working through the mountain of internal requests awaiting her attention.

As she worked in focused silence, her phone vibrated.

She glanced at the screen and declined the call. Moments later, it rang again. This time, it was an unknown number.

After a brief hesitation, she answered. "Hello?"

"Miss Scott? This is the Crestwood Police Department. We require your assistance in clarifying some details regarding the assault incident involving Brandon Miller and Samuel Mitchell last night. Please come down to the station."

"Understood," Elise replied. "Should I come now?"

"Immediately would be best."

"I'll be there shortly," Elise said before ending the call. She gathered her work materials and exited her office.

Matthew was surprised to see her leaving; she rarely departed early.

"Miss Scott."

"My computer remains on. Numerous internal requests are still pending. I need you to handle them. Contact me if anything requires my direct input."

"Of course," Matthew responded promptly.

Elise hailed a cab to the police station. She was an cautious driver at the best of times, and after a largely sleepless night, she had deemed it safer not to drive herself to work, fearing fatigue would impair her.

Upon arriving at the station, she found Samuel was already there.

She had ignored his earlier call, unwilling to engage with him. She had been so swamped with work that the previous night's altercation with Brandon had nearly slipped her mind.

"Miss Scott, Mr. Mitchell. Please accompany me to the office. We have a few points we need you both to clarify."

"Certainly," Elise agreed readily, fully prepared to cooperate.

She was determined to press charges against Brandon. On this matter, there was no room for negotiation. It was precisely why she had ignored all calls from Margaret since Brandon's detention.

An eye for an eye. That was a principle Elise had always lived by.

She spent over an hour at the station. As she and Samuel stepped outside, he spoke up.

"Let me drive you back. I have my car."

"That won't be necessary," Elise declined.

"Finding a cab at this hour will be difficult."

"I'm in no particular hurry."

"Then have dinner with me?" Samuel suggested.

Before she could refuse, he continued, "I don't mean anything by it. After everything Mitchell Corp has been through recently, I've come to realize how much you did for me back then. I deeply regret my actions toward you. I know it's far too late for apologies, but I just want one chance to express my remorse sincerely. After this, I promise I won't bother you again."

"That really isn't necessary," Elise refused, her tone leaving no room for doubt.

Samuel clenched his jaw, frustration simmering. He hadn't anticipated her being so utterly cold. It made him question whether her past feelings for him had ever been genuine at all. Here he was, humbling himself, and she remained so detached, so unmoved.

"Samuel, if you truly felt any regret or guilt toward me, you wouldn't be pestering me now. This sudden kindness can only mean you have an ulterior motive."

Samuel flushed with anger, exposed by her blunt words.

"I know exactly what you're thinking, and I won't give you any opportunity to take advantage of the situation," Elise stated flatly before turning to leave.

Rage burned in Samuel's eyes, his body trembling with the effort to contain it.

Just as he felt his control was about to shatter, Elise suddenly collapsed right before him.
###Chapter 255

Elise never imagined she would actually faint.

She had been awake nearly all night, pushing herself beyond her limits. Yet she believed she could endure it.

But she collapsed right in front of Samuel Mitchell, of all people.

As darkness swallowed her, a terrible premonition washed over her.

Elizabeth waited at home for Elise to return from work. She had cleared her entire schedule today. She’d ordered an incredibly nutritious meal from Crestwood's finest private chef, determined to provide Elise with some much-needed nourishment. Elise had seemed to be losing weight lately. Her wrists were so slender they looked fragile enough to snap.

Elizabeth didn't understand why her heart ached so fiercely seeing Elise like this. Elise was a strong woman, tougher and more radiant than Elizabeth could ever be. Yet, fate had been unkind. It was true what they said—sometimes, the more beautiful the flower, the more tragic its fate.

She waited until half-past seven. Anxiety began to gnaw at her. There was still no sign of Elise. Elise hadn't rested properly last night; she couldn't possibly be working through another night, could she?

Did Elise think she was invincible?

The thought infuriated Elizabeth. She snatched her phone and called Elise, only to find the line disconnected. The phone was off.

That was impossible.

Elise's phone was always on. Twenty-four hours a day, seven days a week. The possibility of a dead battery was equally unlikely; Elise was meticulously careful about such things.

Impatience surged through Elizabeth. She was not one to wait passively. Without a second thought, she grabbed Elise's car keys and drove straight to Stellar Group.

She burst into Elise's office, throwing the door open.

Matthew jumped at the sudden intrusion. He had been sorting through internal requests on Elise's computer, stuck on one he couldn't resolve. He'd sent her a message but received no reply and was about to call her when the door flew open.

His eyes widened. W-Was that his favorite idol, Elizabeth York?! In the flesh? Was he really seeing her up close?

His idol rushed toward him. "Where is Elise?"

Matthew took a moment to steady his frantic nerves. "Miss Scott left the office some time ago."

Elizabeth's heart hammered against her ribs. "Did she have any business dinners tonight? Any appointments?"

"No. There was a dinner, but she said she wasn't feeling well and canceled it," Matthew replied hastily.

"Then where is she?!" Elizabeth's voice rose, sharp with panic.

A dreadful feeling solidified in her gut. She knew Elise had no other close friends. Elise's personal life was an open book to her. Elise was also intensely sensible; she wouldn't recklessly jeopardize her health. If she was overworked, she would have come straight home to rest.

Most importantly, Elise would never, ever turn off her phone without a compelling reason.

"What's wrong?" Matthew asked, his worry growing as he saw his idol's face flush with distress.

"Nothing."

Elizabeth forced herself to focus, mentally chastising her panic. She turned on her heel without another word and hurried out.

Matthew didn't get a chance to speak. He had wanted an autograph...

Elizabeth slid back into the car, ignition roaring to life. She called William as she sped away from the building.

"William, Elise is missing!"

On the other end, William's slender fingers stilled over his work. His voice was harsh. "What?"

"Elise is missing! I was waiting for her at home for dinner, and she never showed up! I called her, but her phone is off. I went to her office, and her secretary said she left work a while ago. He confirmed there were no business engagements. William, you know she doesn't have other friends. She wouldn't just disappear—"

The call cut off abruptly.

Tears welled in Elizabeth's eyes. She clutched her phone tightly, her knuckles turning white.

Why did it feel like Elise was constantly walking into danger?

Please, she prayed silently, don't let the world be so cruel to her. Not again.
###Chapter 256

In the General Manager's office at Levine Holdings, William ended his call with Elizabeth and immediately dialed another number. "Ethan, can you discreetly ask Amanda if Samuel is home?"

"What's going on?" Ethan's voice held concern.

"Just find out and text me. I only need that answer," William insisted urgently before disconnecting.

He then dialed another contact. "I need you to scan all city surveillance cameras immediately. Locate Elise's movements after she left work tonight. Initiate a city-wide search for any information about her current whereabouts. Report directly to me with any findings. Do not take action without my explicit authorization."

"Understood, sir," the voice responded respectfully.

As William started to hang up, a sudden thought made his eyes narrow dangerously. "Wait. One more thing—trace the last call received on Elise's phone before she disappeared."

"Right away, sir."

The moment he ended that call, Ethan's message appeared.

[Samuel isn't home.]

William's knuckles turned white as he clenched his fists, his expression hardening into something terrifying.

If Samuel had dared to touch her, he'd just signed his own death warrant.

William had held back from acquiring Mitchell Corp out of consideration for Ethan's relationship with Amanda. He'd shown restraint for family connections.

But this time...

William's face contorted into a frightening mask of rage.

Even Samuel Wilson, standing nearby, didn't dare ask questions after seeing that expression. Whatever had happened clearly involved Mrs. Levine, and when it came to her, William became unpredictable.

Samuel Wilson sighed inwardly. His boss excelled at everything—except when it came to matters of the heart.

Elise awoke to a dizzying headache. Low blood sugar, she guessed—she hadn't eaten properly all day. Exhaustion had stolen her appetite earlier.

"You're finally awake," a familiar male voice said.

Elise blinked until her vision cleared, revealing Samuel Mitchell sitting before her.

Her heart hammered against her ribs.

Despite her calm exterior, primal fear surged through her veins.

She tried to move, only then realizing she was tied to an unfamiliar bed. This wasn't a hotel or Mitchell Manor.

Elise forced calm into her voice. "Samuel, do you realize this is kidnapping?"

He laughed coldly, studying her beautiful face with obsessive intensity. Her composure infuriated him—how could she remain so collected even now?

He'd suppressed his rage toward this woman for far too long.

Not just because she'd coldly refused to help save Mitchell Corp, but because she'd dared to stop loving him as if flipping a switch. That was unacceptable.

He would never allow Elise to love anyone else.

"If I'm doing this, I've already covered my tracks. I'm not stupid enough to leave evidence for you to use against me, Elise. What happens between us will simply be... mutual passion," Samuel said with a triumphant smile.

He reached out to caress her cheek, making her stomach churn with revulsion. Elise wondered how she'd ever been blind enough to love this man.

She'd given him everything, only to be betrayed by the very hand she'd fed.
###Chapter 257

Samuel's twisted features were inches from Elise's face.

She met his gaze with icy detachment.

A wave of humiliation churned in her stomach, making her feel sick.

Samuel seemed to relish her discomfort. A cruel smile twisted his lips. "Do you regret how you treated me?" he sneered. "I begged you. I groveled for a chance to work with you, and you showed me no mercy! Do you regret it now?"

"The only thing I regret is ever loving you!" Elise spat, her voice a venomous hiss. Her cold eyes locked with his. "The memory makes my skin crawl. It makes me want to vomit!"

"Elise Scott!"

Samuel's fists clenched, his knuckles turning white. Her words had struck a nerve.

In a swift, brutal motion, his hand shot out and closed around her throat.

Pain exploded in her neck. The pressure intensified, cutting off her air.

If she died by Samuel's hands, her soul would never find peace!

Yet, she refused to beg. Her eyes, burning with furious tears, remained fixed on his face. If this was the end, she would memorize every grotesque detail of his features. If there was an afterlife, she would haunt him until he paid in blood.

Suddenly, Samuel released her.

No matter how much he hated the defiant hatred in her eyes, he didn't have the courage to kill.

He wouldn't throw his own life away for her.

Elise gasped, coughing violently as air flooded back into her lungs. For a terrifying moment, she had truly believed she was going to die, even though logic screamed that Samuel was too much of a coward.

He just wanted to taste the power of revenge. She wouldn't give him that satisfaction.

"Elise, don't push me to kill you," Samuel threatened, his voice low and dangerous.

Elise ignored him. She wouldn't waste another breath on this man.

Samuel stood up and moved away from her. He pulled a small, innocuous-looking pill from his pocket and held it out for her to see.

Elise's eyes narrowed on the pill. A flicker of raw panic shot through her, betraying her forced calm.

"Scared now?" Samuel mocked.

"What are you planning to do?"

"Do?" He let out a dark, lust-filled laugh. "Elise, we were together for three years, and I never laid a finger on you. Yet, you jumped into William's bed so easily! Don't you think you owe me some payback for trampling all over my pride?"

Rage boiled inside her. "You didn't touch me because Sophia kept you more than satisfied! Did you ever think about me when you were playing the cheating scumbag?!"

"If you hadn't been so cold, could Sophia have seduced me? Our breakup is your fault! You're the one who shut me out! Your stubbornness is what pushed me toward Sophia! But I should thank you. Your icy heart led me to a woman who loves me a thousand times more than you ever could!" Samuel finished, his voice rising to an agitated shout.
###Chapter 258

Samuel's words carried a defensive edge, as if trying to convince himself more than her.

"What? Sophia loves you?" Elise let out a derisive laugh. "Sophia only covets what belongs to me. She feels compelled to take everything I desire."

"Don't waste your breath trying to poison my relationship with Sophia—"

"Then explain this," Elise interrupted sharply. "If Sophia loves you so deeply, why are the Scotts remaining passive while Mitchell Corp faces collapse? Why hasn't she secured the financial backing you desperately need?"

"Your father Benjamin is the one being unreasonable! Sophia actually argued with him, trying to persuade him to help me," Samuel retorted defensively.

"Oh? Really?" Elise's tone dripped with skepticism. "And what was the outcome of her noble efforts?"

"Sophia can't possibly override Benjamin's decisions!"

"Samuel, let me be blunt... Your life wouldn't have spiraled into this ruin if you'd never met Sophia."

"Elise!" Samuel's pride bristled at her cutting words.

"Benjamin is a man who values his public image above all else," Elise reasoned calmly. "While you're engaged to his daughter, he would never openly refuse assistance to you. The scandal would damage his reputation irreparably. In our world, trust and credibility are everything. Without them, how can anyone maintain their standing?" She leaned forward slightly. "If Sophia were genuinely committed to helping you, Benjamin would have provided the financial support by now. The truth is, she doesn't want to help you. She's using this situation to create distance between you."

"Do you honestly expect me to believe your poisonous lies?" Samuel laughed contemptuously. "You're just trying to manipulate me because you're jealous of what Sophia and I share! Let me make this clear, Elise. When Sophia learned about my family's financial crisis and that her father refused to help, she was the one who suggested I approach you. She told me you have the resources I need, and that I should find a way to make you cooperate! She knew we had history, yet she was willing to endure that discomfort to help me. There will never be another woman who loves me as selflessly as Sophia does!"

"Selflessly?" Elise couldn't contain her bitter laughter. The man's delusion was both pitiable and amusing. "Samuel, have you ever stopped to consider? Would someone who truly loves you encourage you to compromise your integrity with another woman? Look at it from another angle. If the Scotts were in trouble, and Sophia used her body to secure financial help from another man, could you accept that?"

Samuel fell silent, rendered speechless by her pointed question. After a long pause, he finally managed, "...Sophia would never betray me."

"I'll spell it out for you. She's setting up this entire charade to play the victim," Elise explained slowly, as if addressing a child. "Once something happens between us, Sophia will use it as justification. She'll claim you cheated first, then righteously break off the engagement. The Scotts will be able to reject your requests for help while maintaining their moral high ground. It's a simple, elegant plan. How can you not see through it?"

Samuel remained quiet, the silence stretching between them.

Elise's argument was logically sound, leaving no obvious flaws to challenge. Every piece fit together perfectly.

No.

He refused to believe Sophia would betray him like this.

Sophia's feelings for him were genuine, weren't they?

She had endured so much suffering when he was still with Elise. In recent years, she had been nothing but devoted and obedient. He shouldn't let Elise's poisonous words cloud his judgment!

Elise was clearly saying all this to manipulate him into letting her go.

No one was coming to rescue her, so she was trying to save herself.

Elise had always been clever—he knew that better than anyone. He wouldn't fall for her schemes!

After several moments of internal struggle, Samuel finally sneered, "Save your breath, Elise. Sophia loves me to the point of self-sacrifice. That's a truth nothing can shake. Did you really think I'd believe your pathetic lies?"

Elise clenched her fists, feeling waves of helplessness and despair wash over her.

Samuel had truly lost his mind to Sophia's manipulation.

"I know exactly what you're trying to do—make me release you. Unfortunately for you, Elise, I'm not that easily fooled!" Samuel mocked.

"No, you're the biggest fool I've ever known!" Elise snapped back.

"Enough!" Samuel had reached his limit with Elise's insults. He forced the pill into her mouth.

Swallow it!
###Chapter 259

The small white pill landed on Elise's tongue.
She clenched her teeth, refusing to swallow.
Anything from Samuel couldn't be good.

They remained locked in a tense standoff.

Suddenly, Samuel pinched her nose shut.

Elise pressed her lips together.

"I need to go out for a while."

"Huh? Why?"

"I have to buy William some clothes."

Elizabeth's eyes widened dramatically; her expression clearly questioned what exactly Elise had done to William overnight.

Well, Elise had definitely done something.

Just...

Not what Elizabeth was imagining.

Elise didn't want to explain either. How could she expect others to believe something she herself found difficult to accept? So she remained silent and simply walked out of the room.

Elizabeth watched Elise's retreating figure.

Somehow, Elise seemed to be radiating a certain...alluring energy today.

Women who were thoroughly satisfied overnight typically glowed like rare beauties the next morning, right?

Elizabeth quickly rushed into the room and approached her cousin with a knowing look.

"William...You two made up, didn't you?"

She appeared even more excited than the people who had supposedly been intimate last night.

"No," William said, pulling the blankets tighter around himself.

"No?"

Elizabeth repeated in disbelief.

"That can't be! Didn't you satisfy her last night?!"

Elizabeth couldn't help but scan William from head to toe with judgmental eyes, despite him being completely covered.

"What nonsense are you thinking about, Elizabeth?!" William scolded, clearly annoyed.

"You and Elise were alone together all night, and she was drugged. What was I supposed to think?" Elizabeth exclaimed, visibly frustrated with her cousin's behavior.

William didn't offer much explanation and said impatiently, "It's really not what you're imagining."

Elizabeth swallowed the words on the tip of her tongue.

William had no patience for anyone except Elise.

If she weren't his cousin, she would have cut ties with him long ago.

"So, how was it? Was it passionate and wild-"

Elizabeth began to fantasize.

"Elizabeth York! What's wrong with you? You're too young for these thoughts!"

Elizabeth shot back, clearly offended.

Then she added deliberately, "If I wanted to, I could have a child old enough to help with household chores by now!"

###Chapter 267

Elizabeth suddenly remembered Elise mentioning she was going out to buy William some clothes.

"Try these on. See if they fit," Elise said, handing the clothes to William.

William saw a black windbreaker, a crisp white shirt, khaki trousers, and black boxer briefs; she'd even remembered to include a pair of black socks.

He threw the covers aside, and Elise spun around instantly. "Are you an exhibitionist?" she exclaimed, her cheeks flushing. Did this man have no shame? "Only for you, Miss Scott," William replied smoothly.

Seriously? He said it as if it were some kind of privilege!

"Which part of me haven't you seen already, Miss Scott?" William added with a teasing smile.

Elise clenched her fists, biting her lip in frustration. She moved to leave, but William's voice stopped her.

"Don't open the door yet, Miss Scott. I'm not fully dressed. Elizabeth is outside. It would be... inappropriate if she saw anything."

He knew it was inappropriate, yet he chose to change right here instead of using the walk-in closet? Elise took a deep breath, forcing herself to remain calm.

After a moment, William finally announced, "I'm done."

Elise turned slowly, half-afraid of what she might see. Her eyes widened slightly as she took in the man before her. She had simply grabbed the first suitable outfit she saw at the mall, letting a store clerk pick something for her. She hadn't expected that even in simple casual wear, William would look this devastatingly handsome.

Did God spend extra time sculpting this man?!

"Does it look that bad?" William asked, noting her expression.

"No, it's fine," Elise said quickly, her usual mask of indifference sliding back into place.

"Does everything fit?"

"Yes," William replied. Without hesitation, he added, "You have an impeccable grasp of my measurements, especially my trouser size—"

Elise didn't wait for him to finish. She turned on her heel and marched out of the room.

Give him an inch, and he'd take a mile! Elizabeth was bored out of her mind, having waited so long she thought she might perish from sheer tedium. She'd been slightly worried they might start their morning... no, afternoon activities again. Thank goodness they emerged. She'd been starving all day, waiting to eat with them.
###Chapter 268

"At last! I'm starving!"

Elizabeth exclaimed with excitement as she approached them.

Elise felt a pang of guilt. She knew Elizabeth had been patiently waiting for them, going without food all day just to share the meal together.

Elizabeth's enthusiasm was palpable, but William's gaze remained fixed on Elise. Just then, his stomach emitted a loud, unmistakable growl.

William had been suppressing a smile, but the corners of his lips curved upward at her response.

Elizabeth also wore a knowing smile at Elise's words.

Somehow, Elise felt like she had just fallen into a carefully laid trap by the two of them.

Elizabeth, being the most animated of the trio, immediately started pointing out various dishes. "This herbal chicken soup is for you, Elise. It replenishes energy and strengthens kidney function.
