She would never consider leaving James alone at home. Taking his small hand, she turned to William. "You can go back first if you have work. I'll take good care of Jamie."

William remained silent.

Elise didn't press further.

Instead, she led James toward the nearby supermarket. William followed closely behind them.

Several times, Elise considered telling him to leave, but ultimately kept quiet.

The three of them moved through the grocery aisles together.

"Mommy, what are we buying?"

James bubbled with enthusiasm. He had never been to a regular supermarket before. Their home was always fully stocked, with Charles ensuring they never lacked anything. This was his first experience seeing rows of colorful products lining the shelves.

"I'll buy whatever you'd like to eat."

"I want fish, lobster, crab..."

James listed numerous seafood items.

A smile touched Elise's lips.

Their tastes aligned perfectly.

She headed directly to the seafood section, selecting various fresh catches.

After consideration, she decided seafood alone wasn't sufficient. She added fresh vegetables, premium steak, and some snacks to their cart.

Finished, she turned to William. "Could you watch Jamie for a moment? I need to pick up some personal items. Please wait for me at the checkout."

"Alright."

Elise walked away, returning with several packages of sanitary pads in her hands.

William and James were already scanning items at the self-checkout counter.

Spotting her approach, William extended his hand. "Let me check those out with the rest."

Elise hesitated briefly. William's brow furrowed slightly.

Several customers waited behind them in line.

Feeling the impatient stares, Elise reluctantly handed the sanitary pads to William.

He examined the packages in his hands.

Elise felt such embarrassment she had to look away.

"Sir, we're currently running a promotion on sanitary pads," the cashier explained cheerfully. "With every purchase, you receive a complimentary box of condoms. We have three varieties available. Please choose one."

"No, thank you," William declined.

"But they're completely free," the cashier insisted. "It's a manufacturer promotion. If you like them, you can purchase from this brand again later. Just indicate your preferred type. Oh, and please note the different sizes available!"

Elise noticed William's unusual discomfort. His ears turned faintly pink. She felt an unexpected satisfaction at his predicament.

Serves him right for tagging along uninvited. Just as a smirk touched her lips, William turned to her. "Which one do you prefer?"

The cashier's gaze shifted to Elise. Her cheeks flushed crimson.

A young couple stood directly behind them. The woman remarked, "Wow, not only is he handsome, but he's considerate enough to ask his girlfriend's preference. Unlike you, always buying whatever you like without checking if I'm comfortable with it..."

"Don't you like it too?" the man retorted, clearly annoyed by the comparison.

"Hmph." The woman refused to acknowledge his point.

"Fine, I'll let you choose next time. We'll buy whatever you prefer, okay?"

The man finally conceded.

Listening to their conversation, Elise wished the ground would swallow her whole.

"Miss, please help your husband select a type," the cashier urged, adding, "Your husband is so thoughtful. I rarely see husbands who care about their wives' opinions."

Elise glanced at William, noticing the smug smile playing on his lips.

She felt utterly speechless.

Her earlier amusement at his discomfort had backfired spectacularly.

Closing her eyes briefly, Elise randomly pointed at one box. "This one, please."

"Excellent choice."

The cashier promptly packed it with their other items.

After completing payment, the three exited the supermarket.

Elise rarely visited grocery stores and suddenly realized she might have purchased too much. William carried two large bags filled to capacity.

"Let me take those."

Elise stopped in front of William. "I don't want to inconvenience you further."

"Are you certain?" William asked Elise, his gaze intense.
###Chapter 362

Elise frowned deeply.

What could possibly be uncertain about this? William didn't resist and handed the two shopping bags to her.

As she took them, she immediately had to set them down on the floor.

She hadn't anticipated their weight. Lifting them without any mental preparation was impossible. She had watched William carry them with such effortless ease...

"Ms. Scott, still determined to put on a brave front?" William inquired, a hint of amusement in his voice.

Elise pressed her lips together. She hadn't consciously bought that many items while shopping, yet the evidence was undeniably heavy in front of her.

William decided not to push the issue further.

He simply retrieved the two bags from her and strode ahead.

After a brief hesitation, Elise took James's small hand and followed him into the apartment.

Once inside, Elise began unpacking the groceries. It was then she discovered the box of condoms.

She glanced toward the living room where William was seated with James, both engrossed in a cartoon. How was she supposed to hand these over to him?

"Do you require assistance?" William turned his head, his gaze meeting hers.

It was uncanny, as if he possessed a sixth sense for her thoughts, no matter the situation.

Elise gave a slight nod.

William rose from the couch and walked over to her.

Elise promptly held out the box to him.

William's eyes dropped to the item in her hand.

"I thought you preferred this brand?" Elise averted her gaze, feeling a flush of heat on her cheeks.

"I have no use for them."

"Neither do I."

His response was blunt and direct.

Elise looked at him, comprehension dawning instantly.

William and Victoria probably didn't need them either.

They weren't getting any younger. Starting a family was likely part of their marital plans.

"I'll dispose of them, then," Elise said, moving to toss the box into the nearby trash bin.

"Leave them. One never knows when they might come in handy." William suddenly reached out and took the box from her grasp.

Elise's brow furrowed in confusion.

William pocketed the box and walked away without another word. Elise didn't press the matter. They were free, after all. He could do whatever he pleased with them.

After finishing the unpacking, Elise moved to the kitchen to start dinner preparations.

Just as she was deciding on a menu, William entered the kitchen again.

"I can manage on my own..."

"It's almost eight. Jamie is hungry," William stated flatly.

Elise glanced at the clock. He was right.

"It will be quicker if I cook," William suggested. "You can observe. Your skills might improve faster that way."

Elise took a quiet, steadying breath and nodded in agreement.

In her open-plan kitchen, Elise became William's sous-chef.

William patiently explained each step of the cooking process as he worked.

They moved together with a surprising, seamless rhythm.

Before long, three flavorful dishes and a steaming bowl of soup adorned the dining table.

William took a seat and began eating without any ceremony.

"Mommy, this food you made is amazing," James chirped, eager to compliment his mother.

Before Elise could respond, William spoke. "Then eat plenty. Don't let your mother's hard work be for nothing."

"I'm going to have two whole big bowls!" James declared proudly.

A profound warmth spread through Elise's chest.

No matter how cold or distant the outside world seemed, with James in her life, every single day felt like a radiant, hopeful spring morning.
###Chapter 363

"Mommy, I love seeing our family like this," James said suddenly.

Elise paused for a moment.

"Like what?"

"The three of us together," James explained naturally. "Going shopping, cooking dinner, and eating together."

Elise pressed her lips together.

She didn't know how to respond to James.

"Don't you like it too, Mommy?" James blinked his innocent eyes at her.

"Of course." Elise smiled in agreement. "Mommy loves whatever Jamie loves."

"Mommy, you're the best person in the whole world! I love you!" James said sweetly.

Elise wondered how someone as reserved and serious as William could raise such an expressive child like James.

After dinner, Elise busied herself washing dishes.

William had offered to help, but she firmly declined. She clearly didn't want him getting too close.

The father and son sat together on the sofa. James suddenly asked with seriousness, "Daddy, how much longer until you win Mommy over?"

William had been watching Elise in the open kitchen when his son's question pulled his attention. He turned to look at James.

"I can't keep pretending much longer."

William couldn't help but chuckle.

James acted completely different around Elise compared to his usual behavior at home.

"If you don't succeed soon, I won't be able to help you anymore," James complained.

"I'm doing my best."

William had no defense against his son's criticism.

"Is it really that hard to chase girls? So many girls at my school like me. I don't even have to try," James said sulkily.

He had always held his father in high esteem. Why was William so slow about this one thing?

"I used to think that too, until I met your mother," William explained. "Everyone has their weakness. Do you understand?"

"No, I don't."

"You will when you meet the right person."

"That won't happen to me," James declared confidently. "So many girls like me already. When it's time to get married, I'll just pick one and that's it."

William didn't bother arguing.

What did children know about love? Besides, James might actually be lucky enough to find his perfect match among his admirers, unlike William, whose path was much more complicated.

When Elise finished the dishes, she returned to the living room to find James having an intense conversation with William.

William showed no signs of leaving. She glanced at her watch. "It's getting late. You should head home. Jamie and I are going to bed soon."

"Alright."

William nodded unexpectedly cooperatively.

Elise walked him to the door.

"Thank you," she suddenly said to him.

William looked slightly surprised.
###Chapter 364

"I appreciate your assistance with the legal proceedings," Elise stated clearly.

William was the person who uncovered all the crucial evidence, particularly the proof showing the finance department had hired someone to forge her signature on those documents.

"I was the cause of everything that happened. You don't owe me any thanks."

Elise didn't say anything more. She genuinely hoped from the depths of her soul that William and Victoria would marry soon, completely severing any remaining connection between her and William.

Yet, because William was the reason her situation had stabilized, she felt she had no right to make such a request.

"Oh, right." Elise changed the subject. "Are you aware that Elizabeth and Nathan are dating now?"

"I am," William replied.

Elise wasn't surprised. She never had reason to question his capability or the speed at which he acquired such information.

"From what Elizabeth has shared with me, she and Nathan have a very good relationship. I won't speculate on whether their bond is purely platonic or romantic, but I can feel Elizabeth's happiness. Therefore, I hope you won't interfere. To be perfectly blunt, I don't want you telling Elizabeth about everything Ethan kept from her in the past. I don't want him disrupting her current peace and happiness."

"Don't worry, he won't," William assured her. "Even though Ethan is family, I've always valued my friends just as highly. If Nathan truly loves Elizabeth, and if she genuinely accepts him, they have my blessing and my wishes for their happiness. Not just from me, but from Ethan as well."

Elise looked at him. She felt William trusted Ethan unconditionally.

"Ethan and I were there last night when Nathan and Elizabeth confirmed their feelings for each other. Ethan turned and left afterward."

William noticed the disbelief in Elise's eyes and clarified further. "If he had intended to sabotage their relationship or fight for Elizabeth's affections, he wouldn't have walked away. Ethan is a good man."

Elise nodded in agreement. She recalled the time Ethan had taken her to the hospital when she was drunk.

Even though he knew there was history between her and William, she was still practically a stranger to him, someone he could have easily ignored.

Yet, driven by simple kindness, he ensured she got to the hospital, settled in, and was alright before he left.

"Matters of the heart are difficult to control." William could sense Elise's sympathy for Ethan.

"That's precisely why I don't dare let go. The moment I do, I know it will only drift further away from me."

With those words, William walked away.

Elise watched his retreating figure.

She understood the meaning behind his final sentence perfectly, of course.

Elise took a deep breath.

William's persistence was starting to affect her, just a little, but she refused to admit it.

On Monday, Sophia arrived at the weekly board of directors meeting for Scott Enterprises earlier than the scheduled time and took the chairman's seat.

The other directors exchanged glances.

Sophia had always been sensible and knew her place.

Why was she sitting there today? Even if Benjamin was busy and unable to attend, delegating her to chair the meeting, she should have had the self-awareness to sit in the adjacent seat instead.

Of course, since she was a member of the Scott family, the other directors remained silent.

At the last moment, Benjamin entered the meeting room with Daniel.

His face darkened immediately upon seeing Sophia seated in his chair.

Although Benjamin had affection for Sophia and sometimes felt a twinge of guilt over his favoritism towards his son, he was visibly upset by her audacity in taking his seat.

Daniel had always been impulsive and hot-headed.

Following his father into the room and seeing Sophia in the chairman's seat, his blood boiled. "Sophia, have you lost your mind? How dare you sit in Dad's chair! Get up now! You're making a fool of yourself!"

Sophia glanced at Daniel and completely ignored his outburst.

Daniel was shocked by her dismissive attitude.

He was accustomed to being the prince of the Scott household, the heir to Scott Enterprises, after all.

Who would dare challenge him? He stormed towards Sophia, fully intending to yank her out of the chair, when Sophia produced a document stating she was the majority shareholder of Scott Enterprises.

She announced loudly, "I now hold forty-one percent of Scott Enterprises' shares, making me the largest shareholder. According to the company's bylaws, the majority shareholder holds the position of chairman of Scott Enterprises! In other words, effective today, I officially assume the role of chairman of Scott Enterprises."
###Chapter 365

Her declaration left the entire room in stunned silence.

Even Benjamin and Daniel were rendered speechless.

Sophia handed the documents to her assistant standing behind her.

"Distribute these to my father, my dear brother, and every senior director present."

"Right away, Madam Chairman." The assistant quickly began passing out the copies.

The documents laid out everything in undeniable black and white, forcing everyone to accept the reality that Sophia had become the largest shareholder of Scott Enterprises.

Sophia observed the disbelief etched on Benjamin and Daniel's faces and let out a cold, mocking laugh.

She had intended to reveal her trump card only when the Scotts pushed her to her absolute limit, but the burning desire to stand tall and triumphant before them had proven too overwhelming to resist.

So she had claimed the chairman's seat during this board meeting without hesitation.

The sensation of looking down upon all of them from this position of power was utterly intoxicating. "If there are no further objections, Father, Daniel, these seats are for you."

Sophia gestured coolly to the chairs beside her.

For years, Benjamin had always occupied the highest position at Scott Enterprises.

How could he possibly stomach such public humiliation, especially under Sophia's triumphant gaze? Though he restrained himself from causing a scene in front of the other directors, staying for the remainder of the meeting was out of the question.

He turned abruptly and stormed out, the door slamming shut with a resounding crash behind him.

Daniel hurried after him without a backward glance.

A smug, victorious smile played on Sophia's lips. Her father's anger provoked neither worry nor fear within her.

"Let us proceed with the meeting," she announced with unwavering authority.

She conducted the rest of the meeting as if the dramatic exit had never occurred.

Once the meeting concluded, Sophia returned to her office.

She had barely settled into her chair when her door was violently kicked open by Daniel.

Sophia lifted her gaze, her expression utterly unperturbed.

"What is the meaning of this?" Daniel roared. "You secretly acquired so many shares of Scott Enterprises? Where did you get that kind of money? How did you pull this off?! Sophia, how dare you challenge me for the family inheritance? When did you become so utterly shameless?! The Scott household is cursed with shameless women—you and Elise are exactly the same!"

His words were venomous and crude.

"Daniel, take a good look at yourself. You're a worthless bum, yet you dare to dream of inheriting Scott Enterprises? If this company ever fell into your incompetent hands, it would be bankrupt within a year!"

"Shut your mouth, Sophia!"

Blinded by fury, Daniel lunged forward, his hand raised to strike her.

At that precise moment, the security guards stationed outside rushed in and swiftly restrained him, pinning his arms and rendering him immobile.

"Let me go! Do you have any idea who I am? I'll have every one of you fired!"

"And who are you, really? Nothing but a rabid dog!" Sophia spat viciously.

"A mad dog who only knows how to bite indiscriminately. Listen carefully, Daniel, if you dare cause any more trouble, I will personally throw you out of Scott Enterprises!"

"You wouldn't dare!"

"Get him out of my sight. Now!" Sophia commanded.

The security guards forcefully dragged Daniel away.

"Release me! I'll kill every last one of you!"

His curses and threats echoed down the hallway as he was unceremoniously removed from the premises.
###Chapter 366

"Release him!"

Sophia's voice cut through the tense atmosphere.

Benjamin suddenly strode into the room, his presence commanding immediate attention. The security guards glanced toward Sophia, awaiting her decision.

Sophia decided to grant her father this small dignity. She gave a curt nod. "Let him go."

The moment Daniel was freed, he lunged toward Sophia, his fists clenched in rage. But Benjamin moved swiftly, intercepting him with practiced ease.

"Enough of this disgraceful behavior!" Benjamin's voice thundered through the office.

Daniel immediately stilled. Of all people in the world, his father was the only one he truly feared.

Sophia watched the scene unfold with cold amusement. Her mocking smile made Daniel's blood boil with renewed fury.

"Sophia, hand over your shares." Benjamin struggled to maintain his composure. "We're still family. I promise not to pursue what happened here today."

A bitter laugh escaped Sophia's lips.

"Father, now I understand why Elise despises the Scott family so deeply. Every single one of us is utterly repulsive!"

Benjamin's face darkened with displeasure.

"What gives you the right to demand my shares? To speak so magnanimously about letting this go? I don't need your mercy! Since I have the power to sit in this chair, I won't pretend to be humble before any of you!"

She continued, her voice dripping with contempt. "Don't blame me for being ruthless. You forced my hand. Daniel is worthless, yet you all defend him endlessly. I've sacrificed everything for this family, and none of you care. You expected me to serve Daniel my entire life. Where's the justice in that? Do you really believe the Scott family deserves such blind loyalty?"

"Stop this nonsense, Sophia! I'm only speaking to you civilly because of our relationship—"

"Save your breath. I saw how you treated Elise. Family means nothing to you. You only care about your own interests. Anyone who threatens your position becomes disposable. If you could betray Elise, you'd do the same to me. I should thank Elise for showing me the Scott family's true colors and motivating me to claim this position!"

Benjamin's eyes narrowed. "You only hold one percent more shares than I do. I can purchase additional shares whenever I choose. Do you really think you can maintain this arrogance?"

"Elise holds ten percent. The other directors control nine percent combined. Let me be clear—Elise would never sell her shares to you. You have only yourselves to blame. Remember when grandmother publicly declared she would never ask Elise for help? You severed your own lifeline!"

Sophia's triumphant laughter echoed through the office. "As for the nine percent held by outsiders—if those shares were available for purchase, would you even stand a chance? I would have acquired them already."

"You—!" Benjamin's veins bulged on his forehead. He never imagined the daughter he trusted could be so calculating.

"This is entirely your doing. If you had shown even a shred of conscience, you wouldn't have backed yourself into this corner."

Sophia's smirk widened. "Father, if there's nothing else, please take your precious son and leave. I have work to do."

"Are you certain you want to be this cruel, Sophia?" Benjamin threatened once more.

Sophia dismissed his words with a wave of her hand.

Gritting his teeth, Benjamin turned to leave.

"Oh, one more thing." Sophia's voice stopped him at the door.

"Since you're no longer chairman, I'll have your office cleared out. I'll be using it from now on. Feel free to select any other available office for yourself."

Benjamin stormed out, his face dark with fury. Daniel followed closely behind.

They stepped out of Scott Enterprises only to be swarmed by reporters waiting downstairs.

News of Sophia's takeover had already spread like wildfire.
###Chapter 367

The internet was ablaze with the news of Sophia taking control of Scott Enterprise.

No one had anticipated that the meek and gentle Sophia would transform into the chairwoman of Scott Enterprise, seizing power from Benjamin while he was still in his prime.

Public opinion about Sophia was sharply divided.

The most trending comment highlighted how exceptional the daughters of the Scott family were, regardless of circumstances.

Sophia proved this, just as Elise had before her.

Benjamin, meanwhile, had lost real authority over the Scott household and became the laughingstock of the entire business community.

Benjamin found this humiliation impossible to digest.

Of course, he wasn't the only one furious—Victoria and Margaret were equally enraged.

At the Scott residence, Margaret was boiling with anger as she shouted, "After everything I've done for Sophia! I've raised a viper in my bosom! She's betrayed us so thoroughly behind our backs—I wish I could slap some sense into her!"

She turned on Victoria, fuming, "This is all your fault for giving birth to that wretched girl! How did you raise her to become so deceitful toward her own family?"

"Mother, I truly don't understand why Sophia turned out this way either. You know how obedient she's always been..." Victoria protested indignantly, her signature pitiful and innocent act in full display.

'All you do is cry all day long—it's infuriating to watch. Did Sophia learn this from you? Appearing so pure and harmless on the surface, but wicked and calculating underneath," Margaret scolded harshly.

"That's not true! I've always done everything for this family's benefit. I nearly died giving birth to the Scott heir—have you forgotten that, Mother?" Tears welled up in Victoria's eyes.

Margaret was about to retort when...

"Enough!" Benjamin roared, "Are you trying to drive me mad? You women constantly bicker day and night—this family is being torn apart precisely because of your endless fighting!"

The room fell silent the moment Benjamin spoke.

After a long pause, Margaret couldn't restrain herself from asking, "Benjamin, what should we do now? Are we just going to let Sophia gloat over her victory? I can't accept this."

Benjamin's expression was icy cold. He couldn't stomach being outmaneuvered by his own daughter either.

"Can't we just buy more shares to surpass Sophia's holdings?" Margaret pressed again.

"I've already approached all the senior directors holding the remaining 9% of shares—they refuse to sell," Benjamin said, venom creeping into his voice. "They hold original shares passed down through two or three generations. They won't part with them for sentimental reasons—they don't need the money."

Margaret's face darkened with displeasure, but then she remembered something crucial.

"Didn't you give Elise 10% of the shares before?!"

"How dare you bring that up!" Benjamin snapped, his temper finally breaking as he turned on his mother without respect.

"If you hadn't struck Elise back then, would I have been forced to let her take those 10% so easily?!"

"I..." Margaret tried to defend herself.

She never expected her own son to point fingers and scream at her so viciously.
###Chapter 368

She always had the final word in the Scott household! "What on earth were you thinking when you humiliated Elise in front of the media, declaring that the Scott family would never beg for her help? You've destroyed our only chance! You have no sense at all, despite your age!"

Benjamin scolded harshly.

He dumped all the blame and responsibility onto Margaret.

"How dare you speak to me like that?! I'm your mother, Benjamin. Aren't you afraid of divine retribution?!"

Margaret's face turned pale as she shouted back.

"If you weren't my mother, I would have thrown you out years ago! You've lived comfortably in our home all these years, yet you show such ingratitude!"

Rage burned in Benjamin's chest as he spoke.

The thought of becoming everyone's laughingstock made him furious, with no outlet for his anger.

After yelling at Margaret, he suddenly stood from the couch and violently kicked the coffee table.

The cups on the table shattered across the floor, creating a loud crash.

Then he stormed upstairs in a temper.

Margaret's eyes reddened with fury. She had never been treated so disrespectfully in all her years. She never imagined the day would come when her own son, whom she had raised from infancy, would berate her like this.

"Grandmother, you can't blame Father for scolding you. You were completely unreasonable."

Daniel tossed the remark at her before turning to leave as well.

Margaret was absolutely livid.

The grandson she had spoiled rotten, the one she had given everything to, was now lecturing her?! "Ungrateful wretches, all of you!"

Margaret exclaimed, her entire body trembling with rage.

Victoria felt a secret thrill.

Having lived under Margaret's tyranny for years, Victoria had never been allowed to be her true self.

In fact, after learning that Sophia had taken control of Scott Enterprises, she understood exactly why she had done it.

She had given birth to Sophia and knew precisely what she was thinking—she wanted to become someone powerful, someone everyone would bow down to and obey.

Outwardly, however, Victoria continued her performance, comforting Margaret with feigned concern.

"Actually, Mother, there might still be a way with Elise..." Margaret looked at Victoria. "What do you mean?"

"Elise is still family. She doesn't really have any use for that 10% of shares. Wouldn't it be better for her to sell them for money?!"

Victoria suggested.

"She just needs a reason to do it. Back then, you were the one who publicly severed ties with Elise. If you retract that statement and explain to the media that you spoke out of anger previously, you could approach Elise again, treat her kindly, and ask her to sell her shares back to us."

"Victoria Scott, are you trying to kill me? I am Margaret Scott, for heaven's sake. Wouldn't retracting my own statement be like slapping my own face?!"

Margaret was incensed.

"But we have no other choice. You saw how determined Sophia is, and none of us can persuade her otherwise. I, as her mother, want to teach her a lesson too, but I'm not capable enough. The only way now is for you to set your pride aside and beg Elise for help."

Victoria instigated.

"I won't go. I'd rather die than beg Elise!"

Margaret would never stoop so low.

"Then we'll just have to watch Scott Enterprises fall into Sophia's hands." Victoria sighed helplessly. She stood up, preparing to leave.

"Wait."

Margaret gritted her teeth, knowing she was largely responsible for their current predicament.

"I'll call Elise."

"Elise certainly won't answer the phone. If you're truly sincere about this, I suggest you meet Elise face-to-face. It would appear much more genuine," Victoria said with false kindness.
###Chapter 369

Margaret finally decided to compromise.

She couldn't let Sophia walk all over their family like this! That girl was completely out of control! Once she got the shares back from Elise, she would make that wretched Sophia pay dearly.

Margaret brought Victoria with her to Stellar Group's headquarters, where Elise was currently in an important meeting.

Due to some unexpected issues that emerged during the recent bidding process, they had to completely reorganize and replan everything.

All three bidding companies had eventually withdrawn their offers.

They needed to either reopen the bidding or consider investing in their own manufacturing facility.

With numerous factors requiring careful consideration, the meeting stretched on for hours.

When the meeting finally concluded and participants began filing out, Matthew leaned close to Elise's ear.

"Ms. Scott," he whispered, "your grandmother is waiting in your office."

Elise tensed immediately.

Matthew quickly explained, "She caused quite a scene at reception, and I couldn't interrupt your meeting. To maintain office decorum, I had no choice but to escort her to your office. I hope you understand..."

"That's fine," Elise nodded.

She offered no further comment.

Matthew had become significantly more cautious lately.

Ever since the lawsuit incident where he felt partially responsible, he had vowed to work even harder.

It was completely understandable that Matthew couldn't make firm decisions without clear instructions from her, especially regarding family disputes.

Elise walked into her office.

Margaret's face had darkened with impatience after waiting so long, but she immediately forced a bright smile upon seeing Elise.

"Elise, you're finally finished!" Margaret approached warmly, reaching for Elise's hand. "I had no idea your days were so packed with meetings..."

Elise lifted her hand and walked directly to her desk, maintaining distance between them. She knew exactly why Margaret was here. She was surprised that Sophia had managed to take over Scott Enterprises so quickly, though after a moment's thought, she understood completely.

This was Victoria's reward to Sophia.

Though the plan hadn't succeeded this time, Sophia remained valuable to Victoria.

Thus, Victoria was willing to invest in her once more.

With that, Sophia would become a complete puppet dancing to Victoria's tune.

Of course, that was Sophia's own choice.

It wasn't Elise's place to comment on her decisions.

That was simply how things stood.

She had originally intended to teach Sophia a lesson, but suddenly realized that letting the Scott family destroy themselves from within might be the better outcome.

It was time for the Scott family to reap what they had sown.

"Elise."

Margaret watched Elise's indifferent expression but proceeded anyway, settling into the chair opposite her. "I was wrong to treat you the way I did in the past. I'm filled with regret now, especially after seeing how ungrateful Sophia has been. I wronged you before and was completely blinded by Sophia's act. Now that I've finally seen her true colors, I've come to apologize. I was wrong, and I hope you can forgive me."

Elise smiled faintly, remaining unmoved. "Okay," she said simply.

Then silence.

Margaret waited expectantly, but Elise said nothing more.

"Elise," Margaret tried again.

Elise ignored her, keeping her attention fixed on her computer screen.

"I have a request to make of you this time," Margaret finally stated her true purpose.
###Chapter 370

Elise offered a faint, knowing smile.

"You're aware Sophia currently controls Scott Enterprise with merely one percent more shares than your father." Her voice remained calm. "You hold ten percent of Scott Enterprise shares, correct? Selling just two percent to your father would strip Sophia of her arrogance at Scott Enterprise."

Margaret felt fury rising in her chest, but she forced it down, adopting an ingratiating tone. "Don't worry, I'll be completely fair this time. If you're willing to sell those shares to us, I'll purchase them at double the market value."

"No, thank you."

Elise turned to face Margaret directly, her refusal absolute. "I won't sell those shares regardless of the price offered."

"Elise, what use are they to you just sitting there?" Margaret pressed, frustration creeping into her voice. "If Scott Enterprise truly falls into Sophia's hands, it's destined for ruin. Besides, you're so occupied with Stellar Group—how could you possibly manage affairs at Scott Enterprise too..."

"Whether I have time or not is my concern." Elise's voice turned icy. "Those shares were my mother's final legacy to me. Even if Scott Enterprise goes bankrupt, I will never sell them."

She continued mercilessly, "As for Sophia's capabilities? Honestly, she'd probably manage the company better than Father ever could."

"Elise Scott!"

Margaret finally lost control, her voice rising sharply.

Elise didn't even flinch, long accustomed to her grandmother's outbursts.

"Would you prefer I take drastic measures?!" Margaret threatened.

"Do whatever you want," Elise replied indifferently.

"Don't think I won't go out there right now and tell every reporter how you disrespect and abuse me! I'll destroy your reputation completely!" Margaret spat through clenched teeth.

"Mrs. Scott." Elise deliberately avoided using 'Grandmother.' The title no longer fit.

"Have you forgotten what you told the reporters previously? You stated I have no connection to the Scott family anymore, that the Scotts would never seek my help, and you'd be grateful as long as I didn't covet the family assets." Elise's tone dripped with sarcasm. "Rest assured, I'm fully capable of honoring every word you said."

"You!"

Margaret realized she was powerless against Elise. Deep down, she'd expected this resistance.

"I'm very busy."

Elise had no intention of wasting more time on this conversation.

"Matthew."

"Right away."

Matthew entered promptly.

"Please escort our guests out," Elise instructed, her voice devoid of warmth.

"In the future, don't allow any outsiders entry. If they cause trouble, simply call the police."

"Understood."

Matthew approached Margaret and Victoria. "This way, please."

Margaret shot Elise a venomous glare. "Elise, circumstances change. You'll witness my power one day!"

"Better direct those words at yourself, considering your current predicament."

As they reached the building's lobby, they encountered waiting reporters.

Margaret froze. She hadn't called any press.

Victoria smirked inwardly. She had arranged this.

Margaret couldn't bring herself to apologize to Elise before the media—it would be a public humiliation. But that was exactly what Victoria wanted to force.

She refused to believe Elise would still refuse to help under such circumstances.

"Mrs. Scott, what brings you to Stellar Group's headquarters today?"

"Didn't you state that Elise Scott has severed all ties with the Scott family?"

"Didn't you declare the Scotts would never seek Elise's assistance? Are you here to beg her to help your son regain control of Scott Enterprise?"

Margaret's face paled with shock. Victoria whispered urgently in her ear. After a moment's hesitation, Margaret deliberately collapsed backward.

Victoria immediately caught her, and the reporters gasped at the sudden drama.

"Yes, my mother came here today to ask Elise for help," Victoria confirmed, her eyes welling with tears. "She did say some harsh words to Elise before, but Elise is still her granddaughter. This is no way to treat an elder. Not only did she reject my mother's sincere apology, but she even... she even..."

Victoria's voice broke, her expression filled with sorrow and helplessness.
###Chapter 371

The reporters swarmed them relentlessly.

Victoria answered with visible reluctance. "Elise Scott verbally assaulted my mother-in-law. She even shoved her several times. My mother-in-law is seventy. She would have fallen if I hadn't caught her. She's deeply distressed. It took a long time to calm her down enough to leave. Please, don't disturb her further. She only wants what's best for the children. She wants family peace."

Having said that, she helped Margaret depart.

The journalists didn't dare to follow. Margaret was indeed very elderly. No one could shoulder the responsibility if she fainted. Or if something more severe occurred.

Elise stood before the glass wall of her office. She watched the scene below with a cool detachment. The journalists eventually dispersed.

She turned to Matthew, who stood behind her. "Go to security. Request the footage from my office. The footage of Margaret Scott. Send it to the media immediately."

Elise would never show mercy to the Scotts.

"Yes, Miss Scott," Matthew replied respectfully.

By afternoon, numerous media outlets published stories. They detailed Elise's alleged abuse towards her grandmother, Margaret Scott. The topic quickly trended online.

However, it had barely reached the top spot. New articles emerged, refuting the initial claims. These rebuttals promptly shot to first place on the hot searches.

It was a massive, public slap in the face. Margaret was reduced to a laughingstock. Almost instantly, public opinion turned against her.

Comments flooded in. 'Senile.' 'Age doesn't bring wisdom.' 'She got what she deserved.' The criticism was scathing. It was enough to give the old woman a heart attack.

Furious, Margaret swung the back of her hand. She struck Victoria hard across the face. A large, crimson welt immediately bloomed on Victoria's cheek.

Despite this, Margaret's rage was not quelled. She was determined to vent all her frustration onto Victoria.

"You wretched woman! You knew Elise wouldn't let this slide! Yet you suggested such a stupid plan! How can I possibly show my face in public now?!"

Victoria was equally furious.

Even after all these years, Margaret still struck and scolded her.

She was a person, not a dog!

She raised her own hand. She slapped Margaret back with equal force. The old woman was left utterly dumbstruck.

Margaret's fury reignited. How dare Victoria lay a hand on her!

Where did this woman find the nerve?!

"I'll kill you, Victoria Scott!" Margaret snarled, her expression turning vicious.

Before she could act on her threat, Victoria suddenly collapsed. She fell to the floor beside the tea table. She appeared to be in immense pain.

Margaret hadn't even processed this when Benjamin started yelling unexpectedly. "Haven't you caused enough trouble for this family?!"

Margaret turned to him quickly. "Benjamin! She hit me!"

"Did she?!" Benjamin snapped, his patience with his mother completely gone.

"She just did—"

"Benjamin, it's alright." Victoria's voice was tearful as she pushed herself up from the floor. She looked utterly feeble. "She's just upset and needed to vent. Please don't blame her. I told her not to confront Elise. She refused to listen. She was certain Elise would hear her out. The media reported what happened. She couldn't handle the backlash and…"

"Victoria! This was your idea! How dare you—"

"Enough!" Benjamin barked. He wanted no explanations from Margaret. "What do you do besides embarrass us all?! I sometimes wish it was you who died instead of Father! All you do is create more problems for me!"

"W-What?!" Margaret never expected such venom from her son.

Benjamin ignored her stunned expression. "Someone get in here!" he shouted. "Lock her in her room! Make sure she doesn't leave!"
###Chapter 372

Margaret was absolutely furious. "How dare you treat me this way?!"

Benjamin didn't even glance in her direction. He simply turned and walked away without a word.

"You'll regret this, Benjamin Scott!" Margaret screamed, but her protests were useless as the household staff forcefully escorted her upstairs.

They locked her inside her bedroom, ignoring her struggles and shouts.

Victoria felt a deep sense of satisfaction watching Margaret's humiliation.

She had been anxious when Sophia took control of Scott Enterprise. In her mind, the company rightfully belonged to Daniel Scott. But seeing Margaret being treated this way wasn't entirely unpleasant.

After all, Sophia was her own daughter. She doubted her daughter would completely disregard her mother's feelings.

Elise was working in her office at Stellar Group when Matthew knocked gently on the door. "Miss Scott? There's a Mr. Patrick Montgomery here to see you."

Patrick who?

"He mentioned he's interested in discussing investment opportunities with Stellar High Fashion," Matthew clarified.

Elise remained silent for a moment.

After the failed tender invitation, she had reached a preliminary agreement with the board during the morning meeting. They would continue investing in their company independently. While this approach carried significant risk and would strain their capital management, it also meant maintaining full control over sales and operations without third-party involvement. Having complete management rights would ensure smoother operations.

Still...

"Please show him in," Elise decided. She believed in business, one should never dismiss potential opportunities outright.

Soon, a man dressed in a sharp black suit entered her office.

He carried himself with an upright posture that commanded attention. While not classically handsome, he possessed a presence that made him stand out in any crowd.

"Miss Scott," Patrick greeted courteously, extending his hand.

Elise stood to receive him. "Mr. Montgomery."

They exchanged polite greetings before taking their seats. Elise got straight to business. "My secretary mentioned you're interested in Stellar High Fashion?"

"Indeed, I am," Patrick confirmed.

"Why approach me? You must be aware of the constant scandals surrounding me, which caused my previous partners to withdraw," she stated bluntly.

Any man knocking on her door would have done his homework beforehand. There was no point in hiding the truth.

Honesty formed the foundation of any potential partnership.

"True opportunities often emerge during turbulent times, Miss Scott. Besides, I've personally reviewed your designs—I'm confident in your ability to succeed." Patrick smiled, though his tone conveyed firm determination.

"In that case, may I ask for an introduction to your company, Mr. Montgomery?" Elise inquired.

"Of course," Patrick replied, retrieving a dossier from his briefcase. "This is the company I own—nothing extraordinary, but we certainly don't lack financial resources."

Elise studied him intently.

Few people could speak about money with such casual confidence!

"Fair enough," Elise responded, her eyes still scanning the documents as she casually asked, "What's your connection to the Montgomery family of Rockville?"

Patrick's lips curved into a knowing smile. "Now that you mention it... I suppose Victoria Montgomery could be considered my cousin."

Elise's eyes narrowed slightly as he continued, "Her grandfather is my grandfather's brother. You're probably aware that half the Montgomery family is in business while the other half is in politics? I belong to the latter group."
###Chapter 373

Elise Scott shut the company file Patrick Montgomery had handed her. Her expression turned resolute after finding the answer she sought.

"I'm certain you're aware of my history with your family, Mr. Montgomery," she stated.

"Naturally," Patrick responded with cheerful confidence. "That's precisely why I approached you for a novel drama partnership."

As Elise raised an eyebrow, he returned her gaze with a polished smile. "The enemy of my enemy becomes my ally—wasn't that the foundation of Victoria and Sophia Mitchell's partnership?"

Elise studied him carefully. He knew so much about Victoria and Sophia's arrangement.

Yet it was exactly this ability to uncover every detail about her and those around her that made her hesitant to partner with him.

Before Elise could voice her refusal, Patrick added bluntly, "Don't dismiss me so quickly, Miss Scott. Hear me out first. Understand what I hope to achieve through our partnership, and why I have grievances against Victoria."

Elise paused her immediate refusal.

Patrick began his explanation. "Decades ago—during my great-great-grandfather's era—the family established an arrangement. Montgomery Stuart, my grandfather, would pursue politics. William Stuart, Victoria's grandfather, would handle business affairs."

"Decades passed with this arrangement functioning smoothly. Both family branches consistently supported each other."

"However, in the last ten years, William set his sights on political influence. He began involving himself in various political events in the capital, with James Stuart at his side."

"Though James is the heir to Stuart Group, Victoria actually controls everything."

"Their objective was clear. They aimed to establish their own political dynasty while usurping our family's political influence. Ambitious, certainly. But William's strength alone wouldn't suffice for such an endeavor."

"He requires support from multiple factions. William's first target became the Levines—Crestwood's wealthiest family. This led to the sudden announcement of Victoria and William Levine's engagement."

Patrick watched Elise closely. "You might be wondering—the Levines focus solely on business. How could that impact politics?"

Elise remained silent, so he continued. "First, politics isn't solely about influence. Substantial capital is always necessary. Even if William's family branch possesses considerable wealth, one never complains about excessive funds."

"Similarly, their path to political establishment remains rocky. They need reliable allies. They must ensure Stuart Group never falters. The Levines become strategic allies in this cause."

"So don't blame William Levine for turning from you. It's not just him either. Even Henry Levine lacks authority in this matter."

Elise looked up, surprised he would defend William.

"Which brings us to the second point—the man named William Levine," Patrick continued. "I've researched him thoroughly. His father was a prodigy like him."

"Tragic, losing that man so early. There was a time when everyone presumed Levine Holdings would collapse when circumstances turned bleak."

"Yet William himself resurrected them from the brink at twenty. A commercial legend. Hence William Stuart's keen interest in bringing William Levine into his fold."

"Sometimes, being excessively capable proves detrimental."

"Which brings us to the present situation."

Despite his lengthy explanation, Patrick showed no interest in Elise's response. He simply continued. "My family branch has halted William's political advancement."

"You should understand, Miss Scott, that politics operates differently from business. Political seats are limited, especially at the hierarchy's peak."

"To be perfectly blunt—if William rises to power, my grandfather falls. I'm certain you comprehend how these dynamics function."
###Chapter 374
Patrick Montgomery leaned forward, his expression serious. "This is precisely why I sought you out, Miss Scott. A partnership between us could ensure my family retains its political influence."

Elise Scott offered a polite smile. "You overestimate me, Mr. Montgomery. I'm far less wealthy than other prominent figures in Crestwood, and certainly no match for the Levines. I've never involved myself in politics, so an alliance with me offers you little advantage."

"Don't underestimate yourself, Miss Scott," Patrick countered with a confident grin. "Your unique position is evident, especially considering how completely William Levine has fallen for you—"

"Sentiment and capability aren't necessarily connected," Elise interrupted sharply.

"True, but that's merely one aspect," Patrick continued, undeterred. "Secondly, if Charles Stuart intends to enter politics, our branch of the family must establish stronger business connections. While you might not currently compete with major corporations, I believe a rising star like you will soon surpass most competitors. I'm willing to place my trust in you."

He paused meaningfully before adding, "Most importantly, we share a common goal: preventing the marriage between William Levine and Victoria Stuart."

"Actually, I'd prefer to see that marriage happen," Elise stated without hesitation.

She wasn't being spiteful or dishonest—she genuinely wished William and Victoria happiness from the bottom of her heart. Their union would allow her to cleanly remove herself from the complicated equation.

Patrick sighed deeply. "Whatever you might believe, Miss Scott, I know with certainty that William would never marry Victoria."

"Then why approach me?" Elise raised an eyebrow. "You should be speaking with him directly."

"That would reveal my intentions too clearly," Patrick explained. "Currently, there's a delicate truce between my family and Charles's faction. Neither side has burned bridges yet. I need your assistance specifically with Victoria."

Elise's eyes narrowed suspiciously, but Patrick pressed on. "Everyone assumes James Stuart is the key to their family's advancement, but they're mistaken. The real responsibility rests on Victoria's shoulders. James is merely a pawn for their next move, while Victoria manages Stuart Group. If anything happens to her, their business empire crumbles and their arranged marriage with the Levines collapses. Without his commercial empire supporting him, Charles's political ambitions become pure fantasy."

"You're vastly overestimating my capabilities, Mr. Montgomery," Elise repeated firmly. "Right now, I want nothing more than to stay far away from Victoria Stuart. I don't believe I possess the resources to challenge her."

"Perhaps, but the choice isn't yours to make anymore," Patrick shrugged. "You've become an obstacle in her path, and she won't rest until you're eliminated. Her previous failed attempt will only make her more determined. You're intelligent enough to understand this. Don't fool yourself into thinking you'll be safe if she marries William Levine. She's not the type to leave potential threats unchecked."

Elise bit her lip, unable to refute his logic.

Patrick pressed his advantage. "I know you're not someone who surrenders easily. Otherwise, you wouldn't have achieved all you have despite your circumstances. Your resilience is admirable, and I'm confident you won't decline my offer."

"But I do decline," Elise stated firmly, her gaze steady and unwavering. "I refuse."

Surprise flickered briefly in Patrick's sharp eyes.
###Chapter 375
Patrick Montgomery fell into a heavy silence.

Elise Scott had no intention of explaining herself further.
But seeing that Patrick had traveled all this way, she felt she owed him some justification for her refusal. "I understand your perspective, and there are clear benefits for me in this arrangement."
"However, I refuse to be drawn into this family vendetta. I simply don't have the capacity for it."
"Our families exist on a level far beyond my reach or comprehension. I'm not an ambitious person—all I truly desire is a peaceful life."
"You could argue that my life lacks peace right now, and I'd agree. But at the very least, I won't actively destroy whatever peace I have left through my own choices."

Patrick's Adam's apple bobbed as he swallowed. "Is this because of your son?"

The change in Elise's expression was immediate and unmistakable.

"It wasn't difficult to discover," Patrick stated matter-of-factly.

Elise glared at him coldly. "If that's the kind of person you are, Mr. Montgomery, I don't believe we have anything further to discuss—"

"I'm not so despicable as to threaten you using your child," Patrick quickly clarified. "I'm well aware of the saying about a mother's strength. But don't you think your son deserves the same peace you're seeking?"

"That's my concern alone," Elise replied coolly. "If you truly mean what you say about not threatening me, then don't even consider using my son as leverage."

"Very well," Patrick nodded, his tone shifting subtly. "But may I ask... don't you want to take control of your own destiny? Are you content to keep enduring the humiliation Victoria Stuart heaps upon you? Or perhaps you're placing your hopes in William Levine? He's a businessman first. While I don't deny he has feelings for you, few men possess the resolve to stand by one choice to the very end, especially when the losses begin to outweigh the gains."

"Well, you're a businessman too, Mr. Montgomery," Elise countered with a slight scoff. "Moreover, we've only just met. Why should I trust you enough to form an alliance? Shared goals? What happens when our interests no longer align?"
"Political alliances shift constantly, and you are a Montgomery at your core. If your family eventually reconciles, wouldn't I simply become disposable collateral?"

"That's precisely why I've brought you a gift, Miss Scott." Patrick produced another document envelope from his briefcase.

Elise stared at him, immediately assuming he was attempting to bribe her.

Before she could refuse, Patrick said earnestly, "Please, examine its contents first."

Reluctantly, Elise opened the envelope. Her face paled the moment she saw what was inside.
She never expected he would go to such lengths to secure her cooperation!

She turned back to Patrick, who offered a faint smile. "If you ever feel I've betrayed you, you may use that to completely destroy my reputation. However, I must emphasize one thing—regardless of how things unfold between William Levine and Victoria, I'm currently the only one who can guarantee your protection. At the very least, if you work with me, she wouldn't dare use her influence to harm you."

Despite his words, Elise remained silent.

Patrick rose to his feet. "Take your time to consider my proposal, Miss Scott."

"Wait. You forgot this." Elise extended the envelope toward him.

"Keep it," Patrick told her. "Consider it a token of my sincerity regarding our potential partnership. I believe I still have a chance before you completely reject me."

"Alright," Elise said suddenly. "You have a deal."

A smile touched Patrick's lips, though he quickly concealed his satisfaction.

The Montgomerys truly were masters of maintaining composure, weren't they?

Yet it wasn't his sincerity that had convinced her; it was his earlier words.

A mother's strength.

And Elise knew she had to try, for James's sake.
###Chapter 376

Elise stared at Patrick Montgomery, her expression thoughtful. "I can't promise I'll be useful in your fight against Victoria Stuart. What I can do right now is increase the value of our partnership."

"That's sufficient," Patrick replied calmly. "When pursuing significant goals, haste often ruins everything—even past accomplishments."

"Shouldn't I at least brief you on our business strategy?"

"Not necessary," he dismissed. "I'm merely an investor. You have complete control over operations."

"Understood." Elise didn't press further, aware that his expertise lay in politics rather than commerce.

"Would you allow me to buy you dinner to celebrate our collaboration?" Patrick offered politely.

"I'm afraid I'm occupied for the next few days," Elise declined. "I need to pick up my son from school."

"Then I won't intrude," Patrick said with the refined manners of someone well-bred. "Here's my contact information. Please keep in touch."

"Of course."

After exchanging numbers, Patrick departed. The moment he left, word reached William Levine's faction.

Victoria's face darkened with displeasure, mirroring her brother James's scowl. "What does he hope to achieve by partnering with Elise Scott?"

James snorted derisively. "He's clearly attempting a business venture. Probably believes it'll help him challenge our position!"

He smirked contemptuously; Montgomery Stuart's health was failing rapidly.

This contrasted sharply with William Levine's active involvement in capital politics, gradually shifting the family's support to their side. Patrick had no chance of reversing the situation—certainly not now!

"Still, Elise Scott? He could have chosen anyone else! Does he genuinely think she can establish his business foothold? He overestimates her capabilities!" Victoria's prejudice against Elise remained evident.

"Don't underestimate her," William interjected, and both siblings immediately straightened with respect.

Turning toward them, he continued, "If she were easily dismissed, she would already be imprisoned."

Victoria lowered her head in embarrassment, but James quickly defended her. "That's primarily because William Levine has been secretly assisting her!"

"Which makes him the crucial element," William stated coolly. "As long as he doesn't marry Victoria, our position remains unstable. In my assessment, Patrick approached Elise specifically to draw William to his cause. It's a strategic move on his part!"

"But William isn't cooperating, and we can't force him—"

"I'll speak with Henry again. This matter must be resolved permanently." William's eyes narrowed dangerously, his gaze so sharp it could send chills down anyone's spine.

One week later, William arrived at Elise's residence the moment he returned to Crestwood.

It was already ten in the evening. Elise had just settled James for the night.

She was surprised to find William standing at her doorway, looking visibly fatigued. "James is asleep," she explained. "You or Charles can collect him after school tomorrow."

"We need to talk," William insisted, his tired eyes locking intensely with hers.
###Chapter 377

Elise sensed what William was about to say.

Even though her partnership with Patrick Montgomery hadn't been officially announced, everyone in the inner circles knew. The news had spread through backchannels, stirring controversy among business elites.

Why would the prestigious Montgomery family choose to partner with Elise's small enterprise?

They should be aligning with a powerhouse like Levine Holdings instead.

But the decision was made. Everyone was now watching to see how it would unfold.

"Come in," Elise said, opening the door.

William followed her inside. He settled on the couch and got straight to the point. "Why did you choose to partner with Patrick Montgomery?"

"It was a perfect alignment of opportunity and mutual benefit," Elise responded, her tone both formal and truthful.

William let out a derisive sound. "Do you understand the current dynamics within the Montgomery family?"

Elise shrugged. "All I know is this partnership benefits me."

William struggled to contain his frustration. "Elise, can't you see what he's really after?"

"It's a straightforward exchange of value. That's how business partnerships work."

"Patrick told you that Charles Montgomery wants to usurp his family's political influence," William stated clearly. "But he didn't mention his grandfather is terminally ill."

Elise blinked, remaining silent.

Seeing her quiet response, he pressed on. "Charles Montgomery is boldly challenging family rules because the timing favors him. Do you really think getting involved now is wise?"

"Patrick can protect me from Victoria Montgomery," Elise said coolly.

"And if he falls from power?"

"I haven't considered that possibility."

"You were never this impulsive before, Elise."

"So you expect me to place all my trust in you instead?" Elise's voice rose sharply.

William's knuckles tightened. "Am I really that unreliable?"

"You just said the timing favors Charles Montgomery's rise. If Patrick Montgomery—an actual Montgomery—can't defeat him, why would I believe you can?"

William stared intently as Elise spoke, her eyes growing red and moist.

"I never wanted to be part of this conflict! You dragged me into it! I have no means to fight back, so I had to find a way to protect myself! To be blunt, Victoria Montgomery wants me dead. I'm constantly watching my back, worrying about her next move as long as her family remains powerful. Patrick Montgomery is my only hope. With him, I at least have a fighting chance. Without him, it's certain death! What would you choose if you were in my position, William Levine?"

William maintained his composure. "But you need to understand... it's not just Victoria anymore. By partnering with Patrick, you've made enemies of everyone on her side of the family!"

"Let them come," Elise said flatly. "Facing one wolf or an entire pack makes no difference to me now."

William swallowed hard, visibly restraining his emotions.

Elise fell silent, and quiet settled between them.

"It's late," she finally said. "You should go—"

"Could I have some pasta?" William asked.

Elise blinked in surprise. The tension between them had been so thick she assumed he wouldn't want to stay.

"I rushed back immediately," William continued. "The airplane food was terrible, and I haven't eaten since."

Elise pressed her lips together but ultimately relented. "Wait here."

"Thank you," he replied.

Elise rose and went to the kitchen to prepare the meal.

She had been practicing cooking over the past few days, though she had to admit she lacked natural talent. The results were consistently disappointing.

After considerable effort, she finally drained the pasta. She was about to call William when she found him asleep on the couch.

The weariness on his face was unmistakable. She knew exactly how demanding his schedule had been, and how much of it was for her sake.

She sighed softly and retrieved a blanket from her room to drape over him.

He stirred slightly, as if resisting the touch, but remained asleep.

Turning away, she headed to her study. She would work there while waiting for him to wake.
###Chapter 378

William had drifted off for a while.

Elise glanced at the wall clock. It was well past midnight. If he kept sleeping here, she wouldn't wake up in time for work.

She saved her documents, closed her laptop, and walked over to him. "William, wake up," she said softly.

He stirred, his brow furrowing in agitation.

"It's very late. You should go home and sleep properly—oh!"

In a flash, Elise found herself pinned beneath William on the couch, his weight pressing her into the cushions.

Before she could process what was happening, his warm lips captured hers in a firm kiss.

"Mmph!"

She struggled against him, but his strength overwhelmed her. He held her so tightly she could barely breathe. The intensity made her fear she might suffocate.

Narrowing her eyes, she bit down hard on his lip. The metallic taste of blood instantly filled both their mouths.

William seemed to snap back to reality, his eyes focusing on her delicate form trapped beneath him. Her face was flushed with anger as she glared at him.

"Finally awake? Get off me—mmph!"

He kissed her again, but this time the savagery melted into tenderness so quickly it felt like a different man entirely. For a moment, Elise almost lost herself in it.

The next second, she wriggled fiercely, fighting to free herself from his embrace.

He finally released her, watching as she stared at him with pure disdain, his name a growled accusation through clenched teeth. "William!"

Her fury was palpable, yet inexplicably, William began to laugh. He even touched the blood on his lips, amused.

Elise turned away sharply. That handsome face wouldn't work its magic on her anymore.

"Sorry," William apologized, though Elise knew it was merely empty words.

"Get off," she commanded coldly.

He complied, rising from her soft body.

While asleep, he'd had a nightmare—Elise was drifting further and further away from him...

The despair had made him lose control the moment he woke, and even now, the lingering fear chilled him.

But he buried those feelings deep, maintaining his composure as Elise urged, "You should leave now."

"What about my pasta?" he asked.

"It's congealed into paste by now. Would you still eat it?" she retorted sharply.

He had no comeback, forced to accept defeat and leave.

Elise followed him to the door, slamming it shut before he could utter another word.

She really, truly didn't want to see him at all.

William's eyes narrowed dangerously—it seemed he needed to accelerate his plans.
###Chapter 379

December had arrived in Crestwood, bringing with it the full force of winter.

Andrew Saunders’s birthday fell on the twelfth, and the celebration was nothing short of spectacular. Aside from his usual circle of friends, every prominent figure in Crestwood was in attendance.

Elise Scott had received an invitation. She had been tirelessly working on launching Stellar High Fashion into the market, so she decided to use the event as a chance to relax.

She moved through the grand banquet hall wearing a gown that was both understated and elegant. In the distance, she spotted Samantha. Samantha was holding onto Andrew’s arm, gracefully mingling with the guests.

Elise had never seen Samantha wear red before. As a lawyer, Samantha typically dressed in a more conservative manner. The vibrant red gown was therefore exceptionally striking.

For some reason, Samantha looked as though she truly belonged by Andrew’s side. She almost embodied the perfect image of a trophy wife.

"Elise," a familiar male voice called out just then. Elise turned to find Ethan standing beside her.

He appeared to have lost a significant amount of weight. His once fair complexion now seemed unnaturally pale.

"It's been a while," she greeted him.

"Yes, it has," Ethan replied with a polite smile.

A pang of sorrow struck Elise's chest. Ethan seemed to be handling things better than Elizabeth was, yet there was a palpable sense that he was broken inside.

"William is here," Ethan informed her, his gaze fixed on the entrance.

Elise turned as well, just in time to see William and Victoria making their entrance into the banquet hall together.

In the next moment, Ethan watched as Elise quietly slipped away, disappearing into the distance.

Stepping out into the secluded back garden, Elise sought refuge on a swing. She let out a soft sigh, thinking to herself how utterly tedious socializing with business partners could be.

Nevertheless, it was a necessary part of her life.

She heard footsteps on the gravel path and turned to see Samantha approaching. "Elise."

Elise offered a warm smile. "Feeling tired already?"

"Absolutely. I feel like my legs are about to give out," Samantha confessed bluntly as she settled onto the swing next to Elise's. "I'm really not accustomed to wearing high heels."

"Why don't you take them off? Let your feet breathe for a moment. I used to do that all the time," Elise suggested kindly.

Samantha glanced around discreetly before slipping off her shoes. The immediate relief was undeniable.

"Elizabeth has been doing quite well lately," Samantha mentioned after a comfortable pause.

"She's far more resilient than people give her credit for," Elise remarked, a genuine grin spreading across her face as she nodded in agreement.

"Yes, she is," Samantha agreed, a visible wave of relief washing over her.

Their quiet moment of conversation was abruptly interrupted by the arrival of a man Elise didn't recognize. She had a faint feeling she might have seen him somewhere before, but she was fairly certain they had never actually met.

"So, this is where you were hiding," the man said to Samantha, his tone noticeably tender.

Elise could practically feel Samantha's posture stiffen beside her.

So, there was something between them...

"It's getting quite chilly out here, Samantha. I think I'll head back inside," Elise said tactfully, preparing to take her leave.

Samantha simply nodded in response.
###Chapter 380

Elise rose from her seat and departed. Samantha shot her a look filled with gratitude.

But as Elise turned to leave, she seemed to catch a glimpse of the tears shimmering in Samantha's eyes under the dim glow of the back garden lights.

"It's been years. You've become a woman now," the man chuckled softly, his voice still carrying that familiar tenderness.

Samantha bit her lower lip; it took her a moment before she could speak. "Ryan."

"I thought perhaps you'd forgotten me," Ryan Saunders grinned. "It's chilly out here. We should head inside."

Samantha bent to retrieve her discarded shoes, but his hand gently stopped her. "Allow me."

He knelt before her, his tall frame crouching low. His long, elegant fingers carefully picked up her shoes. He held her foot steady as she slipped them on.

In the quiet space between them, a cool, slightly mocking voice cut through the air. "That seems like rather inappropriate contact with your brother's wife, don't you think?"

They both turned. Andrew stood nearby, using his height to look down at them.

"I've always considered Samantha a younger sister. That hasn't changed," Ryan stated firmly.

"But she is my wife. Your sister-in-law," Andrew retorted sharply, a stark contrast to his usual careless demeanor. "That should be my duty."

As he spoke, he closed the distance between them.

Ryan's fingers twitched almost imperceptibly, but he placed the shoes gently on the ground, stood up, and stepped back.

"By the way," Andrew added with clear disdain, "your girlfriend has been searching for you in the main hall. It's her first time visiting our home, and she doesn't know anyone. You really shouldn't leave her alone."

Ryan said nothing, his expression unreadable as he turned and strode away briskly.

Andrew shifted his gaze back to Samantha, a contemptuous smile playing on his lips as he noted her evident surprise. "What? Is it so hard to believe my brother has found a girlfriend?"

Samantha quickly composed herself, masking her emotions behind a neutral facade. She was, after all, merely a pawn in Andrew's life.

He let out a cold, humorless chuckle and began to turn away, but paused, spinning back to face her. "Well? Aren't you coming? Did you actually think I would help you with your shoes?"

Samantha had certainly never expected that.

Not only did they share no affection, but Andrew had held a deep-seated resentment towards her since their childhood.

She was acutely aware of this. Yet, she had been compelled to marry him due to her family's dire circumstances.

Suppressing a sigh, she quickly finished putting on her shoes. As she stood, she winced, a sharp gasp escaping her lips.

She rarely wore high heels. The unfamiliar straps had already begun to rub the skin raw on her heels.

Andrew turned back towards her once more. He slid one hand elegantly into his trouser pocket, his brow furrowed in a cool, detached frown as he looked at Samantha.

No one could see the way his fingers clenched tightly inside his pocket.

Samantha, however, was not one to show weakness. She gritted her teeth, enduring the pain, and quickly fell into step beside Andrew as they returned to the bustling hall.

The party was in full swing inside.

Samantha typically avoided social events with Andrew unless they were mandatory family functions. They were rarely seen this close together.

They made their way towards the gathered Saunders family. Lindsay Saunders, Andrew's mother, beamed with joy at the return of her eldest son.

A woman stood beside Ryan, a sweet, charming smile gracing her features as she remained close to him.

Lindsay immediately beckoned them over enthusiastically. "Andrew! Samantha! Over here! Ryan came back specifically to celebrate your birthday, Andrew!"

Andrew led Samantha towards the group with an air of nonchalance. His tone, however, was laced with subtle ambiguity. "To think a man as important as my brother, serving the nation, would deign to spare time for my insignificant birthday... I must say, I'm genuinely taken aback."
###Chapter 381

Martin Saunders, Andrew's father, immediately snapped at him. His voice was sharp and stern. "Watch your tone! You're speaking to your own brother!"

"It's fine," Ryan replied calmly. "Andrew has always been this way. Even after all these years, he hasn't changed a bit."

"You're the golden child of our family. No matter what I do, I'll never measure up to you," Andrew retorted with self-mockery. "Anyway, since the family is rarely together, take your time catching up. My friends are waiting over there, so I'll be going."

"Andrew!" Martin huffed in frustration. Still, he couldn't possibly make a scene in front of so many guests.

There was no doubt they were biological brothers, but their personalities were worlds apart. Ryan had always been intelligent and sensible, never causing anyone to worry. His only act of rebellion was leaving Saunderia to pursue scientific research. Lindsay and Martin had been hesitant, but they couldn't bring themselves to stand in the way of his ambitions, especially when it served the nation. In the end, they gave him their full support.

Meanwhile, Andrew never took his studies seriously and was constantly getting into fights as a child. Lindsay and Martin were frequently called to the school. As he grew older, he showed no interest in working, preferring to spend his time drinking and chasing women. Entrusting Saunderia to him would undoubtedly lead to disaster!

Their only hope now was Samantha, who they prayed could help Andrew manage Saunderia properly.

"Are you coming or not?" Andrew asked Samantha, but she wasn't as impulsive as he was—she would never leave during a family gathering.

"Guess not," Andrew scoffed, and walked away alone.

He headed over to William, who was standing with Victoria, Nathan, and Ethan.

Elise seemed to have already left.

"Well, look who it is—the birthday boy!" Nathan teased playfully.

Andrew shot him a glare. "I thought you weren't coming. Where's your girlfriend?"

"She's feeling a bit shy."

"Heh," Andrew snorted dismissively.

"By the way, is that your brother? The prodigy who left to serve the country, finally making his return?" Nathan asked, sneaking a glance at Ryan.

Andrew completely ignored the question, and Nathan grinned. "Still jealous of him after all these years?"

"Bullshit," Andrew grumbled irritably.

"Ah, so I hit a nerve," Nathan chuckled.

"Enough from you!" Andrew snapped, raising his fist and throwing a playful punch at Nathan.

Though it was meant in good fun, Nathan winced dramatically when he blocked it.

"Ouch."

Andrew was speechless—that was an overly dramatic reaction for such light contact! "You've really turned into a softie since you got a girlfriend, huh?"

"My hand is injured," Nathan explained, showing everyone the cuts on his fingers.

"Just a scratch," Andrew scoffed. "What happened?"

"Cooking with my girlfriend."

Andrew stared at him in disbelief, but Nathan was beaming with happiness. "What? It's called setting the mood, understand? Neither of us knows how to cook, so we're learning together. Someone like you, who lives for instant gratification, would never get it."

William glanced at Ethan, who remained silent throughout the exchange.

"Yeah, I don't—and neither does anyone else," Andrew snorted. "Do you get it? William? Ethan?"

"I actually do," Victoria spoke up, since neither William nor Ethan responded. "It's really nice to cook with someone you care about."

Nathan felt validated hearing that, but he didn't reply since it was Victoria who spoke.

She felt a bit awkward, but knowing that William's friends didn't think highly of her, she said gently, "It's getting late, William. Let's head home—we have to go to Rockville tomorrow, after all."
###Chapter 382

"Alright," William replied, turning to face Nathan. "I'm heading out now."

Nathan gave a slow nod, fully aware of the immense pressure William had been under recently.

But then Nathan watched in disbelief as Andrew made a hasty exit right after William, leaving him momentarily speechless. Nathan fixed him with an intense stare. "Why are you rushing off so early? Come have a few drinks with me after this wraps up."

"No thanks. I need to get home to my girlfriend. She gets lonely when I'm not around."

"Could you stop being so ridiculously lovesick? It's actually nauseating to watch."

"Sounds like someone's jealous, Andrew," Nathan shot back with a casual shrug.

Nathan could only shake his head in defeat. "Fine then. Get lost."

Andrew didn't mind the dismissal at all, his heart too full of genuine happiness to care.

Just as Andrew was about to make his escape, Nathan's voice stopped him. "Wait."

Andrew rolled his eyes dramatically. "Look, I already told you I'm not going clubbing—"

"Here," Nathan interrupted, suddenly producing two adhesive bandages from his pocket.

Andrew's eyes widened so dramatically they nearly popped from their sockets. "Why do you have those on you? Are you... into me, Nathan? Please don't tell me you've developed feelings—"

"Shut your mouth," Nathan snapped, cutting him off. "Just go home to your woman already."

Andrew accepted the bandages, but an uncomfortable shiver ran down his spine as he did so.

After Andrew's departure, Nathan turned his attention to Ethan. "You're not busy either, are you?"

"Not particularly. We can grab drinks once you finish up here."

Nathan actually blinked in surprise—had he heard correctly? The most introverted member of their group had just agreed to hang out with him?!

Ethan had definitely been acting out of character lately.

Still, Nathan wasn't about to refuse the company, and the two slipped away together before the formal conclusion of the banquet.

Martin was seething with frustration—he might have been blessed with two sons, but one constantly embarrassed him while the other, though impressive, couldn't compensate for the first's shortcomings.

After the last guests had been properly seen off, Samantha returned to the Saunders estate with the rest of the family.

During the car ride home, Lindsay spoke to her with earnest concern. "Samantha, it's been three years since you married Andrew. Haven't you considered starting a family?"

Samantha froze at the question.

A child? They hadn't even shared a bedroom!

Lindsay's sigh carried a weight of diminishing hope as she continued. "I know it was unfair that you had to marry Andrew...and I'll admit he hasn't stopped his reckless behavior. But people always say that fatherhood changes men, gives them responsibility. Maybe having a child would finally make him grow up."

"As for Ryan... His heart and soul belong to his research. Even though he brought his girlfriend to the banquet tonight, I don't expect grandchildren from him anytime soon. Saunderia is too large an enterprise to lack an heir, but you know I can't place my hopes on Andrew—no, I'd rather entrust our legacy to the next generation."
