He might appear to be somewhat frivolous at times, but his family pedigree was impeccable.

Nancy likely believed that since Cora was new to their circles, she needed someone beyond her own mother to watch over and protect her.

Nancy truly considered every detail when it came to Cora's wellbeing.

Elizabeth pressed her lips together, struggling to maintain an air of nonchalance.

"But the gap between your family backgrounds is simply too vast for me to choose you over Cora," Yulia stated as she retrieved a bank card from her purse.

Even before arriving, Yulia had known better than to expect Elizabeth's social standing to remain what it once was. Otherwise, Elizabeth wouldn't be living in an apartment funded by Nathan.

Elizabeth glanced at the card and let out a bitter, sarcastic chuckle.

She never anticipated such a dramatic turn of events in her life.

She never imagined that one day, someone would attempt to buy her off with money.

Yulia continued, "There's $700,000 in this account. That's likely a significant amount for someone in your position."

Indeed, it was probably enough to sustain ordinary people for their entire lives.

Given her current spending habits, she could manage comfortably with that sum.

"I'm not trying to humiliate you—I'm offering compensation. I've watched you grow up, and I once even wished you were my own daughter since I never had one." Yulia reminisced fondly.

Her voice carried a hint of genuine regret.

"I won't accept your money, and I won't leave Nathan either." Elizabeth made her position perfectly clear.

Even if the Levines held a knife to her throat, she would never give up on Nathan.

She was determined to be with him and trusted that he felt the same way about her.

Yulia studied the resolve in Elizabeth's eyes and asked, "Aren't you concerned that Nathan might end up penniless like you?"

"Money has nothing to do with our relationship. It's merely an accessory we can live without," Elizabeth declared emphatically.

"You've experienced what it's like to have nothing, haven't you?" Yulia pressed.

Elizabeth bit her lip as memories surfaced of nearly selling her body for money.

The desperation she felt back then was enough to drive anyone to madness.

"I'm not trying to frighten you or fabricate stories, but Nathan isn't the only son in the Davis family. His father has an illegitimate child with another woman—a boy who just turned ten this year," Yulia revealed frankly, seemingly with no intention of hiding this truth from Elizabeth.

Elizabeth was momentarily stunned.

Weren't Charles Davis and Yulia Young childhood sweethearts who had always shared a loving relationship?!

"Don't believe everything you hear through gossip." Yulia saw right through Elizabeth's thoughts. "This is precisely why I want Nathan and Cora to be together. Their union would enhance Nathan's standing and ensure the Davis family assets return to his hands. If Nathan chooses to be with you, Charles would undoubtedly disinherit him given his temperament."

"It's not that I doubt your ability to overcome hardships together or question your love for each other. But for Nathan's future, I must separate you two. Don't blame me for being cruel—I'm part of high society. If I lose everything because of Nathan's choices, including my reputation, and become the laughingstock of our circles, I would rather take my own life to make him regret his decisions."

"Don't think I'm merely threatening you—I've always been a woman of my word!"
###Chapter 459

Elise stared at Yulia.

Grace had mentioned that Yulia was a formidable woman.

She appeared gentle and generous, but when pushed to her limits, she could retaliate with ruthless precision.

"Elise, I don't know how much of what I've said has gotten through to you, but I hope you'll consider it carefully. Is it worth Nathan sacrificing everything, even at my expense, for what you call love?"

"Why? Why are you forcing me into this position?" Elise's eyes welled up with tears. She looked at Yulia and questioned, "Have you ever truly loved someone in your life?"

"I have, but what does that change?" Yulia smiled bitterly, tears glistening in her eyes. "He still betrayed me for another woman. Tell me, how long can passion truly last? When Charles and I first started dating, he would stay up for three days straight just to prepare a surprise for me. Even that intense love couldn't withstand time's erosion. Of course, I'm not doubting the love between you and Nathan. I just want to remind you—if your relationship is destined to fade eventually, do you really think Nathan won't regret choosing you? Choosing you and giving up everything he possesses?"

Elise bit her lip.

She didn't know—all she knew was she didn't want to leave Nathan now, and she was certain Nathan wouldn't leave her either.

Otherwise, Yulia wouldn't have come directly to her instead of delivering the ultimatum to Nathan.

But Yulia was right.

Years from now, would Nathan truly have no regrets about giving up everything just to be with her? Or would Elise regret being the reason Nathan lost everything, even his family and friends?

She knew all too well what it felt like to be betrayed by family and friends.

She would never forget the pain of being abandoned by her own flesh and blood.

"I'll come see you again tomorrow. Until you've made up your mind, I hope you won't tell Nathan about our meeting."

With that, Yulia stood up to leave.

She was a woman of the world, well-versed in the ways of high society.

There was no need for her to drag things out.

"I'll leave Nathan," Elise suddenly said.

Tears streamed down her face the moment the words left her lips.

Yulia turned to look at her.

She hated to admit it, but her heart ached at the sight of Elise's sorrowful expression.

Still, this was a reality no one could change.

Yulia sat back down on the sofa and handed Elise the cold, hard credit card. "Take this."

Elise glanced at it.

"Accepting the money doesn't mean you don't love Nathan. On the contrary, giving him up today proves just how much you care. Taking my money simply helps ease my conscience."

Yulia pressed the card into Elise's palm, but Elise pushed it away.

"But how will you explain to Nathan that you truly want to break up with him?" Yulia countered.

Elise clenched her fists.

"Take it as a way to make Nathan completely give up on you. Otherwise, having an empty shell of a son in the Davis family means nothing to me." In other words, Yulia wanted Nathan to lose all hope in Elise and have no lingering attachments.

"I have a better way to destroy all his feelings for me."

"Then take this money as my token of gratitude," Yulia explained. "I know your current situation, and accepting this money can only benefit you. Or you can treat it as a loan and repay me once you're able to."

Elise looked at Yulia.

Yulia continued, "I'm not a kind person, but I'm not entirely wicked either. Elise, if only you were still Nancy Levine's daughter, everything would have been perfect."

Elise laughed.

She laughed until tears streamed down her face again.

Indeed, she had prayed countless times that everything was just a nightmare she hadn't woken from. That when she awoke, she would still be Nancy's daughter and a member of the Levine family.

"As long as you're willing, you can come to me for help if you encounter any difficulties. I can still assist you with minor matters without Nancy interfering."

Yulia was telling Elise that, even at the risk of offending Nancy, she wouldn't stand by idly as long as it didn't violate her principles.

Yulia was a good person.

It was life's circumstances that forced her to put up an indestructible, cold facade.

Yulia left.
###Chapter 460

Elizabeth's tears fell freely, staining her cheeks as she stared at the credit card on her coffee table.

She remembered Nathan's hopeful expression this morning when he kissed her goodbye.

Who could have predicted that their rushed farewell would become permanent?

Her phone screen suddenly illuminated the dim room.

Elizabeth glanced down and saw Nathan's message. [I'll be there by 8 PM at the latest. I'm going to scrub myself clean and make sure I smell amazing for you tonight.]

Her vision blurred with fresh tears, making the words swim before her eyes.

She could almost feel Nathan's excitement as he typed those words... and the crushing disappointment he would feel when he discovered her betrayal!

Wiping her tears with the back of her hand, Elizabeth picked up her phone and deliberately ignored all of Nathan's unread messages.

Her trembling fingers moved across the screen.

She found the contact she needed and began typing with unsteady hands.

The message grew longer and longer.

Minutes passed, yet she couldn't bring herself to press send.

She knew this single action would sever everything between her and Nathan forever.

He would despise her for the rest of his life.

Finally, she tapped the button.

She squeezed her eyes shut as hot tears streamed down her face.

In that moment, she understood that misfortune never comes alone.

The happiness she had briefly known was merely an illusion.

She would probably never experience true joy again...

No one would ever love her the way Nathan did.

Thirty minutes later, her doorbell chimed.

By then, Elizabeth had composed herself somewhat.

She had forced herself to regain control.

She dried her tears, washed her face, changed into fresh clothes, and even applied makeup and perfume out of habit.

She opened the door to find Ethan standing there.

He was slightly breathless.

Her message hadn't been particularly dramatic.

Just one simple line. [I need to talk to you. Come to my apartment immediately and confirm when you get this.]

Ethan had replied instantly. [On my way.]

And here he was.

Somehow, Elizabeth had known Ethan would come without hesitation, perhaps driven by his lingering guilt toward her.

She didn't want to explore that thought further.

"Come in," Elizabeth said to Ethan.

Ethan studied her face with concern. "What's wrong? Have you been crying?"

She thought she had concealed the evidence well enough.

"Did you and Nathan have a fight?" Ethan asked worriedly. "Should I call him? He can be impulsive sometimes, but he doesn't mean any harm. Don't take it personally..."

"We didn't fight." Elizabeth watched as Ethan pulled out his phone, ready to dial Nathan.

She continued softly. "But I'm ending things with Nathan."
###Chapter 461

Ethan was completely taken aback.

He stared at Elizabeth, wondering if he had misheard her words.

Everything between them had been progressing so smoothly. Why would she suddenly suggest ending things?

"Has... something occurred?" Ethan struggled to maintain his composure as he questioned her.

Yet his voice betrayed a subtle tremor that he couldn't completely conceal.

Elizabeth remained oblivious to his emotional state.

She wasn't even making eye contact with him.

"Nathan's mother approached me." Elizabeth had no intention of concealing the truth from Ethan.

Since she had decided to seek his assistance, complete honesty was necessary.

Understanding dawned on Ethan's face.

He had suspected as much. Their relationship couldn't have deteriorated so rapidly in such a short time.

He could clearly sense the deep affection they shared for each other.

But when family elders became involved, situations inevitably grew complicated.

Given the current circumstances with the Davis family determined to form a marriage alliance with the Levines, Elizabeth represented nothing more than an obstacle they would never accept.

"Have you spoken with Nathan about this?" Ethan inquired seriously.

"Would I have asked for your help if I had?" Elizabeth retorted, her gaze finally meeting his.

She seemed distinctly unreceptive to his display of concern.

"I believe you should have an honest conversation with Nathan regarding this matter." Ethan could feel Elizabeth's irritation toward him, yet he remained sincere in his concern and advice.

"What would that accomplish? Should I pressure Nathan to abandon everything for my sake? To have him rejected by his family just as I was?"

"The situation differs for Nathan. He's an integral part of the Davis family. They would never truly cast him aside."

"Are you aware that Nathan has a half-brother?" Elizabeth challenged Ethan.

Ethan's heart skipped a beat.

This was entirely new information to him.

"If the Davis family has an alternative heir, Nathan could indeed become disposable to them."

"But I genuinely don't believe Nathan would end your relationship for the sake of his family's business interests..."

"Yulia will threaten suicide." Elizabeth composed herself before continuing, "Yulia has always resented the existence of Nathan's half-brother. She would never permit an illegitimate son to inherit the Davis family assets. If Nathan defies his family for me, it would represent total defeat for Yulia. That might genuinely drive her to take her own life."

Ethan stood in stunned silence, rendered speechless by Elizabeth's revelation.

"How could a relationship burdened by someone's death possibly lead to a happy ending?" Elizabeth questioned him.

She had resigned herself to this fate.

She had exhausted all her luck and blessings before turning twenty-five.

She didn't know what remained for the remainder of her life.

"So you summoned me here to use me as leverage to break up with Nathan?" Ethan finally comprehended Elizabeth's motive for calling him.

"Exactly," Elizabeth confirmed with a nod. "Since I've decided to leave him, I want a clean break. I don't want to leave any hope for Nathan or myself. Nathan knows I once had feelings for you. If we stage a performance together, he'll undoubtedly believe I no longer love him."

Ethan didn't immediately agree to Elizabeth's proposition.

He was contemplating whether a less extreme solution might exist.

If Yulia's primary concern was securing the Davis family assets, would she accept Elizabeth if Nathan could guarantee that outcome?
###Chapter 462

Ethan was lost in his thoughts.

"Don't you want to help me?" Elizabeth's voice carried a subtle mocking tone.

She had anticipated his reluctance.

After all, agreeing to assist her would inevitably strain his friendship with Nathan.

To Ethan, self-interest had always been his guiding principle.

"I simply believe things don't need to escalate to this point. Yulia doesn't want you with Nathan because she's concerned Charles might disinherit him if he angers him. But if Nathan takes the entertainment business seriously and proves his capabilities, Charles wouldn't need to force a marriage alliance."

"Having been around the Levines for years, do you truly think this is about inheritance? Social compatibility is the foundation of every elite marriage. With my current status, what right do I have to marry into the Davis family?"

"I see no issue with your status. Nathan loves you, and that gives you every right to be with him. Social compatibility is merely an excuse. True love conquers all barriers, including social status..."

"But I'm also the person Nancy despises most. Would she ever allow me to be with Nathan, especially now that she's determined to match him with Cora? If she discovers Nathan and I are together, I can't even imagine what she might do to me!" Elizabeth couldn't contain her agitation.

"Regardless, I don't think making such a hasty decision is right. Matters of the heart shouldn't be rushed. I suggest you talk things through with Nathan properly. There must be another solution..."

"Ethan, are you worried no one else will ever love me and I'll end up alone?" Elizabeth interrupted him.

Ethan felt a sharp pang in his heart.

In Elizabeth's presence... no, even the mere thought of her often sent sharp pains through his chest.

"You're just concerned that with no one else to love me, I'll cling to you," Elizabeth stated plainly.

The corner of her lips curled into a sneer.

"That's why you're so anxious about me breaking up with Nathan." Elizabeth's eyes reddened slightly, as if holding back tears.

Yet her expression clearly suggested she was laughing.

It was impossible to discern what she found amusing.

"That's not true. I just believe that since you and Nathan love each other, breaking up over external factors isn't worth it. You really shouldn't be impulsive about matters of the heart. You'll definitely regret it."

He could attest to that from personal experience.

"That's my concern. I didn't ask you here to dissuade me or to preach from your moral high ground about love conquering all. I asked you here to act with me, to make Nathan believe I've always loved you and that my feelings for him were merely gratitude, nothing more."

Elizabeth didn't wish to discuss this further with Ethan.

The moment she agreed with Yulia, her decision was made.

She might come to regret it, but she knew she had no other choice in this matter.

Ethan swallowed hard, his throat constricted, uncertain whether to agree to Elizabeth's request.

He worried that by agreeing, he would be enabling her wrong choice.

He wanted her to live a happier life. He wanted to see her tell him with a genuine smile that she was happy, just as she had last night.

"Elizabeth, let's sit down and properly discuss this with Nathan..."

"Ethan, why is it so difficult for you to help me with just one thing?" Elizabeth asked.

Her voice was soft but trembled with emotion.

"I've never felt so helpless with anyone else. You're the only person who always opposes me, no matter what I do or what I ask of you. How much do you truly hate me? What have I done to make you despise me so much?"
###Chapter 463

Ethan's throat was bone dry. His fists clenched, trembling uncontrollably.

He saw the utter despair in Elizabeth's eyes. It was a hopelessness directed entirely at him.

In that moment, he felt as if a thousand blades were shredding his heart. It left him speechless, utterly paralyzed.

"If there was anyone else I could turn to, I wouldn't be here asking you," Elizabeth whispered, her voice fragile. "But right now, you're the only person Nathan will believe. The only one who can convince him I don't love him. It's pathetic, really. In twenty-five years, I've only loved two men. How I wish I had another choice."

Ethan fought to steady himself. His entire body shuddered with the effort.

Through tightly gritted teeth, he forced the words out. "I'll help you."

Elizabeth looked at him, her expression unmoved.

What reason did she have to be moved?

He had no other choice after hearing her plea.

"Thank you," she breathed, her voice cracking with the weight of her gratitude.

She still had to thank him.

Thank him for agreeing, even when it placed him in an impossible position.

That evening, just before eight, the doorbell chimed.

Nathan had the passcode to her apartment. But he always knocked out of respect, a habit he insisted upon.

He said he loved the feeling of her opening the door for him.

He claimed the sight of her smile was enough to fuel his happiness for days.

He never imagined that the one time he used the code would lead to a scene that shattered his world.

He punched the numbers in, one by one.

Inside, Elizabeth looked at Ethan.

Since his agreement, they had been waiting on the sofa for Nathan's arrival.

Elizabeth had said she would initiate the physical intimacy.

She asked him to endure it.

No matter how repulsed he might feel, she begged him to bear it.

Now, Elizabeth leaned close to Ethan. She wrapped her arms around his neck, pulling him nearer, creating an image of deep intimacy.

Ethan's body went rigid against her. He probably detested her proximity, remaining stiff as a statue, but she pressed on without retreating.

She couldn't afford to care about his disgust. She was that selfish.

In front of the man she loved, her only concern was making Nathan lose all hope in her.

The lock clicked open. Elizabeth pressed her lips to Ethan's.

Ethan's heart hammered violently against his ribs.

His arms hung limply at his sides, fists clenching instinctively. His mind went completely blank.

He forgot entirely that she was doing this for another man. All his senses zeroed in on the softness of her lips.

Sparks seemed to fly where their lips met, sending his entire system into overdrive.

He had dreamed of this moment countless times. Yet every time he opened his eyes, he only found the cold reality and his own tears.

He didn't dare move a single muscle.

He didn't know what Elizabeth ultimately intended.

He was terrified that if he showed any response, she would pull away.

He could only fight his instincts, letting Elizabeth take full control.

The door swung open.

Nathan stood in the doorway, a broad smile on his face. It froze the instant his eyes landed on the scene inside.

His smile solidified into a grotesque mask.

Was...

Was something wrong with his vision?
###Chapter 464

Could he have walked into the wrong apartment?

No, that wasn't possible.

Elizabeth couldn't possibly be...

But that man was Ethan. He knew exactly what Elizabeth used to feel for Ethan and the heartbreak she endured from his rejection.

She had loved Ethan for so many years, countless years...

He was nothing compared to Ethan!

His vision grew hazy, yet he didn't retreat. He didn't shout accusations, nor did he interrupt their intimate moment.

He simply watched as Elizabeth initiated the kiss with Ethan, standing frozen as his own heart shattered into a million tiny fragments.

From her peripheral vision, Elizabeth saw Nathan standing motionless at the doorway.

She could vividly imagine his devastation in that moment, desperately wishing he would push them apart, maybe even slap her. But he did nothing.

He just stood there, consumed by his agony.

Elizabeth's eyelids fluttered. She closed her eyes as tears streamed uncontrollably down her cheeks, then deepened her kiss on Ethan's lips.

What began as a gentle peck transformed into something hotter, more fervent, and Ethan's heart hammered wildly in his chest.

A simple brush of lips was enough to drive him insane, let alone this passionate exchange...

He didn't dare move.

He shouldn't respond at all, he sternly reminded himself.

That resolve lasted until someone forcefully shoved him away.

The warmth of her lips vanished...

Reality came crashing back.

"Bam!"

Nathan's fist connected hard with Ethan's face.

Ethan didn't fight back, even as his cheek reddened from the impact.

He just stared at Nathan.

He saw the unshed tears making Nathan's eyes bloodshot, the raw fury contorting his features, and the murderous hatred blazing in his gaze.

Nathan couldn't take it anymore.

His clenched fists trembled violently at his sides as he glared at Ethan with unrestrained rage.

"Ethan Parker, Elizabeth is my girlfriend!" he roared, directing all his anger at Ethan.

Ethan pressed his lips together and remained silent—Elizabeth had instructed him not to speak, promising she would handle everything.

Then he heard Elizabeth's voice. "Nathan, I'm sorry."

Nathan turned to look at her.

He saw the tears glistening in her eyes and the profound guilt and apology written across her face.

"I realized I can't forget about Ethan," Elizabeth stated, meeting his gaze directly.

She looked straight into his pain-filled eyes, seeing how completely she had broken his heart.

"Yesterday, when Ethan brought you home...I couldn't control myself any longer. I asked Ethan to come over today because I want to start over with him," Elizabeth explained calmly, almost coldly.

Her voice was distant and detached, yet tears continued to stream down her face uncontrollably.

"Hah..." Nathan let out a bitter, broken laugh at her explanation.

He chuckled with such profound sorrow it was physically painful.

"Elizabeth York, am I really that worthless? Am I truly no match for him? Tell me what I lack compared to him—I can change! I'll transform myself into someone you can love, please?"

He was desperate, humbling himself completely in his heartbreak.

Elizabeth had never seen Nathan like this before.

He was always so vibrant and cheerful, so proud and full of life.

She hated seeing him reduced to this.

Suppressing the ache in her own heart, Elizabeth said, "Nathan, no matter how much you change, you'll never be Ethan. You know how many years I've loved him. I thought I could use you to move on, but I overestimated myself. I'll never be able to forget Ethan for as long as I live..."

"I don't care about that." Nathan looked at Elizabeth with unwavering devotion. "It's fine if you can't forget him—I don't even need you to.

I just want to be with you, to stand by your side even after he abandoned you."
###Chapter 465

"I can't deceive myself or you any longer," Elizabeth stated with unwavering resolve.

"Deceive..." Nathan let out a bitter laugh.

The sound was laced with painful self-mockery.

"So all this time, every moment with me has been a lie? Every smile you gave me was fabricated? Each time you whispered 'I love you' - were those words empty?" Nathan's voice cracked.

Tears streamed down his face with every question.

His heart felt like it was being torn apart.

This agony surpassed even the pain he'd felt witnessing Elizabeth lean close to Ethan.

He realized now that losing something you once possessed was far more devastating than never having it at all.

"I'm sorry." Elizabeth avoided answering directly.

Nathan's laughter intensified.

He laughed until his shoulders shook, unable to stop the flood of tears.

He had believed fate had finally smiled upon him.

He'd even felt grateful for the circumstances that brought them together.

Now he understood his own wickedness must have been why the universe refused to let them be.

"Has Ethan agreed to be with you?" Nathan's voice was raw.

Elizabeth pressed her lips together, uncertain how to respond.

She wouldn't be with Ethan.

Ethan wouldn't want her either.

If she claimed otherwise, Nathan would quickly uncover the deception.

She doubted Ethan would maintain this charade much longer - his involvement today had already stretched his limits.

"Yes, we're together now," Ethan interjected.

He stepped forward as Elizabeth hesitated.

Nathan turned his gaze toward Ethan.

"Ethan Parker, I considered you my closest friend. I always believed nothing could break the bond between us four - you, me, William, and Andrew. No matter how serious the conflict." Nathan's voice broke. "I never imagined I could feel such hatred toward you."

"I'm sorry." The apology was all Ethan could offer.

He had no other words that could mend what was broken.

"From this moment forward, we're no longer friends. I'll pretend I never knew you."

As Nathan spoke these words, his fist connected with Ethan's face.

The impact was powerful, sending Ethan stumbling backward.

Ethan made no move to defend himself.

Elizabeth watched helplessly, her arms lifting slightly before falling back to her sides.

"Twenty years of friendship - gone!" Nathan declared with finality.

Ethan remained silent, accepting Nathan's decision.

Nathan turned and walked away from them both.

Though tall and strong, he moved as if carrying an unbearable weight.

He reached the doorway, his hand gripping the frame for support.

"Ethan Parker," he said without turning. "Don't ever make Elizabeth sad. If you truly love her, take care of her."

Even in his pain, he still wished for her happiness.

Elizabeth's vision blurred until Nathan's features became indistinct.

If only she were still a Scott heiress... but that life was gone forever.

A happy ending with Nathan was impossible now.

The door slammed shut with finality.

Elizabeth felt her heart close along with it.

She loved him, truly and deeply.

If she didn't, the pain wouldn't be this overwhelming.

Silence enveloped the apartment.

Neither Ethan nor Elizabeth spoke.

Even her tears fell soundlessly.

They continued to fall for what felt like an eternity.
###Chapter 466
Elizabeth had no idea how long she had been sitting there.
When she finally glanced at the clock on the living room wall, she was startled to see it was well past noon.
It was already the next day.
She turned her head slowly, her eyes landing on Ethan.
He was simply sitting beside her in silence.
Just being there.
The bruise on his face from the two punches Nathan had thrown had darkened significantly. The swelling made it look even more grotesque.
"Should you go to the hospital?" Elizabeth asked quietly.
Ethan seemed momentarily startled. It took him a second to realize she was referring to the injury on his face.
"It's fine. It'll heal on its own in a few days."
"Thank you," Elizabeth said then.
She was expressing gratitude again.
Ethan pressed his lips together, about to speak, when Elizabeth continued in a soft voice, "I don't know how I can ever repay you."
"Don't worry about it."
"But your friendship with Nathan... it's been damaged because of me."
"It'll be okay."
Everything that happened had been driven by raw emotion.
Ethan's bond with Nathan wasn't that fragile.
"I suppose you're right," Elizabeth murmured, almost to herself. "It's not like I have anything valuable to offer you anyway. No money, no influence. Nothing."
"Elizabeth..."
"Would you want my body?" Elizabeth suddenly asked.
Ethan's heart stuttered in his chest.
"Isn't that how it works in the entertainment industry? Trading physical favors for opportunities and fame. You're a famous director, after all. You must be accustomed to these kinds of transactions, right? I've heard about the many actresses you've made famous," Elizabeth said, her tone deliberately casual.
"I have never engaged in anything like that," Ethan stated firmly.
Elizabeth let out a hollow laugh.
When it came to sordid matters like these, who would ever admit their involvement?
Besides, Ethan had never shown any real interest in her.
She had initiated that kiss earlier, and she had felt absolutely no response from him. Not even a flicker.
His consistent indifference toward her was precisely why he was using this excuse to reject her now, wasn't it?
"I was just joking," Elizabeth said, meeting his gaze. "You despise me. If you were to sleep with me, it would just create another debt I owe you. I imagine you'd be more concerned that I might use it against you later..."
Elizabeth's words trailed off as she looked at Ethan again. She suddenly realized how close his face was to hers. Before she could process it, his lips were on hers.
Elizabeth froze instantly.
For a moment, she was certain she was hallucinating.
Ethan Parker was kissing her? Of his own volition?
How could that possibly—
"Mmph!"
Elizabeth's fists clenched tightly. It didn't seem like Ethan intended to stop at just a kiss. His actions suggested he wanted more...
Had she completely misread him?
Her mind was in a fog, but she could vaguely sense a certain urgency in his movements.
The truth was, Ethan had been pushed to his absolute limit.
Only he knew the immense self-control he had been exercising to keep himself from doing this to her.
Only he knew how desperately he wanted to pull her into his arms and hold her.
Only he knew the depth of his love for her...
Ethan didn't even have a chance to confess his feelings before he was utterly lost in the warmth of her body.
Elizabeth lay beneath him. Her hands lifted, intending to push him away, but then she let them fall back silently.
Was this part of their transaction?!
Or was it... regret?
Or perhaps... had he finally surrendered?
If she allowed this to happen, it would sever any remaining possibility with Nathan!
That night, snow began to fall. For the first time, Ethan found the snow beautiful instead of cold.
He knew, with absolute certainty, that he would never forget the woman in his arms on this snowy night.
###Chapter 467

Elizabeth stirred awake slowly the next morning.

She turned over groggily, reaching out for the space beside her.

Her hand found only cold, empty sheets.

A bitter smile touched her lips. She had expected this.

Forcing herself upright, the covers slipped from her body.

Her eyes dropped to her skin, taking in the scattered bruises and marks.

Without this physical evidence, she might have believed last night was a dream.

That Ethan, so different from his usual self, had been nothing but her imagination.

She lifted the duvet and gathered the discarded clothes from the floor.

Placing them neatly aside, she headed straight for the shower.

Every muscle in her body ached with a deep soreness.

She had always thought post-intimacy soreness was just fictional exaggeration.

The warm water cascaded over her, washing away the night's traces.

After her shower, she towel-dried her hair and changed into fresh pajamas.

Returning to make the bed, her eyes caught on the sheets.

Small spots of blood stained the fabric.

It was gone now.

Perhaps that was for the best.

It would save her from endless overthinking.

She quickly stripped the bed and replaced the linens.

As she turned to leave, a note on the nightstand caught her eye.

Her fingers trembled slightly as she picked it up.

"Wait for me. - Ethan"

The words stirred conflicting emotions in her heart.

Why had he left so early?

Was he giving her space before delivering his final decision?

She crumpled the note and tossed it in the trash.

Then she calmly continued with her morning routine.

Ethan had departed at seven that morning.

His assistant had collected him for the remote mountain shoot.

Yesterday's abrupt departure from the set had disrupted the filming schedule.

He was supposed to leave before six, but found himself unable to pull away.

Watching Elizabeth sleep peacefully in his arms made leaving unbearable.

He had lowered his head to brush his lips against her forehead.

She slept so soundly, so trustingly.

Emboldened by her deep slumber, he stole a kiss from her lips.

Just like he had throughout the night.

He still couldn't believe how far he had let himself go.

Elizabeth had even asked, "Isn't this compensation getting excessive?"

Remembering her expression from the night before, he smiled involuntarily.

His phone rang insistently, pulling him from his thoughts.

Reluctantly, he slipped from the warm bed.

Before leaving, he scribbled the note.

He also pocketed a small piece of rope from her bedside table.

Outside the building, his assistant paced anxiously beside the car.

Spotting Ethan, the man rushed forward immediately.

"Sir, the entire crew has already left for location. The leads are en route too."

"Understood," Ethan replied calmly.

He showed no urgency, his mood noticeably lighter than usual.

His assistant watched him, thoroughly baffled.

Ethan was normally more punctual than anyone.

Something was definitely different about him today.
###Chapter 468

Ethan was moving with deliberate slowness?!
And what was this location anyway?
His assistant knew perfectly well that Ethan's new residence wasn't in this neighborhood!

The assistant didn't dare question further, quickly following Ethan into the vehicle. During the drive, he began briefing Ethan on the upcoming filming schedule and the specific scenes planned for the day.

"You won't be accompanying me to the set today," Ethan interrupted abruptly, cutting off his assistant's detailed report. "Once we leave the city limits, you can get out. Just hand me the schedule."

His assistant stared at him, completely bewildered.
Was he... being dismissed?!

Ethan produced a small piece of string. "Find a ring in this exact size. When you reach the shopping district, send me photos of all the available styles."

The assistant's eyes widened in disbelief.
Wait... What was happening?!

"Also, arrange for a bouquet of nine hundred and ninety-nine roses. Ensure they're the freshest available."

Was Ethan planning a marriage proposal?!
Wasn't this incredibly sudden?!
Ethan didn't even have a girlfriend, did he? So why was he making preparations as if wedding bells were imminent?!

The assistant had always assumed that with Ethan's particular temperament, the man would likely remain single forever.
He'd never witnessed his employer showing genuine interest in any woman except Elizabeth, who clearly didn't return his affections!

Had Ethan experienced some sudden life revelation and decided to switch teams?

"According to today's shooting timetable, I'll probably return quite late. I'll call you when I'm heading back," Ethan continued.

"Uh... Understood," his assistant nodded hurriedly.

Ethan left his assistant standing by the roadside and sped toward the filming location. Upon arrival, the intense shooting schedule commenced immediately.

They were producing this film with specific foreign award submissions in mind, making their timeline exceptionally tight without room for any delays.

Ethan poured his entire focus into the work, and by the time they wrapped for the day, the clock showed ten at night.

The crew and several cast members were enjoying warm soup prepared on set. Given the chilly weather, the hot beverage provided welcome relief from the cold.

"Would you like some, sir?" one crew member offered.

"I need to return home for a while."

"Driving back now? Don't we resume at seven tomorrow morning?"

"I'm aware."

"The forecast predicts snow tonight," the crew member cautioned. "The mountain drive will take approximately two hours. Wouldn't it be better to stay overnight? There's excellent accommodation nearby, and they've prepared rooms specifically for our team."

"That won't be necessary. I'll drive myself back. The rest of you should stay. I'll be here punctually tomorrow."

Though the crew member attempted further persuasion, recognizing Ethan might have urgent matters requiring attention, he ultimately relented.

Ethan took medication for his stomach condition before beginning the drive home.
He'd consumed very little throughout the demanding workday, and there hadn't been opportunity after wrapping either.
An unexpected craving for the apple pie near Elizabeth's residence surfaced, despite his general aversion to sweets.

He navigated the narrow mountain road carefully. During the descent, he phoned his assistant. "Is everything prepared?"

"Yes, sir."

"In thirty minutes, bring everything and wait for me at the neighborhood entrance where you collected me this morning."

"Sir, returning this late? Snow is imminent. Please exercise extreme caution."

"Understood," Ethan responded before ending the call.

The moment he set his phone down, blinding headlights suddenly illuminated his face, plunging everything into darkness.

Frantic honking echoed in his ears, accompanied by the harsh screech of emergency brakes.
His stomach lurched violently as his vehicle crashed through the guardrail, tumbling down the steep mountainside.
###Chapter 469

The clock struck midnight.

Ethan was nowhere to be found.

Elizabeth didn't wait up for him any longer.

His absence spoke volumes.

Still, she sent him a text. [Are you coming home?]

No response came.

The next morning, she called Clara.

Clara no longer lived with her since moving into Nathan's house.

She had found work and moved out.

Perhaps she sensed Elizabeth's discomfort and left willingly.

They hadn't spoken since her departure.

Elizabeth had kept her distance.

Clara seemed too ashamed to reach out.

This was Elizabeth's first call to her.

Clara's voice trembled when she answered. "...Elizabeth?"

The uncertainty in her tone was palpable.

"Yes. I'm leaving Crestwood," Elizabeth stated calmly.

She wasn't sure why she felt compelled to tell Clara.

Perhaps because Clara was her only remaining family.

Clara sounded shocked. "What's wrong? Did something happen? Is it about Nathan?"

"Nathan and I are over."

"What happened?"

"That's not your concern," Elizabeth said coldly.

Silence filled the other end.

Elizabeth pressed her lips together.

She could feel Clara's hurt, but couldn't bring herself to comfort the woman.

She lacked the energy for even basic courtesy.

"Jeffrey is in mandatory drug rehabilitation," Elizabeth added. "With good behavior, he could be out in three years."

Jeffrey Bennett was Clara's husband.

After Elizabeth moved out, Nathan had Jeffrey arrested to eliminate potential trouble.

Drug rehab typically lasted three to five years.

Early release was possible with good conduct.

"You can stay in Crestwood if you want," Elizabeth continued. "If you prefer to leave, send me your ID. I'll book your ticket."

"I'll go with you," Clara said immediately.

She didn't even ask where they were going.

Elizabeth's lips tightened.

Her feelings were complicated.

She wasn't sure if she wanted Clara's company.

But the offer had been made.

"Send me your information and pack lightly," Elizabeth said. "We'll take the three o'clock train. Time is limited."

"Okay. I don't have much to pack. I'll start right away."

Elizabeth ended the call.

Emotions washed over her in waves.

Her feelings toward Clara were conflicted.

Her attachment to the city felt equally complicated.

She had lived here for twenty-five years.

She drew back the curtains.

Standing before the floor-to-ceiling windows, she gazed at the winter landscape.

Crestwood was unusually cold this year.

Heavy snow had fallen overnight.

Though road crews had cleared most pathways, snow still clung to tree branches.

The world remained draped in white.

Elizabeth took a deep breath.

She glanced at her phone, considering messages to Elise and Samantha.

Her fingers hovered over the screen.

Then she decided against it.

She would contact them after settling elsewhere.

Right now, she needed to maintain her resolve to leave.

Nothing remained here worth staying for.

The only complication was her debt to Nathan.

Leaving was the best gift she could give him.

Time would heal all wounds.

At Rockville, William and Elise were having breakfast.

William had been going out more frequently lately.

His outings remained brief.

Every time he returned, he immediately changed clothes.

He wanted no outside scents lingering on him.
###Chapter 470

The breakfast was peaceful until William's phone shattered the silence. He glanced at the screen, and his expression tightened instantly.

"It's Grandfather."

Elise's eyes flickered slightly, but she kept her gaze lowered, continuing to eat. If it were a private call, William would take it elsewhere, away from her hearing.

That was his usual pattern whenever Victoria called.

"What is it?"

A moment later, panic flashed across William's face.

Elise wondered what could possibly shake William so profoundly. She noticed his hand, holding the phone, had begun to tremble.

His eyes were also reddening visibly.

"Understood. I'm on my way."

William ended the call and sat in heavy silence, his eyes fixed on nothing in particular.

"What's wrong?" Elise finally asked, her voice soft.

What news could overwhelm William to this extent?

"Ethan... he's had an accident," William said, his voice strained as he fought to control his emotions.

Elise felt her heart constrict painfully.

Judging by William's devastated expression, this was no minor incident.

"Last night, around eleven, his car went off the mountainside. It was a different cliff from where your accident occurred. The rescue team only found him at five this morning. He's in critical condition at the hospital. The doctors aren't sure if he'll pull through."

William's voice cracked, his composure clearly crumbling.

Elise felt a surge of anxiety, unsure what to say or how to comfort him.

"I need to return immediately," William stated bluntly.

Elise nodded. "I'll come with you—"

"No. You stay here. It's safer than Crestwood," William insisted firmly.

Elise opened her mouth to protest but then closed it. Now wasn't the time for arguments.

"I'll return as soon as I can."

"Take your time. I'm perfectly fine here alone," Elise replied honestly.

She didn't want William to miss seeing Ethan because of her. What if...

A cold dread settled in her stomach. She refused to finish that thought.

As long as Ethan was fighting, there was hope.

Modern medicine had advanced tremendously. His chances were good.

William said nothing more. He abandoned his half-eaten breakfast and left abruptly.

Elise's appetite vanished completely. She grabbed her phone and crutches, heading to the back garden to sit.

News of Ethan's accident weighed heavily on her conscience.

She had once set him up and never properly apologized!

Opening a browser, she searched for news about the accident but found nothing. The family had likely suppressed the information, avoiding media scrutiny.

In that case...

Did Elizabeth know?

After a moment's hesitation, Elise decided not to interfere.

Elizabeth was with Nathan now. Elise didn't want to disrupt their happiness. Besides, Nathan was close to Ethan; he would find out soon. If Nathan chose to tell Elizabeth, that was his decision. It wasn't Elise's place to meddle in their relationship.

She would respect their privacy.

Elise took a deep breath, reflecting on Ethan's life and his current plight...

Was fate being excessively cruel to him?
###Chapter 471

Twenty-four hours had slipped by without any media mention of Ethan's accident.

Intrigued, Elise found herself typing a message to William.

[How is Ethan?]

Minutes stretched without a reply, until Victoria's WhatsApp call flashed on her screen.

They hadn't exchanged contacts, making Elise pause before answering.

"Elise, William is occupied and asked me to call you instead," Victoria explained, her voice laced with exhaustion.

So Victoria had returned home with William and stayed overnight with him?

"How is Ethan's condition?" Elise inquired calmly, her focus solely on Ethan's wellbeing.

"He hasn't regained consciousness since surgery," Victoria shared. "The doctors say he might remain in a vegetative state, but there's still hope. William assembled an international team of specialists who just examined him. He's currently discussing treatment options with them."

"Is it really that serious?" Elise asked, concern tightening her chest.

"Yes," Victoria confirmed. "His heart stopped twice during the operation, but they revived him both times. His blood pressure remains dangerously low, and the specifics... I can't really predict his recovery. It's in the experts' hands now. Don't worry, I'll keep you updated if anything changes."

"Thank you."

"Of course."

The polite exchange ended as Elise hung up.

She hadn't anticipated Ethan's condition being so critical.

Over the following days, Victoria periodically texted updates about Ethan's status. Elise wasn't sure if this stemmed from genuine kindness or Victoria's desire to prevent her from contacting William directly.

Still, Elise didn't press for more details, grateful simply to be informed.

That evening as she prepared for bed, William finally called.

He hadn't reached out in three days, likely aware that Victoria had been keeping Elise updated.

"I'm taking Ethan abroad," he informed her. "Crestwood's medical facilities and technology have limitations, but he's stable enough for transport. We're flying out for advanced treatment overseas."

"What do you mean by stable?" Elise caught the careful wording.

"He's not in immediate danger, but remains completely comatose. There's a blood clot in his brain pressing against an artery, preventing him from waking. It poses a constant life-threatening risk. No surgeon would operate under these conditions globally. Our only option is moving him abroad, using advanced medical technology to stabilize his vitals, then performing craniotomy based on his response."

"I understand." Elise nodded quietly.

Essentially, Ethan's vital signs were stable, but his life hung by a thread, danger lurking at any moment.

"Will Victoria be accompanying you?" Elise asked before ending the call.

Not that she particularly cared, but it would be easier to get updates from Victoria than disturb William.

"No," William answered briefly.

"Alright."

"Wait for my return."

Elise ended the call without responding.

The following night, William messaged through WhatsApp confirming their safe arrival.

Next morning, Elise stared at a photo in her chat with Victoria showing her taking James to Rockville.

Victoria looked utterly tender hugging James in her private jet's cabin, though the boy appeared distinctly unenthusiastic. His little face held a snobbish expression that somehow didn't detract from his exceptionally handsome features.

The photo's caption read: [First winter break for our little scholar! Daddy's busy working, so it's just mother and child on our own.] Elise's fingers tightened around her phone.
###Chapter 472

The Levines had all gone abroad. William left James in Victoria's care. Without consulting Elise. Without asking if James wanted to stay with Victoria.

Elise gritted her teeth. She had to accept this reality. She was reluctant to bother William under the current circumstances.

His text messages over the past few days were sparse. There was nothing to say. Ethan wasn't recovering anytime soon.

Yet Victoria constantly shared updates about her time with James. It was a complete mess. Feeding him. Bathing him. Leaving his little face covered in soap bubbles.

She took James outdoors. Hiking. Amusement parks. They played on all sorts of thrilling rides.

Elise remained silent. Watching Victoria perform her maternal role.

Until Victoria sent a photo of James with a hurt leg.

In the picture, the boy hung his head. He stared at his leg. It was wrapped in white bandages. His expression was blank.

Victoria's caption read: [I'm so sorry, sweetheart! Mommy was careless.] It was accompanied by crying emojis.

That was the final straw. Elise texted Victoria immediately. [What happened to James?]

Victoria replied: [It's nothing serious. Just a minor sprain from our hiking trip. The doctor said he needs rest. He'll be fine.]

Elise was skeptical. [Where is he right now? I'm coming to get him.]

[William left him in my care. I won't hand him over to anyone without his explicit permission. Don't worry—I'll take excellent care of him.]

Elise's fingers clenched around her phone. Victoria added another message: [As long as he remains my son, I will be responsible for him until the very end.]

It sounded like a normal statement. But it was a clear threat from Victoria. A warning that she could harm James if pushed.

Elise felt the air leave her lungs. She wondered if Victoria had intentionally hurt James. She was almost certain Victoria had been updating their usually inactive chat for this precise reason. To deliberately remind Elise of her power.

Naturally, Elise couldn't stay calm when James was involved. She called William immediately.

He sounded exhausted on the other end. Elise didn't care about the time difference. She spoke bluntly. "I want James with me."

She was frantic with worry leaving him alone with Victoria. That vicious woman was capable of anything to achieve her goals.

"I'll be back the day after tomorrow," William said. "I'll take him back then."

"I want James back now!" Elise snapped. Her tone was firm and severe.

"Just wait—"

"Do you honestly believe he's safe with Victoria?!" Elise demanded, her voice trembling with agitation.
###Chapter 473

"Just stay calm for now," William said, his voice steady as he tried to reassure Elise. "Victoria won't harm James."

"You have so much faith in her?!" Elise retorted, her anger flaring.

"Not particularly," William replied, each word deliberate and firm. "But she wouldn't cross that line."

"He got hurt under her watch!"

"Children have accidents. It's part of growing up."

Elise's fingers tightened around her phone, the rage simmering just beneath the surface.

"Don't go anywhere without me," William instructed calmly. "Victoria won't hurt James, but she wouldn't hesitate to target you."

Elise ended the call abruptly. When it came to James, her composure always shattered.

Even so, she knew resisting William was futile.

True to his word, William returned two days later.

Elise was a light sleeper. She heard the faint sounds from outside but remained in bed. She shifted positions, feigning sleep as the door creaked open quietly.

She continued her pretense, unwilling to acknowledge the intruder.

Then she felt the mattress dip beside her, irritation instantly prickling her skin.

Annoyed, she turned sharply, but froze when she saw the bright little face beaming at her.

"Mommy!" James greeted in his sweet, tiny voice, immediately throwing his small body against hers, his arms wrapping around her neck.

Feeling his tender warmth, Elise's heart melted instantly.

"Did you miss me, Mommy? I missed you so much!" James complained, squeezing her tighter. "Victoria insisted I go to Rockville, but I didn't want to... Then Daddy said you were here, and I couldn't wait to see you!"

"Your daddy brought you here? At this hour?"

"Yes!" James exclaimed, releasing her neck to look into her eyes. "I was sleeping, but Daddy woke me and said we're coming to see you right now. I'm not sleepy anymore! Did I wake you, Mommy?"

"No." Elise smiled faintly, caressing his soft cheeks.

Remembering his injury, she asked, "How's your leg?"

"It's fine," James said quickly. "Just a little sprain. It stopped hurting the day after we went to the hospital."

So it really was minor. Victoria had exaggerated to upset her.

"Mommy," James yawned. "Can I sleep with you? I'm really tired."

"Of course," Elise said softly. "Let me help you with your jacket."

"Thank you, Mommy. I love you so much!"

Such a charmer.

Soon they were nestled under the blankets, James curling into her arms. "I love sleeping with you, Mommy. You smell so nice."

"It's late now." Elise smiled. "Close your eyes and sleep."

"Okay. Goodnight, Mommy."

"Goodnight, James."

The room fell quiet. James yawned repeatedly and was asleep within minutes.

Elise envied how easily children could sleep. She tucked him in carefully, knowing sleep wouldn't come for her now.

Being confined here had been incredibly dull. She occasionally worked on her laptop, but still had too much free time. That led to excessive napping during the day, which ruined her nighttime rest.

Naturally, her idle mind wandered to various things, including the argument with William two days prior.

Now that Elise had calmed down, she realized Victoria truly wouldn't harm James. In fact, given the current circumstances, Victoria had every reason to win the boy's favor.

In other words, Elise had lost her composure that day—exactly what Victoria wanted. Victoria knew it would upset Elise, deliberately driving a wedge between her and William.
###Chapter 474

Elise had to acknowledge she had walked right into Victoria's trap.

Still, she couldn't dismiss the reality that Victoria was definitely using James to threaten her.

Rolling over in bed toward James, Elise studied his small face, peacefully asleep in the soft glow.

It was her nightly ritual when sharing a bed with James—keeping a gentle light on so she could watch him in the darkness and adjust his covers.

Only a true mother would understand that indescribable joy of seeing your child sleeping soundly beside you.

Elise couldn't resist stroking his soft cheeks once more. She silently vowed to protect her son at any cost.

Elise had grown accustomed to sleeping past ten these days, and this morning was no exception.

James remained asleep, likely because he stayed up too late last night, though he looked utterly adorable.

Elise didn't disturb him and carefully slipped out of bed, grabbing her crutch and limping to the bathroom to freshen up before leaving the bedroom, quietly closing the door behind her.

Downstairs, only the household staff moved about the living area. The sight made her momentarily distracted, wondering if William had actually returned home last night.

Perhaps he was still sleeping?

One of the attendants approached her. "Would you like breakfast now, Miss Scott?"

"Let's wait a bit," Elise replied. "I'll eat with James and William since I'm not very hungry yet."

"Actually, Mr. Levine is already awake," the attendant informed her promptly.

Elise felt surprised since the man was nowhere in sight.

The attendant explained further, "He returned to his room since neither you nor Master James were awake yet, and instructed me to notify him when you woke up. I'll go inform him now."

Elise nodded, then reconsidered. "Never mind. I'll go myself."

"Very well, Miss Scott."

Ultimately, Elise still felt somewhat guilty about their argument over James yesterday. Besides, she also wanted to ask about Ethan's condition.

When she reached William's room and prepared to knock, she found the door slightly ajar.

Without hesitation, Elise entered and discovered William standing in the middle of the room, his hair damp from a recent shower.

He turned, sensing someone's presence, and immediately noticed Elise's frozen expression.

Meeting his gaze, she instantly flushed crimson.

As she turned to leave, William suddenly stopped her. "Elise."

She bit her lip and kept her back to him.

He said, "Wait a moment. I need your help."

"Please get dressed first," Elise reminded him.

"You're not losing anything by looking."

But she definitely was!

After a moment, he announced, "I'm decent now."

Elise slowly turned to find William wearing a white bathrobe, loosely tied around his waist.

It felt somewhat provocative, though he wasn't actually exposing anything inappropriate...

"What do you need?" Elise asked.

"Some injuries on my thigh haven't fully healed. Could you help apply this ointment? It's difficult for me to reach properly."

Elise frowned. "When did you get injured?"

"During the car accident before."

"And it still hasn't healed?" Those should have been superficial wounds!

"Which is why it requires extra attention," William replied, as if stating the obvious. "I believe I wasn't careful enough, and it became infected."
###Chapter 475

William settled onto the plush sofa in his private suite.

His first-aid kit was already open on the coffee table.

Elise wondered if he would have tended to the wound himself had she not entered.

Since she was responsible for his injury, she felt obligated to assist.

She retrieved the iodine and anti-inflammatory ointment, kneeling to apply them to the inflamed area.

"You need to be more cautious," she advised.

"It's not exactly a spot I can easily monitor," William replied weakly.

Elise didn't argue, focusing intently on disinfecting the sensitive skin.

"Ah!" William suddenly gasped.

"Does it hurt?" Elise inquired.

"Not at all," William insisted, though his strained expression betrayed him.

Elise remained silent but lightened her touch.

The room grew quiet, filled only with their breathing as she worked meticulously.

After cleaning, medicating, and bandaging the wound, she glanced up.

William's intense gaze made her heart flutter.

She suddenly became acutely aware of their compromising position.

Flushing, Elise quickly stood, feigning nonchalance. "All done. Avoid getting it wet and minimize friction when moving."

"Understood," William replied, his voice thick with restraint.

Elise turned to organize the medical supplies, deliberately avoiding eye contact.

Seeking to diffuse the tension, she asked while arranging bottles, "How is Ethan progressing?"

"Barring complications, he's receiving excellent specialized care overseas," William explained. "He remains comatose. The specialists believe the cranial hematoma requires careful monitoring. They're observing whether it resolves naturally before considering risky surgical intervention."

"So Ethan's recovery depends on the clot dissolving spontaneously?" Elise clarified.

William nodded grimly. "Essentially, yes."

"And you left him abroad alone?" Elise asked with concern.

"Currently, there are no better alternatives. His condition has stabilized, so my presence wasn't necessary," William responded. "My grandfather remained with him. Since retiring, he has ample time to provide companionship."

Elise acknowledged this silently. Additional visitors wouldn't accelerate Ethan's recovery.

They could only hope his gentle spirit would overcome this challenge.

After securing the first-aid kit, Elise grabbed her crutches and hobbled toward the bathroom.

She needed to wash the residual ointment from her hands.

Upon entering, she noticed black boxer briefs near the faucet.

An awkward tension filled the space.

William suddenly entered, swiftly scooping up the garment and discarding it in a nearby bin.
###Chapter 476

Elise glanced up and noticed William was breathing heavily.
A faint flush colored his cheeks.

She pressed her lips together, lowering her eyes.
She focused on washing her hands, pretending not to notice anything as William exited.

But her mind wandered as she scrubbed her fingers.
Various images flashed through her thoughts...

She quickly shut off the faucet and dried her hands.
Then she stepped out of the bathroom.

William wasn't in the room.
She caught a glimpse of movement from the walk-in closet.

Elise walked over slowly and entered the closet.
She found William changing his clothes.

He wore another pair of black boxer briefs.
They looked nearly identical to the pair he had just discarded.

He noticed Elise in the full-length mirror before him.
Surprise flickered in his eyes briefly.
"I'll be out once I'm dressed," he said.

"Can you manage getting dressed on your own?" Elise inquired.

"Yes," he replied.

"Won't you risk irritating your scars?"

"I'll be careful... Huh?" William murmured suddenly.
He swallowed hard as he felt Elise's warm, delicate hands touch his back.

Elise likely had no clue how perilous her actions were in that moment.

"I'm sorry, William," she apologized softly. "I become overly protective when it comes to James. I shouldn't have confronted you like that the other day."

William's throat felt dry and tight.
His voice emerged husky. "So? Are you planning to comfort my wounded pride with your body?"

He asked with a slight smile, making it sound like a joke.

He didn't actually expect much.
He knew Elise would never get physically intimate with him while he remained entangled with Victoria.

That was why he often restrained his urges, forcing himself to endure.

"If you'll allow me," Elise whispered softly, resting her cheek against his back.

Her voice was barely audible, but William heard every word.
And he heard them clearly.

His body tensed immediately.
He even wondered if Elise was joking like he had been... but this kind of joke could easily escalate beyond control!
###Chapter 477

Even after Elise made her intentions perfectly clear, William remained frozen in place.

He had touched her once before, and it had left her with years of trauma around men. It had made her despise him for just as long.

If he dared to do it again now, he feared she might disappear from his world forever. That he would never see her again.

So he chose to endure the torment instead.

"You don't want me?" Elise asked, when he didn't respond.

"I'm terrified," William admitted honestly.

Elise was taken aback, a complex mix of emotions swirling inside her.

It was almost amusing, yet it also pierced her heart with a sharp pain.

Still, her hands began to wander boldly over his rigid body. "I won't hate you this time," she whispered.

"What's come over you, Elise?" William caught her delicate hand.

But when he turned and saw her flushed cheeks, he realized she wasn't as composed as she seemed. He could actually feel her hands trembling.

"Listen to me," he said seriously. "You don't need to apologize or repay me for saving your life. Not like this, when you're clearly reluctant. I don't need you to repay me for anything I do for you. Just as you shouldn't feel guilty about anything you've done to me. Everything I do, I do willingly. Do you understand?"

He would never force her. Even if it meant remaining celibate for the rest of his days.

"I'm willing," Elise said, her gaze meeting his with raw sincerity. It was as if that invisible wall between them had finally crumbled.

In this moment, her heart was completely open and true.

"Elise... mmmph."

William's eyes widened in shock as Elise suddenly threw her arms around his neck.

She rose on her toes as high as she could, pressing her lips against his to silence whatever else he might say.

William stood frozen, afraid to move, allowing her to kiss him for what felt like an eternity.

He was fighting for control, watching as Elise continued kissing him before finally pulling away.

"Don't say anything," she breathed. "Or I might change my mind."

"I would never let you regret—"

"No," Elise cut him off firmly. "I won't regret it if I do it now."

"Elise—"

William tried to speak, but her lips found his again, more insistent this time.

Truthfully, if William had any shred of rationality left, he wouldn't be doing this with Elise.

Given his current circumstances, he didn't want to cause her any more pain.

But all reason shattered under Elise's determined seduction.

Somewhere in the distant corner of his mind, he thought he heard the door open quietly, then close just as softly moments later...

Outside, the servants were surprised to see James turning back so soon. "Master James, weren't you going to fetch your parents for breakfast? Why have you returned?"

"Shhh." James pressed a finger to his lips, gesturing toward the closed door. "They're busy right now. I'll eat by myself."

While the servant stood confused, James beamed with excitement. "Don't disturb them. I might be getting a brother soon... or maybe a sister?"

He knew it all along—his daddy was amazing in every way. Daddy definitely knew how to make Mommy swoon.

Elise didn't open her eyes until night had fallen.
###Chapter 478

A subtle shift in the mattress stirred Elise from her slumber.

To be precise, William was already awake, simply waiting for her to open her eyes.

"Hungry?" William's voice was deep, laced with unmistakable satisfaction.

He radiated vitality, looking completely refreshed and energized.

In stark contrast, Elise felt utterly drained, as if every ounce of strength had been sapped from her body.

"What time is it?" Elise murmured, her voice husky.

"Ten o'clock," William replied smoothly.

"So late?" she gasped, disbelief coloring her tone.

"What did you expect?" William chuckled, the sound rich with barely concealed triumph.

"Where's James?"

"He declared himself old enough to put himself to bed, and he did just that, like the responsible young man he's becoming," William answered, then added meaningfully, "He also emphasized what an exemplary older brother he intends to be."

Elise shot him a sharp look before struggling to sit up.

As William moved to assist her, the blanket slipped, revealing vivid evidence of their passionate encounter scattered across her skin.

Elise turned to glare at him again. He cleared his throat slightly. "Getting up?"

"Yes."

"I'll fetch your clothes."

"Thank you."

William threw back the covers and rose from the bed, completely unclothed despite having been awake for some time.

Elise averted her gaze pointedly.

In response, he flashed her a devastatingly handsome grin. "You've seen everything already, Miss Scott."

Elise ignored him pointedly.

He continued, undeterred, "I mean, considering we made love in broad daylight—"

"Must you?" she cut him off, unable to bear his teasing.

William walked away, his grin still firmly in place.

Elise watched him go—even his retreating back seemed to radiate pure masculine satisfaction, which only deepened her scowl.

After they had both dressed and left the bedroom, the household staff had a late dinner waiting for them.

They ate in relative silence until Elise suddenly spoke, her tone firm. "I need to return to Crestwood."

William's hand stilled, his fork hovering mid-air.

So this was why she had given herself to him? Just to secure her departure?!

"It's not that dangerous anymore," Elise stated seriously, setting down her cutlery. "I'll be perfectly safe as long as you remain here with Victoria."

"No. I won't stay with her."

"You should marry her," Elise said, her words crisp and clear.

William's fingers tightened around his knife and fork. "So that's what this was really about?" he asked, his voice turning icy.

"Consider it settling a debt between us."

"Really?" William let out a bitter, humorless laugh at her cruelty.

He had thought today meant they loved each other. He'd believed they had finally connected. But it was all one-sided, just his own foolish hope.

He never should have surrendered to her seduction. He knew it would end badly.

"Let's just end this the way it began," Elise said, meeting his gaze with solemn intensity.

"Stop lying to yourself, Elise!" William snapped, his rage barely contained. "You're not that indifferent toward me. If you were, you never could have lost yourself like that... You never could have responded to me the way you did today!"
###Chapter 479

Elise simply stared at William with complete indifference. "I'm a grown woman—I have needs too. And honestly, I need to move past this trauma."

William maintained his cold smile while she continued to look at him seriously.

"Our relationship was a mistake from the very beginning—even our first encounter was wrong. I've told you before that the best solution is for you to return to Victoria, while I live my life freely."

She spoke without any emotion, only showing determination to end things.

Still, William asked her, "Don't you feel anything after everything we've been through?"

After enduring so much, did she really have no intention of changing her mind?

"Nothing," Elise answered with absolute certainty. "All I feel is fear—fear of when I might accidentally upset Victoria again, fear of when she might try to kill me again. Death threats, false accusations, and now using James to threaten me? I've had enough."

"It will be over soon," William said confidently.

"No, it won't," Elise replied coldly. "Even if things calm down temporarily, it's just the calm before the storm. I don't know if I can survive what's coming next. I'm only human—flesh and blood—and I can't keep having my life shortened like this."

"Elise—"

"If I really wanted to be with you, I wouldn't have tried to leave you so many times. I've thought this through carefully, and there's something else I need to be honest about..."

Elise paused, while William continued to stare into her emotionless eyes.

"When your grandfather took me to Levine Manor after the Levine Holdings anniversary celebration, he had a long talk with me. He told me that James is my child, and that you're the one who slept with me all those years ago—before he forced me to leave you."

"My grandfather—"

"Let me finish," Elise interrupted him.

William pressed his lips together and remained silent.

"Yes, I'm not someone who gives up easily, nor do I compromise willingly. But he made me an offer I couldn't refuse."

"What was it?"

"If you marry Victoria without any issues, I can take James with me."

William's eyes narrowed while Elise looked at him coolly, carefully controlling her emotions. "I was reluctant and angry about being coerced, but I agreed anyway. James means more to me than anyone else—I would die to keep him safe, let alone lose you."

Her tone suddenly shifted from indifference to misery. "So this isn't a discussion. I'm begging you, please go back to Victoria and stay with her? We're both James's parents. I'm willing to sacrifice everything for him, and I hope you can be a little selfless too. Please?"

William swallowed hard, his expression gradually shifting from restraint to calmness.

"Is this really what you want?" he asked.

"Yes, it is," Elise replied bluntly without hesitation.

"Fine," William agreed.

His response came so unexpectedly that Elise felt a sharp pain in her chest.

The feeling was too strong to ignore, but she knew this was the best outcome for everyone. "When are you leaving?" William asked.

"Tomorrow."

"I'll book your flight."

"No, that's fine. Patrick will handle the arrangements," Elise said bluntly.

As William stared at her, she explained, "Since we're cutting ties anyway, let's not unnecessarily involve ourselves in each other's lives."

"...Okay," he replied.

"Thank you," Elise said sincerely, picking up her knife and fork again.

She ate very slowly, as if she couldn't taste the food at all.

She watched as William stood up and left, while she herself had barely eaten anything.
###Chapter 480

Elise had no appetite whatsoever.

But she finished every last bite of her steak.

She was a mother now. She had responsibilities. She needed to stay strong, to be there for James.

After the meal, she returned to the bedroom.

He was sleeping soundly, his face the picture of pure innocence.

Watching him, her heart settled. She would never regret this choice.

The next morning came too soon.

It was time for a temporary goodbye.

James would remain in William's custody until his marriage to Victoria. Elise wasn't heartless enough to rip him away right now.

Besides, she was certain James was safest here, with William.

"The car is ready," William's voice was flat, devoid of emotion.

"Alright." Elise knelt, cupping James's soft cheeks. "You'll see Mommy again soon, sweetheart. When school starts back in Crestwood in a few days."

"Okay!" James nodded, his little face serious.

He'd thought, after yesterday, his daddy and mommy would be together forever.

He was such a good boy. So understanding. He never complained, just accepted everything.

It broke her heart.

"Here." William held out a small pill.

The morning-after pill.

She'd actually remembered it herself, planning to pick one up later. There was still time.

But she took it from him without a word. No reason to refuse.

'I'll wait in the car,' he said, turning to leave without a second glance.

Last night's burning passion had cooled into today's icy detachment. Such was the way of pragmatic adult relationships.

Elise swallowed the pill with a sip of water.

She hugged James one more time, breathing in his little-boy scent.

His big, doleful eyes followed her as she walked away, as she slid into the passenger seat of his father's car.

She kept her eyes on his small frame until he was out of sight. The ache of leaving him was a physical pain in her chest.

She turned forward, forcing herself to calm down.

She would get him back. She would bring her son home.

William was silent beside her, the air in the car thick and heavy.

It was only as they drove away that Elise realized the mansion was nestled deep in the mountains, surrounded by dense, winter-bare forest. A perfect, hidden fortress.

Strangely, the car began to climb upward, toward the peak, not down the mountain.

She didn't ask. She simply stared out the window at the stark, white landscape, her heart as cold as the frost on the trees.

They finally stopped on a large, flat clearing at the mountain's summit.

Two helicopters stood waiting, their rotors still.

Elise got out. William followed.

"The helicopter will take you down. Patrick is waiting for you at the landing zone," William stated.

"Thank you." Her voice was just as formal.

He looked at her then, giving a slight, almost imperceptible nod.

No goodbye was spoken. This was a silent farewell.

Elise climbed into the helicopter. William didn't follow. He just stood there, watching.

The engine roared to life. The rotors began to spin, whipping up a whirlwind of dust and dead leaves.

The helicopter lifted off the ground.

Through the window, their eyes met one last time.

The distance between them grew, the world falling away beneath her.

He became a small, solitary figure, then a dot, then nothing at all.

Gone.
###Chapter 481

Elise's eyes were red-rimmed and glistening with unshed tears. She deliberately avoided looking out the window at William's retreating figure.

She couldn't quite describe the hollow ache spreading through her chest. It felt like part of her soul had been torn away, leaving her struggling for breath.

The helicopter touched down in a suburban clearing exactly thirty minutes later. Patrick's black sedan waited below, surrounded by additional security vehicles. His protection detail had significantly increased since the accident.

Elise struggled to disembark with her crutches. The awkward movement made her wince in discomfort.

Suddenly, a pale, well-manicured hand appeared before her. She looked up to find Patrick in his wheelchair, offering assistance.

Though he had sustained serious injuries in the same incident, she didn't hesitate to grasp his arm for support. Together, they made their way to his waiting car.

The helicopter lifted off behind them as they drove away. Elise finally understood why William had insisted his estate was secure.

The location was nearly impossible to find. Tracking a helicopter presented its own challenges. Even if someone managed to locate the property, assaulting such an elevated position offered no tactical advantage.

Elise stared blankly at the sky where the helicopter had vanished. Patrick studied her profile thoughtfully.

"You really didn't want to leave him, did you?" he asked quietly.

"That's not true," Elise denied, lowering her gaze.

Patrick observed her carefully composed expression. Despite her efforts to conceal it, the evidence of recent tears was unmistakable.

"Why not stay with William? There's no doubt he can protect you," Patrick commented neutrally.

"Sorry to disappoint you, but I told him to marry Victoria," Elise replied, meeting his gaze directly.

Patrick looked momentarily surprised before shrugging. "If you believe that's the right decision. We're merely business partners, and you're entitled to make your own choices. You've been clear about your boundaries from the beginning, and I never held excessive expectations. Still, I'm curious what prompted such a definitive break."

"That's personal," Elise said firmly.

"Understood," Patrick accepted without pressing further.

The car entered Rockville's bustling downtown district, eventually stopping before an upscale café.

"Are you certain about going in alone?" Patrick verified.

"I wouldn't have requested you bring me here otherwise."

"And you're absolutely sure you don't want me to accompany you?"

"Given your current condition, I think it's better if I handle this myself," Elise said with a slight smile, glancing meaningfully at his injured leg.

Patrick chuckled softly. "That's rich, coming from someone on crutches."

As Elise prepared to exit the vehicle, Patrick suddenly caught her wrist. "Wait."

Elise's fingers twitched involuntarily. Even knowing his preferences, she still felt uncomfortable with physical contact.

Patrick seemed unaffected as he pressed a small device resembling a USB drive into her palm. "Press this button if you sense any danger. My team will intervene immediately."

Elise studied Patrick's determined expression. "I always repay my debts. You saved me, so I'll ensure your safety until this situation is resolved."

Elise accepted the device without argument, slipping it into her purse.

"Thank you," she said before stepping out alone.

"Be careful," Patrick called after her, watching her retreating form with an unreadable expression.

Two men in dark suits stood guard outside the café's private VIP room.
###Chapter 482

The men in black exuded an intimidating aura that kept most people at a distance.

Yet Elise Scott approached them without hesitation.
