One guard spoke quietly into his lapel microphone. After receiving approval from inside, he opened the door for her.

The WIP room's interior was vastly different from ordinary rooms. Its lavish decorations would dazzle anyone who entered.

Elise had no interest in admiring the opulence. She limped painfully toward the coffee table where Victoria Stuart sat elegantly sipping tea.

Four bodyguards stood guard in the room. Two positioned themselves directly behind Victoria.

The precaution clearly demonstrated her wariness toward Elise.

A flicker of surprise crossed Victoria's eyes when she saw Elise alone. But years of training helped her conceal it instantly.

"Have a seat," Victoria gestured gracefully.

Elise didn't refuse. She settled into the chair opposite.

"What would you like to drink, Miss Scott?"

"Blue Mountain coffee."

After Elise placed her order, Victoria set down her teacup. "I must admit, I'm surprised you came alone. Quite unexpected."

This was genuine—Victoria had been stunned when she received Elise's message requesting this meeting.

After all, Elise was intelligent enough to understand Victoria's behind-the-scenes manipulations.

Given Victoria's multiple attempts on her life, why would Elise boldly request a private meeting?

Victoria suspected a trap, but curiosity overpowered caution. She had to see what Elise was planning.

Now, surrounded by bodyguards, she wondered if she had overprepared. Perhaps she was being overly cautious.

But as a Stuart heir, she would never reveal such doubts.

"To what do I owe this pleasure?" Victoria asked directly, preferring straightforwardness.

"I came to clarify things between myself and William."

Victoria's expression tightened slightly. Was Elise here to boast?

Anyone with eyes could see William loved Elise and felt nothing for Victoria.

"What exactly are you trying to say?" Victoria pressed.

"I've severed all ties with William," Elise stated bluntly.

Victoria's heart skipped, but she resisted celebration.

Not only did she suspect deception, but her previous attempts to eliminate Elise had all failed.

"So please stop wasting your efforts on me," Elise said calmly.

Victoria responded with a cold sneer.

So Elise simply wanted her life spared?

How ridiculous—did Elise think Victoria would believe such obvious lies?

As if Victoria had survived this long by being naive!

Elise observed the contempt in Victoria's eyes. Without reaction, she retrieved her phone and slid it across the table.

Victoria frowned, annoyed by Elise's unshakable composure.

Elise always seemed unaffected, no matter the circumstances.

Before meeting Elise, Victoria believed no woman could be both more beautiful and smarter than her.

Now, for the first time, that conviction wavered.
###Chapter 483

Victoria's expression instantly darkened the moment she took the phone and saw the images displayed.

No matter how skilled she was at masking her emotions, her fury was unmistakable.

"What is the meaning of this?!" she demanded sharply.

Elise had just declared she severed all connections with William Levine, yet here she was showing Victoria a photo of herself intimately entangled with him in bed?!

Was this some kind of boast?

A deliberate attempt to humiliate her?!

These thoughts only fueled Victoria's rage further.

After all, her engagement and wedding date had been public knowledge for some time, yet there was absolutely no spark between her and William—they hadn't even held hands!

"Do you genuinely believe I won't eliminate you, Elise?!" Victoria snarled through clenched teeth.

Elise disregarded her outburst and stated calmly, "I'm showing you this not to flaunt any intimacy between William and me, nor to boast. This is simply to inform you that if you push me beyond my limits, I will make this public."

Victoria's gaze turned icy as she scrutinized Elise.

"To be perfectly blunt, if you harm the person who means the most to me, I will drag you down with me, even if it costs me my life," Elise continued steadily. "The world will learn about my illicit relationship with William. I'm quite certain your family would find such a scandal utterly unbearable."

Victoria's eyes blazed with unrestrained anger.

So Elise had come to threaten her—to retaliate in kind after Victoria had threatened Elise using James.

"I'm alone in this world, after all. Even if my career has progressed this far, it means nothing compared to the Levines or your family's standing. Losing it would be trivial, as this isn't my first setback. However, if you were to lose William... the loss would be catastrophic, wouldn't it?"

"You're far more cunning than I initially assessed, aren't you?" Victoria glared at Elise with pure contempt. "But I can't help but wonder, what would William think if he discovered you've deceived him, even after he willingly risked his life to save you? Would he still believe you were worth such a sacrifice?"

"Absolutely not, which is precisely why I made it unequivocally clear that I've cut all ties with him," Elise replied, meeting Victoria's gaze directly. "I used him as a tool specifically because I have no intention of reconciling with him."

Yes.

Everything Elise had said yesterday... about coincidence, adult impulses, and repaying debts; those were all deliberate falsehoods.

It was all part of an intricate scheme—ever since Victoria threatened Elise using James, she had been meticulously planning how to make Victoria completely abandon any attempts to harass her.

Elise had no means of controlling Victoria, given her family's immense wealth and unparalleled influence. She needed leverage; however, acquiring any form of leverage against someone like Victoria seemed virtually impossible in such a constrained timeframe.
###Chapter 484

The only person who could truly endanger Victoria now was William.

If William became embroiled in scandal and faced severe public backlash, the Stuarts would immediately terminate Victoria's engagement to protect their family reputation.

But such an annulment would devastate both the Stuart family and Victoria personally. The Stuarts' ambitious plans would crumble, while anyone could see how obsessively in love Victoria was with William.

Therefore, if the Stuarts wouldn't harm Elise for that reason, Victoria certainly wouldn't either.

Still, Victoria wasn't one to be easily intimidated. "Do you honestly believe my family and the Levines can't enforce complete media silence even if you release this? Nobody even knows Ethan nearly died in that car crash. You're well aware our families' influence extends far beyond your wildest imagination!"

"But what if it's Patrick who releases these photographs?" Elise countered calmly.

Victoria's expression darkened immediately, the threat clearly hitting its mark.

Elise maintained her cool composure as she continued, "I understand my own power pales in comparison to your families' reach. But what do you suppose Patrick will do once he obtains these? He won't hesitate for a second to move against you and have your engagement to William dissolved immediately."

"I'm impressed you managed to devise a strategy that maximizes your advantage so quickly. But you're being overconfident!" Victoria's eyes flashed with deadly intent. "You know exactly why those previous 'accidents' occurred, don't you?"

As Elise watched her warily, Victoria's lips curved into a sinister smile. "If I dared to go that far before, what makes you think I'd let this opportunity slip when you came to me directly? The moment you requested this meeting, I never intended to let you leave here alive!"

"Aren't you concerned I might have backup arrangements?" Elise asked without showing fear. "If anything happens to me, everything I just showed you will become public knowledge."

"But I have your phone now," Victoria retorted smugly. "How exactly will you contact Patrick? And I have the resources to reach your backup before Patrick even knows it exists. If he were already aware of this evidence, we wouldn't be having this civilized conversation."

Her meaning was clear—if Patrick knew about the photos, he would have exposed them already.

Yet Elise remained unshaken. "Has it occurred to you that I came prepared? Perhaps I've set alternative plans in motion so Patrick can't be immediately intercepted, ensuring your engagement gets annulled if anything happens to me. In that scenario, I doubt you could reach him first."

Victoria's scowl deepened—never in her entire life had anyone pushed her this far!

Even though William didn't love her, he always indulged her whims.

But Elise... Victoria wanted nothing more than to eliminate her right here, right now!

Her gaze burned with vicious intensity as she glared at Elise, but Elise calmly picked up her purse, casually pulling out a tissue to wipe her fingers.

The gesture appeared deliberately provocative to Victoria, though only Elise knew she was actually steadying her racing heartbeat while discreetly signaling Patrick.

"I'll admit you made a clever move today, Elise. You've managed to pose a genuine threat, and under normal circumstances I'd have to tread carefully around you... if you hadn't forgotten one crucial thing: you never bothered to study me properly! If you had, you'd know I prefer taking risks over leaving threats alive! Even with less than perfect odds, you'll find I'm quite willing to gamble!"

With those final words, Victoria made a sharp gesture. One of the black-clad bodyguards behind her immediately drew a gun, aiming the barrel directly at Elise's head.
###Chapter 485

Elise stared at Victoria with icy composure. Her muscles tightened almost imperceptibly. An involuntary shiver traced its way down her spine.

She suppressed the fear constricting her chest. Her outward calm remained absolute.

Even without visible tells, Victoria knew Elise was terrified. No one could remain unmoved in such a dire situation.

"Didn't expect I'd go this far, did you?" Victoria's laugh was savage. "Any regrets about dying by my hand like this? I'll admit my past attempts on your life failed. But you delivered yourself right to me. You really shouldn't have gotten so arrogant."

"Do you think William would ever forgive you for killing me?" Elise countered.

"Would he forgive me if I didn't?" Victoria shot back. "His treatment of me wouldn't change. Eliminating a threat is simply practical. With you gone, perhaps he'd finally surrender when nothing remains for him."

Elise pressed her lips together. Her gaze turned frigid.

Victoria's patience had evaporated. She rose, looking down at Elise from across the room. Triumph glittered openly in her eyes.

"Make it clean," she ordered the bodyguards as she moved past them.

"Yes, ma'am," they responded in unison.

Victoria signaled another guard to open the door. "My apologies, Elise, but I won't stay for the mess. You've overstayed your welcome in this world. Don't waste time on regrets—you were meant to die long ago!"

The door swung open.

Victoria froze.

A black gun barrel was aimed directly between her eyes.

She narrowed her gaze. The two bodyguards behind her instantly drew their weapons, targeting the man outside.

They found over a dozen men in black stationed in the corridor. All had their guns drawn, trained on the doorway.

The formation parted deliberately.

Patrick Montgomery sat in his wheelchair behind them, his view clear.

His eyes held no emotion. His expression was lethally cold.

The two guards Victoria had posted outside lay unconscious on the floor.

Victoria's scowl deepened. "What is the meaning of this, dear cousin?" she snarled through clenched teeth.

"Exactly what it appears to be," Patrick replied flatly.

"You'd turn against me over this woman?"

"Is she truly the cause of our conflict?" Patrick's tone was derisive. "We can discuss misunderstandings later. Our grandfathers can settle any disputes."

Despite Victoria's deliberate invocation of Montgomery Stuart and Charles Stuart, Patrick remained unmoved. "You're skilled at twisting words. I'm done playing games. Release Elise. Now."

"And if I refuse?" Victoria challenged bluntly.

"Then you will die with her."

"How dare you?!" Victoria shrieked, fury blazing in her eyes. "Kill me openly? Do you believe my grandfather would let you live?"

"He wouldn't let me live even if you survived!" Patrick's retort was sharp and direct.

His icy tone stunned Victoria into momentary silence.

He continued, "We know who was responsible for my accident. I'd be dead if Elise hadn't insisted William save me too. I've been considering how to return the favor."

"What proof do you have that I was involved?" Victoria demanded.

"None," Patrick stated coldly. "I don't require any."
###Chapter 486

Still, Victoria refused to surrender. "Do you honestly believe you'd remain a Montgomery after this? That your grandfather could shield you from the consequences? Forget your grandfather—you'd face arrest! Your entire family legacy would crumble!"

"If you phrase it that way..." Patrick murmured, his resolve visibly wavering.

A smug glint flashed in Victoria's eyes, pleased with her own clever manipulation.

The Stuarts had never been known for their scruples. They buried their conscience whenever profit was involved.

Elise had saved Patrick's life, but what did that matter? Family loyalty meant nothing when power was at stake.

Yet that same ruthless logic ultimately undermined Victoria's scheme, as Patrick suddenly declared, "Actually, it just occurred to me... If this standoff continues, your grandfather or brother would eventually order you to eliminate Elise anyway. I'd have to kill you in retaliation, giving them the perfect excuse to have me arrested immediately. With me gone, my family would be leaderless and powerless against yours. They'd achieve so many objectives with one move—naturally they'd love to see that happen, wouldn't you agree?"

Victoria's expression darkened. A cold shiver traced its way down her spine.

Patrick's scenario was frighteningly plausible—highly probable, even if not absolutely certain.

If this deadlock persisted long enough for Charles Stuart or James Stuart to discover it, they would undoubtedly issue the order.

The bodyguards assigned to her ultimately answered to Charles, and they would execute his commands without hesitation.

"Dear cousin, you don't have unlimited time to ponder," Patrick reminded her pointedly.

Victoria gritted her teeth, unwilling to accept yet another defeat.

Even so, she refused to sacrifice her life merely to eliminate Elise. That woman's existence paled in comparison to her own importance!

Though she had already reached her decision, frustration made her reluctant to concede. "What is it about Elise that drives you to such extremes?! You're willing to throw away your own life! You...and William!"

"I can't speak for William's motivations," Patrick admitted. "But Elise saved me. Ironically, I have your family to thank for that, since you've all been so determined to eliminate me... Otherwise, we wouldn't have forged this bond through adversity."

With that, he pressed her further. "So? Do you need more time to consider?"

His words served as a stark reminder that time was running out, and soon it would be too late for regrets.

Victoria clenched her jaw.

Despite her efforts, the words she needed to say stuck in her throat.

Finally, balling her hands into fists, she suppressed her overwhelming rage and snapped at the men behind her, "Release Elise Scott!"

Elise emerged from the room, leaning heavily on her crutches as she made her way toward Patrick.

Victoria watched her departure, her face contorted with bitter frustration and fury.

Yet she could only stand helplessly as Elise left with Patrick, followed by Patrick's bodyguards filing out one by one, leaving Victoria completely alone.

She turned to find her bodyguard on the phone, answering several rapid-fire questions before ending the call.

"Who was that?" Victoria demanded.

"Your brother, ma'am."

"What did he say?"

"He asked if Patrick left with Elise Scott..."

Victoria scowled. "And?"

"I confirmed he did, and your brother hung up."

Victoria bit her lip, her heart still racing from the narrow escape she had just experienced.
###Chapter 487

Elise finally allowed herself to exhale, the tension slowly leaving her body as she settled into the passenger seat of Patrick's car.

Her calm exterior had been a complete facade.
No one could truly remain fearless staring down the barrel of a gun.

All she could do was endure.
And trust that Patrick was worthy of that trust.

If that trust proved misplaced... well, she would face the consequences.

"I thought you claimed not to be afraid," Patrick teased, though his tone quickly turned serious. "What were you thinking, confronting Victoria alone like that?"

Elise turned her head to look at him. "But you came, didn't you?"

"You'd be dead right now if I hadn't given you that transmitter," Patrick stated grimly.

It was fortunate he had been cautious enough to anticipate Victoria's ruthless nature.
He knew she would stop at nothing to get what she wanted.

"To be honest, I asked for your escort precisely because I believed you would protect me," Elise admitted. "But I was prepared to face her alone if necessary."

Patrick glanced at her, his expression shifting. "So you knew from the start I'd protect you? That I'd intervene during that meeting? You're clever, woman. You anticipated my moves without saying a word. Did it ever occur to you that you might not be as important to me as you assumed?"

"You always repay your debts. That's what you told me," Elise said solemnly.

"I could have been lying."

"Regardless, my gamble paid off in the end."

Patrick fell silent for a moment, momentarily speechless. He was both frustrated and impressed. "You know my preferences lie elsewhere, Elise. Why did you trust me so completely?"

"That's precisely why I found you trustworthy," Elise replied quietly. "Relationships between men and women are often the most fragile things in this world."

Patrick had more he wanted to say.
He was genuinely upset about the reckless risk Elise had taken today.

If Victoria had hesitated even a moment longer back there, she wouldn't have been able to spare Elise even if she wanted to.

Yet seeing Elise's composed demeanor now, he found he couldn't bring himself to scold her further.
He decided to let the matter drop.

After all, she had been pushed to her limits.
Otherwise, she never would have risked her life to meet Victoria.
Or given up on William.

Patrick's car soon arrived at a private airfield.
He wouldn't be accompanying her this time-his injury required attention, and Rockville was his base of operations anyway.

With the tense situation between his family and Victoria's faction, it was necessary for him to stay and manage the fallout.

Escorting her to her plane was already stretching the limits of his generosity.

After exchanging brief farewells, Elise boarded Patrick's private jet.
Hours later, she was back in Crestwood.

Four men in dark suits stood waiting as she disembarked.
One approached her respectfully. "We have orders from Mr. Montgomery to escort you home."

Elise frowned and called Patrick immediately.
His tone brooked no argument. "Don't think you're safe just because you're back in Crestwood. Let me be clear-no place is truly safe for you given the current circumstances. These four gentlemen will be watching over you twenty-four seven. Don't refuse me-I'm too busy to arrange your funeral."

She hadn't intended to refuse.
In fact, all she wanted to say was, "Thank you."

Patrick seemed momentarily taken aback, but quickly recovered. "Just don't die on me. Build your business into something massive. That would be the best repayment you could give me."

"I'll do my absolute best."

Ending the call, Elise followed Patrick's bodyguards to her waiting car.

As she gazed out at the familiar streets of Crestwood passing by, she realized she had somehow become entangled in conflicts far beyond her control.
And she had no idea when it would all end.

Nevertheless, Elise's life gradually returned to some semblance of normalcy after her return to Crestwood.

She had no knowledge of the chaos unfolding in Rockville-such matters wouldn't appear in regular news reports, and she didn't ask Patrick about them either.
###Chapter 488

Days passed in quiet routine. Nothing unusual disturbed the rhythm of life. Until the morning every news outlet exploded with coverage of William Levine and Victoria Stuart's upcoming wedding.

Only three days left? That was remarkably fast.

Elise closed the news app. She stood from her desk, preparing for her next meeting. The four bodyguards Patrick assigned followed her seamlessly. They had become her constant shadows since their arrival. Only the restroom and dressing room provided moments of solitude.

Her staff had initially found the presence unsettling. But familiarity bred acceptance. Now it felt almost normal.

During the meeting, Sophia's calls came repeatedly. Elise declined each one. Finally, she silenced her phone completely. She understood Sophia's motives perfectly. This was about gloating. In the end, Victoria Stuart had won William Levine.

Sophia had become Victoria's devoted follower. But Elise wondered how long that loyalty would protect her. Useful pawns often met ugly ends once their purpose was served.

At Scott Enterprise, Sophia glared at her phone. How dare Elise ignore her! She desperately wanted to rub this victory in her face. After all Elise's arrogant displays, this defeat tasted particularly sweet.

Still, Sophia's mood remained buoyant. She sat in her magnificent office, enjoying her power. No one could order her around here. The memory of her family's shocked faces when she triumphed brought particular satisfaction. They never expected their overlooked daughter would achieve such success. Their preference for her brother had blinded them.

Her phone rang, interrupting her thoughts. She answered with syrupy sweetness. "Miss Stuart."

"There's an important dinner tomorrow night. Be in Rockville."

"But I have prior commitments—"

"Know your place, Sophia Scott. You don't get to argue."

Sophia's grip tightened on her phone. Victoria continued coldly, "Make sure you look presentable. Don't embarrass me."

The line went dead. Sophia ground her teeth. Every month, Victoria summoned her to Rockville for several days. There, she was forced to entertain wealthy, influential older men. Sometimes she provided physical favors to secure their support for the Stuarts. Other times, they filmed compromising videos for blackmail.

Each encounter left her feeling filthy. The memory made her stomach churn. But maintaining appearances required compliance.

Rage burned in her eyes again. She blamed Elise for everything. Without Elise's interference, she wouldn't have needed desperate measures. She wouldn't have sunk this low.

But Victoria wouldn't tolerate Elise's existence forever. Sophia refused to believe Elise could continue thriving. She would wait patiently for Elise's downfall.

Three days later, William and Victoria's wedding dominated all media. Every news outlet competed for coverage. Social media trends overflowed with wedding content. Video platforms streamed the event live.

The wedding was publicly broadcast. Netizens everywhere tuned in via their devices.

Elise watched too, though more from weekend boredom than genuine interest.
###Chapter 489

Elise stared at her phone screen, watching William appear in his crisp white swallowtail tuxedo and bow tie. He looked sharp and commanding, every inch the powerful heir.

The moment he appeared, the live chat exploded with comments. They all blurred together in a sea of admiration.

[So handsome!]
[I'm swooning!]
[Why isn't he on the big screen?]

Elise was about to close the chat window when her phone vibrated with an incoming call. She hesitated when she saw Patrick's name, but ultimately answered.

"Watching the news?" he asked without preamble.

"Which story?" she deflected, though she knew exactly what he meant.

"You can still joke. That's a good sign."

"Why wouldn't I be fine? This was always the inevitable outcome."

"I admire your composure. The world could be ending and you'd remain unshaken."

Elise didn't feel like entertaining his banter. "Shouldn't you be there right now?"

"I am," Patrick confirmed.

"Then why call me?"

"I'm concerned."

"Don't be."

"I have a feeling this won't go as planned. What's your take?" His tone was cryptic.

"I disagree. Who would dare disrupt a union between the Levines and the Stuarts?"

"We'll see," he said ominously.

Elise dismissed his foreboding words and returned her attention to the wedding livestream. William stood patiently at the altar, awaiting his bride.

The ceremony reflected the union of two powerful dynasties. Every detail was more elaborate than any typical wedding.

After considerable fanfare, Victoria finally emerged from the hotel's grand entrance. Her white wedding gown shimmered under the bright lights as she stepped into the waiting limousine.

The live chat erupted once more.

[Stunning!]
[Absolutely breathtaking!]
[A perfect match!]

The camera cut to William's face. His expression was solemn, devoid of any smile. He looked more like an observer than the groom.

Though the shot quickly changed, sharp-eyed viewers had already noticed.

[Does William seem unhappy to anyone else?]
[I thought I saw him smile earlier, but he looks detached... almost like he's not the one getting married.]
[Maybe he doesn't love Victoria. Family pressure?]
[Even the golden spoon crew has their struggles. Suddenly my student loans don't seem so bad...]

The enthusiastic chat quickly buried these observations. More heartwarming scenes demanded attention.

Victoria's limousine proceeded to Rockville's grandest cathedral. The livestream switched to the cathedral's interior, where final preparations were underway. The pews were filled with distinguished guests, though most faces remained off-camera to protect their privacy. The scale of the event was undeniably magnificent.

The journey took thirty minutes. As Victoria arrived, wedding bells pealed through the air, marking the auspicious moment.

She stepped out of the limousine as Mendelssohn's "Wedding March" filled the cathedral. All the guests rose in unison.

Every eye followed Victoria as she walked down the petal-strewn aisle on her father's arm, moving steadily toward William.

William's white swallowtail tuxedo seemed to enhance the sanctuary's sacred atmosphere. He stood like a prince from a fairy tale, waiting for his bride.
###Chapter 490

Elise watched as Victoria approached William, their gazes locking. Tears shimmered visibly in Victoria's eyes.

Andrew Stuart, Victoria's father, gave his daughter's hand a reassuring pat. He then placed her hand into William's and gave the younger man a firm, meaningful clap on the shoulder.

It was a silent gesture, a father entrusting his daughter's future to her husband.

William responded with a respectful nod. Together, he and Victoria turned to face the priest.

The priest delivered the opening rites with fervent passion. Then came the moment everyone anticipated—the exchange of wedding vows.

"William Levine," the priest began, his voice resonating through the grand cathedral. "Do you take Victoria Stuart to be your lawfully wedded wife, in accordance with God's holy ordinance? Do you promise to be faithful to her, to love and to cherish her, for better or for worse, in sickness and in health, forsaking all others, for as long as you both shall live?"

Every eye in the cathedral was fixed on William. The camera lenses focused intently on his face, capturing his every micro-expression.

[Oh my god, he's impossibly handsome! Don't zoom in too close, I might actually lick my screen!]

[What incredible karma did I accumulate in a past life to be blessed with this visual feast?]

[Victoria Stuart is officially the luckiest woman alive. No arguments accepted.]

A profound silence had fallen over the cathedral.

Seconds stretched into an uncomfortably long pause. William had not yet spoken the expected words.

Victoria's composure began to crack. She discreetly extended her pinky finger, gently hooking it with William's where his hand rested at his side.

The intimate gesture was instantly captured by the cameras. Online viewers immediately interpreted it as a sweet, flirtatious prompt.

[That little pinky hook is everything! So adorable!]

[My heart can't take this sweetness... it's melting!]

[She didn't just tug his finger, she tugged directly on my heartstrings.]

Simultaneously, Elise found she could no longer bear to watch. Her famed composure was a fragile facade, crumbling under the weight of the scene.

The camera feed abruptly cut away from their intertwined fingers, zooming back onto William's face.

William's gaze lifted, meeting Victoria's expectant eyes briefly before shifting to the priest.

He finally spoke, his tone somber and deliberate. "I—"

Elise tapped the 'exit' button on the live stream feed before he could finish.

She was perhaps lying to herself, a little. But life offered enough inherent pain without voluntarily subjecting oneself to more.

She switched on the television, selecting a random movie to fill the silence and distract her mind. She needed to calculate how much longer until she could take James away.

After that… she and William would return to being strangers, their paths diverging once more.

Her movie was only halfway through when Patrick called again. His first question was laced with pointed curiosity. "Well? Wasn't that absolutely thrilling?"

Elise frowned, confused by his meaning. "What are you talking about? It was… predictable."

"Oh, come now. Your heart must be made of stone," Patrick remarked, a note of genuine admiration in his voice. "If William knew you were this utterly indifferent, he'd probably be weeping in despair all the way to the detention center."

"What?" Elise's expression froze as his words fully registered. "What did you say?!"

"You didn't know?!" Patrick's surprise was evident. "Wait… did you stop watching the wedding livestream because you couldn't handle seeing it through?"

Elise didn't answer. Instead, she frantically navigated back to the livestream page, only to find it had been terminated.

Failing that, she opened a search engine, typing William and Victoria's names alongside 'wedding.' The search results were bafflingly empty.

Every single article, every post, every piece of content covering the much-hyped 'wedding of the century' had vanished from the internet.

It was as if the entire event had been meticulously erased from digital existence.

"Finished searching over there?" Patrick's unhurried voice came through the phone; he had likely deduced her frantic actions.

"What happened?" Elise asked, forcing her voice into a semblance of calm.
###Chapter 491

Patrick laughed softly, a teasing glint in his eyes. "I never thought I'd see the day you'd actually get nervous."

"I simply want to know what happened," Elise replied, her voice steady despite the turmoil inside. "Victoria's wedding to William directly impacts my safety and my future."

Yes, she was being entirely selfish. All she cared about was whether she could secure James's custody now that the wedding had collapsed.

Patrick shrugged, deciding to end the suspense. "Right before William could say 'I do', the Police Commissioner of Rockville himself stormed in with an arrest warrant. Apparently, William is the prime suspect in a major corporate conspiracy case."

Elise's fingers tightened imperceptibly. Patrick studied her reaction. "Do you understand what this means?"

"The wedding is canceled," she stated flatly.

Patrick chuckled at her composure. "Even I find this hard to watch, Elise. The man went to such extremes for you, willingly destroying his reputation and future just to stop this wedding. And you're completely unmoved?"

"How should I react? Can I change anything?" Elise countered. "I don't see how this differs from simply calling off the engagement. His reputation would be ruined either way!"

"I expected more insight from you," Patrick remarked skeptically.

Elise remained silent, though irritation flickered across her features. Patrick elaborated, "Did you truly believe rejecting the engagement would go smoothly? That Victoria's family wouldn't retaliate against his? Common citizens should never provoke government officials—or did you forget that basic principle?"

"But he's lost everything now," Elise argued.

"In exchange, he protected certain people," Patrick stated unequivocally. As Elise bit her lip, he continued, "Regardless, the Stuarts wouldn't touch William with a ten-foot pole now that he's facing criminal charges. The rejection is coming from their side, not his. William gave them the perfect excuse to annul the engagement without losing face."

"Aren't you concerned they might plot against him?" Elise questioned, skeptical of the logic behind this risky move. "And Victoria will definitely seek revenge for losing William. You know how ruthless she can be."

"Perhaps, but the Stuart family won't be involved," Patrick said plainly. "Our family has a reputation to maintain. Charles wouldn't openly attack William while he's down. In fact, he'd consider the Levines worthless as allies now and would immediately seek a replacement."

"Therefore, no matter how furious or vengeful Victoria feels, she'll be acting alone... So there's truly nothing to fear."

Elise fell silent, completely stunned.

She couldn't determine whether William had truly sabotaged his future for her sake, or if he was genuinely arrested for breaking the law.

Hadn't they agreed he would marry Victoria?

How could he allow the wedding to be called off midway?

"Anyway, I've gathered some information," Patrick informed her. "He's currently being held at Rockville Detention Center. Since the crimes were committed in Crestwood, he'll be transferred there for trial within five days. Do you want to see him now, or wait until he's moved to Crestwood?"

"Right now, I need to process this," Elise replied.

"Then I won't disturb you further," Patrick said, promptly ending the call.

Elise leaned back on her sofa, staring blankly at the ceiling.

There were too many factors to consider, such a complicated mess that she suddenly couldn't distinguish anything clearly.

The next day, news of William Levine's arrest during his own wedding spread nationwide.

The initial media blackout, followed by sudden widespread coverage, clearly indicated the Stuarts had abandoned him.

Just thirty minutes after the first headlines broke, the Stuarts officially announced the annulment of William and Victoria's engagement. Their statement emphasized that their family had served the country with honesty and diligence for over a century, completely free from criminal associations. Therefore, any connection with individuals facing criminal charges would dishonor their legacy.

With the annulment now official, the news spread like wildfire...
###Chapter 492

The internet was ablaze with opinions, as it always was.

Some netizens condemned the Stuarts as cowards, abandoning ship at the first sign of trouble and showing no loyalty.

Others defended the Stuarts' decision, arguing that crime should never be tolerated and deserved immediate, harsh consequences.

Power spoke volumes. The majority sided with the powerful Stuarts, while the Levines had no voice left to defend themselves.

Five days later, a text arrived from Patrick. It informed Elise that William had been transferred back to Crestwood. The location of his detention was included.

She hesitated, her fingers hovering over the screen. Finally, she decided to go.

Patrick had clearly arranged everything in advance. She was granted access to see William without any questions.

They sat across from each other, a heavy silence pressing down on them. Elise suddenly found herself at a loss for words.

His weary appearance, the shadows under his eyes, tugged painfully at her heart.

She had to know the truth. "Are the charges against you real?" she finally asked, her voice low.

"No," William replied, his gaze steady.

Her heart clenched. "You did this just to annul the engagement with Victoria? You fabricated all of it?"

"Yes."

"Why?" The word was a whisper, laden with confusion and hurt.

"So everyone could walk away unscathed. So no one else would get caught in the crossfire."

"And what about you?" Elise demanded, her emotions threatening to break through her carefully constructed calm. "What happens to you?"

"Do you actually care?" A faint, tired smile touched his lips. Despite the stubble and the bleakness of his situation, his handsomeness remained, sharpened by hardship.

Elise didn't answer. What was the point of such questions now? Patrick had told her the charges involved embezzling three billion dollars. A conviction meant William's life was effectively over.

Anger, sharp and sudden, flared within her. "How is this any different from actually marrying Victoria? The outcome is the same for me! You're still gone!"

"Were you worried I'd be executed?" William asked, seemingly amused by her outburst.

"No," she stated coldly, locking her feelings away. "I'm thinking of James. He's suffering because of his father's irresponsible choices. But it's fine. I'll be more careful next time. I'll find him a father who won't throw his life away."

"I'm not dead yet, and you're already planning to replace me?" William let out a dry, humorless laugh.

"I'm completely serious."

"I'm not going anywhere," William said, his voice dropping to a low, intense whisper. "The charges won't stick. I'll be free. This was the only way to force the Stuarts to break the engagement permanently."

"And then what?" Elise shot back, her eyes narrowing. "When they realize you played them, what do you think they'll do? They won't just let this go."

"By then," William said, a dangerous glint in his eyes, "they won't have the power to do anything about it."

Elise frowned, sensing a deeper scheme at play, a hidden card he hadn't revealed.

"Knowing that is enough for now," William continued, his gaze piercing through her. "As long as I'm breathing, Elise, I will never allow James to call another man his father."
###Chapter 493

Elise snapped impatiently, "Just focus on your own situation right now."

Still, William's unwavering confidence offered her a sliver of comfort.

He wouldn't truly destroy himself simply to end an engagement, after all.

No rational person would go that far, and William was exceptionally sharp.

She decided he must be holding back a crucial card for the right moment.

"I'm leaving now," she announced, rising to her feet. She had the reassurance she needed.

"Elise," his voice halted her, unusually soft, almost wistful.

She paused, turning back to face him. "Something else?"

"Take good care of James for me."

"Of course."

"And yourself."

"I always do."

"Don't get involved with anyone else," William added firmly.

Elise was momentarily speechless.

Really? Now, of all times?

"I'll win you back properly once I'm out," he finished.

Her response was swift. "Don't keep me waiting too long."

William's eyes flickered, a brilliant, hopeful light shining within them.

She quickly turned away, a faint blush warming her cheeks.

As she walked out, his words followed her. "I promise."

Stepping outside the detention center, Elise released a heavy sigh.

Despite William's assurances, a lingering unease clung to her chest, stubborn and persistent.

She headed toward her car when a familiar figure caught her eye.

Victoria Stuart.

Victoria had vanished from public view since William's arrest. Official statements claimed she was devastated, confined to her bed by illness.

While she wasn't actually sick, her pallid face clearly showed her distress.

Elise felt no concern for her, but she was puzzled. Why had Victoria come to Crestwood to see William?

Why leave Rockville? Had her family even permitted it?

Ignoring her, Elise continued walking toward her car.

"Elise Scott," Victoria's voice rang out, sharp and hostile.

Elise stopped. "Yes?"

"Were you visiting William?"

"Is that a problem?"

"How dare you?!" Victoria's composure shattered, her voice rising hysterically. "You did this to him! How can you show your face here?"

Elise pressed her lips together, momentarily stunned by the outburst.

"William did all this for you, didn't he? He deliberately committed crimes so my family would annul the engagement! He'd rather ruin his life than be with me! How could he choose this?!"

Victoria grew increasingly agitated, her rage barely contained.

Elise shrugged coolly. "That's between you and him. Take it up with him directly."

"I will make you pay, Elise Scott! If anything happens to William, I'll destroy you!"

With that final threat, Victoria stormed away.

Elise watched her retreating back.

There was no doubt Victoria loved William, but the Stuarts only valued relationships that benefited them.

Once William lost his value, Victoria's one-sided devotion was doomed to end in tragedy.
###Chapter 494

Victoria stood in the visitor's room, her eyes brimming with tears as she stared at William.

He remained completely unmoved by her emotional display.

"Was it worth it?" she finally whispered, her voice trembling. "Sacrificing everything for Elise Scott?"

"This isn't about her," William replied coolly. "The circumstances demanded this course of action."

"I know you did it for her," Victoria insisted, her voice growing stronger with conviction.

"I don't play games when my life is at stake."

"You would die for her!"

William fell silent at her accusation. Victoria laughed bitterly even as tears streamed down her face, making her appear almost hysterical.

"Did you know, William?" she asked suddenly. "I'm getting engaged again."

His eyes flickered with something unreadable.

It seemed he still had some capacity for emotion after all.

Meanwhile, Victoria's tears continued to flow freely, though she seemed completely unaware of them. "That's my family for you—we're all just tools from the moment we're born. My parents, my brother, myself—none of us are exceptions. I always believed I was blessed to be born into this family, even though I had no freedom in life. Even though I don't get to choose who I marry, at least it was someone I loved."

"Even if you didn't love me back, I would have been content just having you by my side after we were married... So how could you do this to me? How am I less than Elise Scott? I met you before she did. I've been with you far longer than she ever was. I love you more than she ever could! Why can't you love me? Why do you refuse to even look at me?"

William's gaze remained distant and detached throughout her emotional outburst. Victoria was working herself into a complete state of distress.

The moment William had been taken away, she had wanted to confront him immediately. But her family had kept her under strict surveillance, forbidding her from leaving. Her grandfather only permitted this visit after she agreed to marry another man.

"I'm sorry," William finally said.

Victoria gasped in disbelief. After everything she had poured out to him, he offered only these two cold words?

She laughed harshly as she glared at him. "You'd rather die than marry me now, wouldn't you?!"
###Chapter 495

"You'll regret this, William Levine!" Victoria Stuart's voice trembled with rage. "Mark my words—I'll make you pay!"

"Don't even try," William's expression turned to ice.

"You left me no choice!" she shrieked, her composure shattering.

"Consider your family's safety before making threats," William warned coldly.

A bitter laugh escaped Victoria's lips. "And what if I refuse?"

"Then your suffering will far exceed what you've experienced today."

"You think you can intimidate me? You?" Victoria scoffed, her eyes blazing. "Your greatest mistake was rejecting me, William! Without my family's protection, you're nothing! Everyone you care about will pay the price!"

"Choose your next actions wisely," William glowered, his knuckles turning white.

Victoria stared at him, disbelief warring with fury.

He had always been distant, but to turn against her like this—over Elise Scott?!

"You'll live to regret this!" she spat out savagely, wiping away angry tears before storming out of the room.

William watched her departure with cold detachment, tension radiating through his clenched fists.

Elise Scott was driving back to her apartment when her phone rang. William's name flashed on the screen.

"Elise Scott," his stern voice came through the speaker.

"Mr. Levine," she responded, maintaining polite formality.

"Come to Levine Manor. I'm waiting."

Her fingers tightened around the steering wheel. Memories of being forcibly taken to Levine Manor before resurfaced vividly.

"Alright," she agreed, though apprehension coiled in her stomach.

Her primary reason for agreeing was James. She needed to take her son away from that place.

Besides, after everything William had done for her, Henry would likely show his grandson some respect and refrain from harming her.

The past was behind them now. She had to navigate whatever came next.

Even with William's arrangements, convincing Henry to let James go wouldn't be easy.

Seeking compromise, she ventured, "Could I at least visit him regularly, like before?"

Henry had few family members left around him now.

His son and daughter-in-law had passed years ago. Ethan's fate remained unknown after his accident. William was currently detained awaiting trial. Only his daughter Nancy and her daughter Cora remained at Levine Manor after Nancy's secret divorce from Ivan York, who had long since departed.

To think the mighty Levine family, once the epitome of wealth and influence, had crumbled to this state...

Henry heard Elise's request but remained silent for a long moment, leaving her feeling increasingly disheartened.

She lacked the power to forcibly take James away, and her conscience wouldn't allow such drastic measures anyway.

Still, James was her son. She couldn't pretend he didn't exist or stay separated from him indefinitely.
###Chapter 496

Suddenly, Henry spoke. "Take him with you."

Elise blinked, certain she had misheard.

She lifted her gaze to Henry. In his eyes, she saw a flicker of resignation. A quiet surrender to the relentless tides of fate.

Even at seventy, confined to a wheelchair, he radiated vitality. His posture remained elegant, his presence commanding. She never imagined witnessing his spirit bow to circumstance. She believed age might weaken his body, but never his indomitable will.

"James will live with you from now on," Henry repeated, his voice steady.

"What about you?" Elise didn't refuse. She couldn't.

Besides, if Henry had reached this decision, he had undoubtedly weighed every consequence.

"I've lived a long life. It's time to let go," he said, his tone somber. "I was once consumed by ambition. I demanded too much. The family prospered as I envisioned… but years of autocratic rule bore bitter fruit. Perhaps my foundation was flawed from the beginning."

There was no denying it.

When a man of such stature uttered these words, it signaled a profound capitulation.

Henry's eyes glistened with unshed tears as he continued. "I was the one who sent William's parents abroad for that business negotiation. They would never have been in that fatal accident if not for me. I was the one who forbade Ethan's relationship with Elizabeth, leading directly to his accident. If I had set aside my pride and prejudice, allowed Elizabeth to stay, Ethan would have been content here at the manor. So much tragedy… avoidable."

"Ethan's situation wasn't your fault…" Elise began, her voice soft.

Henry shook his head, a weary gesture that accepted the burden of all sins.

"And now, William. If I hadn't stubbornly insisted he marry Victoria Stuart, if I had supported him instead… he wouldn't be forced to such extremes to break the engagement."

Elise fell silent. Henry was being crushed beneath the weight of his life's choices. He seemed to have aged decades in mere moments.

"I will go abroad. Stay with Ethan during his treatment. That leaves Nancy and Cora. I intend to leave the majority of Levine Holdings' affairs in Nancy's hands. Though I doubt she can manage it. Her focus was never on business…"

His tone was detached, as if the colossal enterprise of Levine Holdings no longer mattered. A newfound openness colored his words. "And if Nancy cannot sustain it… I will liquidate Levine Holdings. No more fame. No more glory. We'll return to being ordinary people. Perhaps then… there will be less tragedy."

When ambition fades, wealth and influence lose their luster. They become trivial.
###Chapter 497

Henry repeated the words firmly. "Go. Take James with you."

Elise studied him for a long moment, her gaze unwavering. Then she spoke softly. "If you miss him, call me anytime. I'll bring him to you, whether you're in Crestwood or overseas."

Henry gave a slight nod.

"Take care of yourself," Elise said, rising from her seat. She offered him a respectful bow before turning to leave his study.

As she descended to the main lobby, she found James sitting patiently on the sofa, waiting for her.

The moment he spotted her, he immediately rushed forward. "Mommy!"

That clear, youthful voice filled Elise's heart with warmth—her son remained the greatest comfort life had given her.

Kneeling before the boy, she asked gently, "You're going to live with me from now on, alright?"

"Alright. Daddy told me I'd be staying with you after he married Victoria...but he didn't, did he?" James asked, confusion evident in his young voice. "Did Daddy do something bad? Why did they take him away?"

A bitter taste filled Elise's mouth.

The boy had just started elementary school—far too young to be burdened with adult troubles when he should be enjoying a carefree childhood.

"Don't worry about that. Your daddy will be just fine," she reassured him.

"That's what he told me too," James said with conviction. "He promised nothing bad would happen to him, and he told me to take good care of you while he's gone."

Tears welled in Elise's eyes. She felt she owed James so much, especially when he showed such maturity beyond his years.

"Don't cry, Mommy. I'm a big boy now! I'll protect you," James declared seriously, placing his small hands on her cheeks. "No matter what happens, you have me. I'll never leave your side."

"Okay," Elise said, composing herself.

She stood up and took James's hand, leading him toward the exit. Just as they stepped through the front door, she glanced back.

James followed her gaze, and they both saw Henry in his wheelchair, watching them from the second-floor balcony.

Elise whispered to James, "Go on. Say goodbye to your great-grandpa."

James obediently waved at Henry. From that distance, Elise couldn't tell if tears glistened in the old man's eyes.

William's arrest had undoubtedly become a major scandal. Media outlets continued reporting on Levine Holdings for weeks, digging up every piece of dirt they could find about the Levine family.

Reports detailed how Levine Holdings' management was deteriorating daily, along with the mysterious disappearance of Ethan Parker. Rumors spread online claiming Ethan had fled because he knew the Levines faced serious trouble, and since he was adopted anyway, he had no real loyalty.

Despite the wildfire spread of various rumors, the Levine family never came forward to deny any of them.

Instead, they finally made an official announcement: Levine Holdings would undergo liquidation.

No one could have predicted that the wildest rumors would prove true. Countless netizens expressed disbelief at the official statement.

Though only two months had passed since William's arrest, were they really giving up without a fight? It seemed the family had fallen apart immediately after William's departure—was there truly no one left who could hold things together?

Yet that was simply the reality they faced.

Once Levine Holdings announced its liquidation, every major corporation began circling like vultures.

After all, Levine Holdings had been a corporate giant that dominated multiple industries in Crestwood. Any company that acquired even part of it would immediately rise to prominence.

Naturally, no single corporation possessed the resources to acquire all of Levine Holdings alone.
###Chapter 498

Elise Scott was one of many submitting tenders to acquire Levine Holdings.

She had no interest in the Levine family estate itself. But she felt compelled to preserve something for James.

Who could predict the future? Sometimes a single ember could reignite an entire blaze.

Besides, she remained partners with Patrick Montgomery, who was equally determined to acquire Levine Holdings. He offered her unlimited financial backing without hesitation. They frequently strategized about which subsidiaries or shares to target for a successful takeover.

It became routine for Patrick to share insider information after their business discussions. Though he presented it as casual generosity, Elise suspected he simply enjoyed gossiping like a bored socialite.

"Victoria will announce her new engagement in just a few days," Patrick revealed.

Elise frowned.

She'd heard from Patrick previously that Victoria's family had swiftly reined her in. But this development seemed remarkably fast!

William's trial hadn't even begun, yet Victoria was already remarrying?

"Which family this time?" Elise inquired.

She'd asked before, but Patrick had claimed ignorance until now. He never withheld information from Elise—it served no purpose, and she lacked sufficient influence anyway.

"The Cranstons from the capital," Patrick stated solemnly.

Elise's brow furrowed deeply.

The Cranstons were the wealthiest family in the nation. The Stuarts, despite their considerable influence, ranked only fourth.

Somehow, they'd managed to latch onto the Cranstons' coattails?!

This maneuver perfectly aligned with the Stuarts' character—with William no longer an option, they needed someone superior to avoid losing face.

"Doesn't that concern you?" Elise asked Patrick.

Previously, Charles Stuart had operated within established boundaries, cultivating political connections through conventional methods while maintaining business dominance. Now he was vaulting ahead by aligning with a more powerful family. Success would easily dethrone the Montgomery family.

"Absolutely. Haven't you noticed I've been visiting less frequently lately?" Patrick admitted.

His family had been thrown into disarray upon learning Victoria would marry Frederick Cranston, the Cranstons' third son.

"Do you have a strategy?" Elise pressed.

"It's all subterfuge at this stage," Patrick replied. "I'd prefer not to discuss details yet."

Elise didn't press further. Sometimes ignorance provided protection, and she didn't want to burden Patrick unnecessarily.

"Incidentally, William's trial begins in two weeks. Has he gathered evidence for his defense? Without proof of innocence, the enormous capital involved could mean twenty-five years to life. Possibly even a life sentence."

Elise bit her lip.

She hadn't visited William often, but each encounter left her with the distinct impression he would emerge unscathed.

She'd previously wondered about the source of his confidence. Now, her only choice was to trust him completely.

"Well, we enjoyed two months of peace," Patrick sighed. "Now the ominous feeling of impending chaos returns. Fortunately, the Stuarts are keeping Victoria tightly controlled. Otherwise, she'd have harassed you relentlessly by now. Her family fears any outburst could jeopardize her wedding to Frederick Cranston."
###Chapter 499

Elise had always been cautious of Victoria.

The two months of tranquility had left her unsettled, a constant unease gnawing at her. She feared Victoria might strike unexpectedly, a sudden storm on a clear day.

William’s perspective offered a sliver of relief. At least she was safe until Victoria’s wedding.

With that thought, Elise didn’t prolong her call with William. He was a busy man, and they soon ended the conversation.

She sat in her office for a moment, hesitating. Then, accompanied by the four bodyguards William had arranged, she made her way to the detention center.

In his prison uniform and with a shaved head, William looked noticeably thinner.

He had been losing weight steadily since his arrest.

Even though he always smiled during her visits, the change was impossible to ignore. He wasn’t doing well.

“Don’t look at me like that,” he said, his voice weak. “I know I’m not much to look at right now.”

He was a far cry from the dazzling scion of the Levine family he once was.

Elise averted her gaze. It wasn’t that he looked hideous to her. It was the pain of seeing him so gaunt.

She remembered his gastric issues. He had been so careful, keeping them under control.

A relapse would bring him agonizing pain.

Didn’t he regret putting himself in this position?

“Your grandfather is liquidating the family estates,” Elise told him.

“I know,” William replied. “We spoke before my arrest. Success attracts too much attention. Liquidating is the best way to preserve our legacy. When we have nothing, no one will care about us anymore.”

“Don’t you think it’s a shame?” Elise asked.

Despite his words, it was a vast fortune, a remarkable legacy. To let it all go just like that?

“We’ve had enough money. We’ve enjoyed our time in the spotlight.” William seemed utterly indifferent.

Even Henry wasn’t as relieved about letting go.

Perhaps because William hadn’t built this legacy himself, the loss didn’t cut as deep.

“How is your trial progressing?” Elise couldn’t help but ask.

“It’s going well,” William said. “I just received the court summons for the 27th. That gives me twelve days.”

“Don’t lie to me, William,” Elise said.

Her heart skipped a beat. She was suddenly afraid to face the trial’s outcome, her confidence wavering.

She had no idea how confident William truly was. What if it was the end for everyone?

“I wouldn’t dare,” William said humbly.

“I’m serious.”

“Don’t worry,” William assured her. “If I’m locked up for life, I’ll let you marry someone else.”

When Elise remained silent, he added, “I’m just kidding. You’re not even married to me—”

“I’ll marry you if you get out unharmed,” Elise said suddenly.

William stared at her, stunned.

Happiness had come out of nowhere, leaving him struggling to process it.

“I always keep my promises,” Elise stated clearly.

“Okay.”

His voice quivered, thick with emotion.

“By the way…” Elise quickly changed the subject. Flirting felt inappropriate in these surroundings.

“Yes?”

“Victoria is marrying Frederick Cranston, the third son of the nation’s wealthiest family.”

“Really?” William raised an eyebrow, but showed little interest.

“Aren’t you surprised?”

“Not at all,” William replied. “The Stuarts need power and money to support their ambitions. There aren’t many dynasties in the country that would interest them. Someone was bound to arrange a marriage for Victoria, one way or another.”

Elise asked, “Don’t you feel like you owe her at all?”
###Chapter 500

From the very beginning, it was only Victoria who was truly in love with William—every terrible thing she had done was for his sake.

But did William feel any guilt about her current situation?

"I do, but not as much as you might expect," William confessed. "I can't pretend to overlook her repeated attempts to have you killed."

"But she's genuinely in love with you—"

"You matter more to me than anything," William stated firmly.

In other words, he was willing to endure anything Victoria directed at him, but he wouldn't allow her to harm Elise.

Elise's heart fluttered, and she felt deeply moved by William's protective nature.

William raised an eyebrow, his expression turning playful. "What, would you prefer I be kinder to her?"

Despite his current weakened state, he still carried himself with the authority of a powerful CEO.

Did he not realize his family's fortune was nearly depleted?

"No," Elise replied simply.

"Really?" he pressed.

Elise shrugged lightly. "I was just curious."

A smile touched William's lips. "You know, Elise? It makes me happy when you show this possessive side."

Elise thought to herself that there was definitely something unusual about him. Still, she didn't stay long at the detention center and soon departed.

Her mood darkened the moment she stepped outside. William only had twelve days remaining.

What would happen? Would he be exonerated and released?

The following twelve days weighed heavily on Elise, filled with stress and anxiety. Since it wasn't the weekend, she had to take James to school before heading to the courthouse.

"Mommy? Is something bothering you?" James asked, his young eyes studying her intently.

"What makes you say that?" Elise forced a cheerful smile.

"You gave me milk when I asked for yogurt," he explained. "And you brought out my winter uniform even though I told you we need spring uniforms for today's special event. You're usually talking and smiling during our drives, but today you're so quiet. What's wrong?"

James listed his observations clearly, leaving Elise scrambling for an explanation.

"I didn't sleep well last night," she admitted truthfully.

It was the honest truth—she had barely slept, leaving her exhausted today.

Naturally, she was also unusually tense because William's trial was happening today.

She hadn't told James about it, believing children shouldn't carry adult worries.

He deserved to remain carefree and innocent...

Yet James suddenly asked, "Is Daddy's trial today, Mommy?"

Elise was taken aback—trying to hide things from her perceptive son was clearly pointless.

But how had he found out?

James seemed to read her thoughts. "I read the news too, Mommy."

"I'm sorry, sweetheart. I just didn't want you to worry."

"I'm not worried, Mommy. Daddy is amazing—he'll be fine. You don't need to worry either."

Her son was comforting her instead.

Elise gently patted his head. "Alright, I won't worry then."

They arrived at James's school and got out of the car. She walked him to the front gates where he waved goodbye.

As she watched him disappear into the school, Elise's vision blurred with emotion. She wondered if they would be reunited with William after today.

Never before had she longed for a reunion so intensely.

Since having James, her desire for their family's happiness had grown exponentially.

'Don't let me down today, William Levine...'
